Title: Data Engineer
Description:
Company overview:

Blue Orange Digital is a boutique data & AI consultancy that delivers enterprise-grade results. We design and build modern data platforms, analytics, and ML/AI Agent solutions for mid‑market and enterprise clients across Private Equity, Financial Services, Healthcare, and Retail.

Our teams work with technologies like Databricks, Snowflake, dbt, and the broader Microsoft ecosystem to turn messy, real-world data into trustworthy, actionable insight.

We’re a builder‑led, client‑first culture that prizes ownership, clear communication, and shipping high‑impact work.

Note: Please submit your resume in English, as all application materials must be in English for review and consideration.

Position Overview

Blue Orange Digital is seeking a Mid‑Level Data Engineer to join a 26‑week enterprise data warehouse implementation for a global commodities trading and supply chain company, embedded full‑time from Week 3 through Week 22.

You will build and maintain Bronze and Silver layer pipelines on Microsoft Fabric, implementing ingestion from multi‑source ERP and CTRM systems and transformations that feed Gold‑layer data products across trading, risk, and finance domains.

Working within a project‑based delivery team alongside a Senior Data Engineer and Solutions Architect, you will contribute to a structured, phase‑gated delivery with clear milestones and real production output.

Responsibilities


Implement Bronze ingestion pipelines from source systems including CTRM instances (Irely) and ERP/SAP S/4HANA feeds using Microsoft Fabric Data Factory
Build Silver‑layer transformation logic in Fabric notebooks using PySpark and SQL on Delta Lake tables, covering cleansing, standardization, and business rule application
Support development of Gold‑layer outputs aligned to Trader P&L, Global Position, and Financial Consolidation data products
Write and maintain pipeline code on OneLake/Lakehouse following medallion architecture patterns (Bronze/Silver/Gold) established by the Technical Lead
Participate in code reviews and apply BOD and engagement‑level coding standards consistently
Conduct unit and integration testing for pipeline outputs; document data flows and transformation logic
Coordinate with client‑side Fabric/Azure engineers to align ingestion patterns and shared schemas
Support documentation of data lineage, transformation logic, and pipeline metadata throughout the engagement


Requirements


5–6 years of data engineering experience with hands‑on pipeline development across multi‑source environments
Proficiency with Microsoft Fabric (OneLake, Lakehouse, Notebooks, Data Factory) or a comparable Azure data stack (ADF, ADLS Gen2, Databricks/Spark)
Solid SQL and PySpark skills with working knowledge of Delta Lake table formats
Experience building end‑to‑end pipelines for multi‑source integration
Familiarity with Bronze/Silver/Gold medallion architecture patterns
Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related technical field (or equivalent experience)


Preferred Qualifications


Exposure to ERP or CTRM source system data (SAP, Irely, or similar commodity/trading platforms)
Microsoft Azure data certification (DP‑203 or equivalent)
Experience in commodity trading, logistics, or financial services data environments
Basic


Salary: $ 5,500 - 7,200 [ USD ] monthly

Background checks may be required for certain positions/projects.

Blue Orange Digital is an equal-opportunity employer.

  • Conduct unit and integration testing for pipeline outputs; document data flows and transformation logic
  • Coordinate with client‑side Fabric/Azure engineers to align ingestion patterns and shared schemas
  • Support documentation of data lineage, transformation logic, and pipeline metadata throughout the engagement

Requirements

  • 5–6 years of data engineering experience with hands‑on pipeline development across multi‑source environments
  • Proficiency with Microsoft Fabric (OneLake, Lakehouse, Notebooks, Data Factory) or a comparable Azure data stack (ADF, ADLS Gen2, Databricks/Spark)
  • Solid SQL and PySpark skills with working knowledge of Delta Lake table formats
  • Experience building end‑to‑end pipelines for multi‑source integration
  • Familiarity with Bronze/Silver/Gold medallion architecture patterns
  • Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related technical field (or equivalent experience)

Preferred Qualifications

  • Exposure to ERP or CTRM source system data (SAP, Irely, or similar commodity/trading platforms)
  • Microsoft Azure data certification (DP‑203 or equivalent)
  • Experience in commodity trading, logistics, or financial services data environments
  • Basic

Salary: $ 5,500 - 7,200 [ USD ] monthly

Background checks may be required for certain positions/projects.

Blue Orange Digital is an equal-opportunity employer.

Title: Senior Data Analyst (Relocate to Cyprus)
Description:
We are looking for an experienced Data Analyst to join our Player Care team at Scorewarrior. The Player Care team is at the forefront of providing customer service to our players, as well as safeguarding the community integrity through enforcing Fair & Safe Play, improving moderation efforts, and ensuring operational excellence while at it.




Scorewarrior is a game developer and publisher based in Limassol, Cyprus. The company focuses on the 4X strategy genre, with an ambitious goal to grow its hit game, Total Battle, to become the #1 strategy game in the world, and this role is key to achieving this. Our team consists of 200+ passionate, talented, self-driven, and continually developing professionals who love making high-quality games.




Our product: We are currently working on Total Battle — an MMO 4X strategy game with a deep political system and lots of player interaction. Players take on the role of a commander and need to develop their city, forge alliances, fight against monsters, and conquer other players. We're constantly developing new game mechanics, and the project is continuing to grow.




At the heart of our Player Care department, our mission is to excite our players with exceptional customer care, ensuring they feel valued and supported throughout their journey with us.




What you’ll do

Build and maintain the single "Source of Truth" semantic data layer for the entire Player Care department, enabling non-technical leads to perform ad-hoc analysis.
Design, build, and maintain reliable metrics dashboards tailored to the operational needs of different Support and Trust & Safety teams.
Ensure all operational metrics (CSAT, SLA, etc.) are clearly defined, reliable, and calculated correctly across all reports.
Assist and mentor the support team with SQL query composition and data literacy.
Conduct A/B tests to measure the effect of Support and Trust & Safety initiatives on relevant departmental and broader business metrics.
Analyze the correlation between support quality, bug-related friction, and subsequent player behavior, with a strong focus on VIP cohorts.
Partner with the safety team to analyze data for fraud detection, including social engineering and account sharing.
Perform time-series forecasting for workload volume based on the LiveOps calendar to optimize shift headcount and SLA coverage.
As a future ambition, build and maintain an early warning predictive model for VIP player churn.




What you have

Strong SQL skills and data modeling experience
Experience with BI tools (e.g. Tableau or equivalents)
Proven experience in product or operational analytics
Ability to design analytical events and metrics aligned with product goals
Strong analytical thinking and ability to translate data into actionable insights
Confident knowledge of mathematical statistics and probability theory
Hands-on experience with causal inference and A/B testing
Patience and willingness to advocate for data literacy and help operational teams learn basic data concepts




Nice to haves

Hands-on experience with ClickHouse and dbt
Experience analyzing LiveOps features, complex game economies, or VIP player behavior, ideally in mid-core or 4x strategy games.
Experience working with Customer Support or Trust & Safety (fraud detection, toxicity)
Experience working with large player bases and complex event-driven systems




Where you’ll work

We value face-to-face communication, and we strongly believe that genuine human interactions move us closer to our ambitious goal. This is part of our culture, and this is why this job offers no remote or hybrid work opportunities.

You will be working in Limassol, Cyprus. If you’re not from around here, fear not! Our dedicated relocation team will provide comprehensive support helping you and your family move to Limassol.




What we offer

The best team in the world!
An excellent remuneration package, including a 13th salary.
Fully-covered relocation to Limassol, Cyprus for you and your family.
A modern and sunny office space close to the sea.
Private health insurance for you and your family members.
A monthly school allowance for your children at pre-school and school-age.
Lunch compensation.
Personal development opportunities paid for by the company: professional and language courses, books, etc.
Bonuses for outstanding results, years worked, getting married, and the birth of a child.
Monthly office parties and epic, annual offsite company birthday and Christmas parties.

Title: Risk Data Analyst (m/w/d) Reporting und Data Analyst für Risikoklassifizierungsverfahren
Description:
Die Sparkassen Rating und Risikosysteme (SR) ist der zentrale Dienstleister für Verfahren des Risikomanagements in der Sparkassen-Finanzgruppe. Wir unterstützen die Institute mit Standardlösungen in den Bereichen der Risikomessung und -steuerung, der regulatorischen Banksteuerung sowie im Vertrieb mit Data-Analytics-Lösungen. Gemeinsam mit unseren Partnern bieten wir den Instituten, von der Konzeption der Verfahren, über deren Weiterentwicklung, IT-Umsetzung und Umsetzungsunterstützung, zentrale und standardisierte Lösungen an.

Risk Data Analyst (m/w/d) Reporting und Data Analyst für Risikoklassifizierungsverfahren
ab sofort suchen wir in dem Fachbereich Reporting und IRBA-Betrieb




Das erwartet Dich:
Datenmanagement und Programmierung: Arbeite mit verschiedenen Programmiersprachen, um große Datenmengen gemäß fachlichen Anforderungen aufzubereiten und zu analysieren.
Reportentwicklung: Entwickle bestehende Auswertungen weiter und konzipiere neue Reports zur Unterstützung unserer zentralen Risikoklassifizierungsverfahren.
Datenanalyse und Strukturierung: Analysiere und strukturiere Portfoliodaten, um fundierte Kennzahlen bereitzustellen und mit Benchmark-Informationen anzureichern.
Ergebnisinterpretation und Kommunikation: Interpretiere und kommuniziere Analyseergebnisse effektiv an die Institute der Sparkassen-Finanzgruppe.
Technologieimplementierung: Baue die vorhandene Infrastruktur aus und implementiere State-of-the-Art-Technologien, einschließlich künstlicher Intelligenz, um eine automatisierte und innovative Reporting-Strecke zu ermöglichen.
Schnittstellenfunktion: Stimme dich mit internen und externen Stakeholdern (z. B. Fachbereiche, IT, externe Dienstleister) zu fachlichen Themen ab.




Das bringst Du mit:
Erfolgreich abgeschlossenes Studium mit quantitativer Ausrichtung in den Fachrichtungen (Wirtschafts-)Informatik, (Finanz-)Mathematik, Physik, BWL / VWL oder vergleichbarer Abschluss
Idealerweise erste Berufserfahrung im Umfeld eines Kreditinstitutes oder einer Beratungsgesellschaft
Ausgeprägte analytische Fähigkeiten und die Fähigkeit, komplexe Sachverhalte verständlich darzustellen
Idealerweise sehr gute Programmierkenntnisse vorzugsweise mit R oder Python, sicherer Umgang mit SQL und Kenntnisse in einem gängigen Datenbankmanagementsystem
Berührungspunkte mit einer Datenvisualisierungssoftware (z. B. Qlik Sense)
Sehr gute Deutschkenntnisse in Wort und Schrift auf C1-Niveau
Ausgeprägtes teamorientiertes Handeln mit einem Schuss Humor




Wir bieten Dir:
Eine unbefristete Tätigkeit in einem hoch versierten Arbeitsumfeld mit anspruchsvollen Aufgaben und einem attraktiven Vergütungspaket
30 Tage Urlaub + 2 Bankfeiertage und zusätzlichen Sonderurlaub für besondere Anlässe
Betriebliche Altersvorsorge und vermögenswirksame Leistungen
Individuelle interne | externe Weiterbildung über unsere Akademie
Flexible Arbeitszeiten mit Gleitzeit und Option zum mobilen Arbeiten für eine echte Work-Life-Balance
Betriebliches Gesundheitsmanagement
Zuzahlung zum Jobticket und Essensgutscheine
Dienstrad-Leasing (JobRad) als zusätzliches Mobilitätsangebot
Kostenfreie Girokontoführung inkl. Visa Card Gold bei der Berliner Sparkasse
Interne Veranstaltungen: Meet-Ups, Afterworks, Sommer- und Weihnachtsfeste
Zentraler und moderner Arbeitsplatz im Herzen und über den Dächern von Berlin




Kontakt:

Herr Emanuel Reitzenstein
per E-Mail an karriere@s-rating-risikosysteme.de

Wir freuen uns auf Deine vollständigen Bewerbungsunterlagen, bitte ausschließlich über unser Online-Bewerberportal unter
www.s-rating-risikosysteme.de/ Karriere_bei_uns

Bitte beachten Sie, dass die S Rating und Risikosysteme GmbH keine unaufgefordert durch Personalvermittlungen zugesandten Profile akzeptiert. Kommt es zur Berücksichtigung oder Einstellung von Kandidat:innen, deren Profile durch Personalvermittlungen mit denen kein Vertragsverhältnis besteht, übersandt wurde, besteht daher kein Vergütungsanspruch.

Title: Data Engineer (m/w/d)
Description:
About us

At Venatic Intelligence, we build software for complex data analysis, focusing on the challenge of making sense of massive, fragmented, and unstructured datasets. To address this, we develop applications that integrate disparate data sources, resolve complex entities, and map hidden networks.




We are looking for a Data Engineer (m/w/d) to join our team in Berlin. You will play a central role in expanding our core architecture, connecting new data streams, and building the analytical tools that power our applications.




What you'll do

Design and build robust data pipelines that extract, clean, and load complex datasets from highly diverse sources.
Engineer custom data acquisition tools that rely on proxy-routed networks to securely navigate and extract information from complex digital environments.
Manage and scale our core database architecture. You will work with graph databases (e.g., Neo4j) for entity resolution and search indices (e.g., Elasticsearch) for rapid retrieval.
Develop resilient APIs and backend services to seamlessly integrate new capabilities into our platform.
Build and refine specialized analytical dashboards and UI components using Vue.js/Nuxt 3, allowing users to interact directly with complex data models.
Deploy, optimize, and secure our infrastructure across various hyperscalers (e.g., AWS, GCP, OCI).
Take technical ownership of your projects, from initial architecture decisions to deployment, using standard workflows (e.g., GitHub, Jira, Confluence).




What we're looking for

Roughly 2 years of hands-on experience in software engineering, data engineering, or backend development.
Strong programming skills in Node.js, TypeScript, and Python.
Demonstrable experience in building data pipelines, handling unstructured data, or developing specialized data extraction tools.
Solid understanding of data modeling. You should know your way around NoSQL, graph (e.g., Neo4j), and search (e.g., Elasticsearch) architectures.
Practical frontend experience, ideally with Vue.js/Nuxt 3, to build intuitive interfaces for complex data.
Comfortable managing cloud environments (e.g., GCP, OCI, AWS) and navigating modern engineering toolchains.
You are a pragmatic problem-solver. You care about building secure, highly performant software that works reliably in the real world.
Fluent English. Proficiency in German is a plus, but not required.




What we offer

A permanent role in a fast-growing, technology-driven firm.
The opportunity to build high-impact software and tackle complex architectural challenges.
Close mentorship within a small team of highly skilled engineers and analysts.
Competitive compensation.
28 days of annual leave.
Flexible working hours with the option to work remotely.
A tailored benefits package that fits your personal needs.




To apply, please send your CV and a brief introduction to hr@venatic-intelligence.com.




Venatic Intelligence is committed to building a diverse and inclusive team. We welcome applications from people of all backgrounds, and we believe that a range of perspectives, experiences, and languages makes our work sharper and our analysis stronger. We hire on the basis of merit and are committed to equal opportunity for all candidates, regardless of background, identity, or circumstance.

Title: Data Engineer (m/f/d)
Description:
Berlin Mitte


Hybrid (2 days/week remote)
€60,000-75,000 + VSOP equity


Help us decarbonize cement and concrete - not tomorrow, today.

alcemy is on a mission to keep 100 million tons of CO₂ out of the atmosphere every year by 2030. Making concrete accounts for around 8% of global CO₂ emissions, and our machine-learning software runs inside cement and concrete plants 24/7 to cut it - without sacrificing quality or driving up costs. We're already live in a third of Germany's cement plants and 30+ ready-mix concrete plants.

The role

As our Data Engineer, you'll own the data that powers everything we do - the flow of cement and concrete plant data from raw, messy ingestion through cleaning, to the pipelines our machine-learning recommendations run on. The split is roughly 50% data engineering, 50% backend, and it's refreshingly hands-on with customers: you'll dig into real-world data, explain what you find to non-technical people, and make getting data into alcemy dramatically easier.

What You'll Do


Build and own the pipelines that ingest and clean messy plant data into something our ML can use
Help us move from strict, structured data requirements toward flexible, AI-powered ingestion - so customers onboard with far less friction
Track down data anomalies and explain your findings to customers in plain language
Build and maintain the APIs and PostgreSQL databases behind our products, and keep production running smoothly
Work shoulder to shoulder with customers, sales, and customer success - translating between the technical and the non-technical


Requirements

You'll thrive here if...

Above all, you care about climate change and want your work to count.

Must-haves


You've deployed production-grade Python applications
You love getting to grips with an unfamiliar codebase quickly, and you're eager to start delivering work that matters from the start
You're comfortable working directly with customers, sales, and customer success - patient, plain-spoken, and at ease with non-technical enterprise teams
You're open to using AI tools to augment how you work


Nice-to-haves


Experience with Flask or Django and Fast API
PostgreSQL or similar SQL databases
Some exposure to machine learning


Don't tick every box? We'd still love to hear from you.

How We Work


No lone warriors - you'll work collaboratively alongside senior and junior engineers, owning features end to end: build, test, review, deploy
Small team, direct customer exposure, fast startup pace - so async communication, sharp prioritisation, transparency, and direct feedback are the norm, and how we work is something we shape together, and keep improving
We use Claude Code responsibly - AI to move fast, engineers always in charge and accountable, no AI-slop merge requests
Hybrid by design (Berlin Mitte office, up to 2 days remote, plus offsites and boat trips), with real room to grow along technical-expert or management tracks - and the chance to lead a product area within a couple of years


Benefits


25 days' vacation - plus an extra day for every 2 years here, and the days between Christmas and New Year off
€1,500/year and 3 days/quarter for your own development
The Mac of your choice
An office in the heart of Berlin Mitte


Our Tech Stack


Backend & data: Python throughout - FastAPI (auto-generated Swagger/OpenAPI), SQLAlchemy, pydantic, pytest, mypy; pandas, polars, numpy, sklearn; uv for dependencies
Infra: PostgreSQL, AWS on Kubernetes managed with Terraform, ArgoWorkflows for scheduled jobs, GitLab for code
Frontend: React & Next.js in TypeScript with TailwindCSS; Blueprint.js / Radix UI; TanStack Query or axios; Streamlit for internal tools


Our Hiring Process is fully remote


Intro call with our Recruiter
Meet our Hiring Manager
A take-home task that mirrors the real work you'd be doing
Technical interview (90 min) with two engineers, including 30 min of live coding
Team-fit interview (45 min) with two future teammates


At alcemy, we're building a team as diverse as the industry we're transforming. We welcome applications from everyone and assess every candidate on merit alone - regardless of gender, age, ethnic or national origin, religion or worldview, disability, sexual orientation, or gender identity. We especially encourage people who are underrepresented in tech to apply. If you need any adjustments to take part fully in our hiring process, just let us know and we'll make them.

Note: We don't currently offer visa sponsorship, so you'll need a valid EU work visa.

  • Work shoulder to shoulder with customers, sales, and customer success - translating between the technical and the non-technical

Requirements

You'll thrive here if...

Above all, you care about climate change and want your work to count.

Must-haves

  • You've deployed production-grade Python applications
  • You love getting to grips with an unfamiliar codebase quickly, and you're eager to start delivering work that matters from the start
  • You're comfortable working directly with customers, sales, and customer success - patient, plain-spoken, and at ease with non-technical enterprise teams
  • You're open to using AI tools to augment how you work

Nice-to-haves

  • Experience with Flask or Django and Fast API
  • PostgreSQL or similar SQL databases
  • Some exposure to machine learning

Don't tick every box? We'd still love to hear from you.

How We Work

  • No lone warriors - you'll work collaboratively alongside senior and junior engineers, owning features end to end: build, test, review, deploy
  • Small team, direct customer exposure, fast startup pace - so async communication, sharp prioritisation, transparency, and direct feedback are the norm, and how we work is something we shape together, and keep improving
  • We use Claude Code responsibly - AI to move fast, engineers always in charge and accountable, no AI-slop merge requests
  • Hybrid by design (Berlin Mitte office, up to 2 days remote, plus offsites and boat trips), with real room to grow along technical-expert or management tracks - and the chance to lead a product area within a couple of years

Benefits

  • 25 days' vacation - plus an extra day for every 2 years here, and the days between Christmas and New Year off
  • €1,500/year and 3 days/quarter for your own development
  • The Mac of your choice
  • An office in the heart of Berlin Mitte

Our Tech Stack

  • Backend & data: Python throughout - FastAPI (auto-generated Swagger/OpenAPI), SQLAlchemy, pydantic, pytest, mypy; pandas, polars, numpy, sklearn; uv for dependencies
  • Infra: PostgreSQL, AWS on Kubernetes managed with Terraform, ArgoWorkflows for scheduled jobs, GitLab for code
  • Frontend: React & Next.js in TypeScript with TailwindCSS; Blueprint.js / Radix UI; TanStack Query or axios; Streamlit for internal tools

Our Hiring Process is fully remote

  • Intro call with our Recruiter
  • Meet our Hiring Manager
  • A take-home task that mirrors the real work you'd be doing
  • Technical interview (90 min) with two engineers, including 30 min of live coding
  • Team-fit interview (45 min) with two future teammates

At alcemy, we're building a team as diverse as the industry we're transforming. We welcome applications from everyone and assess every candidate on merit alone - regardless of gender, age, ethnic or national origin, religion or worldview, disability, sexual orientation, or gender identity. We especially encourage people who are underrepresented in tech to apply. If you need any adjustments to take part fully in our hiring process, just let us know and we'll make them.

Note: We don't currently offer visa sponsorship, so you'll need a valid EU work visa.

Title: Working Student AI & Process Automation (f/m/x)
Description:
At Seven Senders, we connect people and markets, making international shipping simple.

We are Europe’s leading platform for cross-border parcel delivery, supporting e-commerce companies in scaling internationally and reaching millions of consumers across borders. By combining cutting-edge technology with strong local carrier networks, we ensure parcels move efficiently from warehouse to doorstep — whether speed or cost efficiency is the priority.

Founded in Berlin in 2015, Seven Senders has grown into a team of 200+ logistics and tech experts. Today, we proudly work with clients such as Flaconi, Shop Apotheke, and Tamaris, shaping the future of European delivery together.

Job Description

AI is one of our top strategic priorities at Seven Senders, and we are serious about making it real. We have launched a dedicated AI Taskforce to help our teams identify, design, and implement use cases that make everyday work simpler and faster.

As a Working Student in our AI Taskforce, you are at the center of that effort, not just supporting it. You will work directly with business teams across the company — attending workshops to understand where the friction is, translating that into concrete requirements, and building solutions hands-on. You will be a builder and a bridge: technical enough to execute, structured enough to define, and communicative enough to work side-by-side with colleagues who do not share your technical background.

Your main responsibilities:


Partner with teams across departments to turn AI use cases with measurable impact into working solutions from scoping to production
Translate process knowledge from operational colleagues into clear, executable requirements
Build automation hands-on using LLMs, no-code/low-code platforms (Claude, Make.com, or similar), scripts, and APIs
Measure what you ship: track time saved, error rates, and volume handled to verify solutions actually deliver
Document every engagement thoroughly so that solutions can be maintained, understood, and iterated independently


Qualifications

This is what you need to succeed:


Currently enrolled in Computer Science, Software Engineering, Data Science, or a closely related technical degree (Bachelor's or Master's)
Eagerness to learn & fully leverage agentic AI using LLMs and automation platforms (Make.com, or similar); you pick them up fast and go beyond the surface, and actively track what's new in the field
Comfortable with APIs and scripting (Python or similar); you understand how software systems connect and exchange data
You explain technical concepts clearly to non-technical colleagues, and you're genuinely good at it
Structured, independent, and action-oriented; fluent in English (German is a plus)


Additional Information

What we offer:

Flexible&Hybrid: Our office is in Berlin, but in this role, you can work anywhere within Germany. If you wish, you can work from almost anywhere for up to 2 months per year. And if you wish, you can work from almost anywhere for up to 2 months per year.

Benefits: We offer company lunches twice per month, a corporate benefits platform, and German language courses.

Mission Zero: Climate-neutral and long-term impact. With our green initiatives, we are reaching climate neutrality for ourselves, and hopefully, we're inspiring the entire logistics sector to join us to create a sustainable future.

Vibrant Culture & Community: We embody collaboration, open doors, and no-fuss results focus, and we are successful with our data-driven and efficient working approach. We like to celebrate and come together, and we strive for a workplace buzzing with energy and camaraderie, highlighted by regular team events, and company-wide celebrations.

Inclusive Environment: We value diversity and welcome all applicants regardless of ethnicity, religion, national origin, sexual orientation, gender identity, age, or disability.

If you meet the above qualifications and are excited about the opportunity to join our team, we encourage you to submit your application today. Please scroll down to submit your English CV.

We look forward to hearing from you!

Title: About Atlantic
Description:
Investment Associate — AI and Compute @ Atlantic Investment Team, Berlin

About Atlantic

Atlantic leads pre-seed rounds across Europe, backing founders in AI, Energy, Industrial, and Space sectors since 2016. We are a small 20-person team with nearly a decade of experience, a non-hierarchical deal culture, and strong autonomy for each team member.

The role

This is a sourcing-focused position for someone deeply embedded in AI ecosystems who can identify founders, evaluate technology, and support early-stage companies throughout the investment cycle.

Ideal candidate


Computer science or engineering background, AI investment experience, or AI startup founder/operator experience
Daily AI tool usage and deep familiarity with the AI landscape
Strong sourcing instincts and willingness to travel extensively across Europe
Berlin-based or willing to relocate
Fluent English; European language skills advantageous


Key Responsibilities


Source AI founders across Europe
Travel extensively to meet founders
Evaluate investment opportunities with technical depth
Develop market insights on AI trends
Support portfolio companies operationally
Represent the fund to founders

Title: Data Analyst (m/w/d) – Industrial & Automotive Consulting
Description:
Beschreibung

Du willst im Bereich Data Science & Analytics dort arbeiten, wo es wirklich zählt – an komplexen Industrieprojekten mit direktem Einfluss auf Entscheidungen auf Management-Ebene?

Bei DRIVE Consulting arbeitest du an anspruchsvollen Fragestellungen führender Industrieunternehmen. Du unterstützt dabei, datengetriebene Entscheidungen in Transformations- und Beratungsprojekten umzusetzen. Unsere Analysen und Dashboards fließen direkt in das Reporting und die Entscheidungsgrundlagen unserer Kunden ein.

In dieser Rolle arbeitest du hands-on an Analytics-Lösungen und unterstützt bei der Entwicklung von Dashboards, der Analyse komplexer Daten sowie beim Aufbau von Datenstrukturen und -pipelines. Dabei begleitest du Projekte von der Anforderungsanalyse über die Konzeption bis zur Umsetzung und entwickelst deine fachlichen und methodischen Fähigkeiten kontinuierlich weiter.

Deine Aufgaben


Du arbeitest an Analytics-Workstreams in Kundenprojekten und übernimmst Verantwortung für eigene Aufgaben und Teilprojekte
Gemeinsam mit deinem Team entwickelst und betreibst du Datenpipelines zwischen Cloud-Quellen und Analytics- bzw. Dashboard-Tools und stellst deren Qualität, Automatisierung und Performance sicher (insbesondere über SQL)
Du konzipierst und entwickelst Dashboards mit Fokus auf Usability und klare Entscheidungsunterstützung für das Management
Du analysierst komplexe und heterogene Datenstrukturen, bereitest Daten auf und führst verschiedene Datenquellen effizient zusammen
Du führst Ad-hoc-Analysen und explorative Auswertungen durch und bereitest Ergebnisse adressatengerecht auf
Du arbeitest eng im Projektteam und mit relevanten Stakeholdern zusammen und treibst die Umsetzung datengetriebener Lösungen aktiv voran
Du unterstützt bei Architekturfragen, Priorisierungen und der Weiterentwicklung von Analytics-Lösungen
Du bringst eigene Ideen ein und entwickelst dich fachlich und methodisch kontinuierlich weiter


Dein Profil


Du hast erste Berufserfahrung (ca. 1–3 Jahre) im Bereich Data Analytics, Data Science, Data Engineering oder einem vergleichbaren datengetriebenen Umfeld
Du verfügst über gute Kenntnisse in Python und bist in der Lage, komplexe Datenstrukturen eigenständig zu analysieren und aufzubereiten
Du hast erste Erfahrung im Aufbau von Datenpipelines und/oder im Dashboarding gesammelt
Du bringst Erfahrung im Umgang mit BI-Tools (z. B. AWS QuickSight, Grafana, Tableau, etc.) mit
Du arbeitest analytisch und strukturiert und hast Freude daran, komplexe Fragestellungen zu lösen
Du denkst dich schnell in neue Themen ein und entwickelst dich fachlich kontinuierlich weiter
Idealerweise hast du erste Berührungspunkte mit Prozessen in der Automobilindustrie oder im industriellen Umfeld
Du arbeitest gerne im Team, kommunizierst klar und trittst auch im Austausch mit Kunden sicher auf
Du bist bereit, regelmäßig vor Ort bei unseren Kunden zu arbeiten


Unser Angebot an dich

Bei DRIVE Consulting triffst du auf ein junges, wachsendes Team, dessen Herz für technische Beratungsprojekte in der Industrie schlägt – insbesondere im Automotive-Umfeld.

Dich erwartet ein Umfeld mit hohem fachlichen Anspruch, schneller Lernkurve und individueller Weiterentwicklung. Du arbeitest eng mit erfahrenen Kolleg:innen zusammen, übernimmst früh Verantwortung für eigene Themen und entwickelst deine fachlichen und methodischen Fähigkeiten kontinuierlich weiter.

Offenheit, Vertrauen und Teamspirit prägen unsere Zusammenarbeit – in einem motivierten Team mit klarer Umsetzungsorientierung.

Außerdem Erwarten Dich


Individuelles Coaching und persönliches Buddy-Programm für deinen Einstieg und deine kontinuierliche Weiterentwicklung
Dienstwagen oder Jobrad bzw. Jobticket – flexibel nach deinen Bedürfnissen
Täglicher Lunch-Zuschuss (6 €)
EGYM Wellpass für Fitness- und Gesundheitsangebote
Attraktive Mitarbeiterrabatte über Corporate Benefits
Zuschuss zur betrieblichen Altersvorsorge
Obst, Kaffee und Erfrischungsgetränke im Office
Regelmäßige Teamevents zur Stärkung des Teamzusammenhalts
Monatliches Happy Friday Frühstück
100 € Spendenbudget zur freien Verfügung
Sabbatical-Optionen


Über uns

DRIVE Consulting GmbH ist eine Unternehmensberatung mit Hauptsitz in Aachen sowie Standorten in München, Berlin, Peking und Shanghai. Unser Herz schlägt für technische Beratungsprojekte mit Fokus auf die Automobil- und Industriebranche. Zu unseren Kunden zählen sowohl deutsche Premium OEMs als auch junge, aufstrebende Automobilhersteller sowie große und mittelständische Industrieunternehmen.

Unser Name steht für unseren klaren Fokus auf die Mobilitätsbranche und repräsentiert gleichzeitig unseren Spirit – denn wir sind ein Team, in dem jede*r Einzelne DRIVE hat.

Wir stehen unseren Kunden als Wegbereiter ins Unbekannte und als Katalysator für Innovationen zur Seite. Denn wir sind überzeugt, dass man nur mit dem Mut, neue Wege einzuschlagen, einzigartige Erfolgsgeschichten schreiben kann.

Wir schlagen Brücken zwischen kleinen Ideenschmieden und großen Konzernen sowie zwischen Management und Technologieexpert*innen.

Durch den engen und partnerschaftlichen Austausch mit unseren Kunden entwickeln wir maßgeschneiderte und individuelle Lösungen. Ein entscheidender Erfolgsfaktor ist dabei unser einzigartiges Partnernetzwerk aus Industrie und Forschung. Dank unserer Nähe zu namhaften Universitäten wie der RWTH Aachen verbinden wir Wissenschaft und Praxis.

Begeisterung wird bei uns großgeschrieben und bestimmt unseren Arbeitsalltag – denn sie ist der beste Antrieb.

Title: Junior Specialist Master Data (m/f/d) - Hybrid Option GER
Description:
WELCOME TO BASF

In Global Business Services, we support BASF's divisions with a cross-functional network and offer modern solutions tailored to the needs of the business. As a specialized service unit we ensure a high level of customer orientation and we design our services to be cooperative, efficient and future-oriented.


What You Can Expect

Our Regional Treasury EMEA department is responsible for the Treasury and Cash Management activity of BASF Group companies in the EMEA region. Within our Bank team, we are responsible for validating customer and supplier master data, ensuring adherence to internal Fraud Prevention requirements.


In this role, you are responsible for the creation and maintenance of business partner hierarchies in SAP S/4HANA.
You make an essential contribution to the completeness, consistency and accuracy of our data by verifying the information using various sources.
As part of your daily business, you are responsible for knowledge transfer to colleagues and maintaining an up-to-date knowledge database, while also providing reports and analyses for stakeholders (e.g. Credit Risk Managers) on a regular basis.
Lastly, you are in regular contact with different units within the BASF Group.




What You Offer


commercial vocational training or studies and ideally first professional experience in the field of Master Data
basic knowledge of accounting or business administration in relation to legal forms and management
confident handling of MS Office and first experiences with SAP S/4HANA
comprehensive knowledge of process flows, Business Partner master data workflow, products, customers and entity goals
strong analytical sense and an eye for details is required, goal-oriented, precise working methods and high customer orientation
fluent and convincing communication in German and English, further language skills are welcome




What We Offer


Gross annual salary (full-time): 35.000 - 39.000 €, depending on qualifications and professional experience - In addition, you can expect a bonus dependent on the company's success
30 days of paid vacation, special leave and sabbatical options
Flexible working hours so that you can organize your working and private life according to your needs
Location-independent working models with defined days of presence in Berlin and residence in Germany – here a first impression
Work mobile from other EU countries for up to six weeks




How To Reach Us


Katarina Borisov (Talent Acquisition), Tel: +49 30 2005-59269 will be happy to answer your questions for this position. You are welcome to book an online appointment.
You can also reach our recruiting team here.
First information about our application process can be found here and in our Q&A video.




About Us

As an international service hub, the BASF Services Europe GmbH bundles a wide range of services for the BASF Group throughout Europe. In the heart of Berlin, we combine expertise in the areas of finance, HR, supply chain, procurement, product safety and IT. We focus on digital and customer-focused solutions, to create services for future. Learn more about us and our career opportunities here.

Diversity is our greatest strength!

Become a part of our winning formula for success and develop the future with us - in a global team that embraces inclusion and equal opportunities irrespective of gender, age, origin, sexual orientation, disability or belief.

  • Katarina Borisov (Talent Acquisition), Tel: +49 30 2005-59269 will be happy to answer your questions for this position. You are welcome to book an online appointment.
  • You can also reach our recruiting team here.
  • First information about our application process can be found here and in our Q&A video.



About Us

As an international service hub, the BASF Services Europe GmbH bundles a wide range of services for the BASF Group throughout Europe. In the heart of Berlin, we combine expertise in the areas of finance, HR, supply chain, procurement, product safety and IT. We focus on digital and customer-focused solutions, to create services for future. Learn more about us and our career opportunities here.

Diversity is our greatest strength!

Become a part of our winning formula for success and develop the future with us - in a global team that embraces inclusion and equal opportunities irrespective of gender, age, origin, sexual orientation, disability or belief.

Title: Director of Data
Description:
About Us

What if your work could drive change in a globally established industry, shaping processes that touch every corner of the world? At Forto, we are at the forefront of change, harnessing the power of AI to revolutionise logistics. We want to reinvent digital supply chains to be transparent, frictionless and sustainable. From day one, our mission has been to simplify global trade – creating a seamless and efficient logistics process.

Your role & Mission

As Data Director here at Forto, you will be responsible for creating a unified data organisation consisting of Data Science, Data Analytics and Data Engineering. You will dramatically raise the profile of data across the business, partnering with stakeholders in a number of domains to assess and support their data needs. You will own and implement a company wide data strategy, prioritizing competing demands into one cohesive and well understood approach.

What you will do

Be responsible for the teams (each with their own manager/technical lead) that own the 3 “pillars” of data at Forto: Data Engineering, Data Analytics and Data Science, bringing them into a unified org whilst also encouraging and respecting their specialised skills

Ensure that the Data org operates a Centre of Excellence, supporting and educating the “customers” of data across the business.

Break down siloes and form strong working relationships with stakeholders.

Define and shape a data strategy fit for purpose across our analytical and agentic business needs.

Own data governance and quality. Establish the policies, standards, ownership model, and forums that make data trustworthy. Manage day-to-day compliance with GDPR and emerging AI regulation, and set practical guardrails for data and AI use.

Sponsor the data platform. Work with engineering and architecture to maintain a reliable, performant, cost-aware platform across ingestion, storage, transformation, BI, and ML. Make build/buy/partner recommendations and manage key vendor relationships.

Scale analytics and AI capability. Define how use cases are sourced, prioritised, productionised, and governed. Curate a portfolio that balances quick wins, strategic bets, and long-term capability building. Champion self-service BI without losing control of metrics and a single source of truth.

Partner across the business. Build strong working relationships with leaders in every function. Translate technical choices into commercial language, resolve cross-functional disputes over data ownership and priorities, and make sure the function is genuinely useful to the people it serves.

Required Skills And Experience


Data leadership track record. 7+ years in data, analytics, or related disciplines, including 3+ years leading teams that span more than one sub-discipline (e.g. engineering and analytics, or analytics and science).
Strategy and roadmap experience. Has shaped and delivered a data strategy for a business
Centre of Excellence or shared-services exposure. Has worked inside, built, or substantially evolved a CoE-style function: service model, hub-and-spoke design, intake and prioritisation.
Modern data architecture. Strong working knowledge of warehouse/lakehouse patterns, streaming, orchestration, BI, and the basics of GenAI. Cloud platform fluency (AWS / GCP / Azure) sufficient to challenge decisions.
Governance and compliance. Hands-on accountability for data governance, quality, and regulatory compliance (GDPR at minimum). Comfortable owning data risk at a working level.
AI and ML operating experience. Has experience supporting GenAI usecases and setting a strategy that supports productisation
Senior stakeholder management. Proven ability to partner with department heads and VP-level leaders across functions. Comfortable in difficult conversations and resetting expectations without burning relationships.
Commercial fluency. Owns and defends a budget; builds business cases with realistic benefits and ranges of uncertainty; manages vendor relationships pragmatically.
People leadership. Hires, develops, and retains managers and senior ICs.
Strong written and verbal communication. Can move between a strategy document, a leadership update, and a working session with engineers without losing credibility in any of them.


Preferred Skills And Experience


Experience in a high-growth or scale-up environment where the data function had to be built or significantly reshaped, not just maintained.
Background in industries with operational complexity, real-time data needs, or supply chain / logistics / marketplace dynamics.
Hands-on past as a data engineer, analytics engineer, or data scientist — enough to retain technical credibility with the team.
Experience implementing a data mesh or federated data product model.
Familiarity with EU AI Act and emerging AI governance frameworks.
Experience evaluating, procuring, and rolling out AI tooling (LLMs, coding assistants, analytics copilots) at organisational scale, including licensing and policy.
Track record of contributing to public technical discourse — conference talks, writing, open source, or community involvement.
Graduate qualification in a quantitative, computer science, or related discipline.
Multi-language working environments and experience operating across European or global teams.


Don’t fit all of our criteria? That’s okay! We know that you might be hesitant to apply if you don’t meet all our requirements, but here at Forto, we pride ourselves on embracing diverse perspectives and celebrating potential. If you are passionate about this position and the Forto values, please apply anyway. There could be a place for you in this role – or another one that’s a perfect fit!

Why work with us?

Our team is hard-working, constantly seeking to maximise the impact of their work, but we put our people first, always winning with care. We value efficient systems and swift, direct communication. We want everyone to have their time to speak, so that we can embrace diverse perspectives to help drive towards solutions always.

Title: Junior Data Analyst militärische Fahrzeuge (m/w/d)
Description:
Beschreibung der Stelle: Wollen Sie unser Team der Datenanalyse für militärische Fahrzeuge verstärken?

Möchten Sie Verantwortung für die Auswertung von verschiedenen technischen Daten übernehmen? Haben Sie Lust sich in der Tiefe mit militärischen Fahrzeugen und deren Instandsetzung auseinander zu setzen?

Sie verstärken unser Datenanalyseteam und bringen Ihre Kenntnisse und Erfahrungen im Bereich Data Analytics in unsere laufende Projektarbeit ein.

Gleichzeitig unterstützen unsere Kolleg:innen Sie insbesondere zu Beginn intensiv bei der fachlichen Einarbeitung in unsere Datenlandschaft.

Der Geschäftsbereich Defence & Security ist in besonderer Weise den Streitkräften verpflichtet und ein anerkannter technischer Dienstleister auf nationaler und internationaler Ebene bei der Konzeption von Lösungen, der Begleitung von Analyse, Entwicklungs- und Realisierungsvorhaben und der Bereitstellung von Serviceleistungen.


Tätigkeit:



Analyse von Fahrzeug- und Instandsetzungsdaten aus dem Landbereich, nach gründlicher Einarbeitung
Mitarbeit bei der Strukturierung der Analyseaufgaben und Herunterbrechen von Teilaufgaben für das Team
Zusammenarbeit in einem interdisziplinären Team mit Experten aus dem Betrieb, der Instandsetzung, der Konstruktion und der Logistik
Umgang mit komplexen Daten sowie Konsolidierung der wesentlichen Inhalte in Zusammenarbeit mit erfahrenen Expert:innen
Mitwirkung bei der Durchführung von Experteninterviews bei unseren Kunden vor Ort im nationalen und internationalen Umfeld
Einbringung von Vorerfahrungen zur Unterstützung bei der Identifikation und Umsetzung von Automatisierungsansätzen



Voraussetzungen:



Abgeschlossenes Studium im Bereich Physik, Mathematik, Elektrotechnik, Maschinenbau oder vergleichbar
Idealerweise Erfahrungen in der Analyse von Daten, Datenzusammenhängen und deren Strukturierung
Bereitschaft zur Einarbeitung in militärische Fahrzeuge und in Prozesse des Betriebs, der Instandhaltung und der Entwicklung
Sehr gute Deutsch- und Englischkenntnisse in Wort und Schrift



Ziele: Durch den perspektivischen Ausbau unserer Beratungs- und Analysekompetenzen leisten Sie einen entscheidenden Beitrag zur Weiterentwicklung unserer Leistungen, zur Zufriedenheit unserer Kunden und damit zum nachhaltigen Geschäftserfolg.

Title: Robotics Data Collector (Germany)
Description:
CNTXT AI is a leading artificial intelligence company focused on building next-generation AI systems, products, and infrastructure. We specialize in developing multimodal AI, robotics, and intelligent agents, powered by high-quality real-world data. Data is at the core of our innovation. We invest heavily in data collection, annotation, and AI training pipelines to enable advanced systems such as embodied AI and Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models.

Role Summary

CNTXT AI is looking for a Robotics Data Collector to support the collection of high-quality robotics and egocentric data used to train AI models.
This role involves working hands-on with robotic systems, sensors, and real-world environments to capture multimodal datasets, including video, motion, and interaction data. You will play a key role in enabling the development of intelligent robotic systems and AI agents.
The ideal candidate is detail-oriented, technically curious, and comfortable working in lab and controlled real-world environments.

Key Responsibilities

1. Data Collection Execution

Collect robotics and egocentric (first-person) data using:

Cameras (handheld, head-mounted, or robot-mounted)

Sensors (IMU, depth cameras, etc.)

Execute data collection tasks based on predefined scenarios and protocols.

Ensure consistent and accurate recording of actions, interactions, and environments.





2. Robotics Operation Support

Assist in operating robotic systems (humanoid or mobile robots) during data collection.

Support setup and calibration of:

Cameras and sensors

Recording systems

Ensure proper functioning of hardware during experiments.





3. Data Quality & Validation

Review collected data for:

Completeness

Clarity

Accuracy

Identify and flag issues such as:

Missing data

Recording errors

Sensor misalignment

Follow quality guidelines to ensure high usability for AI training.





4. Experiment Setup & Execution

Prepare environments for data collection sessions.

Execute tasks such as:

Object manipulation

Navigation scenarios

Human-robot interaction recordings

Follow detailed instructions to simulate real-world use cases.





5. Documentation & Reporting

Maintain records of:

Data collection sessions

Equipment usage

Issues encountered

Provide feedback to improve data collection processes and protocols.





6. Safety & Compliance

Follow all lab safety protocols and operational guidelines.

Handle robotics equipment and sensors with care.

Ensure compliance with data privacy and security requirements.





Required Qualifications


0–3+ years of experience

Data collection, field operations, or lab environments

Technical or hardware-related roles

Comfortable working with:

Cameras, sensors, and recording devices

Basic computer systems and tools

Strong attention to detail and ability to follow instructions precisely.





Preferred Skills

Experience with robotics systems or hardware environments.

Familiarity with:

Egocentric or first-person data collection

Computer vision or AI-related datasets

Basic understanding of:

Robotics concepts

Sensors and data recording tools

Experience working in technical labs or research environments.





Key Traits

Detail-oriented and quality-focused

Hands-on and execution-driven

Reliable and consistent in following protocols

Comfortable working in dynamic environments

Curious about robotics and AI





Success Metrics

Volume and quality of data collected

Accuracy and completeness of datasets

Adherence to data collection protocols

Reduction in data errors and rework

Efficiency in executing data collection tasks





Why Join CNTXT AI

Work on cutting-edge robotics and AI systems

Contribute to building next-generation intelligent agents

Gain hands-on experience with robotics labs and real-world data

Be part of a fast-growing, innovation-driven company

Title: Working Student IT-Systems
Description:
We are seeking a motivated and tech-savvy individual to join our team as a Working Student in IT-Systems. In this role, you will take on a diverse range of responsibilities, including supporting IT operations, managing user requests, assisting with system administration and driving your own IT projects from start to finish. The ideal candidate is a proactive problem solver with a passion for technology and the ability to thrive in a dynamic, team-oriented environment. You’ll work collaboratively with experienced professionals and gain valuable hands-on experience in shaping innovative IT solutions!


Aufgaben






Managing IT Operations:








Support us in organizing and optimizing our IT systems and bring structure to our digital processes.








Supporting IT administration:








Take on tasks such as user administration in our systems, setting up workstations and monitoring and maintaining cloud systems.








Providing reliable support:








Be the first point of contact for our colleagues and solve IT issues with your expertise and commitment.








Realizing your own projects:








From idea to implementation - you will be given the responsibility to carry out your own IT projects. Whether it's about new tools, automation or IT security, we encourage your creative drive.








Helping to shape the digital transformation:








You will work closely with our team, develop innovative ideas and help us make the IT landscape fit for the future!






Qualifikation



You are studying informatics, business informatics or a related course and are at least in your second semester.


You have already gained some experience in IT.


You can communicate in German and English.


You live in Berlin and can work in our office if required (of course we also offer you the opportunity to work from home).


You work independently, are solution-oriented and have a quick grasp of things.


You enjoy working in a team and constantly developing your skills.



Benefits



Personal development: You will take on exciting and responsible tasks that will help you develop professionally and personally.


Flexibility: Your studies are always a priority, we offer you flexible working hours that are ideally suited to your studies.


Technology: You will work with the latest technologies and have the opportunity to grow in an innovative IT environment.


Creativity: You have the ability to think outside the box and create unique, engaging solutions.


Collaboration: You are not alone - our experienced team is always on hand to help and advise you.


Drive: Your work has a real impact on our company and creates real added value.

Title: Data Engineer
Description:
As a Data Engineer at PERGOLUX, you will build and own the data pipelines that enable fast, reliable operational insights across the business. Your work ensures the company can trust its data, automate confidently, and make high-impact decisions in real time. You will join a lean, senior data team where reliability, clarity, and business value—not technical complexity—define success.

At PERGOLUX, we combine Scandinavian-inspired design with a collaborative, ambitious culture grounded in integrity, innovation, and unity.

What You’ll Do


Build and maintain a dependable ingestion and delivery layer that moves data into Snowflake and feeds operational tools with high accuracy and low maintenance overhead.
Own the ingestion backbone across systems such as HubSpot, Klaviyo, Google Ads, and Canary7, selecting the tools and approaches that maximize stability and simplicity.
Design monitoring, observability, and alerting that eliminate silent failures and give stakeholders full visibility into data health and freshness.
Define pragmatic orchestration patterns that ensure scalable, cost-efficient, and resilient pipelines—introducing new tooling only when it clearly reduces failure modes or maintenance costs.
Lead simplification initiatives within the first 90 days by improving stability, reducing pipeline breaks, and implementing a transparent, well-instrumented data platform.


What You’ll Bring


A pragmatic mindset focused on simplicity, reliability, and business value over unnecessary technical complexity.
Strong systems thinking and the ability to evaluate downstream impact when designing pipelines and data flows.
Fluency in Python, SQL, and Git, with a deep understanding of production-grade data engineering concepts such as data contracts, schema evolution, idempotency, and failure-state management.
Ability to create force-multiplying infrastructure that removes ambiguity, eliminates manual work, and accelerates Analytics Engineering workflows.
A disciplined approach to protecting platform stability, reducing technical debt, and ensuring predictable, trustworthy data delivery.


Benefits


Competitive Salary & Bonus Structure – Your success is rewarded with strong compensation and performance-based bonuses.
Real Ownership – Take meaningful responsibility in your role and directly influence key business outcomes.
International Team – Collaborate with passionate colleagues across Europe, gaining exposure to diverse perspectives and ideas.
Outdoor Lifestyle Discounts – Enjoy exclusive discounts on PERGOLUX products.
Fast-Track Career Opportunities – As we scale quickly, you’ll have significant opportunities to grow into senior leadership roles.
Team Culture – Be part of regular team events, retreats, and a culture that celebrates wins—big and small.
Bike Leasing Program – Benefit from a sustainable and cost-effective bike leasing option to support commuting and an active lifestyle.


How to Apply

Ready to shape the data foundation of a fast-scaling global brand? Apply through our career channels:

PERGOLUX LinkedIn

PERGOLUX Career Page

  • Build and maintain a dependable ingestion and delivery layer that moves data into Snowflake and feeds operational tools with high accuracy and low maintenance overhead.
  • Own the ingestion backbone across systems such as HubSpot, Klaviyo, Google Ads, and Canary7, selecting the tools and approaches that maximize stability and simplicity.
  • Design monitoring, observability, and alerting that eliminate silent failures and give stakeholders full visibility into data health and freshness.
  • Define pragmatic orchestration patterns that ensure scalable, cost-efficient, and resilient pipelines—introducing new tooling only when it clearly reduces failure modes or maintenance costs.
  • Lead simplification initiatives within the first 90 days by improving stability, reducing pipeline breaks, and implementing a transparent, well-instrumented data platform.

What You’ll Bring

  • A pragmatic mindset focused on simplicity, reliability, and business value over unnecessary technical complexity.
  • Strong systems thinking and the ability to evaluate downstream impact when designing pipelines and data flows.
  • Fluency in Python, SQL, and Git, with a deep understanding of production-grade data engineering concepts such as data contracts, schema evolution, idempotency, and failure-state management.
  • Ability to create force-multiplying infrastructure that removes ambiguity, eliminates manual work, and accelerates Analytics Engineering workflows.
  • A disciplined approach to protecting platform stability, reducing technical debt, and ensuring predictable, trustworthy data delivery.

Benefits

  • Competitive Salary & Bonus Structure – Your success is rewarded with strong compensation and performance-based bonuses.
  • Real Ownership – Take meaningful responsibility in your role and directly influence key business outcomes.
  • International Team – Collaborate with passionate colleagues across Europe, gaining exposure to diverse perspectives and ideas.
  • Outdoor Lifestyle Discounts – Enjoy exclusive discounts on PERGOLUX products.
  • Fast-Track Career Opportunities – As we scale quickly, you’ll have significant opportunities to grow into senior leadership roles.
  • Team Culture – Be part of regular team events, retreats, and a culture that celebrates wins—big and small.
  • Bike Leasing Program – Benefit from a sustainable and cost-effective bike leasing option to support commuting and an active lifestyle.

How to Apply

Ready to shape the data foundation of a fast-scaling global brand? Apply through our career channels:

PERGOLUX LinkedIn

PERGOLUX Career Page

Title: (Assistant) IT Manager (m/w/*)
Description:
Department: Information Technology (IT)

Location: Germany - Berlin



Description

Das Hotel Adlon Kempinski Berlin steht für zeitlose Eleganz und erstklassigen Service. Um diesen hohen Standard auch technologisch perfekt zu untermauern, suchen wir für unser Hotel einen proaktiven IT Manager (m/w/*).

In dieser Position verantworten Sie den reibungslosen und zukunftsorientierten Betrieb der gesamten IT-Infrastruktur und sorgen dafür, dass alle Abteilungen durch effiziente Anwendungen optimal unterstützt werden. Neben der technischen und budgetären Gesamtverantwortung ist uns unsere Führungskultur besonders wichtig: Sie führen Ihr Team mit Leidenschaft, identifizieren Potenziale und fördern die Talente im Haus aktiv auf ihrem Karriereweg.

Key Responsibilities


IT-Infrastruktur & Support: Sie sind die erste Anlaufstelle für alle IT-Fragen im Hotel. Sie stellen den reibungslosen 24/7-Betrieb aller Kernsysteme sicher und optimieren die Performance aller Anwendungen
Sicherheit & Compliance: Sie setzen die globalen Kempinski IT-Richtlinien sowie gesetzliche Vorgaben um. Dazu gehören regelmäßige Backups, das Einspielen von Sicherheits-Patches, die Gewährleistung von Datenschutz und PCI-Compliance sowie die Durchführung jährlicher Self-Audits
Prozessoptimierung: In enger Zusammenarbeit mit den anderen Abteilungsleitungen implementieren Sie neue softwarebasierte Abläufe, um die Effizienz, Wirtschaftlichkeit und das Gästeerlebnis im Hotel kontinuierlich zu verbessern
Budget & Controlling: Sie sind für die zeitgerechte Erstellung, Überwachung und Einhaltung des gesamten IT-Budgets verantwortlich
Krisenmanagement: Sie erstellen und testen Business-Continuity- sowie Notfallpläne und schulen das Hotelpersonal im Umgang mit diesen Systemen
Teamführung & Talent Development: Sie rekrutieren, führen und motivieren Ihr IT-Team. Sie identifizieren Talente, fördern deren Karriere durch gezielte Trainings, Cross-Exposure sowie Entwicklungspläne und arbeiten hierbei eng mit der Personalabteilung zusammen
Schnittstellenfunktion: Sie koordinieren die Zusammenarbeit mit externen IT-Dienstleistungsunternehmen und stehen im engen Austausch mit der Hotelleitung sowie dem regionalen und globalen Corporate IT-Team


Skills, Knowledge and Expertise


Berufserfahrung: Mehrjährige fundierte Erfahrung in einer vergleichbaren IT-Führungsposition, idealerweise in der gehobenen Hotellerie oder einem dienstleistungsorientierten Umfeld
Technische Expertise: Tiefgehendes Verständnis von Netzwerkarchitekturen, Systemadministration, IT-Sicherheit und Backup-Systemen. Kenntnisse im Bereich PCI-Compliance und Datenschutz sind zwingend erforderlich
Führung & Coaching: Ausgeprägte Führungskompetenz - Sie verstehen es, Teams zu motivieren und Potenziale zu entfalten.
Wirtschaftliches Denken: Starke analytische Fähigkeiten sowie Erfahrung in der Budgetplanung und Kostenkontrolle
Arbeitsweise & Mentalität: Eine proaktive, lösungsorientierte und strukturierte Arbeitsweise. Sie agieren vorausschauend statt rein reaktiv
Flexibilität: Bereitschaft zur Übernahme von Wochenend- und Bereitschaftsdiensten, um den reibungslosen 24/7-Hotelbetrieb jederzeit zu gewährleisten
Kommunikation: Exzellente Kommunikationssfähigkeiten auf allen Hierarchieebenen sowie fließende Deutsch- und Englischkenntnisse


Benefits


Planbare Arbeitszeiten
Kostenfreie Reinigung von Dienstkleidung
Übernahme des Deutschlandtickets (bzw. BVG Jobticket)
Vielfältige Gestaltungsmöglichkeiten und ein aufregendes Arbeitsumfeld
Facettenreiche Entwicklungsmöglichkeiten sowie interne und externe Trainings
Staff und Family & Friends Raten für alle Kempinski Hotels sowie alle Häuser der Global Hotel Alliance
"Adlon-Benefits" innerhalb Berlins sowie Corporate Benefits
Kostenlose Verpflegung im Staff-Restaurant
Regelmäßige Team-Events

Title: Business Analyst Production Support – Wealth & Front
Description:
Founded and headquartered in Switzerland, Avaloq is continuously expanding its global footprint with around 2,500 colleagues in 12 countries, and more than 170 clients in 35 countries. We are an industry-leading provider of wealth management technology and services for financial institutions around the world, including private banks and wealth managers, investment managers, as well as retail and neo banks. Our research led approach and continual innovation is powered by the passion and creativity of our colleagues.

We are always looking for talented people to join us on our mission to orchestrate the financial ecosystem and democratize access to wealth management. Avaloq offers the opportunity to work closely with some of the world’s leading financial institutions as we jointly develop and shape careers. Championing a collaborative, supportive and flexible work environment empowers our colleagues to reach their full potential.

Job Description

You will be part of the SaaS Operating Center which is responsible to ensure stable platform operations of our Avaloq software and products for our clients.

As a Business Analyst in this team, your task will consist of interacting with clients to assure service delivery, analysing customer requirements and solving incidents/issues in productive environment and monitoring to ensure application availability. You will coordinate with the service desk and other delivery units such as L3, project delivery and client management to resolve incidents and problems.


Follow incident management process end-to-end in a timely manner including triage of incident
Impact analysis, troubleshooting and involve/co-ordinate with other teams
High level analysis and consolidation of new client requirements
Support the development team implementing features by translating business requirements into technical requirements
UAT Coordination & Support
Manage end user’s expectation and other relevant stakeholders
On-boarding of new bank services in service delivery
Be proactive and contribute new ideas to further improve the quality of our services


Qualifications


Proven track record of successful performance in a similar role
ITIL Incident, Problem and Change process knowledge
Technical degree in IT or business related area
Full professional fluency in both English and German


It would be a real bonus if you have:


Knowledge of Avaloq Core & Front Platform with certification
SQL and basic knowledge of Oracle DB
Knowledge of payments, trading or regulatory reporting


Additional Information

We realize that managing work life balance is a challenge we all face in our daily lives and in order to support with this we are pleased to offer hybrid and flexible working for most of our Avaloqers to maintain work life balance and still continue our fantastic Avaloq culture in our global offices.

In Avaloq we are proud to embrace diversity and understand the success of our business is built on the power of different opinions, we are whole heartedly committed to fostering an equal opportunity environment and inclusive culture where you can be your true authentic self.

We hire, compensate and promote regardless of origin, age, gender identity, sexual orientation or any other fantastic traits that make us all unique, we have done our best to write this advert in an inclusive and neutral way.

Please be aware that we will not accept speculative CV submissions for any of our roles from recruitment agencies, and any unsolicited candidate submissions will be exempt from any payment expectations.

Title: Founder's Associate
Description:
We're building the AI-native operating system for logistics.




For an industry that moves the world, logistics still runs on spreadsheets, PDFs, and manual work. Decades of unstructured data and disconnected systems kept logistics work manual.




AI unlocks what the industry has been missing: a platform that does the heavy lifting in the background so teams can focus on what truly matters.




Today, our AI platform helps retailers and logistics operators save millions by automating their cost management and transforming messy data into a clean, reliable source of truth.




We're venture-backed, growing fast, and working with some of the most innovative names in digital retail.


Aufgaben


You will work directly with the founding team as a force multiplier across the entire business. This is a hands-on, high-ownership generalist role with real responsibility from day one — designed for someone who wants to learn how an early-stage AI company is built, operated, and scaled from the inside out.




Rather than focusing on a single department, you will tackle the highest-impact challenges facing the company at any given moment: building the smart automations that make the whole team more effective, driving commercial momentum, and keeping operations running like a well-oiled machine.




Key Responsibilities:





AI Automation & Systems: Identify manual or repetitive processes across the company and replace them with smart, automated workflows. You'll be hands-on with tools like Claude (for AI-powered automation and internal tooling) and Lovable (for rapidly spinning up functional internal apps) — as well as workflow tools like Make or n8n. You'll own the automation layer of how Mello operates, turning one-off tasks into scalable systems.


Commercial Growth: Support go-to-market initiatives by sharpening our positioning, refining our ICP (Ideal Customer Profile) based on market data, and helping turn early traction into repeatable commercial processes.


Operational Excellence: Help build the "Mello Playbook" — structured processes for everything from customer onboarding to team workflows — so that what works once, works every time.


Product & Customer Success: Act as the bridge between our early customers and the engineering team, ensuring feedback is translated into product features that drive real value.


Founder Support: Assist the founders with strategic topics, including investor relations, board preparation, and key hiring decisions.



Qualifikation


Mindset





You take full ownership and pride in execution


You enjoy solving a wide range of problems in fast-moving environments


You're curious about startups, AI, and how companies are built


You aspire to build something meaningful, potentially your own company one day





Experience





1–2 years in generalist or high-agency environments (early-stage startups, consulting, venture, or operations)


Hands-on experience with automation tools (Claude, Lovable, Make, n8n, or similar) — or a strong drive to go deep on them fast


Strong intuition for software, data, and AI (technical background is a plus, not a must)


Comfortable working with incomplete information and figuring things out independently


High integrity, strong work ethic, and a strong sense of urgency


Clear and confident communication skills in German and English



Benefits



Attractive compensation with meaningful early equity upside


A high-ownership role with real responsibility and direct exposure to company-building


Close collaboration with the founding team in a small, fast-moving environment


Direct access to customers and the full loop from problem discovery to deployment


The opportunity to help build a product that redefines how logistics teams work





We move fast - end to end in ~2 weeks.





Application review → every application is reviewed by the team


Intro call → 30-minute conversation with a founder


On-site → meet the team, work on a real problem together, and get a feel for how we operate


Offer → clear decision and fast feedback

Title: Koordinator Ausbildungsprogramme (m/w/d) - Berlin
Description:
What To Expect
Tesla steht für die Beschleunigung des Übergangs zu nachhaltiger Energie. Innerhalb weniger Jahre wurden revolutionäre Strategien und Produkte entwickelt und diese in großem Umfang erfolgreich auf den Markt gebracht. Dies ist nur möglich durch außerordentliche Geschwindigkeit, Innovationskraft und Effizienz.

Ziele der Stelle

Konzeption, Implementierung und Steuerung von Projekten im Bereich der betrieblichen Aus- und Weiterbildung unter Berücksichtigung von bildungswissenschaftlichen Erkenntnissen

What You'll Do


Du konzipierst, implementierst und steuerst die zukünftige Strategie für die Ausbildungsprogramme in unseren Fachbereichen in Zentraleuropa (inklusive der Koordination von Schulungskursen, Praxisphasen und Prüfungen)
Du arbeitest eng mit den Fachbereichen und Ausbilder:innen in der Entwicklung und Aktualisierung von Lehrplänen sowie der Erstellung von Tätigkeits- und Stellenprofilen zusammen und berücksichtigst dabei moderne Lehrformen und Methoden in der Ausbildung. Darüber hinaus baust du kontinuierlich die Ausstattung aus (z. B. Werkzeuge, Maschinen, Software) und passt diese an die aktuellen Standards und Bedürfnisse der Fachabteilungen an
Du managest die Beziehungen zu externen Bildungseinrichtungen und Behörden (Berufsschulen, Bildungszentren, IHK, Hochschulen, Universitäten, Handwerkskammern) und setzt Projekte mit diesen und internen Fachbereichen um
Du vernetzt die jeweiligen Beteiligten aus den Fachbereichen und Abteilungen des Unternehmens und entwickelst und aktualisierst Ablaufpläne, um die Anforderungen an Ausbildungsrahmenpläne zu erfüllen
Du stellst Ausbilder:innen ein und betreust diese, und unterstützt das Recruitingteam bei der Erstellung von Auswahl- bzw. Einstellungskriterien und Einstellungstests für neue Auszubildende
Du führst Schulungsveranstaltungen sowie Methodenworkshops für Ausbilder:innen und Auszubildende durch
Du unterstützt bei der Öffentlichkeitsarbeit im Bereich der Ausbildung, des Ausbildungsmarketings sowie des Ausbildungsrecruitings



What You'll Bring


Du hast mehrjährige Berufserfahrung sowie nachgewiesene Erfahrung in der Konzeption von Aus- und Weiterbildungsprogrammen in der DACH-Region
Du hast Kenntnisse in der Konzeption und Implementierung von betrieblichen Aus- und Weiterbildungskonzepten
Du zeichnest dich durch Überzeugungskraft, Durchsetzungsstärke, Konfliktfähigkeit und Empathie, Hands-On Mentalität aus
Du besitzt sehr gute Deutsch- und Englischkenntnisse in Wort und Schrift
Du bringst sehr gutes technisches Verständnis, Eigeninitiative und Motivation zur kontinuierlichen Verbesserung der Prozesse mit
Zu deinen Fähigkeiten zählen strukturierte Problemanalyse und Lösungsfindung
Du bist teamfähig und hast eine organisierte und selbstständige Arbeitsweise
Du besitzt sehr gute Kommunikationsfähigkeit in Deutsch und Englisch mit internen und externen Ansprechpartnern



, Tesla

Title: Working Student Go-to-Market (m/f/d)
Description:
1KOMMA5°

At 1KOMMA5°, we pursue a clear vision: Living on wind and sunlight forever for free. To make this a reality, we are building the energy system of the future with Heartbeat AI. Want to be part of it?

We bring together regional craftsmanship and scalable software: We don't think of solar, batteries, heat pumps, and e-mobility as isolated components, but control them as an intelligent, integrated overall system in our virtual power plant. Directly connected to the electricity market – in real time, fully automated. This way, energy is used when it is available from renewables and particularly cost-effective. By 2030, our goal is to transition 1.5 million households to renewable energies. Over 3,000 people are working towards this every day, at more than 80 locations worldwide, from Finland to Australia.

Want to take responsibility and build solutions that truly matter? Apply now and help us shape the energy world of tomorrow.

Learn more about our Product & Tech team!

Deine Position

Heartbeat AI is our energy optimization platform that provides our customers always with the cleanest and cheapest energy. As Working Student Go-to-Market, you join the GTM team during a pivotal phase: we are expanding Heartbeat AI beyond 1KOMMA5° customers to anyone with existing solar and battery assets. You work hands-on alongside the team, contributing to the operational engine that makes this rollout happen.


Support launch-to-scale operations: You track bottlenecks in the customer journey (onboarding, smart meter activation, cancellation handling, compatibility) and help drive concrete fixes in close collaboration with Product and Operations
Keep performance tracking reliable: You maintain dashboards and reporting cadences the team relies on; consolidating launch KPIs, activation metrics, and performance data into clear, decision-ready updates
Contribute to cross-functional coordination: You support GTM initiatives by tracking workstreams, flagging dependencies, and keeping action items moving across Product, Marketing, Sales, and Operations


Dein Profil


You are enrolled in a Bachelor's or Master's program in Business, Management, Economics, or a related field and are looking for a working student role with real responsibility
You are structured by default: you break complex workstreams into clear tasks and set up tracking systems that actually get used
You are comfortable with data and reporting: you can maintain a dashboard, track KPIs consistently, and turn numbers into a concise update
You follow through: you own action items end-to-end, flag blockers early, and make sure alignment doesn't stop at the meeting room door
You communicate clearly in German (C1/C2) and English (C1/C2)
The energy transition context interests you; you want to understand how a hardware-plus-subscription business works, not just support the process around it


Benefits


You are part of an international, dynamic, and highly motivated team of people who have proven to make things happen
With your work, you accelerate the "energy transition" and hence have a direct impact on our climate
Work with and learn from other super-smart colleagues
You will enjoy direct contact with core decision-makers
You will enjoy the best chances of entering full-time in one of Europe’s most thriving scaleups
You work remotely (Germany-wide), with offices in Hamburg, Berlin or Munich
Create a healthy balance alongside your work and enjoy all the benefits of the EGYM Wellpass
Benefits and discounts are yours with Futurebens


Über uns

Die 1KOMMA5° Gruppe ist ein CleanTech Unicorn mit der Mission alles zu elektrifizieren und über unsere KI-basierte Software Plattform Heartbeat AI jeden an den Strommarkt der Zukunft anzuschließen. Wir sind das marktführende Unternehmen für die Installation von CO2-neutralen Energiesystemen, insbesondere bei Photovoltaik, Energiespeicher, Wallbox, Wärmepumpe und Energiemanagement. 1KOMMA5° vereint die besten Unternehmen Deutschlands, Europas und darüber hinaus – wir wachsen weiter und das weltweit.
