Title: Senior Data Engineer
Description:
About Trawa

European energy costs are twice as high as in the United States and China. Electricity demand is expected to double by 2050. Market price volatility and complexity are here to stay - and the backbone of Europe's industrial economy is at risk.

At trawa, we're building the solution: an integrated B2B energy platform combining procurement, energy management, and flexibility steering in a single product. That integration unlocks up to 30% in recurring electricity cost savings while helping companies make the switch to renewables.

We're a Berlin-based scale-up of around 100 people. We've raised over €35M to date - including a €24M Series A in May 2025 - backed by Balderton, Headline, Speedinvest, AENU, Magnetic, and Tiny VC. All three founders are fully committed to building the utility of the future.

The Team

Data Intelligence (DINTEL) is an engineering team that owns trawa's data pipelines, and analytics infrastructure. We manage the full data lifecycle - from ingesting raw metering and market data, to invoicing and self-service analytics.

About The Role

We're looking for a Senior Data Engineer to own and lead the development, reliability, and continuous evolution of our data platform. This is a high-ownership, high-autonomy role. You'll work across production systems at scale - defining technical direction, coordinating cross-team dependencies, and driving measurable impact across data engineering, analytics infrastructure, and operational tooling.

You'll mentor others, raise engineering standards, and operate confidently in high ambiguity -framing problems, aligning teams, and driving convergence.

What You'll Do

Own and lead the design, implementation, and operation of critical data pipelines and platform infrastructure on AWS.

Define and enforce data quality standards, observability practices, and operational controls across the platform.

Lead cross-team technical discussions and align stakeholders on data platform architecture decisions.

Drive large-scale technical initiatives end-to-end - from design through delivery - coordinating dependencies across teams.

Shape the team's technical culture through code reviews, mentorship, and documentation.

Own and evolve our analytics and BI tooling (ClickHouse, Metabase) to serve business intelligence needs.

Build and maintain data pipelines using Python (pandas, polars), dbt, and dlt.

Identify and address technical debt strategically, improving platform reliability and developer experience.

Track and communicate the value and impact of platform improvements to engineering and business stakeholders.

Diagnose and resolve complex, cross-service data and performance issues.

Your First 90 Days

First 6 Weeks - Onboarding

Rapidly build context across the full data stack: AWS, dbt, dlt, PostgreSQL, ClickHouse, Python services.

Begin contributing to production systems and taking ownership of critical platform components

Engage with engineers and stakeholders across the business to understand data needs and priorities.

By Month 3 - Leading

Own significant platform components end-to-end, driving improvements independently

Lead your first cross-team technical initiative and align stakeholders around a clear plan

Identify and begin executing a strategic improvement to platform reliability, observability, or scalability

What You'll Gain

Ownership of a production data platform that directly powers trawa's energy trading, invoicing, and analytics.

Cross-functional influence: work closely with engineering and business stakeholders on high-impact initiatives.

The opportunity to shape technical culture and raise engineering standards across the Data Intelligence team.

Deep expertise across the modern data stack: AWS, Python (pandas, polars, FastAPI), dbt, dlt, PostgreSQL, ClickHouse, Metabase.

A leadership track with clear L3 expectations and meaningful room to grow.

What We're Looking For

Must-have

5+ years of experience in data engineering or a closely related discipline

Expert SQL and strong Python skills (pandas, polars, or similar)

Experience owning and operating production data pipelines at scale

Proven ability to lead cross-team technical projects and align stakeholders

Strong system design instincts - you reason clearly about trade-offs and communicate decisions well

Comfortable operating in high ambiguity: you frame problems, define options, and drive convergence

Track record of mentoring others and raising the technical bar

Nice to have

Hands-on experience with our stack: dbt, dlt, ClickHouse, PostgreSQL, Metabase, AWS

Experience with workflow orchestration (e.g. Airflow)

Familiarity with energy markets, time-series data, or operational data platforms

Prior experience as a technical lead or staff engineer

Why Join Us

🌍 Real impact - Join one of Europe's fastest-growing B2B energy platforms at a pivotal moment. Your work will directly support systems that help industrial companies cut costs and go green.

🚀 Early ownership - We give team members responsibility from day one. You won't be shadowing - you'll be shipping.

🤝 Mentorship that matters - Regular leadership sessions, cross-functional exposure, and engineers who are genuinely invested in your growth.

📚 Learning culture - Annual learning budget, dedicated development time, and a team that takes knowledge-sharing seriously.

🌐 Inclusive by design - 15+ nationalities, family-friendly policies, and a genuine commitment to building a place where everyone can do their best work.

🎁 Solid benefits - A competitive package covering wellbeing, learning, and team experiences.

Title: Data Platform Lead (Remote)
Description:
Join Over99.com — Powering the Future of Crypto Gaming

At Over99.com, we’re building a next-generation crypto gaming platform: fast, data-driven, and player-first.

As we scale, data is becoming one of the most important parts of our platform. We need reliable transactional systems, clean data models, trustworthy reporting, scalable analytics infrastructure, and a long-term data architecture that can support product insights, finance, compliance, fraud detection, player behavior, game performance, and operational decision-making.

We’re looking for a Lead Data Engineer / Data Platform Lead who can take ownership of our data architecture across PostgreSQL, analytics databases, event pipelines, cold storage, and data modeling. You’ll help us design the systems that ensure our data is accurate, scalable, understandable, and useful across the business.

This is a fully remote role. We value autonomy, ownership, clear communication, and practical engineering judgment.

Responsibilities:


Lead the design and evolution of our core data architecture across transactional, analytical, and long-term storage systems.
Own and improve PostgreSQL data design, schema structure, indexing strategy, query performance, relationships, constraints, and data integrity.
Design, review, and maintain ERDs and data models that clearly represent business logic, system behavior, and domain relationships.
Work closely with backend engineers to ensure application data models are scalable, consistent, and suitable for both operational and analytical use cases.
Build and improve data pipelines from production systems into analytics databases and reporting layers.
Design reliable approaches for data ingestion, replication, transformation, validation, rollups, aggregations, and historical storage.
Help define the right architecture for hot, warm, and cold data storage.
Support the design of analytics-friendly datasets for reporting, dashboards, product analytics, finance, compliance, and operational monitoring.
Help evolve our analytics database strategy, including how we use analytical stores, materialised views, denormalised tables, and aggregation layers.
Establish data modeling best practices across the engineering team.
Identify and fix data quality issues, including inconsistent schemas, duplicated logic, missing relationships, poor naming, and unreliable reporting sources.
Collaborate with backend, product, DevOps, and leadership teams to turn business requirements into clean, scalable data structures.
Define best practices for migrations, data retention, archival, backfills, and safe deletion of production data.
Mentor engineers on database design, ERDs, data modeling, and data pipeline thinking.


Technologies you will work with:

Core Data and Backend Infrastructure:


PostgreSQL
Analytics databases / OLAP systems
Event-driven data pipelines
Node.js
TypeScript
Redis
Kafka
Data modeling tools
ERDs
CI/CD pipelines


Nice to have:


AWS
Docker / containerised environments
Kafka or other streaming platforms
Data lakes/lakehouse architecture
Object storage for cold data
dbt or similar transformation tools
Datadog or other observability platforms
iGaming, crypto, fintech, or high-volume transactional systems


Must-haves:


Strong professional experience in data engineering, data architecture, backend engineering, or a closely related role.
Deep understanding of PostgreSQL, including schema design, indexing, query planning, constraints, normalisation, denormalisation, migrations, and performance optimisation.
Strong understanding of ERDs and relational data modeling.
Experience designing clean, scalable data models for complex business domains.
Experience working with analytical databases, reporting databases, OLAP systems, or data warehouses.
Experience building or maintaining data pipelines from production systems into analytics/reporting systems.
Strong understanding of data quality, data consistency, data validation, and historical data management.
Ability to reason about trade-offs between normalised transactional models and denormalised analytical models.
Experience working with backend engineers and understanding how application architecture affects data architecture.
Strong communication skills and the ability to explain complex data structures clearly to both technical and non-technical teams.
Ability to lead technical initiatives and drive best practices across engineering teams.


Nice-to-have:


Previous experience in a lead, staff, or principal-level data role.
Backend development experience with Node.js, TypeScript, or similar technologies.
Experience with real-time or event-driven systems.
Experience designing data retention, archival, cold storage, and backfill strategies.
Experience with data lakes, lakehouse architecture, object storage, or long-term analytical storage.
Experience with finance, payments, wallets, ledgers, player accounts, betting, casino, or other high-volume transactional systems.
Experience with compliance, auditability, fraud detection, or regulated data environments.
Familiarity with cloud infrastructure, especially AWS.
Familiarity with containerised environments such as Docker, ECS, or Kubernetes.
Background in crypto, iGaming, gambling, fintech, or gaming platforms.


The problems you will help solve:


How should our production PostgreSQL schemas be designed as the platform grows?
How do we make our ERDs and domain models easier to understand and maintain?
How do we move data from transactional systems into analytics databases safely and reliably?
What data should stay in Postgres, what should move into analytics storage, and what should be archived?
How do we build reliable rollups, aggregations, and reporting layers without depending too heavily on production table structures?
How do we support product, finance, compliance, operations, and leadership with accurate data?
How do we prevent messy data models from slowing down engineering as the company scales?
How do we build a long-term data platform that can support millions of players and high-volume gaming activity?


Why this role matters:

This is a high-impact role. You won’t just be writing pipelines or optimising queries — you’ll be shaping the data foundation of the company.

You’ll help define how Over99 models its core business entities, how data flows through the platform, how analytics are built, and how teams make decisions based on trusted information.

The right person will bring a mix of database expertise, data engineering experience, backend engineering awareness, and strong product/business understanding.

⭐ Why Join Over99.com?

🌍 100% Remote — flexibility and freedom, wherever you are

🚀 High-growth crypto gaming platform — real scale, real impact

💰 Competitive salary

⏰ Flexible working hours — outcome-focused culture

📈 Career growth in a fast-moving, technically ambitious environment

Ready to build the data backbone of crypto gaming?

Join Over99.com and help shape how data powers the future of crypto gaming.

Title: (Junior) Chief of Staff (w/m/d)
Description:
Über die Rolle


Als (Junior) Chief of Staff (w/m/d) agierst du als zentrale Schnittstelle direkt an der Seite unseres COO und treibst die kontinuierliche Weiterentwicklung unseres Operating Models voran. Mit einem gesamtheitlichen Blick prägst du unsere nächste Wachstumsphase entscheidend mit. Dabei identifizierst du verborgene Potenziale zwischen den Abteilungen sowie in unseren Prozessen und übersetzt diese in durchdachte Lösungen mit messbarem Impact.




Du kombinierst das kritische Hinterfragen des Status Quo mit technologischer Neugier (Vibe Coding & neue Tools), um belastbare Strukturen für unseren nachhaltigen Erfolg zu schaffen. Wenn du lernen willst, wie man ein Unternehmen wirklich baut – nicht nur konzeptionell, sondern operativ und skalierbar – bist du hier richtig!


Deine Aufgaben

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Your new company




You will be joining a well-established and growing organisation that places technology and data at the centre of its operational strategy. The business has a strong reputation for innovation and continuous improvement, offering a collaborative environment where systems and process optimisation are actively encouraged.




Your new role




We are currently recruiting for a Data Analyst to support the IT Manager and wider business prepare for a large data migration project and to support the wider business to better utilise customer data to enable and support strategic business objectives. As the Data Analyst, you will play a pivotal role in ensuring the stability, performance and ongoing development of key business systems.




You will work closely with stakeholders across the business to deliver meaningful data insights. This will involve developing reports and dashboards using tools such as SQL, Excel, Power BI and Tableau, while also supporting data quality and governance standards.

The business is currently in the very early stages of migrating to Salesforce. This role will play a critical part in that project. Longer term, you will take ownership of driving process automation to improve operational efficiency and support business users to make data more accessible and meaningful. You will design and implement workflows, streamline manual processes and take ownership of SharePoint administration, including site structure and permissions. You will also play an active role in troubleshooting and maintaining workflow solutions.




What you'll need to succeed




To be successful in this role, you will need advanced Excel skills (Power Query, pivot tables and complex formulas), strong SQL skills and great communication - bridging the gap between IT/ Data and the wider business. Experience with Power BI and Tableau is advantageous but not essential. You will have a proven ability to analyse data and produce clear, insightful reporting.




What you'll get in return




Salary up to £50,000, 1 day a week working from (possibly 2 days from home), health cash back plan, Life Insurance (2x salary), free food and catered lunches on-site, free on-site parking with electrical charge points. Full benefits package to be confirmed shortly.

In return, you will have the opportunity to work across a broad technology landscape in a role that offers real variety and impact. You will be part of a supportive and forward-thinking team, with opportunities to develop your technical skills and contribute to meaningful business improvements.




What you need to do now... If you are interested in this role, click 'apply now' to forward an up-to-date copy of your CV.

Title: Data Enablement Analyst
Description:
You'll be the person who turns data into something people trust, reach for, and rely on - and you'll feel the difference that makes across every team you touch.

$113M funded. Global footprint. AI-powered. Human-driven. 🚀

INSHUR is the embedded insurance powerhouse for the on-demand world. We partner with giants like Uber and Amazon to provide fair, data-backed coverage for the modern workforce. We're 200 people strong across the UK, US, and Netherlands, using AI as a multiplier to stay ahead of the curve. Want to work where tech actually meets the road? You're in the right place.

Find out more about our history, values, hiring process and more here.

The role

As our Data Enablement Analyst you'll support the Data Enablement Manager in transforming how INSHUR accesses, understands, and uses data, acting as the connective tissue between business users and the data engineering team. Your mission: make data trusted, usable, and easier to consume. You'll be the first dedicated hire in this function, which means a real opportunity to shape how it grows.

We're based in Brighton, with a hybrid working model. You'll come into the office 1-2 days a week, with the rest of your time remote.

What you'll do

Connect Business Needs to Data Solutions: Working with teams across the business to understand their data needs and translate them into reporting requirements, dashboard improvements, and backlog items for data engineering.

Champion Dashboard Adoption and Self-Service: Onboarding teams to new dashboards and reports, gathering feedback on usability, and helping move the business towards genuine self-serve reporting.

Improve the Reporting Experience: Making existing reports clearer, more intuitive, and easier to act on, through better definitions, smarter layouts, and metrics that make sense at a glance.

Strengthen Data Quality and Understanding: Maintaining data definitions, dictionaries, and reporting guides that help people understand what the data means, where it comes from, and what its limitations are.

Spot Opportunities to Automate: Identifying manual reporting processes that can be streamlined or automated, freeing up time and reducing the risk of human error.

Support Change and Build Trust: Supporting the rollout of new data capabilities, gathering user feedback, and working with the Data Enablement Manager to turn insights into better adoption and deeper trust.

What we're looking for

We care more about how you think than what's on your CV. If you meet most of the essentials and are excited about the problem, apply.

Essential

We'd love to hear from you if you have these essentials to thrive in the role:


Comfort working with data and reports: You'll spend your days in dashboards, spreadsheets, and reporting tools, so a genuine ease with data is the foundation everything else is built on.
Understanding of business processes: Familiarity with finance, operations, or reporting cycles means you'll grasp the context behind the numbers and connect with stakeholders more naturally from day one.
Ability to explain data clearly to non-technical people: This role exists at the intersection of data and decision-making, your talent for making complex information accessible is what makes the whole thing work.
Organisation and attention to detail: From maintaining data dictionaries to keeping documentation current, your thoroughness ensures the resources you create are dependable and trustworthy.
Collaborative communication with stakeholders: You'll be gathering feedback, onboarding users, and communicating changes across multiple teams, building warm, responsive relationships is essential to enabling adoption and trust.
Experience in reporting or analytics: Whether you've built dashboards, written SQL queries, or supported reporting workflows, hands-on experience means you can contribute meaningfully from the start.
A curious mindset about how data is produced and used: The best person for this role doesn't just consume data — they want to understand where it comes from, how it flows, and how to make the whole journey better.


Nice to have


Experience in the insurance sector or a high growth tech business is beneficial, but not essential


How the Data team uses AI

Whether you're new to AI or already building with it, we give everyone the tools, time, and support to grow. AI is a multiplier here, it handles the repetitive so you can focus on the work that actually needs you. Here are a few ways the team is using it;


Speeding up everyday analysis tasks
Building dashboards and reports for Excel or Google Sheets data or BigQuery data.
Creating clear documentation for product details, data definitions, and key decisions.
Drafting Jira tickets, release notes, and executive summaries.


We also use AI tools to support our hiring process — not to make decisions, but to help us run a fairer, faster process. You can read more about how we approach that here, including tips for how you can use AI to prepare.

What to expect from the process


Screen: A 30-minute call with the Talent team. We'll cover the role, your background, and your questions.
First interview: 1 hour video call with the Data Enablement Manager and People Ops Lead to assess your experience and team fit
Onsite Case Study: Chance for you to meet more members of the team and work through a real life challenge


We design this to be a two-way conversation. Come with questions — we want you to leave knowing whether this is the right move for you.

What we offer

We've built a benefits package that invests in our people's long-term personal and professional growth and wellbeing. Here's a sample of what this includes;


Competitive salary, benchmarked to market by location. We discuss compensation openly from the first conversation
30 days of holiday annually, plus bank holidays
Private healthcare for you and your family, including pre-existing conditions
Life insurance at 3x your salary
13 weeks parental leave at full pay for new parents
Workplace nursery scheme with Gogeta, allowing you to pay fees pre-tax
Ride to work scheme
Workplace pension scheme to help you save for retirement
Flexible working hours to suit your lifestyle
£500 annual personal training allowance plus regular learning opportunities on a variety of topics
£40 monthly wellbeing to keep you healthy
24/7 Employee Assistance Program, covering you and immediate family members—offering crisis support, counselling, legal advice, menopause CBT, and more
Unlimited AI usage with Claude and Gemini


Working here

You'll Do Well Here If You

🚀 Move through ambiguity without waiting for a perfect brief

👊 Take ownership of outcomes, not just tasks

⚡ Adapt quickly when priorities shift, and help others do the same

🔧 Look for ways to improve how things work, not just how to complete them

🧠 Use tools, data, and colleagues to make better decisions

This Probably Isn't The Right Fit If You

📋 Prefer clearly defined processes and a stable, slow-moving environment

🚧 Are most comfortable staying within the boundaries of your job description

🔄 Find frequent context-switching draining rather than energising

Inclusion and flexibility

INSHUR is committed to building a team where everyone can bring their full self to work. We follow a structured hiring process and train our interview teams to support fairness and reduce bias.

We welcome applications from people of all backgrounds. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, colour, religion, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, disability, or age.

This role is advertised as full-time. We're open to discussing part-time, job-sharing, or other flexible arrangements for the right person.

If you need any adjustments during the process, let us know and we'll do our best to accommodate.

Longer term, you will take ownership of driving process automation to improve operational efficiency and support business users to make data more accessible and meaningful. You will design and implement workflows, streamline manual processes and take ownership of SharePoint administration, including site structure and permissions. You will also play an active role in troubleshooting and maintaining workflow solutions.
What you'll need to succeedTo be successful in this role, you will need advanced Excel skills (Power Query, pivot tables and complex formulas), strong SQL skills and great communication - bridging the gap between IT/ Data and the wider business. Experience with Power BI and Tableau is advantageous but not essential. You will have a proven ability to analyse data and produce clear, insightful reporting.
What you'll get in returnSalary up to £50,000, 1 day a week working from (possibly 2 days from home), health cash back plan, Life Insurance (2x salary), free food and catered lunches on-site, free on-site parking with electrical charge points. Full benefits package to be confirmed shortly.
In return, you will have the opportunity to work across a broad technology landscape in a role that offers real variety and impact. You will be part of a supportive and forward-thinking team, with opportunities to develop your technical skills and contribute to meaningful business improvements.
What you need to do nowIf you are interested in this role, click 'apply now' to forward an up-to-date copy of your CV, or contact us for a confidential discussion about your career.

Title: Pricing Analyst
Description:
Role Description

An exciting opportunity has arisen for a Pricing Analyst to join our growing Animal Health Pricing Team. The team is responsible for delivering cutting edge Radar live capability, pricing optimisation, impact analysis tools and support the market leading brand PetPlan as well as our growing LV pet proposition. Our team is at the heart of achieving the company’s strategy and it is an exciting time for you to join and shape the future of our business.

Why a Pricing Role At Allianz

Pricing will play a key role in delivering this ambition. We are looking for individuals who have an eye for detail, love working with technology and have a passion for analysing data. You’ll be at the sharp end of our business, delivering analytical models that are used to drive commercial decisions, solve challenging problems, and deliver enhancements to our products and pricing techniques.

A great advantage of being a member of the Animal Health Pricing team is the extensive training provided. The UK team has collaborated with the worldwide Allianz Group, to deliver an accreditation scheme designed to train you in all aspects of pricing. From data science modelling in cutting edge software to automating optimisation calculation routines honed across global markets.

Location: This role is based in our Guildford office. We offer a Hybrid working model and will do our best to accommodate your needs, Typically the team works 1 day per week in the office and 4 days at home.

Salary Information

£35k – £41k per annum.

Pay is based on relevant experience, skills for the role, and location. Salary is only one part of our total reward package.

About You

As a valued member of the team, you will:


Build sophisticated pricing tools using Willis Tower Watson tools (Radar and Emblem) to predict the impact of pricing decisions before they are deployed to market
Play a pivotal role in the delivery and enhancement of our WTW Radar Live capability and price optimisation process
Be part of many projects involving Machine Learning: Data preparation, Automation, Risk monitoring.
Be responsible for deploying rating enhancements to market in a timely and precise manner
Contribute to the development of pricing structures and strategies to achieve growth and profitability objectives
Support initiatives to maintain our “Excellent” Technical Price Certification
Share and promote knowledge, skills and best working practices within the team
Be fully supported in your development and wider career ambitions
Be part of a motivated an exciting team where the word boring does not exist


Essential Skills


Experience using WTW Radar is essential
Excellent problem-solving skills, logically working through scenarios and thinking multiple steps ahead to understand potential impacts
Experience of using data analytics tools for example Python, R, SAS, SQL, Excel, VBA
Well Developed analytical and data interpretation skills when working with large volumes of complex data
Ability to communicate findings to technical and non-technical audiences
You will be qualified to degree level and have a high degree of numerical ability: E.g. Mathematics, Economics, Statistics
You embrace innovation and like challenges
You take ownership of your projects
Business acumen as well as an understanding of the insurance sector - pet insurance knowledge desirable but not essential


We support the government scheme “Access to Work” please feel free to ask us about the access to work scheme.

Desirable Skills


Responsible Artificial Intelligence usage


What We Will Offer You

Recognised and rewarded for a job well done, we have a range of flexible benefits for you to choose from- so you can pick a package that’s perfect for you. We also offer flexible working options, global career opportunities across the wider Allianz Group, and fantastic career development and training. That’s on top of enjoying all the benefits you’d expect from the world’s number one insurance brand, including:


Flexible buy/sell holiday options
Hybrid working
Annual performance related bonus
Contributory pension scheme
Development days
A discount up to 50% on a range of insurance products including car, home and pet
Retail discounts
Volunteering days


Our Ways of Working

Do you need flexibility with the hours you work? Let us know as part of your application and if it’s right for our customers, our business and for you, then we’ll do everything we can to make it happen. Here at Allianz, we are signatories of the ABIs flexible working charter. We believe in supporting hybrid work patterns, which balance the needs of our customers, with your personal circumstances and our business requirements. Our aim with this is to help innovation, creativity, and you to thrive - Your work life balance is important to us.

Integrity, Fairness, Inclusion & Trust

At Allianz, we believe in fostering an inclusive workforce and are proud to be an equal opportunity employer. Our commitment to equal opportunities, gender equity, and balanced gender representation, is demonstrated by our numerous accreditations: EDGE certified for gender inclusion, Women in Finance Charter members, Disability Confident employer, Stonewall Diversity Champion, Business in the Community’s Race at Work Charter signatories, and Armed Forces Covenant gold standard employer.

We embrace neurodiversity and welcome applications from neurodivergent and disabled candidates, offering tailored adjustments to ensure your success.

We encourage our employees to advocate for their needs, whether it’s assistive technology, ergonomic equipment, mentoring, coaching, or flexible work arrangements

Accessible Application for All

As part of the Disability Confident Scheme, we support candidates with disabilities or long-term health conditions through the Offer an Interview Scheme, for those meeting the essential skills for the role.

Contact our Resourcing team to opt into this scheme or for assistance with your application, including larger text, hard copies, or spoken applications. Hr-recruitment@allianz.co.uk

For any inquiries or to submit your application, please contact: Conor McCourt

If you are an at-risk candidate facing potential redeployment, please include this information in your CV.

Closing date 14th June 2026

We reserve the right to close the advert early if we reach enough applications.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Commercial Data Science Analyst

Hybrid | Commercial & Product Analytics | Predictive Modelling | AI & Insight




We’re working with a highly recognised consumer-focused organisation undergoing an exciting transformation in how it uses data, analytics and AI to drive commercial performance. This is a newly enhanced opportunity for a commercially minded Data Science Analyst to play a pivotal role in shaping strategic decisions across multiple revenue streams, products and customer journeys.

This is far more than a reporting role.

You’ll sit at the intersection of commercial strategy, advanced analytics and AI-driven insight — partnering closely with senior stakeholders to influence pricing, forecasting, customer retention, revenue optimisation and long-term growth.




The Opportunity

Joining a growing commercial analytics function, you’ll help evolve the organisation’s capability from traditional reporting into predictive, insight-led decision making.

You’ll work across a broad range of commercial areas including customer membership, digital products, travel and leisure services, financial products and customer loyalty — using data to unlock growth opportunities and improve customer lifetime value.

The role offers a genuine chance to shape the future analytics roadmap, introduce modern data science techniques and champion AI adoption across the wider business.

What You’ll Be Doing

Delivering commercially focused analysis and actionable insight to senior stakeholders
Building predictive models to support customer retention, acquisition and propensity analysis
Developing forecasting models for demand, occupancy, revenue and performance trends
Creating dashboards and performance reporting that drive strategic decision making
Conducting root-cause analysis to identify opportunities, risks and commercial drivers
Supporting pricing, promotions and scenario modelling initiatives
Working with large and varied datasets across multiple systems and business functions
Driving AI and automation initiatives to improve insight generation and operational efficiency
Helping shape best practice around data quality, governance and analytics capability




What We’re Looking For

We’re looking for someone who combines strong technical capability with genuine commercial curiosity.

You’ll likely have experience in a commercial analytics, data science or insight-focused role where you’ve worked closely with business stakeholders and translated complex data into meaningful action.




Key Skills & Experience

Strong SQL and advanced data manipulation skills
Experience using Python or R for predictive modelling and machine learning
Knowledge of models such as Random Forest, XGBoost or similar
Strong BI and dashboarding capability (Tableau, Power BI or similar)
Advanced Excel modelling and scenario analysis skills
Experience with forecasting, segmentation or propensity modelling
Ability to communicate complex insights clearly to non-technical audiences
Commercial awareness and an understanding of business performance drivers
Interest or experience in AI/LLM technologies and automation
Degree educated in a numerical discipline such as Data Science, Statistics, Mathematics, Economics or Physics




Why This Role Stands Out

Opportunity to influence commercial strategy at a senior level
Broad, varied analytics environment with real business impact
Strong focus on innovation, predictive analytics and AI
High visibility across multiple commercial functions
Opportunity to help shape and modernise the organisation’s data capability
Hybrid working and collaborative culture
Genuine autonomy and ownership

If you’re looking for a role where you can combine commercial thinking, advanced analytics and modern AI-driven insight to influence real business outcomes, we’d love to hear from you.

Title: Pricing Innovation Analyst
Description:
We’re 1st Central, a market-leading insurance company utilising smart data and technology at pace. Rapid growth has been based on giving our 1.4 million customers exactly what they want: great value insurance with an excellent service. And that’s the same for our colleagues too; we won Insurance Employer of the Year at the British Insurance Awards 2024 and our Glassdoor score is pretty mega too!




We’re looking for a Pricing Innovation Analyst to play a key role in shaping the future of our pricing capability. You’ll drive the evolution of our burn cost models by uncovering new predictive insights, increasing model sophistication, experimenting with innovative techniques, and building cutting-edge reporting solutions.




We’re big on flexible hybrid working. Our teams are based across Haywards Heath (West Sussex) and Salford Quays (Manchester). We primarily work from home but value collaboration, so we come together in person to keep ideas flowing, typically once a week or fortnight. Prefer being in the office more often? That works for us too.




Core skills we’re looking for to succeed in the role:




Analytical & Numerical Skills: Excellent data and statistical analysis skills, with strong numerical capability and a logical, problem‑solving approach
Programming Skills: Experience with Python, R or SAS
Modelling Capability: Some experience or familiarity with modelling techniques (e.g. EMBLEM, Python, GBM builds in Radar), or a strong interest in developing these skills
Industry & Role Experience: 2 years’+ experience gained within a commercial or pricing role within personal lines motor insurance, plus a 2:1 degree (or equivalent) in a numerate discipline.
Communication & Presentation: Strong verbal and written communication skills, with the ability to present complex concepts at a level appropriate to the audience

Title: Process Analyst, EU Central Operations Analytics
Description:
Description

Amazon Logistics (AMZL) is transforming the future of last-mile delivery through Central Operations, a strategic initiative that's revolutionizing how we approach route planning, scheduling, and delivery execution. We are seeking a Process Analyst to join our EU Central Operations team, where you'll play a pivotal role in driving AI-powered automation and enhancing our delivery network operations through advanced analytics and intelligent process improvements.

This role uniquely combines deep business operations knowledge with hands-on technical implementation, positioning you at the forefront of AMZL's AI transformation. You'll work at the intersection of operations, analytics, and automation, focusing on complex analytical problems that impact our intra-week forecasting and capacity management processes while developing and deploying AI-driven solutions that scale across our network.

You'll have the opportunity to architect and implement automated workflows, machine learning models, and intelligent decision-support systems that transform how we operate. Your dual expertise in business operations and technical execution will be essential as you translate operational challenges into automated solutions, leveraging AI/ML capabilities to drive efficiency at scale. Using advanced SQL, Python/R programming, and emerging AI technologies, you'll create intelligent dashboards, predictive models, and automated reporting systems that enable proactive decision-making and help avoid in-station volume risks.

You'll partner with cross-functional teams including Operations, Data Science, and Engineering to develop and deploy automation solutions that streamline processes and implement AI-powered improvements. This position requires someone who can speak both the language of business operations and technical implementation, bridging the gap between operational needs and technical possibilities.

This position offers exceptional growth opportunities as you develop expertise in AMZL systems, AI/ML applications, and automation frameworks. You'll learn to balance tactical execution with strategic thinking while contributing to the scaling of Central Operations through intelligent automation. Your ability to translate data into actionable insights and automated solutions will be crucial in driving continuous improvement in our last-mile delivery network.

The ideal candidate will combine strong technical and analytical skills with deep operational understanding, excellent communication abilities, and a passion for AI-driven process improvement. If you're excited about solving complex operational challenges through automation and contributing to the AI transformation of logistics operations, we want to hear from you.

Key job responsibilities

AI & Automation Development


Design and implement AI-powered automation solutions to streamline operational processes
Develop machine learning models for predictive analytics and intelligent forecasting
Build automated decision-support systems that scale across the network
Create intelligent workflows that reduce manual intervention and improve accuracy
Partner with Data Science teams to deploy AI/ML solutions into production
Evaluate and integrate emerging AI technologies to solve business challenges


Analysis & Process Improvement


Develop diagnostic analyses to identify automation opportunities and operational inefficiencies
Design and implement solutions that combine business logic with technical automation
Analyze complex data sets using advanced analytics and AI techniques to generate insights
Partner with stakeholders to translate business requirements into automated technical solutions
Optimize daily operational tasks through intelligent automation and process redesign
Monitor and analyze key performance metrics using predictive analytics to identify trends


Technical Execution & Implementation


Apply advanced SQL, Python/R programming to build scalable analytical solutions
Create and maintain intelligent dashboards with automated alerting and anomaly detection
Develop automated data pipelines and ETL processes for real-time insights
Build and document repeatable, automated analytical processes and workflows
Implement data quality monitoring systems with automated validation
Deploy code and automation solutions following software development best practices


Project Management & Strategic Thinking


Lead AI/automation projects from conception through deployment and scaling
Balance business requirements with technical feasibility in solution design
Design both tactical solutions and long-term strategic automation approaches
Provide technical and business input on prioritization and identify synergies between initiatives
Manage tradeoffs between speed, quality, scalability, and resources


Stakeholder Partnership & Knowledge Transfer


Partner with Operations, Engineering, Data Science, and technical teams
Translate complex technical concepts for business stakeholders and vice versa
Present AI/automation solutions and recommendations to leadership
Influence cross-functional teams to adopt automated solutions and meet shared goals
Mentor team members on both business operations and technical implementation
Build effective working relationships across business and technical functions


A day in the life

About The Team

Amazon Logistics, or AMZL, handles 'last mile' delivery duties in partnership with third-party distribution businesses. We utilize creative thinking, continuous improvement initiatives, and innovative AI/automation technologies to get millions of physical products into the hands of our customers. Our Central Operations team is at the forefront of AMZL's AI transformation, developing intelligent systems that revolutionize how we plan, execute, and optimize deliveries. Our goal is to make our customers' delivery experience as smooth as possible through automated, data-driven solutions that scale globally.

Basic Qualifications


Bachelor's degree or equivalent qualification in Math, Engineering, Science or Business


Preferred Qualifications


Bachelor's degree or equivalent in a technical or analytical discipline
Experience developing AI/ML models or automated workflows in production environments
Experience with business intelligence and visualization tools (Tableau, QuickSight, Power BI, etc.)
Experience with cloud platforms (AWS) and modern data infrastructure (Redshift, S3, etc.)
Experience writing technical documentation, business requirements, and functional specifications
Background in process automation, workflow optimization, or intelligent systems
Experience working in agile/iterative development environments
Track record of successfully deploying technical solutions that solve business problems


Amazon is an equal opportunities employer. We believe passionately that employing a diverse workforce is central to our success. We make recruiting decisions based on your experience and skills. We value your passion to discover, invent, simplify and build. Protecting your privacy and the security of your data is a longstanding top priority for Amazon. Please consult our Privacy Notice (https://www.amazon.jobs/en/privacy_page) to know more about how we collect, use and transfer the personal data of our candidates.

Amazon is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of protected veteran status, disability, or other legally protected status.

Our inclusive culture empowers Amazonians to deliver the best results for our customers. If you have a disability and need a workplace accommodation or adjustment during the application and hiring process, including support for the interview or onboarding process, please visit https://amazon.jobs/content/en/how-we-hire/accommodations for more information. If the country/region you’re applying in isn’t listed, please contact your Recruiting Partner.

  • Apply advanced SQL, Python/R programming to build scalable analytical solutions
  • Create and maintain intelligent dashboards with automated alerting and anomaly detection
  • Develop automated data pipelines and ETL processes for real-time insights
  • Build and document repeatable, automated analytical processes and workflows
  • Implement data quality monitoring systems with automated validation
  • Deploy code and automation solutions following software development best practices

Project Management & Strategic Thinking

  • Lead AI/automation projects from conception through deployment and scaling
  • Balance business requirements with technical feasibility in solution design
  • Design both tactical solutions and long-term strategic automation approaches
  • Provide technical and business input on prioritization and identify synergies between initiatives
  • Manage tradeoffs between speed, quality, scalability, and resources

Stakeholder Partnership & Knowledge Transfer

  • Partner with Operations, Engineering, Data Science, and technical teams
  • Translate complex technical concepts for business stakeholders and vice versa
  • Present AI/automation solutions and recommendations to leadership
  • Influence cross-functional teams to adopt automated solutions and meet shared goals
  • Mentor team members on both business operations and technical implementation
  • Build effective working relationships across business and technical functions

A day in the life

About The Team

Amazon Logistics, or AMZL, handles 'last mile' delivery duties in partnership with third-party distribution businesses. We utilize creative thinking, continuous improvement initiatives, and innovative AI/automation technologies to get millions of physical products into the hands of our customers. Our Central Operations team is at the forefront of AMZL's AI transformation, developing intelligent systems that revolutionize how we plan, execute, and optimize deliveries. Our goal is to make our customers' delivery experience as smooth as possible through automated, data-driven solutions that scale globally.

Basic Qualifications

  • Bachelor's degree or equivalent qualification in Math, Engineering, Science or Business

Preferred Qualifications

  • Bachelor's degree or equivalent in a technical or analytical discipline
  • Experience developing AI/ML models or automated workflows in production environments
  • Experience with business intelligence and visualization tools (Tableau, QuickSight, Power BI, etc.)
  • Experience with cloud platforms (AWS) and modern data infrastructure (Redshift, S3, etc.)
  • Experience writing technical documentation, business requirements, and functional specifications
  • Background in process automation, workflow optimization, or intelligent systems
  • Experience working in agile/iterative development environments
  • Track record of successfully deploying technical solutions that solve business problems

Title: Data Analyst - Insurance
Description:
Job Specification: Insurance Data Analyst (Operations & Digital Channels)

Salary Band: £47,500 - £55,000 + Benefits

We're looking for an experienced Data Analyst with strong expertise across customer journeys, digital channels, and operational analytics, ideally gained within an insurance intermediary or a similar high-volume, customer-focused environment. This exciting new position reports to the Chief Growth Officer and works closely with the sales leadership team. The role is focused on digital channel performance, customer journeys, and trading insight, supporting day-to-day reporting while helping to shape how data is used to optimise performance across the funnel.

A key part of the role will be developing insight around operational and colleague performance, alongside building out core reporting across the sales funnel. Early priorities will include designing and delivering agent performance dashboards and improving visibility of trading metrics. This is a hands-on role suited to someone with strong core analytical skills and the ambition to grow within a scaling data function.

Key Responsibilities


Support reporting across digital channels and customer funnels
Analyse performance data to identify trends, opportunities and issues
Work closely with the Growth team to provide actionable insight
Help define data requirements and feed into engineering teams
Build and maintain dashboards in Power BI
Support ongoing optimisation of reporting and insight across operations
Contribute to improving how data is used day-to-day within the business


Skills & Experience Profile


Experience in a Data Analyst role, ideally with exposure to digital or operational data
Strong Power BI skills and confidence working with data sets
Good understanding of customer journeys / digital performance metrics
Ability to translate data into clear, practical insight
Strong communication skills and willingness to engage with stakeholders
Ambitious, curious and keen to develop within a growing team


Technical Skills Profile


Solid Power BI capability and experience building dashboards
Good working knowledge of SQL and analysing operational / digital datasets
Understanding of digital performance metrics and customer journeys


Additional Information


Hybrid working (Basingstoke HQ) / 2-3 days per week onsite
22 days holiday + bank holidays
Pension and wider benefits package

Title: Commercial Data Science Analyst
Description:
Purpose of the job

To collaborate with and support the existing Lead Commercial Analyst in providing all commercial analysis and insight across core Club products. This includes: performance reporting; scenario modelling; root-cause and trend analysis; demand/revenue forecasting and phasing; and problem solving. You will evolve and distribute KPI reporting and performance dashboards and introduce new data-science driven predictive analytics and models (which may include the introduction of new supporting tools/systems such as DataBricks or Snowflake). You will also transpose your expertise by supporting the skills development of the existing Lead Commercial Analyst.

Key Tasks And Responsibilities


BAU reporting & MI: Provide accurate and timely Management Information (MI) and performance reporting. Identify and track core KPIs, build and distribute interactive dashboards to colleagues and maintain a central repository of standardised reports.
Analysis & insight: Evaluate multiple data sources to investigate complex business problems and draw out knowledge, providing ‘actionable insights’ to guide product development and business strategy.
Commercial modelling: Use data to build economic models to identify the cost-benefit outcomes of potential scenarios (supporting for example marketing initiatives, commercial promotions, product developments etc).
Predictive analytics & modelling: Introduce and apply reusable predictive modelling utilising data-science techniques to support performance in key areas such as membership (architect models for lapse probabilities), UK campsites (booker propensity modelling) and lifetime value (purchase behaviours across UK campsites, travel and financial services)
Demand/revenue forecasting & phasing: Support the Lead Commercial Analyst in forecasting and phasing demand, yield and revenue across UK campsites and membership joins/renewals as part of the annual budgeting process (noting there is a dynamic occupancy system in place delivered by a Revenue Management System).
AI & automation leadership: Act as a lead for AI adoption across the marketing / commercial / product teams to accelerate insight generation and deliver efficiencies.
Market intelligence & insight: Synthesise structured and unstructured data to provide broader understanding of competitors pricing / product propositions and support the Research & Insights manager in identifying changing trends amongst consumers.
Operational excellence: Support our IS colleagues in building and maintaining a “single source of truth” by ensuring all data pipelines and processes are robust. Lead initiatives to support a data quality roadmap to ensure modelling and reporting is based on sufficient suitable data quality.


Essential Skills & Experience Required


Data analysis and insight: Data preparation, modelling, analysis and visualisation techniques including predictive analysis, business intelligence, pattern recognition, economic modelling.
Expert data manipulation: Expert proficiency in Excel / GSheets for daily reporting and scenario modelling is essential.
BI & visualisation: Proficiency in Tableau / similar to create dynamic dashboards.
Commercial acumen: A deep understanding of commercial drivers, financial terminology, and the levers available to influence bottom-line surplus.
Collaborative autonomy: A self-starter capable of working alone on deep-dive technical tasks while also acting as a strategic partner to the wider Commercial, Product and Revenue Management teams.
Integrity & accuracy: You possess a high degree of intellectual integrity and maintain a “zero-error” mindset for both daily reports and complex models.
Predictive & inquisitive mindset: You don’t just ask "what happened?"; you hunt for the root cause and can build the algorithms that explain "what will happen next."
Compelling storytelling: The ability to distill complex data and multi-variable models into actionable, visually engaging strategies for non-technical senior stakeholders.
Programming & predictive modelling: Advanced skills in Python or R for building and deploying predictive models (e.g. Random Forest, XGBoost). Machine Learning.
Data engineering: Advanced SQL skills for transforming and aggregating large-scale datasets from diverse sources.
AI integration: Practical experience or strong knowledge in leveraging AI/LLMs.
Academic requirement: Educated to Degree level in a highly numerical field (Data Science, Statistics, Mathematics, Economics, or Physics).


The Caravan and Motorhome Club is committed to employing a diverse workforce. All applications are treated equally and we recruit purely on the basis of skills and experience. We know our greatest strength is our people, so differences are celebrated, and we strive to create an environment where colleagues feel respected and valued for their unique potential.

Title: Field Insight Specialist
Description:
Summary

Location

Manchester, Reading

Job Family

Digital & Technology

Job Type

Full Time

Posted Date

15-Jun-2026

Ref #

73047

As a Field Insight Specialist, you’ll play a key role in helping the business understand how our field operations are really performing. Sitting within the Field Insight team, you’ll provide an independent, data-led view of end-to-end performance, turning complex datasets into clear, actionable insight that drives smarter decisions and better outcomes for customers.

In this role, you’ll analyse field performance data, including trial results and customer self-install journeys, identifying trends, root causes and opportunities for improvement. You’ll collaborate closely with stakeholders across the business, translating data into meaningful stories that non-technical audiences can understand, and helping shape initiatives that improve performance at scale.

This is a hybrid role based from either our Manchester or Reading offices.

Who we are

At Virgin Media O₂, we connect people to the moments that matter most. Virgin Media leads the UK for broadband reliability, speed and overall performance, while O₂ keeps people connected on the move with award-winning coverage.

We’re also proud to be named one of the Financial Times Top 500 Best Employers for 2026, based on one of the UK’s largest employee satisfaction surveys.

Join us and you’ll be part of a team that stays curious, positive and focused on making life easier for our customers and each other. We’re investing in the future of connectivity, from expanding next-generation 5G to exploring satellite-supported solutions that help reach rural communities and hard-to-connect places.You’ll work with people who back good ideas and give you plenty of room to grow. So if you’re looking for a place where you can feel supported, trusted and genuinely valued, you’ll feel right at home here.

Our ways of working

We’re a flexible-first organisation, because we know people do their best work when they have choice and clarity. To support meaningful collaboration, we ask everyone to spend at least eight days a month connecting with each other in person.

That doesn’t just mean time in the office – it could be team meetings, offsites, volunteering days, cross-functional projects, or away days. What matters is making those moments count – so when we come together, it benefits everyone.

Accessible, inclusive and equitable for allWe believe a better tomorrow starts with the choices we make today. That means moving the dial on diversity, building equity into everything we do and putting inclusion at the heart of our business.Virgin Media O₂ is an equal opportunities employer and we work hard to remove bias and barriers. We want every person who joins us – and everyone thinking about joining us – to feel seen, heard and supported.

When you work with us, you’ll have access to our award-winning employee network groups. These communities bring people together, champion allyship and create uplifting spaces where you can learn, share and feel part of something bigger. They play a big role in helping people feel connected from day one.

The must haves

In order to be considered, you must have the following experience;


Previous experience in analytics or insights
Strong capability in SQL, Excel and PowerPoint
An understanding of statistical techniques for analysis and forecasting
Experience turning complex data into easily digestible information for a variety of audiences
A proven ability to deliver accurate insight whilst working to tight deadlines



The other stuff we are looking for

We'd also love you to bring;


Working knowledge of Tableau or similar visualisation tools
Confidence working with a range of stakeholders to influence outcomes
A passion for problem solving and continuous improvement
A collaborative, positive approach to teamwork



What's in it for you

We know life isn’t just about work, so our benefits are built to support you in the moments that matter. You’ll get Bupa healthcare, wellbeing support, life cover, a pension and generous holiday –plus your birthday off.

And there’s more! Enjoy some extra perks on us, with optional electric vehicle lease and Cycle2Work schemes, dental insurance, neurodiversity assessments, and brilliant discounts on broadband, TV and mobile. You’ll even get access to the Virgin Family platform and the O₂ Priority app, with exclusive rewards, experiences and chances to attend events at The O₂ and beyond.

Next steps

If this feels like a place where you can belong, we’d love to see what you can do. Once you apply, the next steps might include a call with a recruiter, a video interview, an interview with the hiring manager and a task or assessment.

Title: Data & Insights Analyst
Description:
Job Description

This is a hybrid data engineering and data analytics role, sitting within the Data & Insights team and reporting directly to the Head of Data & Insights. You will own the end-to-end delivery of data solutions: from building and maintaining the pipelines that move and transform data across our systems, to developing the reports and visualisations that inform decision-making at every level of the business.
Working closely with functional and regional stakeholders, you will translate business questions into analytical outputs that are accurate, timely, and genuinely useful. You will be the primary point of execution within the team, freeing the Head of Data & Insights to focus on longer-term data strategy, governance, and organisational development.
This role suits someone who is equally comfortable writing SQL/Python and building a Power BI dashboard as they are presenting findings to a senior stakeholder. You will need to bring both technical rigour and commercial curiosity to the work.

Job Requirements

Data Engineering & Infrastructure
Design, build, and maintain data pipelines across our core systems, including Salesforce, NetSuite/OpenAir, and SQL Server, using our Azure Data Factory and Boomi integration platform.

Ensure data flows reliably between source systems and reporting layers, with appropriate monitoring, alerting, and documentation in place.
Support the ongoing migration of our data infrastructure towards Microsoft Fabric, contributing to architecture decisions as directed by the Head of Data & Insights.
Identify and resolve data quality issues at source, working with system owners and regional teams to implement durable fixes.
Maintain and improve the data models that underpin our reporting layer, including dimensional modelling and incremental load strategies.
 
Analytics & Reporting

Build and maintain incisive, well-designed reports and dashboards in Power BI, tailored to the needs of specific stakeholder groups from operational management through to Executive Leadership.
Develop a thorough understanding of The Gap Partnership's commercial model, markets, portfolio, and strategic priorities, applying this knowledge to design analytics that drive actionable insight.
Lead the delivery of reporting across key performance domains including Sales Performance, Utilisation, and Cost to Serve, ensuring measures are consistently defined and accurately calculated.
Respond to ad-hoc analytical requests from Executive and Management leadership, scoping, prioritising, and delivering outputs in line with agreed business priorities.
Present data-led insights clearly and confidently to senior stakeholders, translating complex analysis into concise recommendations.
 Data Governance & Quality

Support the Data & Insights function in embedding data governance principles across the organisation, working with the Process Champion Network to drive data quality and adoption.
Develop and deliver training materials that help colleagues understand performance definitions, data quality responsibilities, and the role of analytics in decision-making.
Put in place monitoring approaches that allow regional management to track and maintain data quality within their areas.
Identify opportunities for data cleansing and hygiene improvements, making practical recommendations and supporting their implementation.
 Stakeholder Engagement

Build effective working relationships with functional and regional peers, understanding their reporting needs and translating them into well-scoped deliverables.
Contribute to the Global Community of Practitioners, participating in joint sessions with regions, markets, and key functions to share knowledge and improve data culture.
Collaborate with Executive and Management leadership to agree and maintain a consistent reporting cadence across the business.
 

Job Responsibilities

Skills & Experience
Essential

Proven experience delivering data analytics or business intelligence solutions that have had a measurable impact on business performance.
Strong SQL skills and demonstrable experience building data pipelines and integration workflows.
Proficiency in Power BI, including data modelling, DAX, and report design for varied audiences.
Hands-on experience with Salesforce and at least one PSA or ERP system, ideally NetSuite or OpenAir.
Ability to work with ambiguous requirements, ask the right questions, and deliver outputs that meet the underlying business need.
Strong communication skills with the confidence to present findings and recommendations to senior stakeholders.
High attention to detail and a practical approach to data quality and governance.
 
Desirable

Experience with Azure Data Factory, Microsoft Fabric, or equivalent cloud data integration platforms.
Familiarity with Boomi or similar iPaaS tools.
Experience working in a professional services, consulting, or multi-regional environment.
Degree in Data Science, Computer Science, Information Systems, Statistics, Economics, or a related field.

Title: Reporting & Data Analyst
Description:
Lloyd’s is the world’s leading insurance and reinsurance marketplace. We share the collective intelligence and risk sharing expertise of the market’s brightest minds, working together for a braver world.

Our role is to inspire courage, so tomorrow’s progress isn’t limited by today’s risks.

Our shared values: we are brave; we are stronger together; we do the right thing; guide what we do and how we act. If you share our values and our passion to build a future that’s more sustainable, resilient and inclusive, you’ll find a home at Lloyd’s – build a braver future with us.

Location: Chatham

Function: Member Services

About The Role

We are seeking a detail-oriented Reporting and Data Analyst to join the Member Services team. This role sits within the Data & Finance function and plays a key part in supporting the accurate administration and reporting of Funds at Lloyd’s (FAL). You will be responsible for delivering high-quality data, reporting, and analysis to ensure effective oversight, regulatory compliance, and informed decision-making. This is a strong opportunity for someone who thrives in a controlled, data-driven environment and is motivated to improve processes and reporting quality.

Key Responsibilities


Deliver accurate and timely regulatory, operational, and management reporting
Maintain robust reporting processes, controls, and reconciliations
Support FAL valuations, corridor testing, and asset reporting activities
Investigate and resolve data and reporting issues in collaboration with stakeholders
Monitor data quality and escalate risks or control concerns where required
Produce insightful analysis to support decision-making, audits, and queries
Continuously improve reporting processes, data quality, and operational efficiency



Key Skills And Experience


Strong analytical capability with exceptional attention to detail
Advanced Excel and data handling skills
Ability to manage priorities and deliver to deadlines
Strong stakeholder engagement and communication skills
Proven problem-solving and process improvement ability
Experience:

Financial reporting, governance, reconciliations, and controls
Data quality management and risk-based oversight

What We’re Looking For


A proactive and accountable individual with sound judgement
Strong problem-solving skills with the ability to interpret data and make recommendations

A delivery-focused mindset, with the ability to work at pace and adapt to change



Why Join Us


You will be part of a team responsible for delivering critical financial data and reporting that underpins underwriting activity at Lloyd’s. The role offers exposure to key financial processes, regulatory requirements, and opportunities to contribute to continuous improvement initiatives

Title: People Data Design Lead
Place: Brighton And Hove, England, United Kingdom
Description:
Legal & General (L&G) is a leading UK financial services group and major global investor.



We’ve been safeguarding people’s financial futures since 1836, and strive to build a better society, while improving the lives of our customers and creating value for shareholders.



We are one of the world’s largest asset managers and provide powerful asset origination capabilities. Together, these underpin our retirement and protection solutions: we are an international player in pension risk transfer, in UK and US life insurance, and in UK workplace pensions and retirement income.



Our Group Functions provide the services that all areas of the business need. This requires a talented and diverse team behind the scenes, who enable everyone at L&G to do what they do best. 



Joining us means helping to improve the lives of our customers and contributing to the success of the business every day.





Job Description



We’re looking for a People Data Design Lead to play a key role in shaping how people data supports transformation across HR. This is a unique opportunity to combine data expertise with change and stakeholder engagement, working across a range of complex initiatives that impact how the organisation operates.



You’ll work at the heart of HR transformation, ensuring data design, reporting and governance are aligned to business objectives, regulatory expectations and great user outcomes. This role suits someone who enjoys influencing decisions, working collaboratively and bringing clarity to complex data challenges.



What you’ll be doing:



Supporting the delivery of HR change initiatives by defining and shaping people data requirements
Designing data models, reporting frameworks and data solutions aligned to enterprise principles
Analysing and interpreting complex datasets to inform decision-making and design choices
Partnering with HR, change and technical teams to align data design with business processes and operating models
Identifying data risks, gaps and dependencies and driving appropriate mitigation
Facilitating stakeholder workshops and gathering requirements across multiple initiatives
Influencing and challenging stakeholders to ensure effective and practical data-led decisions
Supporting the development of reporting and self-service solutions that meet user and programme needs

Who we’re looking for:



Experience delivering data or transformation initiatives, ideally within HR or people data environments
Strong understanding of data design, data governance and reporting principles
Proven ability to work across multiple projects and manage competing priorities
Experience engaging and influencing a range of stakeholders across business and technical teams
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with the ability to interpret complex data
Excellent communication skills, with confidence presenting ideas and challenging constructively
Experience working with tools such as Excel, Oracle reporting or similar platforms (desirable)
Awareness of emerging technologies such as AI, automation or machine learning in a data context (desirable)

Whatever your role, we reward performance and behaviour with a package that looks after all the things that are important to you. Here are some of the benefits we offer:



The opportunity to participate in our annual, performance -related bonus plan and valuable share schemes
Generous pension contribution
Life assurance
Healthcare Plan (permanent employees only)
At least 25 days holiday, plus public holidays, 26 days after 2 years’ service. There’s also the option to buy and sell holiday
Competitive family leave
Participate in our electric car scheme, which offers employees the option to hire a brand-new electric car through tax efficient salary sacrifice (permanent employees only)
There are many discounts we offer – both for our own products and at a range of high street stores and online
We’re creating net‑zero carbon workplaces by 2030 by investing in our sustainable, modern offices across the UK, all designed to bring people together and elevate the in‑person experience


Additional Information



At L&G, we believe it's possible to generate positive returns today while helping to build a better future for all.



If you join us, you’ll be part of a welcoming, inclusive culture, with opportunities to collaborate with people of diverse backgrounds, views, and experiences. Guided by leaders with integrity who care about your future and wellbeing. Empowered through initiatives which support people to develop their careers and excel.



We care passionately about outcomes rather than attendance and are therefore open to discussing all kinds of flexible working options including part-time, term-time and job shares. Although some roles have limited flexibility due to customer demand, we accommodate requests when we can.



It doesn’t matter if you don’t meet every single criterion in this advert. Instead, think about what you excel at and what else you can bring in terms of strengths, potential and connection to our purpose.

Title: Pricing Analyst
Description:
The Role

We’re looking for a curious, analytical and commercially minded Pricing Analyst to join our growing team. This is a broad, high‑impact role where you’ll work across a wide range of pricing projects, collaborating closely with underwriting, marketing and commercial teams to help drive Agria’s continued growth.




You’ll play a key part in shaping pricing decisions across both direct and partner channels - using data, market insight and modelling techniques to support profitable growth, strong customer outcomes and robust governance.




Working alongside colleagues across pricing, underwriting and the wider business, you’ll deliver analysis, monitor performance and contribute to the development of our pricing models and optimisation capabilities.




This role is offered on a 12 month Fixed Term Contract basis.




What You’ll Be Doing

Supporting pricing strategy - translating business priorities into clear analytical recommendations and pricing actions across direct and partnership channels.
Analysing performance - reviewing sales, conversion and retention trends to identify risks, opportunities and drivers of customer lifetime value.
Developing pricing models - contributing to the build, enhancement and monitoring of pricing and optimisation models using statistical, actuarial or data‑science techniques.
Assessing pricing impacts - evaluating proposed pricing and product changes to balance commercial performance, risk and customer outcomes.
Supporting governance - ensuring pricing changes are deployed accurately with full documentation, impact assessments and governance sign‑off.
Resolving pricing issues - investigating queries and errors, improving processes and reducing repeat demand.
Managing day‑to‑day queries - prioritising and responding to requests, escalating where needed.
Collaborating across teams - sharing expertise, supporting colleagues and contributing to a high‑performing, inclusive team culture.
Ensuring fair value - making sure all pricing activity supports good customer outcomes and meets internal governance standards.




What We’re Looking For

Insurance pricing expertise - strong understanding of general insurance pricing processes and methodologies; pet pricing experience is ideal.
Technical capability - experience with pricing software such as Quantee, Earnix or Radar.
Modelling skills - confident in GLMs and/or machine‑learning approaches to risk pricing.
Analytical strength - excellent numerical and statistical skills with the ability to interpret complex data and turn insights into actionable recommendations.
Data‑tool proficiency - comfortable using tools such as Excel, SAS, SQL, Python, R or specialist pricing platforms.
Commercial awareness - understanding how pricing decisions influence growth, retention, profitability and customer outcomes.
Customer‑focused mindset - committed to delivering fair value and strong customer experiences.
Collaborative approach - enthusiastic, proactive and supportive within a team environment.
Strong communication skills - able to explain analysis clearly to both technical and non‑technical audiences.
Organised & detail‑driven - able to manage competing priorities in a fast‑paced environment.
Curious & proactive - always looking for better ways of doing things.




Why choose Agria Pet Insurance:

Agria Pet Insurance is one of the UK's leading specialist pet insurance providers. We only provide lifetime insurance for animals, so truly understand and care about our customers and their beloved pets - especially as most of our team are pet parents, and we're all animal-lovers. Our passion for pets means that we are trusted by The Royal Kennel Club, Lloyds and many other significant affinity partners to provide lifetime pet insurance in their names.

Working closely with animal welfare organisations, vets and breeders, gives us deep insight on what's best for pets. And we give back too - including supporting hundreds of animal rescues, protecting over 100,000 rescued animals, and through donations of over £2m to animal charities.

We are a strong, growing business, having doubled our GWP in the last 5 years, with a supportive ethos and family feel. We became one of the UK’s Best Workplaces™ in 2023, 2024, 2025 and again in 2026, we have the top 4 slots on Which? Best Buy pet insurance, are ranked 4.8 on Trustpilot (higher than any other pet insurance provider) and we are extremely proud to be the only carbon negative pet insurer in the UK.




What we offer:

25 days annual leave which increases with service, plus bank holidays and an extra day off for your Birthday!
Opportunity to buy/sell up to 5 days annual leave per calendar year
Contributory pension and Life Assurance scheme
Access to 25% off insuring your pet with Agria. Friends and family are also entitled to a discount too
We aim to support the health and wellbeing of all our colleagues so you’ll have access to a Health Cash Plan, mental health support and health and wellbeing platforms, including Headspace
Free tickets to a huge variety of pet related events
Complimentary refreshments in our breakout area
Regular team activities and events arranged by our Employee Forum
