Title: R&D Data Analyst
Description:
Job Title: R&D Data Analyst

Business Function: R&D

Location: Leeds Office and R&D

Job Purpose

The Deo R&D Data Analyst will play a key role in supporting the digital transformation journey of Deodorants R&D. The role holder will support the Data & Digital manager to drive engagement in a Digital first Data Culture through driving adoption of key tools & systems; as well as supporting the functional experts with analytics to aid data-led decision making. We are looking for people with a natural curiosity and desire to interrogate and connect data to help us translate R&D product and business data into insights that our R&D teams can use to develop more successful products.

Responsibilities


Support the Deo R&D Data & Digital Manager to drive adoption of key tools & systems which enable data capture for a digital transformation programme.
Identify, develop & implement priority analytics and data visualisation tools (together with functional experts, partners and stakeholders) using our suite of apps (e.g. PowerApps, PowerBI, Datalab) to enable confidence in rapid data driven R&D decisions across product development functions.
Support the Deo R&D Data & Digital Manager direct delivery of digital training (Data Literacy & Data Analytics Tool Awareness & adoption) across Deo R&D teams.
Proactively support the generation of key business/Deo R&D insights through data analytics and the visualisation of complex data sets across product development functions.
Identify and communicate examples of business impact from data analytics practices to further drive operational adoption of digital tools & applications across Deo R&D.
Support the Deo R&D Data & Digital Manager with the execution and monitoring of Data Quality to ensure our data is available for various business, analytical & modelling needs..


All About You

Education: GCSE English and Maths or equivalent, Degree level Scientist/Data Science

Relevant experience


Relevant experience in Digital technology development / application in industry.
Highly competent with data capture and analytics tools, to bring together large volumes of data from multiple sources (and teams)
Experience of applying statistical Design of Experiments approaches
Experience in data reporting, analysis/visualisation & modelling
Experience of working across multi-disciplinary teams
Knowledge of data management, analytics and data science coupled with an understanding of how to support, use and exploit these capabilities.
Strong communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to make data & digital accessible for all.
Strong problem solving skills and the ability to work both independently and as part of a team
Strong data management skills – you understand the importance of data capture & governance
Confidence in manipulating/visualising large volumes of data from multiple sources with applications such as Excel, PowerBI, Access, Power App…
Have coding /modelling experience in any of Python, SAS, R, MATLAB, JMP.


NOTES

What We Offer

Not only do we offer a competitive salary and pension scheme, we also offer an annual bonus, subsidised gym membership, a discounted staff shop and shares. You’ll have the opportunity to work directly with our renowned and exciting brands in a flexible and hybrid working environment.

Whilst the role is advertised on a full-time basis, we would be happy to discuss possible flexible working options and what this may look like for you. We are a key advocate of wellbeing and offer a variety of support for our people including hubs, programmes and development opportunities. We strive to achieve a family-friendly and inclusive workplace and to, above all, create possibilities for all.

Diversity at Unilever is about inclusion, embracing differences, creating possibilities and growing together for better business performance. We embrace diversity in our workforce. This means giving full and fair consideration to all applicants and continuing development of all employees regardless of age, disability, gender reassignment, race, religion or belief, sex, sexual orientation, marriage and civil partnership, and pregnancy and maternity. We are also more than happy to provide reasonable adjustments during our application and interview process to enable you to be present your best self. To find out more, including about our Employee Resource Groups, please click here Equity, Diversity & Inclusion at Unilever | Unilever.

Recruitment Fraud

Cyber criminals advertise fake job adverts with prestigious employers as a way of stealing information or even defrauding individuals out of money. In the most sophisticated cases, they will set up fake websites, which have a similar address to companies like Unilever. They even conduct fake telephone interviews and then offer candidates a role with the proviso they pay a fee for background checks or to cover work visa costs.  These types of attacks are becoming more common as more people are looking for employment in the economic climate.

How is Unilever tackling this?

Many of Unilever’s recruitment sites publish a warning to candidates about recruitment fraud. The Cyber Security team also proactively scan for signs of people setting up fake Unilever sites and act to close them down.

What can I do?

If you become aware of potential recruitment fraud, spot fake Unilever recruitment adverts or fake LinkedIn profiles, report them via Una Live Chat.

Unilever does not accept responsibility or liability for any candidates who are financially impacted by recruitment fraud. Your vigilance is key!

Diversity at Unilever is about inclusion, embracing differences, creating possibilities and growing together for better business performance. We embrace diversity in our workforce. This means giving full and fair consideration to all applicants and continuing development of all employees regardless of age, disability, gender reassignment, race, religion or belief, sex, sexual orientation, marriage and civil partnership, and pregnancy and maternity. We are also more than happy to provide reasonable adjustments during our application and interview process to enable you to be present your best self. To find out more, including about our Employee Resource Groups, please click here Equity, Diversity & Inclusion at Unilever | Unilever.

Recruitment Fraud

Cyber criminals advertise fake job adverts with prestigious employers as a way of stealing information or even defrauding individuals out of money. In the most sophisticated cases, they will set up fake websites, which have a similar address to companies like Unilever. They even conduct fake telephone interviews and then offer candidates a role with the proviso they pay a fee for background checks or to cover work visa costs.  These types of attacks are becoming more common as more people are looking for employment in the economic climate.

How is Unilever tackling this?

Many of Unilever’s recruitment sites publish a warning to candidates about recruitment fraud. The Cyber Security team also proactively scan for signs of people setting up fake Unilever sites and act to close them down.

What can I do?

If you become aware of potential recruitment fraud, spot fake Unilever recruitment adverts or fake LinkedIn profiles, report them via Una Live Chat.

Unilever does not accept responsibility or liability for any candidates who are financially impacted by recruitment fraud. Your vigilance is key!

Title: Data Insights Lead
Description:
DATA INSIGHTS LEAD | LANGLEY, UK




Travel obsessed? Big tech fan? Hey, you’re in good company. If you want to be part of the industry that makes the world go round, then look no further.




Travelport is the brains behind lots of your travel bookings- plane, car or hotel. Our technology is used to book that magical holiday, long awaited bucket-list trip or overdue school reunion. While we can’t solve mosquito bites or lost luggage, we can simplify a lot of the technical parts of travel, and we’re looking for the best thinkers to help us do it.




We’re hiring right now for a Data Insights Lead! Search for #Travelport on LinkedIn and hear from our amazing team.




How you’ll make an impact:




The Team Lead, Data Insights Analysis provides hands‑on analytical leadership while guiding an early‑career analyst with strong potential. This role blends technical depth, people development, and consultative partnership across the People function. The leader ensures that the right teams deliver reporting and insights, in the most automated and scalable way, and that data is used effectively to support performance management, recognition, learning, and broader talent initiatives. A strong partnership with the Corporate Systems group—who own Oracle and other core HR platforms—is essential.




Your role in action:

Lead, coach, and develop one early‑career team member, providing structure, clarity, and growth opportunities while ensuring consistent delivery and accountability.
Serve as a consultative partner to People function teams on Core HR, performance management, recognition, learning, and other talent processes, helping them clarify needs, understand data usage, and anticipate future reporting requirements.
Partner closely with the Corporate Systems group to align data sources, system changes, and automation opportunities across Oracle and related HR tools.
Build Power BI dashboards, Reports Vizualization and measures from pre-built data models.
Ensure reports delivered by the appropriate teams and that end users receive insights in the most automated, scalable, and user‑friendly format.
Maintain data quality, documentation, and consistency across all outputs.
Prioritize incoming requests and manage team workload.
Identify opportunities to streamline processes, improve data flows, and enhance reporting capabilities across the People function.




Could this be you?

Experience in HR analytics, people reporting, or a related HR function role with significant exposure to data and reporting.
Strong understanding of core HR domains including performance management, compensation, learning & development, talent acquisition, and recognition.
Demonstrated ability to gather, document, and validate business and reporting requirements from non-technical stakeholders.
Experience coordinating or leading user acceptance testing, including writing test cases, managing feedback cycles, and obtaining sign-off.
Familiarity with enterprise HR platforms (such as Oracle HCM) and strong understanding of HR data structure and maintained within these systems.
Ability to translate business questions into analytical approaches and build visualizations in Microsoft Power BI, create measures using DAX on pre-built datasets.
Demonstrated experience mentoring, coaching, or leading others.
Strong organizational skills and comfort managing competing priorities.
Ability to understand upstream and downstream data impacts and advocate for data integrity across the People function.
Familiarity with building Oracle OTBI reports
Experience building automated workflows or integrations.
Knowledge of SQL queries on SQL Server and Reporting data warehouse concepts like star schema and normalizations etc.




Discover why our teams love working here:

Where do we start! Our benefits package includes:

We offer a package that includes 25 days annual leave per annum, hybrid working model, this role is bonus eligible, and bonus awards are performance based and governed by the applicable annual bonus plan terms, pension contribution, private medical, life assurance and personal travel insurance.




Search for #lifeattravelport & #travelportUK for our employee stories on LinkedIn!




Your journey at Travelport starts here.

Our application process is quick, easy, and hassle-free — apply in just a few minutes!

We are an equal opportunities employer and will consider all qualified applicants purely on their skills and abilities. We will ensure that individuals with disabilities are provided reasonable accommodation to participate in the job application or interview process, to perform crucial job functions, and to receive other benefits and privileges of employment. Please contact us to request accommodation, if needed.

Title: Data Analytics Engineer
Description:
Full-time, Permanent

Basingstoke (Hybrid)

£65,000 - £70,000




Reassured are looking for a Data Analytics Engineer to help us unlock the power of data across the business. If you're passionate about turning raw data into actionable insights and building robust data pipelines, this is your chance to make a real impact in a fast-paced, collaborative environment.




As a Data Analytics Engineer, you’ll be at the heart of our data platform: designing and delivering scalable solutions using Kafka for data ingestion, Databricks for ETL , modelling data for Power BI, and working closely with stakeholders to create products that drive smarter decisions. You’ll play a key role in shaping our Lakehouse architecture.




What you'll be doing:

Building and optimising data ingestion pipelines using Apache Spark (ideally in Azure Databricks)
Designing semantic models and dashboards for business insights
Collaborating across teams to understand requirements and deliver fit-for-purpose data products
Supporting the productionisation of ML pipelines
Working in an Agile/DevOps environment to continuously evolve our data platform




What we're looking for:

Strong SQL and Python skills
Solid experience with Azure data services and DevOps (CI/CD)
Knowledge of data modelling (Star Schema) and Power BI
A logical, analytical mindset with great attention to detail
Experience in regulated industries like finance or insurance is a plus




Bonus points for:

Real-time data pipeline experience
Azure Data Engineer certifications
Familiarity with Apache Kafka or unstructured data (e.g. voice)




Ready to shape the future of data at Reassured? If you're excited by the idea of building smart, scalable solutions that make a real difference, we’d love to hear from you. Apply now and let’s build something brilliant together.

Title: UK Analytics & Operations Specialist
Description:
UK Analytics & Operations Specialist - Opella

Reading UK

You’ve built experience, now use it where bold ideas meet real impact. At Opella, we’re challenging how self-care works for millions of people and need leaders and experts ready to shape what’s next. As an UK Analytics & Operations Specialist, you’ll bring your skill, your curiosity and your drive to make health simpler, smarter and more human.

About The Job

At Opella, we’re on a mission to make self-care as simple as it should be. As a UK Commercial Operations & Trade & Revenue Management (TRM) Analyst, you will work closely with the UK Country Head and TRM Lead to help execute the UK commercial strategy.

This is a high-visibility, developmental role combining commercial analytics, operational execution, and stakeholder engagement. Designed as a talent pipeline opportunity, it offers hands-on exposure across Trade & Revenue Management and business operations, equipping you with the skills and experience for future growth within Opella.

You will play a key role in enabling data-driven decisions, driving operational excellence, and strengthening collaboration across teams.

Key Responsibilities


Support the execution of TRM strategy, including pricing, portfolio optimisation, and channel mix
Contribute to Integrated Business Planning (IBP), including forecasting, performance tracking, and variance analysis
Maintain and optimise key commercial datasets and tools (e.g. pricing files, trade spend, forecasts)
Generate insights and analysis to support commercial performance and decision-making
Manage operational processes including purchase orders, invoicing, and supplier coordination
Coordinate internal activities, including team events, communications, and stakeholder materials
Lead or support continuous improvement initiatives, including automation and adoption of new tools


Key Requirements


Advanced Excel skills (Pivot Tables, Power Query; VBA/Macros a plus)
Strong analytical experience within commercial, financial, or operational environments
Excellent organisation and prioritisation skills, with the ability to manage multiple tasks
Strong communication skills, with confidence presenting insights to senior stakeholders
Proactive, adaptable mindset with a willingness to challenge and improve existing processes
Ability to work effectively in a fast-paced, matrixed, and cross-functional environment
Collaborative approach with the ability to build relationships across teams (sales, finance, supply, marketing)


What Makes You Stand Out


Experience with coding or automation tools (e.g. Python)
Ability to translate complex data into clear, compelling business insights
High learning agility and strong ownership mindset
High energy, multi-tsking, office and stakeholder management


Why join Opella?

At Opella, we believe in creating an inclusive and empowering workplace where everyone can thrive. You’ll be part of a collaborative, forward-thinking team where your ideas are valued, your development is supported, and your impact is real.

We are committed to building a diverse workforce and welcome applications from all backgrounds.

About Us

Opella is the self-care challenger with the purest and third-largest portfolio in the Over The Counter (OTC) & Vitamins, Minerals & Supplements (VMS) market globally.

Our mission is to bring health in people’s hands by making self-care as simple as it should be. For half a billion consumers worldwide – and counting.

At the core of this mission is our 100 loved brands, our 11,000-strong global team, our 13 best-in-class manufacturing sites and 4 specialized science and innovation development centers. Headquartered in France, Opella is the proud maker of many of the world’s most loved brands, including Allegra, Buscopan, Doliprane, Dulcolax, Enterogermina, Essentiale and Mucosolvan.

As a globally certified B Corp company, we are active players in the journey towards healthier people and planet. Find out more about our mission at www.opella.com.

Network Analyst

Severn Trent isn’t all water and wastewater in the Midlands. Severn Trent Services (STS) is a thriving non-regulated business providing outstanding services in the Utilities sector across the UK. We have an ambitious growth plan and are exploring a wealth of exciting strategic opportunities to cement our place as one of the sector leaders.

If you want to do more, and come on this journey, we want you on our team.

EVERYTHING YOU NEED TO KNOW

We’re looking for an experienced Network Analyst to join our team and make a real impact, ensuring our water and wastewater assets run efficiently, stay compliant, and deliver for our customers.

In this role, you will undertake detailed analysis of water and wastewater data, including consumption volumes, flow and pressure data, and STS operating costs, to help minimise costs, maximise legitimate consumption, and improve overall network performance. You will also prioritise improvement strategies, ensuring the most cost-effective activities and customer-focused initiatives are delivered first.

This role is based in Reading

Key Accountabilities Include


To use understanding of bill tariffs and charging regimes to optimise internal operating processes and site configuration.
Provision of technical responses related to the client estate development and water/wastewater network improvement schemes.
Analysis of client site measurement with recommendations to maximise legitimate water consumption.
Ensure flows and pressures on the client network are sufficient to meet contractual firefighting requirements, establishing root cause of any specific issues and identification of least cost interventions to resolve
Using all available feedback and data to target interventions to minimise the likelihood of sewer flooding or pipe blockages.
Cost benefit analysis to support investment decisions
Understanding of Water and Wastewater network configuration for the purposes of providing technical advice to Asset Management, Field Operations, Contract and Engineering teams.
Ensuring contractual measurement accuracy and network performance monitoring through optimised plans for water meter maintenance and repair
Influencing of Regional Service Delivery Teams, to undertake and deliver plans and programmes within prescribed timescales.
Reporting of performance to peer groups, business and strategic leadership teams
Identifying opportunities for increased reliability and improved operating efficiency


What We Are Looking For

In order to be successful in this role, you will have a strong understanding of how water distribution and wastewater networks operate, alongside the ability to analyse large volumes of data and distil key insights from complex graphical information.

You will be a collaborative team player with proven problem-solving and logical thinking skills, capable of delivering results in environments where accuracy and reliability are critical.

You will also be IT literate with confident use of Microsoft Office, and demonstrate experience of working with and managing expectations across multiple internal and external stakeholders.

This role requires a full UK driving licence and the flexibility to work a nine‑day fortnight when required.

How We’ll Reward And Care For You

It's not just a job; it's a career. We offer benefits that reward great work and award-winning training to help you reach your potential. Plus, you'll contribute to the environment and community too. Here are some of our favourites:


25 days holiday + bank holidays (and the ability to buy/ sell up to 5 days per year)
Salary £37,618.05 - £44,226.73
Annual bonus scheme (of up to 15% of salary dependent on company performance)
Leading pension scheme – we will double your contribution (up to 15% when you contribute 7.5%)
Family friendly policies (including, a year off fully paid maternity and adoption leave)
Sharesave – the chance to buy Severn Trent Plc shares at a discounted rate
Dedicated training and development with our ‘Academy’
Electric vehicle scheme and retail offers
Two volunteering days per year


Apply now »

Title: Quality Assurance Data Analyst
Description:
Overview

Here at Mamas & Papas our beautifully designed nursery products & how we bring them to life with care, insight, quality, style & attention to detail is what makes us the brand of choice, & the brand of trust for an incredible community of new & expectant parents.

There is therefore no surprise that we’re continual winners at the Mumsnet Awards, Dadsnet Awards, Mother & Baby Awards, British Nursery Brand Awards, Which Best Buy, & so on, which make us, & all involved, so very proud to be part of.

Through continued growth, we are looking to strength this commitment to the quality & safety of what we create, by recruiting a QUALITY ASSURANCE DATA ANALYST.

The role of QA DATA ANAYST

This is a key role in delving deep into the data from our Spares & Repairs teams, as well as Customer Care, where our customers may have had an issue with a product. From the trends and data you gather you then work collaboratively with product designers, engineers, product testers as well as warehouse, distribution, stock control, and a multitude of other departments including finance, Buying etc to indentify commercial impacts, quality improvements and product performance.

Adding to this looking into environment reporting & data submissions.

This role being ideal for someone who loves delving into the mysteries of a good spreadsheet or two. Someone who has an inquisitive mind that can translate data into reality for teams to understand and take action upon.

There are not too many Data Analyst roles where the work that you do not only has an impact on an organisation, but a massive impact on a incredible community of new parents and their babies. You can really make a difference.

To apply for this QA Data Analyst opportunity, simply take those amazing first steps and APPLY TODAY, for a member of the recruitment team to give you a call to chat through this role further.

We at Mamas & Papas are committed to championing people of all cultures, ethnicities and gender preferences, and in celebrating diversity in all its forms through our campaigns, recruitment and our actions. We strongly believe in the power of inclusivity to help us all move forward in life.

Benefits

We're a business that cares. Here's just some of our benefits on offer;


33 days holiday, increasing up to 40 with service.
Buy & Sell holiday schemes
Company Bonus Schemes
Employer pension contribution from day 1 enrolment.
Significant staff discounts for family & friends from our shops & online
Ongoing offers & discounts across a variety of external organisations, from holidays, travel, restaurants, gifts & services to many more worthy discounts through our Benefits Hub
Subsidised health & critical illness cover and insurances
Supportive family related leave policies (including company enhanced pay for maternity, IVF & Fertility, surrogacy, paternity, adoption, shared parental leave)
Supportive Foster Care & Carers Leave offering
Support for Loss and Bereavement
A Menopause Friendly Employer
Employee Recognition and Appreciation Schemes
At Mamas & Papas, we’re a business for the future. Every day, we help to welcome a new generation of children into the world, and we want them to grow up in a world that’s sustainable – one that’s full of opportunity and hope.
Oh, and a great bunch of people to work with too.


We also operate at Head Office:


Hybrid flexi working of 3 days in the office.
Flexi-time schemes based around core working hours whether in the office, or working from home
Plus, free car parking, Prayer & Contemplation room, subsidised canteen, free tea & coffee, Games & TV rooms & much more.

Title: Demand Analyst UK
Description:
Who we are:

We are Fyffes, one of the world's leading tropical produce companies. For more than 130 years, we have sourced, shipped and delivered high-quality fresh produce globally. With over 4,000 employees and a strong international network of growers and suppliers, we are committed to shaping wellbeing for the world through sustainable growth, innovation and strong partnerships.

Now we're looking for a Demand Analyst UK to join our team, turning UK Citrus and Melon demand into accurate forecasts, insight and planning support.




Here's what you will do:

Support the commercial team by analysing melon demand across the DACH region and translating orders, forecast trends, promotions, seasonality, weather impacts and market dynamics into actionable insight. Your work will support weekly and seasonal planning, availability, service levels and waste reduction.

You will connect demand insight with sourcing, logistics and supply chain teams, providing early risk visibility and structured performance reporting. The role is analytical, fast-paced and focused on improving forecast accuracy, demand visibility and planning discipline.




Some key responsibilities:

Analyse DACH melon demand using orders, historical sales, promotions, seasonality, weather impacts and market trends.
Prepare and maintain accurate weekly and seasonal melon demand forecasts with documented assumptions.
Track forecast versus actual demand and order movements, identifying variances, trends and root causes.
Produce demand, forecast, order and service reporting, including forecast accuracy, bias, fill rate, availability and waste.
Support demand reviews, planning inputs and internal alignment before market meetings and planning calls.
Identify supply-demand gaps, shortfalls, oversupply, timing risks and changes in demand early.
Support scenario analysis, mitigation planning, allocation discussions and continuous improvement in reporting and forecasting processes.




What you need to bring:

Degree in Supply Chain, Business, Economics, Data Analytics, Agriculture or a related field.
1-3 years of experience in demand analysis, demand planning, supply chain, commercial analysis, fresh produce or FMCG.
Experience with retail demand, forecasts, orders, promotions or seasonal planning is advantageous.
Fresh produce, short shelf-life supply chain or melon experience is beneficial.
Strong Excel and data analysis capability, with ability to translate large datasets into clear insight.
Fluent English; Spanish or another language is advantageous.
Strong attention to detail, proactive communication, structured problem-solving, ownership and ability to work in a fast-paced perishable product environment.




What it is like to work at Fyffes:

We are ambitious but grounded, combining high performance with a people-first culture. Our leadership principles guide us every day: we play to win as one Fyffes, shape the future, cultivate a growth mindset, are transparent and inclusive, and are moved by doing good. We thrive on diversity and treat all candidates equally.




Interested? We look forward to hearing from you!

Title: Incentives Data Analyst
Description:
Why this role matters

At Bytes, Microsoft Incentives play a critical role in the business and ensuring they are calculated, reconciled and delivered correctly is essential. This isn’t just about spreadsheets. It’s about making sure the right people are rewarded, the business has accurate insight, and everything runs smoothly behind the scenes.




Organisations run on data, but data on its own isn’t enough. They need accuracy, clarity and confidence in the numbers that drive decision‑making and reward performance.




If you enjoy working with data, solving problems and bringing clarity to complex processes, this role gives you the opportunity to make a real impact. You’ll help ensure incentives are accurate, transparent and delivered on time, supporting teams across the business to perform at their




Mission

This role exists to ensure the accurate calculation, reconciliation and delivery of Microsoft incentives across multiple programmes. You’ll play a key part in maintaining data integrity, resolving discrepancies and ensuring incentives are processed and paid correctly each month.




With multiple data sources, programme rules and stakeholders involved, you’ll bring structure, consistency and attention to detail to a critical business process. Working closely with internal teams and Microsoft, you’ll help ensure incentives are understood, tracked and managed




What you will deliver within the role

Calculate and publish accurate incentive estimates across multiple Microsoft programmes
Reconcile incentive statements from Microsoft against internal calculations and identify discrepancies
Track and manage incentive‑related data, including logging credits, invoice changes and programme updates
Investigate and raise queries with Microsoft to resolve inconsistencies in incentive statements
Support Account Management and wider teams by responding to incentive‑related queries
Maintain accurate records across multiple data sources, including Microsoft Partner Center and licensing systems
Contribute to data analysis, consolidation and automation initiatives to improve efficiency
Help refine processes as incentive programme rules evolve, ensuring the business remains agile
Build strong internal relationships across sales, accounts and operations teams




Essentials

Strong analytical skills with a high level of accuracy and attention to detail
Solid experience using Microsoft Excel (intermediate to advanced level)
Good numerical ability and confidence working with complex datasets
2–3 years experience working in a data analysis, finance or similar analytical role
Strong organisational and time‑management skills, with the ability to meet deadlines
Clear communication skills and the ability to explain data‑driven insights to non‑technical stakeholders
A proactive mindset with the ability to work independently and solve problems
Comfortable collaborating with multiple teams across the business




Why Bytes




At Bytes you’ll join a supportive, collaborative and high‑trust team where your work has real impact. We move quickly, work closely across departments and value individuals who bring attention to detail, ownership and a drive to improve how we operate.




You’ll be part of a team that plays a vital role in the business, with the opportunity to develop your analytical skills, contribute to process improvements and gain exposure to a wide range of stakeholders. With a people‑first culture and strong support from experienced colleagues, you’ll have the space to grow and succeed.




Ready to apply?

If you’re looking for a role where you can work with data, make a real impact on business performance and be part of a team that values accuracy, ownership and continuous improvement, we’d love to hear from you.




Apply below or send your CV to: jamal.hinds@bytes.co.uk

Title: Senior Data Analyst
Description:
The role

The primary role of the Senior Data Analyst is to assist the firms Data Architect. The role can be based at any of our offices, although travel may be required at times to other Saffery offices as the business required. The postholder will report to the Data Architect.

The role will be responsible for analysing requests for improvements in data-related processes, supporting the Data Architect to challenge these requests and propose workable solutions whilst always adhering to the firm’s data standards and data governance policy. The job holder will be required to act in an advisory capacity to other areas of the business on managing data and process changes, reviewing processes with the stakeholders. They will be required to assist with the data management aspects of strategic projects and assisting with integrations and advising on data processes for projects.

The job holder will be required to build wireframe solutions, manage data warehouse structures and maintain internal databases where external solutions are not available.

The Office

The role can be based at any of our offices, although travel may be required at times to other Saffery offices as the business requires. The Senior Data Analyst will report to the Data Architect.

The Responsibilities


Analyse and interpret request for improvements in data related processes, and data change requests, discussing these with senior colleagues where necessary. Be able to identify the business impact of the requests, advising on change management and identify where larger scale project management may be required.
Provide high level advisory on business processes and data management.
Defining the mapping and synchronisation of shared reference data across systems.
Provide solutions and proposals to assist with data integrations.
Develop and maintain data quality dashboards and create and maintain data dictionary for all Critical Data Elements (CDEs).
Work with stakeholders to analyse data, provide reporting solutions and identify root causes of dysfunction in processes and providing advice on remediation.
Contribute to the design of data models for use by Business Intelligence and reporting tool and reuse in the Enterprise landscape.
Actively promote the firm’s Data Standards and Data Governance Policy.
Contribute to the design of processes.
Participate in cross-functional programme workstreams.
Design and develop data transformation and loading processes for the population and on-going management of the data models and architecture.
Look for opportunities for continuous improvement in data capture processes and integration.
Monitor data quality reports and liaise with data owners to agree remedial action.
Document data lifecycles and how they relate to business processes.
Identify new sources of data and methods to improve data collection, analysis, and reporting.
Create and develop wireframe solutions.
Create and maintain database solutions using Microsoft Power Platform Tools/SQL databases.
Introduce and maintain data quality tools.


You


A motivated self-starter with strong attention to detail, and who understands the value of data and is passionate about accuracy.
Experience in analysing data, processes, data quality stats, root cause analysis. Able to challenge where necessary.
Strong communication and relationship building skills with strong project management skills.
Experience and understanding of the basic operating model of an accountancy/law practice eg Client, Matter and Time Recording is desirable.
A problem solver able to define pragmatic solutions.
Ability to use initiative and be pro-active.
Good at documentation.
Strong organisational skills and ability to prioritise.
Ability to cope with conflicting demands and meet deadlines.
Communicates effectively at all levels. Must have good verbal and written skills.
Takes personal responsibility for getting things done to agreed timeframes.
Remains calm under pressure.
A team player capable of pitching in to help the team.
Promotes the firms’ values and beliefs, demonstrating integrity and maintaining an ethical stance, using flexibility and tact to express views as necessary.


Qualifications


Completion of A level studies is essential.
Degree level education is desirable.
At least two years’ experience of working in a professional services environment, ideally accountancy or legal sectors.
Experience of working on large scale projects and project management.
Experience of working on Data Quality, Remediation and Data Management programmes.
Must be proficient in the Microsoft Power Platform suite with the ability to generate applications for internal use.
Experience with Azure Logic Apps and Azure Functions.
Experience of working with Microsoft Fabric, pipelines, dataflows and pySpark.
Must be proficient in the use of SQL. With advanced T-SQL skills, and familiar with functions, stored procedures, and SQL agent jobs.
Must be able to develop Power BI reports.
Competent in the use of Microsoft Office products such as Word, Excel, Powerpoint, Visio, Teams.
Experience of Elite3E, MS Teams, SmartSheets, and Data Management tools.
Familiarity with Microsoft Purview would be an advantage.


Reward and benefit


A 35 hour working week with a hybrid working policy with the flexibility to work from home for up to three days per week (our core hours are 10-4)
Excellent benefits including; contributory pension scheme, 25 days annual leave, Life Assurance cover and great flexible benefits and family friendly policies,
Eligibility for the firm’s Profit-Sharing Plan. Paid in December.


About Us

Saffery LLP is an adviser to individuals and businesses across a range of sectors, providing market-leading accountancy, audit, business advisory, tax and VAT services. Founded in 1855, we are the UK’s 15th largest accountancy firm.

When you work with us at Saffery, you work with people who know your name, not just a job title. We’re a place shaped by friendly, intelligent people who are individually excellent and collectively exceptional. We’re committed to our clients, but also to our greatest asset – our people.

As a proud member of Nexia, a leading, international network of independent accounting and consulting firms, we’ve got access to local insight on a global scale.

Equality, Diversity & Inclusion

Equality, diversity and inclusion is a top priority for us. With our ED&I strategy, Everyone Belongs, we are building on our positive work culture and implementing initiatives to improve diversity and strengthen our equitable culture of inclusion, led from the top.

We hope you will apply to us, whatever your background, ethnicity, gender, age, sexuality, disability or neurodivergence, because individual differences equal collective strength. Built on values of excellence, integrity, enthusiasm and collegiality, Saffery is where Potential meets Personal.

  • Excellent benefits including; contributory pension scheme, 25 days annual leave, Life Assurance cover and great flexible benefits and family friendly policies,
  • Eligibility for the firm’s Profit-Sharing Plan. Paid in December.

About Us

Saffery LLP is an adviser to individuals and businesses across a range of sectors, providing market-leading accountancy, audit, business advisory, tax and VAT services. Founded in 1855, we are the UK’s 15th largest accountancy firm.

When you work with us at Saffery, you work with people who know your name, not just a job title. We’re a place shaped by friendly, intelligent people who are individually excellent and collectively exceptional. We’re committed to our clients, but also to our greatest asset – our people.

As a proud member of Nexia, a leading, international network of independent accounting and consulting firms, we’ve got access to local insight on a global scale.

Equality, Diversity & Inclusion

Equality, diversity and inclusion is a top priority for us. With our ED&I strategy, Everyone Belongs, we are building on our positive work culture and implementing initiatives to improve diversity and strengthen our equitable culture of inclusion, led from the top.

We hope you will apply to us, whatever your background, ethnicity, gender, age, sexuality, disability or neurodivergence, because individual differences equal collective strength. Built on values of excellence, integrity, enthusiasm and collegiality, Saffery is where Potential meets Personal.

Title: System Analyst
Description:
Systems Analyst (ERP / Supply Chain)

📍 Crawley (Hybrid)

📅 12 Month FTC

💰 Up to £45k




The Role

This is a key role supporting European spare parts operations, focused on improving the quality, accuracy and usability of data across ERP systems and EDI networks.

You’ll sit at the centre of operations, identifying where things go wrong, fixing root causes, and building smarter processes and automation to improve performance at scale.




What you’ll be doing

Own data integrity across ERP systems – ensuring alignment, accuracy and consistency
Monitor ERP/EDI transactions, identifying and resolving errors before they impact operations
Analyse trends in data issues and implement fixes to prevent repeat problems
Build automated controls and processes to reduce manual intervention
Develop and maintain reporting (Excel / Power BI) to support operational and management decision-making
Partner with teams across logistics, planning and operations to improve data-driven processes
Contribute to continuous improvement and global projects




What good looks like

Errors are identified early and reduced over time
Reporting is trusted, accurate and actually used
Manual processes become automated and scalable
Stakeholders rely on you as the “go-to” for data and systems insight




What we’re looking for

Experience working with ERP systems (SAP, Oracle, BaaN or similar)
Strong Excel skills (essential) + confidence working with data sets
Ability to write SQL queries and understand relational databases
Experience with reporting tools (Power BI or similar)
Comfortable spotting patterns, solving problems and improving processes
Strong attention to detail with the ability to prioritise effectively




Nice to have:

EDI knowledge
Supply chain / logistics experience
Additional European language




If you are interested please apply or send your CV to luke.sandilands@cpl.com

Title: Network Analyst
Description:
Severn Trent isn’t all water and wastewater in the Midlands. Severn Trent Services (STS) is a thriving non-regulated business providing outstanding services in the Utilities sector across the UK. We have an ambitious growth plan and are exploring a wealth of exciting strategic opportunities to cement our place as one of the sector leaders.

If you want to do more, and come on this journey, we want you on our team.

EVERYTHING YOU NEED TO KNOW

We’re looking for an experienced Network Analyst to join our team and make a real impact, ensuring our water and wastewater assets run efficiently, stay compliant, and deliver for our customers.

In this role, you will undertake detailed analysis of water and wastewater data, including consumption volumes, flow and pressure data, and STS operating costs, to help minimise costs, maximise legitimate consumption, and improve overall network performance. You will also prioritise improvement strategies, ensuring the most cost-effective activities and customer-focused initiatives are delivered first.

This role is based in Reading

Key Accountabilities Include


To use understanding of bill tariffs and charging regimes to optimise internal operating processes and site configuration.
Provision of technical responses related to the client estate development and water/wastewater network improvement schemes.
Analysis of client site measurement with recommendations to maximise legitimate water consumption.
Ensure flows and pressures on the client network are sufficient to meet contractual firefighting requirements, establishing root cause of any specific issues and identification of least cost interventions to resolve
Using all available feedback and data to target interventions to minimise the likelihood of sewer flooding or pipe blockages.
Cost benefit analysis to support investment decisions
Understanding of Water and Wastewater network configuration for the purposes of providing technical advice to Asset Management, Field Operations, Contract and Engineering teams.
Ensuring contractual measurement accuracy and network performance monitoring through optimised plans for water meter maintenance and repair
Influencing of Regional Service Delivery Teams, to undertake and deliver plans and programmes within prescribed timescales.
Reporting of performance to peer groups, business and strategic leadership teams
Identifying opportunities for increased reliability and improved operating efficiency


What We Are Looking For

In order to be successful in this role, you will have a strong understanding of how water distribution and wastewater networks operate, alongside the ability to analyse large volumes of data and distil key insights from complex graphical information.

You will be a collaborative team player with proven problem-solving and logical thinking skills, capable of delivering results in environments where accuracy and reliability are critical.

You will also be IT literate with confident use of Microsoft Office, and demonstrate experience of working with and managing expectations across multiple internal and external stakeholders.

This role requires a full UK driving licence and the flexibility to work a nine‑day fortnight when required.

How We’ll Reward And Care For You

It's not just a job; it's a career. We offer benefits that reward great work and award-winning training to help you reach your potential. Plus, you'll contribute to the environment and community too. Here are some of our favourites:


25 days holiday + bank holidays (and the ability to buy/ sell up to 5 days per year)
Salary £37,618.05 - £44,226.73
Annual bonus scheme (of up to 15% of salary dependent on company performance)
Leading pension scheme – we will double your contribution (up to 15% when you contribute 7.5%)
Family friendly policies (including, a year off fully paid maternity and adoption leave)
Sharesave – the chance to buy Severn Trent Plc shares at a discounted rate
Dedicated training and development with our ‘Academy’
Electric vehicle scheme and retail offers
Two volunteering days per year

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Data Analyst | Ecommerce Partner Success Analytics | Hybrid Working | Hatfield

We are Ocado Group, and we're bringing world-class automation to online grocery. Our Ocado Smart Platform (OSP) combines cutting-edge robotics, AI, and IoT within our advanced CFCs (Customer Fulfilment Centres). We've mastered the single pick, transforming online delivery for our global partners. Join us and be part of a team pushing the boundaries of retail technology.

About the Role As an Ecommerce Data Analyst (A2) within the Partner Success Analytics team, you will generate critical insights across the full customer journey, from how shoppers discover and browse to how they purchase, retain, and engage with the platform. Operating at the intersection of ecommerce, commercial strategy, and product analytics, your work will help a diverse set of global retail partners understand platform performance, identify growth opportunities, and deliver stronger business results.

In this role, you will collaborate closely with product, commercial, and data engineering teams to solve complex problems spanning digital storefronts, fulfilment, and last-mile delivery. You will take ownership of translating deep analytical findings into clear, actionable narratives for diverse stakeholders. This is a fantastic opportunity to leverage data, statistics, and modern tooling to directly influence the digital customer experience and shape commercial decision-making on an international scale.

Key Responsibilities


Build cohort analysis, customer segmentation, and lifetime value modelling to deepen the understanding of shopper behaviour.
Analyse customer journeys and funnel drop-offs to surface actionable improvement opportunities across the platform.
Track and interpret core ecommerce metrics, including Conversion Rate, Average Order Value, Frequency, and Churn, translating trends into clear narratives for stakeholders.
Evaluate feature performance, feature uptake, pricing structures, and promotional mechanics to inform commercial strategies.
Analyse product range and availability data to support data-driven commercial decision-making.
Design and run A/B tests and other statistical experiments in collaboration with product and commercial teams.
Apply appropriate statistical methods to evaluate experimental results, communicating findings clearly alongside uncertainty and practical significance.
Build and maintain user-friendly dashboards and reporting in Looker to empower stakeholders to self-serve.
Work closely with data engineers to understand available data assets and proactively surface gaps or quality issues.


What We’re Looking For Essential


2+ years of experience in a data analyst or similar data-focused role.
Strong SQL proficiency with comfort writing complex queries across large datasets.
Python proficiency for data analysis, transformation, and automation.
Solid understanding of statistics, with the ability to design, run, and interpret A/B tests and hypothesis tests.
Experience building user-friendly dashboards in a BI tool such as Looker, Tableau, or Data Studio.
Familiarity with core ecommerce metrics and a clear understanding of how they connect to broader business outcomes.
Ability to translate complex analytical findings into clear, actionable recommendations for non-technical audiences.


Nice to Haves


Experience with Google BigQuery and the Google Cloud Platform (GCP) ecosystem.
Familiarity with dbt or structural data modelling concepts.
Experience utilizing web analytics tools such as Google Analytics 4 (GA4).
Exposure to predictive modelling or regression techniques.
Prior analytics experience within the online grocery, retail, or logistics sectors.
Familiarity with version control systems such as Git and GitHub.
Curiosity about AI and experience utilizing tools like Claude or ChatGPT.


Benefits Our employee benefits are designed for you, we care about people and we’ve ensured we have a wealth of benefits that focus on your well-being. We regularly review our benefits to ensure we are supporting our employees appropriately. Currently, we offer technically stretching work, a competitive salary and;


Hybrid working patterns meaning part of the working week can be spent working remotely (typically 3 days per week). However, your working pattern will depend upon your role/team.
30 days working from anywhere policy
Wellbeing support through Apps such as Unmind and an Employee Assistance Programme
25 days annual leave, rising to 27 days after 5 years service (plus optional holiday purchase)
Pension scheme (various options available including employer contribution matching up to 7%)
Private Medical Insurance
22 weeks paid maternity leave and 6 weeks paid paternity leave (once relevant service requirements complete)
Train Ticket loan (interest-free)
Cycle to Work Scheme
Opportunity to participate in Sharesave and Buy as You Earn share schemes
15% discount on Ocado.com and free delivery for all employees
Income Protection (can be up to 50% of salary for 3 years)
Life Assurance (3 x annual salary)


Be bold, be unique, be brilliant, be you. We are looking for individuality and we value diversity. We are an equal opportunities employer and we are committed to treating all applicants and employees fairly and equally. We are committed to making reasonable adjustments to provide a positive, barrier-free recruitment process and supportive work environment. If you have any support or access requirements, we encourage you to advise us at the time of application.

Title: Data Analyst
Place: City Of London, England, United Kingdom
Description:
Who are we?👋

Look at the latest headlines and you will see something Ki insures. Think space shuttles, world tours, wind farms, and even footballers' legs. 


Ki's mission is simple. Digitally disrupt and revolutionise a 335-year-old market. Working with Google and UCL, Ki has created a platform that uses algorithms, machine learning and large language models to give insurance brokers quotes in seconds, rather than days. 


Ki is proudly the biggest global algorithmic insurance carrier. It is the fastest growing syndicate in the Lloyd's of London market, and the first ever to make $100m in profit in 3 years. 


Ki's teams have varied backgrounds and work together in an agile, cross-functional way to build the very best experience for its customers. Ki has big ambitions but needs more excellent minds to challenge the status-quo and help it reach new horizons.


Where you come in?

The Data Analyst at Ki works at the intersection of analytics, engineering and product. They work within teams and across Ki's teams to support and drive design, development, and optimization of analytics, data models, reports, and other data-driven products that support strategic business goals.  


What you will be doing: 🖋️


Applied Analytics

Develop knowledge and expertise in Ki's business domains to generate insights and measurement on initiatives that drive company objectives.
Collaborate with business stakeholders to identify opportunities for leveraging data analytics to drive commercial results and improve operational efficiency.
Identify opportunities to leverage data analytics techniques to extract value from both internal and external data assets to enrich analytical capabilities.


Analytics Engineering

Develop and maintain the core data sets underlying the suite of analytical tools used by the team
Create and maintain data visualizations and dashboards using tools such as Tableau, Power BI, or similar.
Develop scalable data solutions that conform to Ki's technology and engineering principles and industry best practice.


Data Management & Governance

Preserve the integrity of data, making sure it is accurate, consistent, and reliable throughout its lifecycle.
Investigate and lead timely resolution of emerging issues with underlying data systems and models as and when these arise.
Ensure that domain data is managed and used according to Ki's data governance policy and best practice principles.


Other

Ensure all data systems and models are documented in line with Ki wide standards
Recommend ways to improve data efficiency and reliability
Increase the degree of automation within the team's data systems and tools
Investigate new tools that would help the team store, structure and analyse data.


Requirements


Proficiency in SQL and Python to manipulate and analyse datasets 
Experience with data reporting and visualisation tools (e.g. Tableau, Looker, Dash, Streamlit) 
Solid understanding of statistical techniques (e.g. regression, clustering, correlation analysis), with ability to apply these to commercial problem solving. 
Experience working in cloud native environments (especially GCP) would be plus. 
Experience with designing and implementation of data structures to ensure ease of use and accessibility by a broader audience. 
Self-organised, good at documenting and communicating findings and approach. 
Excellent communicator, able to present complex analysis to both technical and non-technical audiences. 
Experience working in a regulated industry


What to expect during the recruitment process:

Initial recruiter screening call
Interview with hiring manager
Technical Interview (this may vary depending on the role)
Values Interview

Title: Analytics Engineer
Description:
Job Description

Analytics Engineer

£53,500 - £70,000 (depending on experience) + benefits, including up to 10% bonus

London (EC2R 7HJ) or Salford (M50 3SP)

Hybrid working – 1-2 days per week in the office

Permanent

Full-time, 35 hours per week

We make health happen

Working in our Group function you’ll play a key part in helping our customer facing colleagues deliver exceptional standards of customer service and patient care not just in the UK but around the globe.

No matter your role, you’ll have an opportunity to do work that matters. Making a difference to the lives of our customers each and every day, by helping shape the strategic direction of our business around the world.

We are looking for an experienced and motivated Analytics Engineer to join our small, but talented, Group Data in One Click (DOC) Analytics Team within Group Functions Technology (GFT). The team’s main objective is to support our Group Functions teams with their advanced analytics goals.

We already have a strong Analytics Product, hosted on our Google Cloud Platform (GCP), that offers advanced analytics capability using Python and SQL, with a direct connection into PowerBI for our stakeholder’s visualisation requirements.

Data is trending, and at Bupa we want to utilise and develop our Analytics Product to accelerate the importance of data across our stakeholders’. The role of the Analytics Engineer will be pivotal in supporting Bupa Group Functions and helping the Analytics Product Owner define and deliver future developments of the Analytics Product, ensuring our stakeholders can drive meaningful insight and deliver business value.

How You’ll Help Us Make Health Happen


Be the data analysis expert and developer for DOC Analytics. They will be used to using methodical investigation and design thinking to identify, shape and deliver value, working with the wider Product team on the most important challenges and opportunities.
Be known for delivery. Own the end-to-end delivery of advanced analytics solutions within the DOC Analytics Product. Focus on the value and getting things done.
Develop code that meets the needs of a highly-regulated organisation. The TDA will write code in line with current best practice, be comfortable taking challenge on their approach and designs, and deliver reliable solutions that our stakeholders trust.
Define the problem, develop user stories (including the breakdown of work) and shape and


constructively challenge the product owner and stakeholder requirements.


Work in an agile manner as part of the team, using design thinking, breaking work into small increments, and building out patterns and processes that can be repeated.
Present back to our stakeholders, demonstrating work-in-progress and articulating feedback and progress clearly.


What You'll Bring


Proven experience of working with SQL and Python as part of a technical delivery team is a minimum requirement for this role.
Strong experience of developing visualisations, preferably in PowerBI.
Experience of analytical work within GCP (and more broad experience in GCP) is advantageous.
Data management experience, inc. data analysis, continuous improvement of data quality and standards. Understanding what we must do to protect both Bupa and customer data is a must.
Experience and track-record of delivering within agile methodologies.
Experience in acceptance and outcome criteria gathering and translation to user stories.
Numerate with strong analytical and problem-solving ability.
Excellent standard of written and presentation skills with proficiency of working in Azure DevOps, Jira or similar.
Experience of collaborating with a wide range of senior business and technology leaders to achieve outcomes.
Strong influencing skills across stakeholders, colleagues and external vendors.
Skills in ‘R’ would be a nice-to-have, but far from essential.


Benefits

Our benefits are designed to make health happen for our people. Viva is our global wellbeing programme and includes all aspects of our health – from mental and physical, to financial, social and environmental wellbeing. We support flexible working and have a range of family-friendly benefits.

Joining Bupa in this role you will receive the following benefits and more:


25 days holiday, increasing through length of service, with option to buy or sell
Bupa health insurance
Access to our wellbeing programmes and mental health support
Bonus scheme up to 10%
Enhanced parental leave
Pension scheme
Discounts on Bupa products and services
Opportunities for career development and internal mobility


Why Bupa?

We’re a health insurer and provider. With no shareholders, our customers are our focus. Our people are all driven by the same purpose – helping people live longer, healthier, happier lives and making a better world. We make health happen by being brave, caring and responsible in everything we do.

We encourage all of our people to “Be you at Bupa”, we champion diversity, and we understand the importance of our people representing the communities and customers we serve. That’s why we especially encourage applications from people with diverse backgrounds and experiences.

Bupa takes pride in being a Level 2 Disability Confident Employer and will aim to offer an interview/assessment to disabled applicants who best meet the minimum criteria for the role. We’re committed to ensuring you’re treated fairly during the recruitment process and offer reasonable adjustments to anyone who may benefit from accommodations to the recruitment process.

Title: Data Scientist
Description:
At OQC, we aren't just theorising about the future; we're building it. Born from a philosophy of bold innovation, we've successfully transitioned quantum computing from an academic dream into a commercial reality. The most exciting thing is that we're just getting started and we've recently closed our £260 million Series C funding round - the largest fundraise ever completed by a quantum computing company in Europe.

The Purpose



As a Data Scientist at OQC, you'll help accelerate the development of next-generation quantum hardware through data-driven insight, predictive modelling, and simulation. Working at the intersection of science, engineering, and software, you'll transform complex datasets into actionable intelligence that shapes critical hardware decisions and drives technological progress.

The Role



We're looking for a Data Scientist to develop predictive models, analyse complex engineering datasets, and build the software tooling that enables informed decision-making across our hardware programmes. You'll collaborate closely with hardware, measurement, and software engineers to improve performance, optimise designs, and support the development of future quantum technologies.

What You'll Be Working On


Develop and optimise predictive models to support hardware design, specification, and engineering decision-making.
Analyse large, multi-parameter datasets to identify trends, quantify performance, and generate actionable insights.
Build, maintain, and improve software tools for data analysis, modelling, and simulation.
Apply software engineering best practices including version control, testing, code reviews, and documentation.
Collaborate with multidisciplinary engineering teams to define requirements and solve complex technical challenges.
Create clear visualisations, technical reports, and documentation to communicate findings and recommendations.
Identify opportunities to improve analytical workflows, modelling approaches, and data-driven processes across the business.
What We're Looking For


Bachelor's degree in Applied Mathematics, Statistics, Physics, Engineering, Computer Science, or a related discipline (or equivalent practical experience).
Experience applying statistical analysis and predictive modelling techniques to solve complex technical or business problems.
Proven ability to independently deliver data science projects or workstreams.
Experience developing software or analytical tools that support data-driven decision-making.
Strong experience working with large-scale datasets and data management infrastructures.
Strong proficiency in Python and scientific computing libraries including NumPy, SciPy, Pandas, and data visualisation tools.
Solid understanding of numerical methods, probability, and statistics.
Familiarity with machine learning techniques and large-scale data analysis.
Understanding of software engineering best practices including Git, code review, automated testing, and CI/CD.
Experience working with databases and object/blob storage technologies.
Strong communication skills with the ability to collaborate effectively across technical disciplines.
Understanding of data governance principles and best practices.
The 'Nice-to-Haves'


Postgraduate qualification in a relevant scientific, engineering, mathematical, or computing discipline.
Experience with scientific or systems modelling, including engineering, thermal, electromagnetic, or materials domains.
Experience with machine learning or deep learning frameworks.
Experience working within Agile software development environments.
Experience implementing automated testing frameworks or test-driven development practices.
Experience mentoring colleagues and engaging with a broad range of technical stakeholders.
Exposure to quantum technologies, radio-frequency electronics, thermodynamics, or systems engineering environments.
Ability to communicate complex technical concepts to non-technical audiences.
Why Join OQC



You will join a world-class team at the forefront of the next computational era. We offer a culture of bold innovation, the chance to work with unique lab infrastructure, and the opportunity to see your work redefine the limits of computation.

Learn more about our benefits and positive work culture here: https://oqc.tech/company/careers-at-oqc/

Title: Student Data Analyst (Tribal SITS)
Description:
Student Data Analyst (15‑Month FTC) | Tribal SITS | Data ETL+ Migration & Manupilation

£40,000 | Hybrid 1-2 days a week (Milton Keynes)




Overview

We are currently partnering with a university that is recruiting a Student Data Analyst to support a fast‑moving period of change as they prepare their data for analytics. This role requires adaptability, strong problem‑solving skills, and the ability to work with complex, evolving data structures. You will use SQL extensively and apply your SITS back‑end expertise to support the preparation.




This is an opportunity that will give you visibilty across the business users including senior management team in order to allow; 1. smooth student journey to manage their experience but also 2. senior stakeholders can make informed decisions.




Essential Technical Skills

Strong SQL Server experience, with excellent usage of SQL- including data extraction, transformation, and cleansing
Experience working with Student Record Systems (SRS) - Deep understanding of SITS back‑end data structures
Proven experience preparing data for analytics using large‑scale datasets
Familiarity with ETL processes and relational database principles




Essential Experience

Experience working within the Higher Education (HE) sector
Hands‑on experience with SITS or another enterprise‑level student records platform
Ability to interpret and work with bespoke or non‑standard system configurations
Experience supporting system changes, data transitions, or platform transformations




Essential Interpersonal Skills

Ability to communicate complex technical concepts clearly to both technical and non‑technical audiences
Strong problem‑solving capability and comfort working with ambiguity
Ability to manage workload independently across multiple priorities
Collaborative approach with internal teams and external suppliers




Desirable Skills

Exposure to integrations or API‑based data flows
PHP or similar scripting languages
Knowledge of HESA returns
Familiarity with SSRS or Power BI
Experience with other student record systems (e.g., Banner)




Why This Role?

Opportunity to play a key role in a major institutional transformation
Work within a supportive, close‑knit environment
Real ownership of data processes and technical solutions
Hybrid working arrangement




If this sounds like it would be of interest to you, please reach out to Yafet Okubet on y.okubet@gravitasgroup.com to discuss further.

Title: Business Analyst
Description:
We exist to create positive change for people and the planet. Join us and make a difference too!

Business Analyst

Location: Milton Keynes (hybrid-role)

The base salary for this position starts from 31 450,00 GBP annually

Purpose Of The Role


To provide daily, weekly and monthly operational KPIs to the EMEA CSC Team and the EMEA System Certification and ISA Director.
In addition, the business analyst role plays a pivotal role working with the EMEA Director, Finance Business Parter and the operational management team to provide insights, identify trends and opportunities, and produce accurate reporting on delivery performance and utilisation of resources



Key Responsibilities & Accountabilities


Publish Daily/Weekly reports for operational guidance and decision making (Daily KPR, Job Movement, Resource Dashboards, Utilisation Dashboards, Visit Reports, One Resourcer Usage, days lost in Month)
Publish monthly operational KPI reports to accurately reflect current performance versus budgets and latest forecasts. Understanding reasons for variances.
Run reports in support of the Risk & Compliance management team (Workflow Reports)
Assist Finance Business Partner with data collection, manipulation and presentation.
Dashboard creation for Customer Planning and Engagement on key indicators of our internal KPI’s and external feedback from clients
Provide regular analysis of overall workload capacity and product mix to identify recruitment needs
Analyse operational data to identify trends and opportunities for improvement and communicate accordingly across the business
Run reports in support of the Operational Managers (UK days Workload Reports)



Skills And Abilities


Intermediate excel knowledge with the ability to create models, analyse and manipulate data
Additional software knowledge eg PowerBI, SAP, preferable
Experience as part of a team scaling and improving processes, systems and implementing best practices.
A good communicator with both finance and non-finance stakeholders
Able to set focuses, have high ambition for self and has high standards
Demonstrates awareness of their own strengths and limitations, continuously seeks to develop self
Readily steps into the most challenging opportunities, positively embracing the risk of failure



KW1

About Us

BSI is a business improvement and standards company and for over a century BSI has been recognized for having a positive impact on organizations and society, building trust and enhancing lives.

Today BSI partners with more than 77,500 clients in 195 countries and engages with a 15,000 strong global community of experts, industry and consumer groups, organizations and governments.

Utilizing its extensive expertise in key industry sectors - including automotive, aerospace, built environment, food and retail, and healthcare - BSI delivers on its purpose by helping its clients fulfil theirs.

Living by our core values of Client-Centricity, Agility, and Collaboration, BSI provides organizations with the confidence to grow by partnering with them to tackle society’s critical issues – from climate change to building trust in digital transformation and everything in between - to accelerate progress towards a better society and a sustainable world.

BSI is an Equal Opportunity Employer dedicated to fostering a diverse and inclusive workplace.
