Title: Data & AI Analyst
Description:
Data Analyst

Our Data & AI team is made up of curious, client‑focused professionals who help organisations turn data into insight, decisions, and action. We’re looking for a Data Analyst who combines strong business analysis capability with data product management skills to shape, define, and deliver high‑value use cases across analytics, AI, and modern data platforms.

If you’re passionate about understanding how businesses work, translating that into well‑defined data and AI use cases, and working closely with both business and technology stakeholders, we’d love to talk.

Together We Do What Matters.

Come join us

As a member of Avanade’s Data & AI practice, you’ll work at the intersection of business and technology, helping clients unlock value from their data across AI, Agentic AI, data platforms, reporting, and analytics. You’ll play a critical role in shaping data‑driven products and solutions, ensuring they are well‑defined, client‑centric, and deliver measurable outcomes.

This role is ideal for analytically minded professionals who are curious, collaborative, and motivated by driving meaningful change through data.

What You’ll Do


Partner with clients to understand business problems, opportunities, and priorities, translating them into clear data, analytics, and AI use cases
Combine business analysis and data product thinking to define scope, success measures, roadmaps, and requirements for data‑driven solutions
Analyse source systems, APIs, and data feeds, assessing data quality, structure, and suitability for analytics and AI use cases
Design and document analytics‑focused data models, including dimensional models and star schemas
Produce source‑to‑target mappings, business rules, and transformation logic to support data engineering and analytics delivery
Work closely with data engineers, architects, and AI specialists to ensure requirements are well understood and effectively delivered
Engage confidently with both business and technology stakeholders, acting as a trusted advisor and translator between worlds
Support the evolution of data products over time, incorporating feedback, learning, and changing business needs


Qualifications

Skills and experience


Proven experience as a Data Analyst, Business Analyst, or Analytics Consultant within data, analytics, or AI programmes
Strong background in business analysis, including requirements definition, stakeholder engagement, and use‑case development
Experience analysing source systems, APIs, and data integrations, with an understanding of how operational data is used for analytics
Solid knowledge of data modelling for analytics, particularly dimensional modelling and star schemas
Hands‑on experience producing source‑to‑target mappings and working with data engineers to support implementation
Understanding of modern data platforms and analytics architectures, ideally within Microsoft or cloud‑based ecosystems
Excellent communication skills, with the ability to explain complex data concepts to non‑technical audiences
Experience with technologies such as Python, SQL, Power BI, Databricks, and Microsoft Fabric is advantageous


About You

Characteristics that can spell success in this role include:


Naturally curious and analytical, with a strong desire to understand why things work the way they do
Passionate about driving change through data, analytics, and AI, not just producing outputs
Client‑centric, with a clear focus on outcomes, value, and measurable impact
Comfortable working across ambiguity, evolving requirements, and complex stakeholder landscapes
Confident engaging with senior business stakeholders and technology teams alike
Pragmatic, collaborative, and able to balance rigour with pace
Keen to continuously learn, develop, and stay current with emerging data and AI trends


Enjoy your career

Some Of The Best Things About Working At Avanade


Opportunity to work for Microsoft’s Global Alliance Partner of the Year (20 times)
A minimum of 80 hours per year dedicated to learning, certifications, and professional development
Access to global Data & AI communities, accelerators, and subject‑matter experts
The chance to work on cutting‑edge initiatives across AI, Agentic AI, analytics, and modern data platforms
Clear and supported career progression across Consultant and Senior Consultant levels


A great place to work

At Avanade, you’ll gain distinctive experiences, limitless learning, and ambitious growth in return for your skills and ideas. We are committed to building a diverse and inclusive culture where everyone feels a strong sense of belonging and is empowered to do their best work.

You’ll join a supportive community of collaborators who will mentor, challenge, and learn alongside you — in a company purpose‑built for business‑critical, leading‑edge technology solutions that improve how people work, interact, and live.

We work hard to provide an inclusive, diverse culture with a deep sense of belonging for all our employees. Visit our Inclusion & Diversity page.

Title: Finance Data Analyst
Description:
At Tracker, our culture is people focused; we believe our people are our greatest assets, and we are committed to being an employer of choice in our industry. Tracker offers an engaging and diverse work environment which permits our people to take pride in their contributions and share in the company’s success. We have a clear focus around the mental health of our people. They can expect the space to showcase their talent, sharpen their skills, develop new capabilities, and be a part of a collaborative, friendly team that delivers for each other and our customers.

Our purpose is to provide peace of mind by protecting what matters to our customers, whether these are individual end users of our services, SMEs, large corporates, dealer groups, or insurers.

We are the leading provider of Stolen Vehicle Recovery services in the UK, with strong additional capability in fleet and insurance telematics. We have two genuinely unique selling points, the proprietary VHF technology we use and the formal relationship we have with UK policing, which clearly differentiate us from our competitors. As part of the work that we do with UK policing we are making a genuine difference to society by helping to tackle organised criminality; in 2025 alone, we helped them close down over 60 chop shops, whilst supporting on multiple investigations.

This is an exciting opportunity for someone who wants to work for a market leading, well-established company with the opportunity to really accelerate our growth, as well as their own.

We operate a hybrid work environment with office-based collaboration Tuesday through Thursday, with the ability to work from home on Monday and Friday. We offer competitive parental leave and place strong emphasis on wellbeing and mental wealth.

Purpose of the Role

Tracker is currently seeking a Finance Data Analyst, to join a well-established market leading Telematics and Stolen Vehicle Recovery Company based in Uxbridge.

Tracker is a subsidiary of CalAmp, a pure-play pioneer of the Connected Car, Connected Truck and broader Internet of Things (IoT) marketplace, based in California.

The Finance Data Analyst will support the Finance team in delivering accurate, timely, and insightful reporting, while developing capability in Business Intelligence (BI) tools and data analytics.

This role sits at the intersection of Finance, Sales, and Data. The successful candidate will work with datasets from finance systems and Salesforce (CRM), supporting reporting, analysis, and the development of Power BI dashboards.

Main Responsibilities

Reporting & Analysis


Deliver regular finance and performance reporting (weekly / monthly)
Analyse revenue, costs, and key business drivers
Support reporting on sales pipeline, customer, and revenue data from Salesforce
Assist in producing KPI and performance reporting packs



Business Intelligence


Build and enhance Power BI dashboards with guidance
Support the transition from Excel-based reporting to BI solutions
Assist in developing data models and KPI definitions
Contribute to improving visibility and usability of data across the business



Data Management


Extract and work with data from finance systems, Salesforce, and Excel
Perform data validation, reconciliation, and quality checks
Support the structuring and maintenance of datasets for reportin



Process Improvement & Automation


Identify opportunities to streamline and automate reporting processes, including the use of AI
Support use of Power Query, Power BI, or similar tools to reduce manual effort
Contribute to improving reporting efficiency and consistency



Primary requirements

At Tracker, our culture is people focused; we believe our people are our greatest assets, and we are committed to being an employer of choice in our industry. Tracker offers an engaging and diverse work environment which permits our people to take pride in their contributions and share in the company’s success. We have a clear focus around the mental health of our people. They can expect the space to showcase their talent, sharpen their skills, develop new capabilities, and be a part of a collaborative, friendly team that delivers for each other and our customers.

Our purpose is to provide peace of mind by protecting what matters to our customers, whether these are individual end users of our services, SMEs, large corporates, dealer groups, or insurers.

We are the leading provider of Stolen Vehicle Recovery services in the UK, with strong additional capability in fleet and insurance telematics. We have two genuinely unique selling points, the proprietary VHF technology we use and the formal relationship we have with UK policing, which clearly differentiate us from our competitors. As part of the work that we do with UK policing we are making a genuine difference to society by helping to tackle organised criminality; in 2025 alone, we helped them close down over 60 chop shops, whilst supporting on multiple investigations.

This is an exciting opportunity for someone who wants to work for a market leading, well-established company with the opportunity to really accelerate our growth, as well as their own.

We operate a hybrid work environment with office-based collaboration Tuesday through Thursday, with the ability to work from home on Monday and Friday. We offer competitive parental leave and place strong emphasis on wellbeing and mental wealth.

Purpose of the Role

Tracker is currently seeking a Finance Data Analyst, to join a well-established market leading Telematics and Stolen Vehicle Recovery Company based in Uxbridge.

Tracker is a subsidiary of CalAmp, a pure-play pioneer of the Connected Car, Connected Truck and broader Internet of Things (IoT) marketplace, based in California.

The Finance Data Analyst will support the Finance team in delivering accurate, timely, and insightful reporting, while developing capability in Business Intelligence (BI) tools and data analytics.

This role sits at the intersection of Finance, Sales, and Data. The successful candidate will work with datasets from finance systems and Salesforce (CRM), supporting reporting, analysis, and the development of Power BI dashboards.

Main Responsibilities

Reporting & Analysis

Business Intelligence

  • Assist in developing data models and KPI definitions
  • Contribute to improving visibility and usability of data across the business


Data Management

  • Support the structuring and maintenance of datasets for reportin


Process Improvement & Automation

  • Identify opportunities to streamline and automate reporting processes, including the use of AI
  • Support use of Power Query, Power BI, or similar tools to reduce manual effort
  • Contribute to improving reporting efficiency and consistency


Primary requirements

  • Proven ability to work with structured datasets and produce reporting outputs
  • Good analytical mindset with high attention to detail
  • Ability to interpret data and communicate insights clearly
  • Experience working with data from multiple systems (e.g. finance, CRM, Excel)

Other qualifications considered a plus

  • Exposure to Salesforce reporting, dashboards and/or visualising Salesforce data via Power BI
  • Understanding of sales pipeline, revenue, or customer metrics
  • Experience with Power Query, SQL, or data transformation tools
  • Experience with Power Automate, Power Apps
  • Exposure to financial concepts (revenue, cost, margin analysis)

Title: Systems Data Analyst
Description:
Asendia UK is seeking a Systems Data Analyst to provide dedicated oversight across IT, Projects, Compliance, and Finance. This role acts as the central bridge between these functions, ensuring that data entering our systems is accurate, complete, and flowing correctly through all downstream processes—from customer‑facing outputs to internal reporting and regulatory submissions. The position provides essential assurance, identifying issues early and preventing operational or financial impact.

This role is also critical to maintaining compliance across GDPR, financial duties, taxes, and system requirements relating to duty calculations and carrier selection. By providing focused, ongoing oversight, the postholder ensures that regulatory obligations are consistently met without placing additional pressure on already stretched teams.




Key Responsibilities:




Data Integrity & Flow

Map data flows across the multi‑application platform estate, from data import through to customer delivery and reporting
Identify gaps, breaks, and inconsistencies in data movement, working with IT and development teams to resolve issues
Design and implement data quality controls, validation rules, and monitoring tools
Maintain accurate and up‑to‑date data flow documentation
Act as the first point of escalation for data quality issues raised by Finance, Operations, or Compliance




Compliance & Regulatory Assurance

Maintain ongoing assurance that systems and processes meet GDPR requirements, including data handling, retention, and subject access
Ensure duty calculation logic and carrier selection rules remain accurate and compliant with financial and customs regulations
Translate regulatory obligations into system controls and monitoring requirements
Support internal and external audits with clear evidence of data accuracy and compliance controls
Monitor regulatory changes and assess their impact on system configurations




Cross‑Functional Bridging

Serve as the dedicated link between IT, Finance, Compliance, and Project teams
Represent data integrity and compliance requirements within project governance forums
Produce clear reporting for leadership on system health, data quality metrics, and compliance status
Support Business Analysts and Project Managers by owning the data integrity and compliance workstream




Technical Skills & Experience:




Essential

Experience in a data, compliance, or systems analyst role within a technology‑led environment
Strong understanding of data flows, system integrations, and multi‑application estates
Experience designing or implementing data quality controls or monitoring frameworks
Working knowledge of GDPR and practical application of data compliance requirements
Ability to read and interpret SQL queries to validate data and system outputs
Proficiency with process and data flow mapping tools (e.g., Visio or equivalent)




Desirable

Background in eCommerce, logistics, customs, or financial services
Experience working across IT and Finance functions
Familiarity with data governance frameworks or lineage tools
Exposure to import/export regulations or carrier compliance requirements
Experience supporting audits or regulatory submissions




Fixed Deliverables During the Contract

Automated processes for data reconciliation
Automated alerting for compliance reporting requirements
Automated handling of incorrect or incomplete customer data
Comprehensive documentation of data flows
Demonstrated process improvements across data and compliance workflows.




Click apply if you are interested in this role.

Title: Revenue Analyst
Description:
Group Revenue Analyst

Location: Our Corporate Office in Central London

Job Type: Full time Monday to Friday - 40 hours per week

Join Our Revenue Management Team

We are currently recruiting for a Group Revenue Analyst this is a new role in our revenue management team reporting to our Director of Revenue.

The role of Group Revenue Analyst serves as the strategic engine for pricing and inventory optimization. Moving beyond basic reporting, the role translates complex data into actionable strategies to maximize profitability.

Key objectives include ensuring the efficient application of pricing and inventory management (precision control). providing leadership with a transparent, data-driven understanding of business (strategic clarity).

What will the role Involve?

This role has three primary areas of focus

Data Analysis and reporting
System Support
Distribution Support




Key Responsibilities:

Data Analysis and reporting

Providing regular (daily, weekly, monthly) reports with supporting commentary
Monitoring daily pickup and trends to support revenue managers in their decisions
Design and maintain dashboards to track key performance indicators (KPIs)
Market Intelligence: Stay ahead of industry trends, seasonal events, and macroeconomic factors that could impact consumer behaviour.
Supports forecast and budgeting process by providing required data/reports.
Data Extraction: Building Power BI/ Power Pivot dashboards to pull insights that the standard system reports might miss

System Support

Auditing the flow of data between the PMS (Opera), the RMS (Duetto), and CRS (iHotellier).
Acting as the "Level 2" support for divisional revenue managers when their systems aren't yielding the correct rates or inventory isn't syncing.
Reviewing and monitoring system configuration (PMS, RMS, CRS) and ensuring setups are correct.




Distribution Support
Monitoring competitor rates using tools like Lighthouse and STR.
Monitoring rate parity within channels and highlight discrepancies.
Maintaining the mapping between the PMS, and CRS.
Maintaining the OTA’s extranets

Are you Hand Picked?

To be considered for this role you will have current revenue analyst experience within the hotel industry.
Technical Skills: Advanced proficiency in Excel (Pivot Tables, Power Pivot, VLOOKUPs, and complex modelling).
Experience with Data Visualization tools (e.g., Tableau, Power BI).
Familiarity with Revenue Management Systems (RMS) or CRM software.
Analytical Mindset: An innate ability to spot patterns in "noisy" data and a healthy obsession with accuracy.
Be able to demonstrate excellent communication skills with the ability to explain the "why" behind the numbers to stakeholders who may not be data-savvy




Who we are…

Hand Picked Hotels was founded in 2001 by owner and Chairman, Julia Hands MBE, and today still remains a family-owned group which has 21 unique luxury country houses and coastal retreats, each hand picked for their individuality. Architecturally distinctive and quintessentially British, every hotel boasts an extraordinary location from Scotland to the Channel Islands, offering breathtaking views from vast countryside to expansive beaches, to lush woodlands.




Dedicated to creating unique and enchanting stays, curated personalised service runs through the heart of every hotel. Whilst each hotel is individual, each property fulfils the company values of family, individuality, community and care, with a team commitment to aiming higher and delight their guests with magical experiences that offer a true escape from the every day.




Hand Picked Hotels are committed to protecting the environment for a sustainable future, and all of our hotels have received the ‘Green Tourism’ accreditation. A Recognition of our progress in reducing energy use and waste, supporting biodiversity, and sourcing responsibly from local and ethical partners.




What’s in it for you?

A competitive salary, discussed at interview stage.
A welcoming, inclusive culture where long-term friendships and diversity are celebrated.
Ongoing career development with in-house and external training, including apprenticeships and industry-recognised courses.
28 days holiday per year including bank holidays, increasing to 33 days including bank holidays with length of service.
Access to wellbeing support through our Employee Assistance Programme.
Company pension enhanced sick pay & Life assurance scheme.
Online retail discount platform offering thousands of savings with high street retailers and restaurants as well as a health and well-being platform.
Discounted stays in our hotels and on food, drink, spa treatments & products.
Annual loyalty awards (like afternoon teas and overnight stays).
Introduction bonus for new colleague recommendations.
Colleague of the Month awards, which leads to our annual awards ceremony.
Regular team gatherings and appreciation events.




Hand Picked Hotels is proud to be an Equal Opportunities Employer. Applicants must have the right to live and work in the UK.

Title: Product Analyst
Description:
Who We Are




Zilch is a payment tech company on a mission to create the most empowering way to pay for anything, anywhere. Combining the best of debit, credit and savings, we give our customers the option to earn instant cashback or spread the cost of pricier purchases, completely interest free and with no late fees. Pretty great, right?




We started in 2018 with a small team and a big dream - to make credit accessible to all. Since then, we've achieved double unicorn status and taken on more than 5 million customers. There are some exciting projects coming up and we’ve got big growth plans.




Want to join us?




About The Role




You will be the embedded into a Product team, offering strategic analytics and meaningful insights to influence the development roadmap and enable self-serve data-driven decision making.




At Zilch, you will learn to understand our customers better than anybody. You will use this to help enable the business to build the best products possible for our customers by proposing great ideas, sizing risks, measuring impact.




This role reports into the Lead Product Analyst alongside two other analysts in the Spend domain. Being a unicorn startup, we work rapidly and flexibly. Expect to work with various business stakeholders and solving various different problems.




This is not a reporting role. You'll sit alongside Product Managers as their analytical counterpart, spotting things they don't, challenging assumptions with evidence, and sharing your own ideas and insights to drive the team forward.




The opportunity to make an impact here is immediate. This domain hinges on understanding user behaviour, the ability to contextualise the value proposition and thinking along product lines, to enable users to build new financial products and optimise our existing suite.




Growth & Support. Your manager is there to support you and mentor you, taking an active role in helping you shape your career path and trajectory




Day To Day Responsibilities.




Be the analytical partner to Product




Work shoulder-to-shoulder with PMs as their thought partner: helping frame problems, pressure-test hypotheses, and make better decisions faster. Think about impact both in terms of potential, speed and quality of delivery, and risk
Proactively investigate the "why" behind metric movements, customer behaviour shifts, and product performance and be passionate about sharing your discoveries with the team
Be a strong communicator, translating complex analysis into clear, opinionated recommendations




Be Passionate




Dig into the data and drill down to the transaction level. Understand our users intimately and recognise edge cases
Have a view on the bigger picture and be strategic; identify low hanging fruit, be it growing GMV or improving unit economics
Own your domain by making data tangible to your stakeholders, making ambiguous and poorly defined problems clear and solvable, and demonstrate Ownership. Share your findings effectively to non- and technical stakeholders alike, by tailoring your communication to your audiences




Design and run experiments




Design and analyse A/B tests and quasi-experiments across different experimental hypotheses and apply the right approach based, balancing out scientific rigour, customer impact and our desire to move fast
Establish incrementally, especially when randomisation isn't feasible, and attributing changes in performance to successive product changes
Partner with Engineering to ensure clean event instrumentation and experiment infrastructure for the Spend domain




What We're Looking For...




Must have's




Ideally, 3–5 years' experience in product analytics, payments analytics, marketplace analytics, or a closely adjacent quantitative role. You have experience working as part of a team and benefit from mentorship
Genuine curiosity. You notice outliers and patterns and must understand them. You generate insight and want to be a strategic partner
Strong SQL and data manipulation skills. You have the ability to explore, wrangle and transform large, messy transactional datasets, define the right cuts, and build robust analyses
Reliable experimentation skills; design clean tests, reason about statistical power and validity, handle edge cases, and present results with appropriate caveats
Strong product instincts. You think in terms of customer behaviour and business outcomes, not just data tables. You can recommend what to build, and are a reliable contributor in product ideation conversations
Clear communication; distil complex findings into crisp, opinionated narratives for engineering, product, commercial, and exec audiences
Comfort with ambiguity. You build analytical foundations in an evolving domain, with consideration for scalability in the future. Our evolving roadmap is an opportunity for you to leave your mark and contribute.




Strong advantage




Experience in payments, fintech, or financial services, with familiarity with interchange, scheme rules, authorisation flows, settlement, or card issuing/acquiring
Proficiency in Python or R for statistical analysis, data manipulation, or modelling.
Experience with modern analytics engineering tooling (dbt, version-controlled transformations, CI/CD for data).
Familiarity with unit economics, margin analysis, or P&L-level thinking in a transactional business.




Bonus




Exposure to payment routing optimisation, decline recovery, or acceptance rate analytics.
Familiarity with experimentation platforms (LaunchDarkly, Optimizely, or equivalent).
Experience working with Finance or accounting functions




What You'll Get




Ownership within High-impact, high-volume domain; the products you work on will be experienced by millions
Strategic partnership. You'll be embedded with PMs as their equal. Your insight shapes the roadmap
Support and mentorship; your manager will invest in your professional growth and has your back. You are part of a team that collaborates, performs peer reviews and enjoys knowledge-sharing
Clear Growth trajectory. Your work directly drives impact. It's a path toward Senior Product Analyst
Modern stack: Snowflake, dbt, Looker, LaunchDarkly, Mixpanel, Python. The Data Engineering team deliver pipeline quality and self-serve infrastructure




Benefits.




Compensation & Savings




Pension scheme.
Death in Service scheme.
Income Protection.
Permanent employees enjoy access to our Share Options Scheme.
5% back on in-app purchases.
£200 for WFH Setup.




Health & Wellbeing




Private Medical Insurance including;
GP consultations (video, telephone or face-to-face).
Prescribed medication.
In-patient, day-patient and out-patient care.
Mental health support.
Optical, dental & audiological cover.
Physiotherapy.
Advanced cancer cover.
Menopause support.
Employee Assistance Programme including:
Unlimited mental health sessions.
24/7 remote GP & physiotherapy.
24/7 helpline for emotional & practical support.
Savings & discounts on everyday shopping.
1:1 personalised well-being consultations.
Gym membership discounts.




Family Friendly Policies




Enhanced maternity pay.
Enhanced paternity pay.
Enhanced adoption pay.
Enhanced shared parental leave.




Learning & Development




Professional Qualifications.
Professional Memberships.
Learning Suite for e-courses.
Internal Training Programmes.
FCA & Regulatory training.




Workplace Perks




Hybrid working: office-based Monday, Wednesday, and Thursday; remote working Tuesday and Friday
Casual dress code.
Workplace socials.
Healthy snacks.

HTML:
Who We Are


Zilch is a payment tech company on a mission to create the most empowering way to pay for anything, anywhere. Combining the best of debit, credit and savings, we give our customers the option to earn instant cashback or spread the cost of pricier purchases, completely interest free and with no late fees. Pretty great, right?


We started in 2018 with a small team and a big dream - to make credit accessible to all. Since then, we've achieved double unicorn status and taken on more than 5 million customers. There are some exciting projects coming up and we’ve got big growth plans.


Want to join us?


About The Role


You will be the embedded into a Product team, offering strategic analytics and meaningful insights to influence the development roadmap and enable self-serve data-driven decision making.


At Zilch, you will learn to understand our customers better than anybody. You will use this to help enable the business to build the best products possible for our customers by proposing great ideas, sizing risks, measuring impact.


This role reports into the Lead Product Analyst alongside two other analysts in the Spend domain. Being a unicorn startup, we work rapidly and flexibly. Expect to work with various business stakeholders and solving various different problems.


This is not a reporting role. You'll sit alongside Product Managers as their analytical counterpart, spotting things they don't, challenging assumptions with evidence, and sharing your own ideas and insights to drive the team forward.


The opportunity to make an impact here is immediate. This domain hinges on understanding user behaviour, the ability to contextualise the value proposition and thinking along product lines, to enable users to build new financial products and optimise our existing suite.


Growth & Support. Your manager is there to support you and mentor you, taking an active role in helping you shape your career path and trajectory


Day To Day Responsibilities.


Be the analytical partner to Product


  • Work shoulder-to-shoulder with PMs as their thought partner: helping frame problems, pressure-test hypotheses, and make better decisions faster. Think about impact both in terms of potential, speed and quality of delivery, and risk
  • Proactively investigate the "why" behind metric movements, customer behaviour shifts, and product performance and be passionate about sharing your discoveries with the team
  • Be a strong communicator, translating complex analysis into clear, opinionated recommendations


Be Passionate


  • Dig into the data and drill down to the transaction level. Understand our users intimately and recognise edge cases
  • Have a view on the bigger picture and be strategic; identify low hanging fruit, be it growing GMV or improving unit economics
  • Own your domain by making data tangible to your stakeholders, making ambiguous and poorly defined problems clear and solvable, and demonstrate Ownership. Share your findings effectively to non- and technical stakeholders alike, by tailoring your communication to your audiences


Design and run experiments


  • Design and analyse A/B tests and quasi-experiments across different experimental hypotheses and apply the right approach based, balancing out scientific rigour, customer impact and our desire to move fast
  • Establish incrementally, especially when randomisation isn't feasible, and attributing changes in performance to successive product changes
  • Partner with Engineering to ensure clean event instrumentation and experiment infrastructure for the Spend domain


What We're Looking For...


Must have's


  • Ideally, 3–5 years' experience in product analytics, payments analytics, marketplace analytics, or a closely adjacent quantitative role. You have experience working as part of a team and benefit from mentorship
  • Genuine curiosity. You notice outliers and patterns and must understand them. You generate insight and want to be a strategic partner
  • Strong SQL and data manipulation skills. You have the ability to explore, wrangle and transform large, messy transactional datasets, define the right cuts, and build robust analyses
  • Reliable experimentation skills; design clean tests, reason about statistical power and validity, handle edge cases, and present results with appropriate caveats
  • Strong product instincts. You think in terms of customer behaviour and business outcomes, not just data tables. You can recommend what to build, and are a reliable contributor in product ideation conversations
  • Clear communication; distil complex findings into crisp, opinionated narratives for engineering, product, commercial, and exec audiences
  • Comfort with ambiguity. You build analytical foundations in an evolving domain, with consideration for scalability in the future. Our evolving roadmap is an opportunity for you to leave your mark and contribute.


Strong advantage


  • Experience in payments, fintech, or financial services, with familiarity with interchange, scheme rules, authorisation flows, settlement, or card issuing/acquiring
  • Proficiency in Python or R for statistical analysis, data manipulation, or modelling.
  • Experience with modern analytics engineering tooling (dbt, version-controlled transformations, CI/CD for data).
  • Familiarity with unit economics, margin analysis, or P&L-level thinking in a transactional business.


Bonus


  • Exposure to payment routing optimisation, decline recovery, or acceptance rate analytics.
  • Familiarity with experimentation platforms (LaunchDarkly, Optimizely, or equivalent).
  • Experience working with Finance or accounting functions


What You'll Get


  • Ownership within High-impact, high-volume domain; the products you work on will be experienced by millions
  • Strategic partnership. You'll be embedded with PMs as their equal. Your insight shapes the roadmap
  • Support and mentorship; your manager will invest in your professional growth and has your back. You are part of a team that collaborates, performs peer reviews and enjoys knowledge-sharing
  • Clear Growth trajectory. Your work directly drives impact. It's a path toward Senior Product Analyst
  • Modern stack: Snowflake, dbt, Looker, LaunchDarkly, Mixpanel, Python. The Data Engineering team deliver pipeline quality and self-serve infrastructure


Benefits.


Compensation & Savings


  • Pension scheme.
  • Death in Service scheme.
  • Income Protection.
  • Permanent employees enjoy access to our Share Options Scheme.
  • 5% back on in-app purchases.
  • £200 for WFH Setup.


Health & Wellbeing


  • Private Medical Insurance including;
  • GP consultations (video, telephone or face-to-face).
  • Prescribed medication.
  • In-patient, day-patient and out-patient care.
  • Mental health support.
  • Optical, dental & audiological cover.
  • Physiotherapy.
  • Advanced cancer cover.
  • Menopause support.
  • Employee Assistance Programme including:
  • Unlimited mental health sessions.
  • 24/7 remote GP & physiotherapy.
  • 24/7 helpline for emotional & practical support.
  • Savings & discounts on everyday shopping.
  • 1:1 personalised well-being consultations.
  • Gym membership discounts.


Family Friendly Policies


  • Enhanced maternity pay.
  • Enhanced paternity pay.
  • Enhanced adoption pay.
  • Enhanced shared parental leave.


Learning & Development


  • Professional Qualifications.
  • Professional Memberships.
  • Learning Suite for e-courses.
  • Internal Training Programmes.
  • FCA & Regulatory training.


Workplace Perks


  • Hybrid working: office-based Monday, Wednesday, and Thursday; remote working Tuesday and Friday
  • Casual dress code.
  • Workplace socials.
  • Healthy snacks.

Title: Business Intelligence Analyst
Description:
LOCATION: Canary Wharf, London, E14

SHIFT PATTERN: Mon - Fri 0800 - 1700

Work type: On-site

SALARY: £45,000 per annum

If you require any additional support or adjustments during the recruitment process, please don't hesitate to contact our Recruitment Department at recruitment@abm.com. We're here to help!

Role overview

The Business Intelligence Analyst will be responsible for designing, developing and maintaining interactive and informative dashboards and reports using Microsoft Power BI. The ideal candidate should have a strong background in data analysis, report generation, and a deep understanding of business intelligence concepts. This role requires collaboration with various departments to gather requirements and ensure the effective use of Power BI and other ABM & third-party platforms to meet & align operational demands & other innovations.

Main Duties:


Design, develop, and maintain Power BI and other dashboards and reports to provide actionable insights for business stakeholders.
Data Modelling: Create and maintain efficient data models, including data cleaning, transformation, and aggregation.
Data Integration: Integrate data from various sources, including databases, cloud services, and APIs, ensuring data accuracy and consistency.
Requirement Gathering: Collaborate and align with business users, clients and third party service partners to understand their needs and translate them into technical requirements and dashboard solutions.
Performance Optimisation: Optimise the performance of Power BI reports and dashboards, ensuring quick load times and responsive interactions.
User Training and Support: Provide training and support to end-users on all platforms, functionalities and best practices.
Documentation: Maintain comprehensive documentation for Power BI solutions, including data models, reports, and dashboards.
Stay Updated: Keep up to date with the latest Power BI features, tools, industry innovations and best practices to continuously improve reporting, analytics capabilities and client experience.


Desired Experience:

Experience:


Education: Level 4 Data Analyst apprenticeship or equivalent.
Experience: Minimum of 2 years of experience in business intelligence and data analytics, with a strong focus on Power BI, dashboards within a client environment


Technical Skills:


Proficiency in Microsoft Power BI, including DAX and Power Query.
Experience with SQL and relational databases.
Knowledge of data warehousing concepts and ETL processes.
Knowledge of Microsoft Power Platforms.
Familiarity with other Microsoft BI tools, such as SSRS, SSIS, and SSAS, is a plus.


Soft Skills:


Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
Excellent communication and interpersonal skills including senior client levels
Ability to work independently and as part of a team.
Strong attention to detail and commitment to delivering high-quality work.


Preferred Qualifications:


Certifications: Microsoft Certified: Data Analyst Associate or similar certifications.
Experience: Experience in Facilities Management Industry is a plus.
Additional Skills: Familiarity with other data visualization tools and programming languages such as Python or R.


Benefits

We're proud to offer a great range of benefits including:


24/7 GP: Both you and your immediate family can speak to a UK-based GP from the comfort of your own home
Mental Health support and Life Event Counseling
Get Fit Programme
Financial and legal support
Cycle to work scheme
Access Perks at Work, our innovative employee app where you can find:
Perks: discounts, gift cards, cashback, and exclusive offers
Life: Search for resources and tools on topics ranging from family and life to health, money and work
Support: Online chat or telephone service for urgent support in a crisis


For More Information About ABM's Benefits, Visit Our

About ABM:

ABM (NYSE: ABM) is one of the world's largest providers of integrated facility, engineering, and infrastructure solutions. Every day, over 100,000 team members deliver essential services that make spaces cleaner, safer, and efficient, enhancing the overall occupant experience.

ABM serves a wide range of market sectors including commercial real estate, aviation, mission critical, and manufacturing and distribution. With over $8 billion in annual revenue and a blue-chip client base, ABM delivers innovative technologies and sustainable solutions that enhance facilities and empower clients to achieve their goals. Committed to creating smarter, more connected spaces, ABM is investing in the future to meet evolving challenges and build a healthier, thriving world. ABM: Driving possibility, together.

For more information, visit .

ABM are committed to employment practices that promote diversity and inclusion in employment regardless of age, disability, gender reassignment, sex, marriage and civil partnership status, pregnancy and maternity status, race, religion or belief. We are proud members of the Armed Forces Covenant Employer Recognition Scheme.

Title: Network and Alliances Capability Analyst
Description:
A career without limits

As the nation’s flag carrier, we take great pride in connecting Britain with the world and the world with Britain.

It’s something we’ve been doing for over 100 years, ever since we launched the world’s first international scheduled air service between London and Paris.

This originality has been in our blood since day one. It’s the spirit we share with the people that fly with us, our partners, and our colleagues.

So, whether you are a reassuring voice on the end of a phone, a smile at the door, under a wing keeping the turbines spinning or landing us gently in far-flung places, a job at British Airways is yours to make.

We know great things can happen when you’re inspired to think big and bring your ambition to work every day, which is why, at British Airways the sky is never the limit.

The role: Network & Alliances Capability Analyst

Join our Networks & Alliances directorate here at British Airways! — A team that plays a critical role in shaping how and where we fly.

This area sits at the heart of our airline, influencing decisions in revenue and costs, and helping to define our long‑term network strategy.

In this role, you’ll help strengthen the analytical capability that underpins these decisions, working with data, models, tools and training to ensure our teams are equipped to make smart, evidence‑based choices in an increasingly complex and competitive aviation environment.

What You’ll Do


Support the implementation of the Network & Alliances Capability Strategy, including data exploitation, analytical tools, models and reporting
Lead and contribute to projects that enhance the department’s technical capability through research, development and continuous improvement
Work closely with Network & Alliances, Technology, Data and Analytics teams to deliver improvements to data and tools that drive better decision‑making
Help identify and meet training needs across the directorate, supporting colleagues to build confidence and capability
Develop and maintain strong working relationships across British Airways, IAG and external partners to support capability development
Provide ad‑hoc analytical support and cover for wider team activities when required


What You’ll Bring To British Airways

You’ll be a curious, analytical thinker who enjoys solving complex problems and challenging existing ways of working. You’re comfortable working with ambiguity and imperfect data, and you take a proactive, collaborative approach to improving tools, processes and insight. Resilient and delivery‑focused, you bring strong communication skills and the confidence to influence others, while remaining flexible, supportive and aligned to BA’s ways of working.

Your Experience


A numerate degree or proven numerical aptitude in a similar analytical role
Strong analytical and problem‑solving skills, with the ability to draw clear insights from complex datasets
Experience using data analysis tools such as Excel, SQL, Python, Tableau or similar
Ability to communicate complex analysis clearly to a range of audiences, including senior stakeholders
Experience working collaboratively in cross‑functional teams and managing competing priorities
Knowledge of airline economics, aviation systems (e.g. Sabre, Amadeus), or BA data tools would be advantageous


What We Offer

We believe that all the people who work with us should feel valued for the part they play. It’s one of the reasons our rewards go far beyond a competitive salary.

From the day you join us, you’ll get access to brilliant staff travel benefits including unlimited basic and premium standby tickets on British Airways flights. You’ll also receive up to 30 discounted ‘Hotline’ airfares per year for yourself, friends, and family.

At British Airways you’ll have the chance to take on new challenges and move forward in a way that feels right for you. We encourage all those who work for us to consider opportunities right across our business to help you develop and progress.

We never stand still, and we don’t expect our people to either.

Inclusion & Diversity

At British Airways we all have a part to play in creating an inclusive place to work. Diverse representation among our people is really important to us and we recognise that all our colleagues are uniquely different and bring their own originality, creativity and identity to work.

Inclusion and diversity is a key driver of innovation and we’re committed to creating a culture where everyone feels that they can be themselves. We’re looking for people from all backgrounds and cultures to join us and be a part of our journey to become a Better BA as we continue to connect Britain with the world and the world with Britain.

Title: Pricing Analyst
Description:
We're Houst 🏠: A professional management service for properties. Our mission is to make hosting on websites hassle-free for everyone using technology to disrupt the short-term accommodation sector. We've grown fast. Since launching in 2015, we've partnered with thousands of happy hosts, helping them to make important earnings. The business has now expanded worldwide - we're operating in over 20 cities globally, from Auckland to Lisbon, and there's a lot more to come. From holidaymakers to full-time landlords, host happiness is central to everything we do.

We are seeking a highly detail-oriented Pricing Analyst to join our team. The ideal candidate will play a critical role in helping us optimise the pricing of properties, with the objective of driving revenue growth, improving occupancy rates, and maximising RevPAR. This position requires someone who is not only comfortable with analysing large sets of data but also proactive in implementing insights and solutions that will directly impact our business and our clients.

Pricing Strategy:


Work hands-on to develop and implement property pricing strategies that optimise both occupancy and revenue
Continuously monitor property performance and recommend price adjustments based on demand, market trends, and other relevant factors
Utilise data to track and compare competitor pricing, ensuring that Houst's properties remain competitive in the marketplace


Data Analysis & Reporting:


Analyse large datasets related to property performance, including revenue, occupancy, and RevPAR
Interpret models and algorithms to predict trends and opportunities for pricing optimisation
Create detailed reports to communicate key insights to the pricing and revenue management teams


Revenue Maximisation:


Collaborate with cross-functional teams to improve overall revenue management and growth
Identify patterns and insights that can be leveraged to maximise the return on properties for clients
Support decision-making processes by providing data-backed insights and recommendations


Market Trend & Analysis:


Conduct regular market and competitor analysis to identify emerging trends and market shifts
Leverage insights from data to stay ahead of market fluctuations and adjust strategies accordingly


Requirements


Strong ability to collect, organise, analyse, and interpret datasets with high accuracy
Exceptional attention to detail, ensuring data accuracy and high-quality insights for decision-making
Willing to take a proactive role in driving property performance improvements and adapting pricing strategies
Strong verbal and written communication skills with the ability to present complex data in a clear and concise manner to stakeholders
Confident with pricing algorithms, revenue management systems, and dynamic pricing tools
Experience with Looker and SQL


Benefits


Culture: Join a team that values innovation, autonomy, and continuous improvement, with regular team events and recognition for outstanding work
Flexibility: Hybrid work model (3 days in office, 2 days remote) for work-life balance
Growth & Development: Access to career development resources, mentorship, and training to support your professional growth
Comprehensive Benefits: Competitive salary, 25 days of paid holiday (plus public holidays), an extra day off for your birthday, and a pension plan. Enjoy employee discounts, wellness resources, and "pawternity" leave for new pet parents


Salary: £45,000- £50,000 (DOE)

Diversity & Inclusion

Houst is committed to creating an inclusive and diverse workplace where everyone feels empowered to contribute and succeed. We value the unique perspectives that drive innovation and encourage applicants from all backgrounds.

Ready to make an impact? Apply now and join us in shaping the future of property management!

  • Growth & Development: Access to career development resources, mentorship, and training to support your professional growth
  • Comprehensive Benefits: Competitive salary, 25 days of paid holiday (plus public holidays), an extra day off for your birthday, and a pension plan. Enjoy employee discounts, wellness resources, and "pawternity" leave for new pet parents

Salary: £45,000- £50,000 (DOE)

Diversity & Inclusion

Houst is committed to creating an inclusive and diverse workplace where everyone feels empowered to contribute and succeed. We value the unique perspectives that drive innovation and encourage applicants from all backgrounds.

Ready to make an impact? Apply now and join us in shaping the future of property management!

Title: Product Analyst
Description:
PRODUCT OWNER / DATA-ALGORITHM ANALYST







The firm is a leading B2B SaaS company that provides a comprehensive and fully integrated SaaS solution to Managing Agents who manage buildings and estates and Accountants who undertake Service Charge Accounting.




The company has a broad Product offering and is continuing to expand it. Utilising desktop and native mobile apps as the user interfaces, and AI Agents are being embedded into the Products moving forward. You will have the satisfaction of being a key contributor to an aspect of the essential growth of the business. And as you take on more responsibility and help the company grow, you will share in the success.




Currently a team of diverse, engaged, passionate and pragmatic people who thrive on solving challenges, achieving success and having fun while doing so!




The role is primarily home based but may require occasional working in and around London.







THE ROLE




The company is looking for a Product Owner / Data-Algorithm Analyst who will have a key position working to building out these new Products. The person will be working with the established leadership team, liaising with clients and internal stakeholders and using their critical thinking, together with design and data analysis ability to help translate feature requirements into working product that can be used by clients. You will understand the technical and data aspects of the system to facilitate a pragmatic approach to design and be able to demonstrate the ability to understand and exploit algorithms supported by AI toolsets we utilise to achieve our goals.










WHAT WILL YOU BE DOING?




Translating high level product features into detailed, unambiguous requirements outline screen/data designs with clear acceptance criteria.
Analysing data and process to look for patterns we can translate optimally to build AI agents.
Structuring and tweaking algorithms utilised in client facing solutions.
Owning the delivery backlog for your Products within the estate of products, refining feature requirements, writing user stories, setting priorities, and managing issues through to completion.
Undertaking user acceptance testing based on acceptance criteria.
Coordinating timelines and releases, ensuring developers are working to clear goals and delivery milestones.
Working closely with internal stakeholders (marketing, development, customer support) to refine requirements, manage expectations, and communicate progress.
Supporting the development team by removing blockers, clarifying requirements, and making delivery decisions quickly.
Drafting release notes and help centre updates associated with Product releases.
Providing assisting as needed to the Customer Support team for the product in the live environment.




WHO ARE YOU?




Demonstrable experience as a Product Owner / Data analyst/ Data scientist Functional Designer
Ideally experience of working in a B2B SaaS environment.
Competent at technical writing; ability to create functional specifications/blueprints and solutions-orientated screen and data designs
Competent at understanding and extracting data in relational databases for assessment and testing(SQL)
Competent a data analysis and basic statistics
Competent at understanding JSON data structures and being able to visual data manipulation.
Ideally some experience in Machine Learning/Artificial Intelligence and / or Native apps.

Title: Data Scientist II – Performance Optimization Squad
Description:
The Performance Optimization Squad is a newly formed team in the Core Infrastructure Studio with a mission to establish a core competency in performance engineering and address systemic inefficiencies across Spotify's platform. With 3,200+ microservices, 40,000 VMs at peak, and 500,000 K8s pods, even minor fleet-wide efficiency improvements result in substantial cost savings. We've already identified $8M+ in annual savings from our first initiative alone, and we're just getting started.

This squad works horizontally across the entire stack — but none of that optimization happens without the data to see it. We're looking for a Data Analyst who will build the measurement foundation that drives every decision we make.

What You'll Do

Optimization at this scale is only possible when someone can see the problem clearly. You'll build the data foundation from scratch; designing and owning the datasets, pipelines, and metrics that make performance inefficiencies visible across the platform.


Design, build, and maintain datasets and data pipelines that surface resource utilization, cost, and performance signals across Spotify's infrastructure
Define and own metrics for efficiency, latency, and resource utilization; turning raw infrastructure signals into insights that drive prioritization
Proactively investigate performance data to surface optimization opportunities, not just respond to engineering requests
Build dashboards and analyses that support decision-making across the squad and partnering platform teams
Work with engineers and platform teams to define guardrail metrics, validate findings, and measure the real-world impact of optimization efforts
Translate complex infrastructure data into clear stories for both technical and non-technical audiences
Own the data foundation: There is no inherited data infrastructure here; you'll design and build it from scratch. What gets measured, and how, is yours to define
See your impact directly: Every insight you surface translates into cost savings. We measure success in dollars saved and efficiency gained; your work shows up in production
Breadth at scale: Work across the entire Spotify platform; 3,200+ microservices, 40,000 VMs, 500,000 K8s pods. Few companies offer data problems at this scale
Greenfield from day one: Help shape the culture, tooling, and data strategy of a brand new squad with strong executive support


Who You Are


You have experience working with infrastructure, platform, or cloud cost data; Kubernetes metrics, cost attribution, utilization signals, or observability data feel familiar
Or you're a strong technical analyst with enough grounding in distributed systems and cloud infrastructure to navigate GKE cost data, JVM metrics, and resource utilization signals
You write clean, efficient SQL and Python; comfortable enough to model data and build lightweight pipelines, not just query existing tables
You're self-directed: at your best when hunting for problems in the data, not waiting to be handed them
Comfortable with ambiguity and able to carve your own path in an early-stage, unstructured environment
Experienced with data visualization tools (Looker or similar) and know how to make a dashboard tell a story, not just display numbers
You communicate clearly and confidently with engineers and non-technical stakeholders alike


Where You'll Be


This role is based in Stockholm or London.
We offer you the flexibility to work where you work best! There will be some in person meetings, but still allows for flexibility to work from home.


Spotify is an equal opportunity employer. You are welcome at Spotify for who you are, no matter where you come from, what you look like, or what’s playing in your headphones. Our platform is for everyone, and so is our workplace. The more voices we have represented and amplified in our business, the more we will all thrive, contribute, and be forward-thinking! So bring us your personal experience, your perspectives, and your background. It’s in our differences that we will find the power to keep revolutionizing the way the world listens.

At Spotify, we are passionate about inclusivity and making sure our entire recruitment process is accessible to everyone. We have ways to request reasonable accommodations during the interview process and help assist in what you need. If you need accommodations at any stage of the application or interview process, please let us know - we’re here to support you in any way we can.

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us. Find our AI notice here: https://lifeatspotify.com/ai-notice

Today, we are the world’s most popular audio streaming subscription service.

Title: Data Analyst III
Description:
About Us

Fanatics is building a leading global digital sports platform. We ignite the passions of global sports fans and maximize the presence and reach for our hundreds of sports partners globally by offering products and services across Fanatics Commerce, Fanatics Collectibles, and Fanatics Betting & Gaming, allowing sports fans to Buy, Collect, and Bet. Through the Fanatics platform, sports fans can buy licensed fan gear, jerseys, lifestyle and streetwear products, headwear, and hardgoods; collect physical and digital trading cards, sports memorabilia, and other digital assets; and bet as the company builds its Sportsbook and iGaming platform. Fanatics has an established database of over 100 million global sports fans; a global partner network with approximately 900 sports properties, including major national and international professional sports leagues, players associations, teams, colleges, college conferences and retail partners, 2,500 athletes and celebrities, and 200 exclusive athletes; and over 2,000 retail locations, including its Lids retail stores. Our more than 22,000 employees are committed to relentlessly enhancing the fan experience and delighting sports fans globally.

About The Team

Launched in 2021, Fanatics Betting and Gaming is the online and retail sports betting subsidiary of Fanatics, a global digital sports platform. The Fanatics Sportsbook is available to 95% of the addressable online sports bettor market in the U.S. Fanatics Casino is currently available online in Michigan, New Jersey, Pennsylvania and West Virginia. Fanatics Betting and Gaming operates twenty-two retail sports betting locations, including the only sportsbook inside an NFL stadium at Northwest Stadium. Fanatics Betting and Gaming is headquartered in New York with offices in Denver, Leeds and Dublin.

We are seeking a Data Analyst III to join our growing data organization in the enterprise reporting team. This data analyst plays a key role in developing and maintaining enterprise reporting frameworks. This role focuses on report creation, data modeling, transformation, and validation while ensuring compliance with internal governance. This developer helps bridge the gap between data engineering and analytics, ensuring that high-quality, reliable data is available for decision-making. This developer plays a key role in creating executive dashboards and reports that clearly communicate KPIs and strategic insights to leadership.

About Fanatics Markets (FMX)

Fanatics Markets is the real-money prediction and trading app where you can invest in moments you care about. Built on a secure platform, we let users predict real-world outcomes and trade on events they actually follow - from sports and entertainment to political elections and beyond. Our mission is to redefine how fans engage with the moments and markets that matter most. We're looking for the right people to help us build the future of prediction markets.

Responsibilities


Design dashboards with a long-term mindset, ensuring they are optimized, scalable, and maintainable for evolving business needs.
Maintain and continuously update dashboards, ensuring data accuracy, performance, and alignment with evolving business needs.
Enhance and refresh existing BI assets, incorporating new data sources, business logic, and stakeholder feedback to keep reports relevant and actionable.
Assist in data governance activities, including metadata management and reporting documentation.
Design, build, and maintain scalable data models that support reporting, dashboards, and advanced analytics.
Support data modeling, transformation, and automation to improve reporting efficiency.
Assist in gathering and refining reporting requirements from finance, compliance, and business teams.
Conduct data validation, reconciliation, and quality control checks to maintain reporting integrity.
Develop processes to streamline data extraction, transformation, and reporting workflows.
Support automated reporting solutions to reduce manual effort and enhance reporting consistency.
Investigate and reconcile reporting variances across reports, ensuring consistency and accuracy of KPIs.
Work with stakeholders to identify root causes of discrepancies and implement fixes in data models or dashboards.
Generate and maintain reports on key business metrics for governance, financial, and operational performance.
Effectively communicate findings and insights to product managers, developers, and other stakeholders through clear reports and presentations.


Qualifications


3+ years of experience as a Data Analyst, Business Intelligence Analyst, or similar role.
Advanced SQL skills
Data modeling utilizing a tool such as DBT, including
Manage and optimize source code control in GitHub, leveraging branching strategies and best practices to ensure efficient collaboration and code quality
Experienced with building dashboards and reporting tools (e.g. Tableau, Sigma, Power BI, Looker).
Customer obsessed and experience focused; highly outcome-oriented and data-driven; experienced in working in high-growth environments.
Demonstrated ability to partner closely with stakeholders, earning trust and prioritizing relationships.
Logical and analytical approach to problem solving. Plus for utilizing AI in analytics.
Strong organizational and communication skills.
Positive and flexible attitude, a willingness to embrace new initiatives.
Understanding of sports betting or experience in customer behavior analysis is a significant plus.
Must be open to travel for team offsites and events.


Depending on the role, your interview and onboarding experience may include in-person components, such as onsite interviews or Launching into Better: LIVE—a multi-day cultural immersion in New York City for full-time, non-seasonal hires. These sessions are designed to build connection and bring our culture to life, though specific travel and participation requirements will be confirmed based on your role and location. Your recruiter will provide clear guidance at each stage of the process

For information about our benefits, please visit https://benefitsatfanatics.com/

By submitting your application, you agree to our terms of service and acknowledge you have read our Candidate Privacy Policy.

Title: Product Data Scientist (Data Labs)
Description:
At Similarweb, we are revolutionizing the way businesses interact with the digital world by revealing to them everything that happens online.

Our unique data and solutions empower over 5,700 customers globally, including industry giants like Google, Meta, and OpenAI, to make game-changing decisions that drive their digital strategies. In 2021, we went public on the New York Stock Exchange, and we continue to reach new heights! Come work alongside Similarwebbers across the globe who are bright, curious, practical, and good people.

About The Role

We’re looking for a sharp and driven Data Analyst to join our fast - paced Data Labs team. As part of a high-performing group of analysts, you’ll handle complex, custom requests from some of the biggest names in the market, turning raw data into strategic, ad hoc insights. The work is intense and constantly evolving - it requires strong analytical skills and the ability to work independently under pressure. You’ll need to be curious, adaptable, and willing to dive deep to find answers where others might stop. If you thrive in a demanding environment and are excited by the opportunity to make a real impact, we want you on our team.

What does the day-to-day of a Product Data Science (Data Labs) at Similarweb look like:


Building custom-made reports end to end - from meeting with the client and understanding their needs, to writing the code and setting up the report for ongoing delivery.
Apply your expertise in quantitative analysis and data mining to turn data into insights.
Conduct research and develop tools which will help answer client's’ business. questions by using Similarweb’s raw data sets and algorithms.
Partner with Engineering teams to establish an infrastructure to scale solutions.
Partner with Advisory Services team of Consultants to answer strategic business questions and deliver custom data products seamlessly


This Is The Perfect Job For Someone Who Has


At least 2 years experience with SQL/pySpark for data analysis - Must
Experience in Python scripting - Must
Excellent verbal and communication skills in English - Must
AWS Infrastructure experience - Big Advantage
Working with clients - Big Advantage.
Proficiency in Online Marketing/Online Services - Advantage
At least 2 years work experience in Online/Tech Company - Advantage
Team player, autodidact, fast learner with excellent analytical, research and business skills.
Demonstrates initiative and a proactive mindset -Must
Experienced in independently managing complex projects from start to finish.
Have a Bachelor's in Statistics, Computer Science, Engineering, or related field.
Master's degree - Big Advantage.


Why You’ll Love Being a Similarwebber

You’ll actually love the product you work with: Our customers aren’t our only raving fans. When we asked our employees why they chose to come work at Similarweb, 99% of them said “the product.” Imagine how exciting your job is when you get to work with the most powerful digital intelligence platform in the world.

You’ll find a home for your big ideas: We encourage an open dialogue and empower employees to bring their ideas to the table. You’ll find the resources you need to take initiative and create meaningful change within the organization.

We offer competitive perks & benefits: We take your well-being seriously, and offer competitive compensation packages to all employees. We also put a strong emphasis on community, with regular team outings and happy hours.

You can grow your career in any direction you choose: Interested in becoming a VP or want to transition into a different department? Whether it’s Career Week, personalized coaching, or our ongoing learning solutions, you’ll find all the tools and opportunities you need to develop your career right here.

Diversity isn’t just a buzzword: People want to work in a place where they can be themselves. We strive to create a workplace that is reflective of the communities we serve, where everyone is empowered to bring their full, authentic selves to work. We are committed to inclusivity across race, gender, ethnicity, culture, sexual orientation, age, religion, spirituality, identity and experience. We believe our culture of equality and mutual respect also helps us better understand and serve our customers in a world that is becoming more global, more diverse, and more digital every day.

We will handle your application and information related to your application in accordance with the Applicant Privacy Policy available here.

Diversity isn’t just a buzzword: People want to work in a place where they can be themselves. We strive to create a workplace that is reflective of the communities we serve, where everyone is empowered to bring their full, authentic selves to work. We are committed to inclusivity across race, gender, ethnicity, culture, sexual orientation, age, religion, spirituality, identity and experience. We believe our culture of equality and mutual respect also helps us better understand and serve our customers in a world that is becoming more global, more diverse, and more digital every day.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Job Description

Here at Rakuten Kobo Inc. we offer a casual working start-up environment and a group of friendly and talented individuals. Our employees rank us highly in terms of commitment to work/life balance. We realize that for our people to be innovative, creative and passionate they need to feel valued and supported. We believe in rewarding all our employees with competitive salaries, performance based annual bonuses, stock options and training opportunities.

If you’re looking for a company that inspires passion, personal, and professional growth – join Kobo and come help us on our mission of making reading lives better.

The Role

Rakuten Kobo Inc. is seeking an experienced Data Analyst to join our Data Storytelling & Insights team, working closely with our engineering foundations. This role blends strong technical ownership of analytics‑ready data with the ability to translate complex information into clear, actionable insights. You’ll work end‑to‑end across the analytics lifecycle—building scalable, trusted data models and partnering with stakeholders to deliver dashboards and analyses that inform key business decisions.

Key Responsibilities


Design, build, and maintain analytics‑ready data models and pipelines using Dataform and BigQuery, including migrating and modernizing legacy logic into clearer, more reusable, and better governed data assets.
Ensure analytics code and pipelines are reliable, well‑documented, and suited for downstream reporting, experimentation, and analysis.
Build and maintain Tableau dashboards and reports that are reusable, trustworthy, and grounded in centralized, well‑modeled data.
Translate ambiguous business questions into clear analyses that explain what happened, why it happened, and what to do next.
Partner with business stakeholders and analytics peers to define requirements, metrics, and scalable analytics solutions.
Contribute to shared analytics standards and best practices across SQL, Dataform, Tableau, and documentation.
Leverage modern AI tools and LLM‑based coding agents to accelerate analytics workflows (e.g., SQL development, modeling, debugging, documentation), while applying strong judgment to validate correctness, performance, and data quality.


The Skill Set

Must-haves


Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Data Science, or related field.
5+ years of experience in data analytics, analytics engineering, or BI, with demonstrated ownership of analytics data pipelines and models.
Strong SQL skills, including complex joins, window functions, and working with semi‑structured or nested data.
Solid understanding of data modeling and designing tables for downstream analytics and reporting use cases.
Strong analytical thinking, problem‑solving, and communication skills.
Experience working with modern cloud data warehouses (BigQuery preferred).
Hands‑on experience with Dataform (experience with dbt is a plus).
Experience with BI and visualization tools such as Tableau, Looker, or Domo.
Familiarity with analytics engineering best practices (e.g., testing, CI/CD for data).


Nice to haves


Experience using LLM‑based coding assistants to support analytics or data engineering workflows.
Working knowledge of Python for analysis, automation, or ML‑adjacent use cases.
Familiarity with cloud platforms (Google Cloud Platform preferred).
Experience with Git‑based version control and analytics workflow automation.


The Perks


Flexible hours and hybrid remote working environment
Subsidized in-office lunches
Four extended long weekends in the summer
Full benefits starting from your first day
Paid Volunteer days, unlimited sick days, and 3% RRSP matching
Work From Anywhere Program for up to 3 months (90 days)
Lifestyle spending account
Talent and development training budget
Free Kobo device + free weekly e-book or audiobook
Weekly Kobo Tech University sessions
Maternity/paternity leave top up
Dog friendly office


About Rakuten Kobo Inc.

Rakuten Kobo Inc. is the world’s digital bookseller created by and for booklovers. Owned by Tokyo-based Rakuten Group, Inc. and headquartered in Toronto, Rakuten Kobo’s millions of worldwide users can read anytime, anywhere, and on any device. With a mission to make reading lives better for all, Rakuten Kobo connects readers to stories using thoughtful and personalized curation of eBooks and audiobooks, and the best dedicated eReaders and apps for reading. With the singular focus of making reading lives the best they can be, Kobo’s open platform allows people to fit reading into more moments in their busy lives. To learn more about Rakuten Kobo, visit www.kobo.com.

Rakuten Kobo Inc. is an equal opportunity employer. Accessibility accommodations for candidates with disabilities participating in the selection process are available on request. Any information received related to accommodation needs of applicants will be addressed confidentially.  

Rakuten Kobo would like to thank all applicants for their interest in this role however only qualified candidates will be shortlisted.  

#RKIND

Five Principles for Success

Our worldwide practices describe specific behaviors that make Rakuten unique and united across the world. We expect Rakuten employees to model these 5 Shugi Principles of Success.

Always improve, Always Advance - Only be satisfied with complete success - Kaizen

Passionately Professional - Take an uncompromising approach to your work and be determined to be the best

Hypothesize - Practice - Validate – Shikumika - Use the Rakuten Cycle to succeed in unknown territory

Maximize Customer Satisfaction - The greatest satisfaction for our teams is seeing their customers smile

Speed!! Speed!! Speed!! - Always be conscious of time - take charge, set clear goals, and engage your team

Rakuten would like to thank all applicants for their interest in this role however only qualified candidates will be shortlisted.
