Title: Data Analyst
Description:
We’re searching for a Data Analyst. Are you searching for new possibilities?

Westland Insurance Group Ltd is growing and has an exciting opportunity for an Data Analyst to join our growing team. This hybrid role is based out of our Head Office in Surrey, BC.

Expected Compensation: The expected hiring range for this role is $62,400- $78,000 with a 37.5-hour work week. Your rate of pay will be determined based on your individual qualifications and experience, while considering internal equity. 

Westland also offers a competitive annual short-term incentive program that enables employees to share in the rewards of the business and emphasizes the measures that are key to our success.

Westland is committed to continuous learning where employees can grow their potential and write the stories of their careers. Financial assistance is available for approved work-related training/development courses leading to certification, and programs that offer professional development, including seminars and conferences.

Discover What’s Possible, With Westland.

The Westland story is all about growth, and that means plenty of possibility for everyone on our team. Every day, Westland proves that building a great business means taking care of clients, communities, and each other with equal commitment. As we continue to open new doors, we're inviting amazing people like you to join us.

As a Data Analyst, you’ll have the unique opportunity to lay the foundation for how Westland can leverage data to make important decisions.

Interested in learning more?

Why You'll Love Westland


An open, flexible and welcoming workplace
Plenty of opportunities to grow and learn
Autonomy to drive your own success
Be part of our inclusive culture, alongside an extraordinarily talented community of people with a wide variety of backgrounds
A total rewards program that takes care of your financial, physical, and mental health
Flexible paid Values Days to celebrate days important to you
Paid Volunteer Days for employees as part of Amplifying Communities, a program to support causes that matter most to our employees and clients


Why We’ll Love You


You strive to understand the data and love seeking ways to improve the quality of the data and reporting
You enjoy collaborating with others to identify and prioritize use cases for analysis and build out models to answer business questions
You love numbers and have a strong background in math, statistics or equivalent such as a bachelor’s degree in those areas or related fields
You can translate complex information and findings for business leaders
At least two years’ experience in a data analytics role
Previous experience with data analysis and modeling using SQL, R, and Python
Bonus: Experience in P&C insurance
Bonus: Experience using data platforms such as Databricks, Snowflake or Microsoft Fabric Bonus: Experience with Git based version control on platforms such as GitHub or Azure DevOps


Once Here, You'll


Be a contributing member of our inclusive culture, alongside an extraordinarily talented community of people with a wide variety of backgrounds.
Working with IT and others to design and improve the quality of our data and reporting by understanding current and future data analysis needs, business opportunities and risks.
Continuously seek out and identify areas to improve data reporting practices, data quality, and processes
Prepare and present findings to diverse audiences, including partners


Want to get to know each other better? Send your resume our way.

You belong here

We strive to be much more than a great place to work. Here, you join a collective of incredibly vibrant, purpose driven, and open-minded people. Every day, Westland proves that building a great business means taking care of communities, clients, and each other with equal commitment. You will be supported and respected for who you are and for the voice you add to the conversation.

At Westland, you will feel the power of community. Westland Insurance Ltd. is an inclusive organization that values diversity in its workforce. We listen, champion equity and diversity, and create safe welcoming spaces where everyone has a voice and is free to be themselves. Westland Insurance Ltd. encourages applications from all qualified individuals and will accommodate applicants' disability-related needs, up to the point of undue hardship, throughout all stages of the recruitment and selection process.

If you require a disability-related accommodation in order to participate in the recruitment process, please contact the recruitment team by email at careers@westlandinsurance.ca

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Company Description

UHN is Canada’s #1 hospital and the world’s #1 publicly funded hospital. With 10 sites and more than 44,000 TeamUHN members, UHN consists of Toronto General Hospital, Toronto Western Hospital, Princess Margaret Cancer Centre, Toronto Rehabilitation Institute, The Michener Institute of Education and West Park Healthcare Centre. As Canada's top research hospital, the scope of biomedical research and complexity of cases at UHN have made it a national and international source for discovery, education and patient care. UHN has the largest hospital-based research program in Canada, with major research in neurosciences, cardiology, transplantation, oncology, surgical innovation, infectious diseases, genomic medicine and rehabilitation medicine. UHN is a research hospital affiliated with the University of Toronto.

UHN’s vision is to build A Healthier World and it’s only because of the talented and dedicated people who work here that we are continually bringing that vision closer to reality.

www.uhn.ca

Job Description

Union: Non-Union

Replacement or New: New

Site: Hydro Place - 700 University Avenue

Department: Finance – Corporate Billing and Accounts Receivable

Reports to: Senior Manager, Finance

Hours: 37.5 per week

Salary: $68,932 to $103,408 annually (To commensurate with experience and consistent with UHN compensation policy)

Status: Permanent Full-Time

Closing Date: June 10, 2026

Position Summary

Reporting to the Senior Manager, Revenue within Corporate Billing and Accounts Receivable (CBAR), the Data Analyst supports revenue reporting with a focus on new revenue generation and identifying revenue leakage. The role is responsible for analyzing data, developing and maintaining reports and dashboards, and translating business requirements into actionable insights, while also supporting ad hoc reporting, stakeholder collaboration, and basic project coordination activities.

Duties


Analyze and interpret data to identify trends, risks, and opportunities related to revenue generation and leakage
Deliver actionable insights and business recommendations to support informed decision-making
Perform advanced data analysis using tools such as Excel, Power BI, SQL, and other query languages
Leverage AI tools and techniques to enhance analytical depth, forecasting, and insight generation
Collaborate with stakeholders to understand business needs and translate them into meaningful analytical outputs
Design and develop insightful reports and dashboards that highlight key performance indicators and drivers
Evaluate existing reports and data flows, recommending improvements to better support business objectives
Proactively identify revenue optimization opportunities and areas for process or financial improvement
Apply structured problem-solving and critical thinking to complex data and business challenges
Communicate findings clearly and effectively, providing regular updates and strategic input to stakeholders



Qualifications


Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Business Administration, or a recognized equivalent
Minimum 2-3 years of related experience, or an equivalent combination of education and experience
Experience in data management and analytics
Proficiency with data analysis tools such as Excel, Power BI, SQL, and other query languages
Strong analytical, problem-solving, and organizational skills
Excellent verbal and written communication skills
Ability to prioritize tasks and meet deadlines in a fast-paced environment



Additional Information

Why join UHN?

In addition to working alongside some of the most talented and inspiring healthcare professionals in the world, UHN offers a wide range of benefits, programs and perks. It is the comprehensiveness of these offerings that makes it a differentiating factor, allowing you to find value where it matters most to you, now and throughout your career at UHN.


Competitive offer packages
Government organization and a member of the Healthcare of Ontario Pension Plan (HOOPP https://hoopp.com/)
Close access to Transit and UHN shuttle service
A flexible work environment
Opportunities for development and promotions within a large organization
Additional perks (multiple corporate discounts including: travel, restaurants, parking, phone plans, auto insurance discounts, on-site gyms, etc.)



Current UHN employees must have successfully completed their probationary period, have a good employee record along with satisfactory attendance in accordance with UHN's attendance management program, to be eligible for consideration.

All applications must be submitted before the posting close date.

UHN uses email to communicate with selected candidates. Please ensure you check your email regularly.

Please be advised that a Criminal Record Check may be required of the successful candidate. Should it be determined that any information provided by a candidate be misleading, inaccurate or incorrect, UHN reserves the right to discontinue with the consideration of their application.

UHN is an equal opportunity employer committed to an inclusive recruitment process and workplace. Requests for accommodation can be made at any stage of the recruitment process. Applicants need to make their requirements known.

We thank all applicants for their interest, however, only those selected for further consideration will be contacted.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Introduction

A career in IBM Consulting is built on long-term client relationships and close collaboration worldwide. You’ll work with leading companies across industries, helping them shape their hybrid cloud and AI journeys. With support from our strategic partners, robust IBM technology, and Red Hat, you’ll have the tools to drive meaningful change and accelerate client impact. At IBM Consulting, curiosity fuels success. You’ll be encouraged to challenge the norm, explore new ideas, and create innovative solutions that deliver real results. Our culture of growth and empathy focuses on your long-term career development while valuing your unique skills and experiences.

Key Responsibilities

Your role and responsibilities


Collaborate with business stakeholders to perform requirements analysis and refine functional and technical specifications
Prepare Source-to-Target Mapping (STTM) documents with accurate data definitions, transformation logic, and mapping rules
Analyze, interpret, and validate data to support business needs and data engineering processes
Work closely with data engineers, architects, and business teams to ensure data quality and alignment with solution designs
Support ETL/ELT development processes by providing detailed mapping, data rules, and validation inputs
Participate in data model reviews and contribute to the design and improvement of data structures in the warehouse environment


Preferred Education

Master's Degree

Required Technical And Professional Expertise

Required Skills and Experience:


Strong proficiency in SQL, with hands-on experience using Snowflake
Solid understanding of data modeling techniques, including dimensional modeling, data vault modeling, and medallion architecture
Good knowledge of ETL/ELT concepts, cloud data platforms, and modern data warehouse practices
Experience working with structured and semi-structured data
Ability to document and communicate analytical findings and technical details clearly
Strong problem-solving skills and attention to detail
Ability to work collaboratively with cross-functional teams


Preferred Qualifications

Preferred technical and professional experience


Experience with Snowflake-specific features (e.g., stages, virtual warehouses, snowpipe)
Experience with data integration tools (e.g., dbt, Databricks, Azure Data Factory, Talend)
Knowledge of data governance and data quality frameworks


Preferred Knowledge Of Tools And Technology


ERWin
Snowflake Snowsight
Python Notebooks
Excel

Title: Business Data Analyst - Business Operations
Description:
Job Description

Business Data Analyst – Business Operations

About Toyota Financial Services

Toyota Financial Services (TFS) provides retail, leasing and dealer financing services to Toyota and Lexus dealerships and customers across Canada. TFS is a member of Toyota Financial Services Corporation (TFSC), a wholly owned subsidiary of Toyota Motor Corporation in Japan with the Canadian operation headquarters in Markham, Ontario

What Sets Us Apart?

At Toyota Financial Services (TFS), you will help create best in class customer and dealer experiences in a collaborative and innovative environment. TFS is a key part of the Toyota family, a global company known for its technological leadership, high quality products, continuous improvement, and environmental responsibility.

TFS currently has an exciting permanent opportunity as a Business Data Analyst – Business Operations in the Business Analytics Department. The position reports to the Business Analytics Supervisor and is based in our Markham Head Office.

In this role, you will provide dedicated analytics support to our Business Operations division, focusing on data-driven insights that improve operational efficiency, process effectiveness, and service delivery. The successful candidate will work closely with Business Operations leaders and teams to track key operational performance indicators and identify opportunities for improvement in areas such as process turnaround times, productivity, and customer service quality. This role will collaborate with other Business Analytics team members and the Senior Data and Insights Analyst to deliver timely insights and advanced reporting solutions and ensure alignment with enterprise data standards and strategy.

What You’ll Be Doing


Operational Performance Analysis
Analyze internal business operations data such as process cycle times, service level metrics, error rates, and productivity measures to identify bottlenecks, inefficiencies, and opportunities for improvement.
Provide insights that help optimize operational processes, enhance customer service delivery, and improve overall efficiency across departments.
KPI Monitoring & Reporting
Develop, maintain, and continuously improve Tableau dashboards and operational reports that track key Business Operations KPIs (e.g., loan/lease processing turnaround times, daily transaction volumes, customer support response times, and quality control metrics).
Ensure that operations managers have clear, up-to-date visibility into performance, against targets, and proactively flag emerging trends or issues (such as backlogs or processing delays) for prompt action.
Process Improvement Support
Work with the Business Operations/Operational Excellence teams on process-improvement initiatives and projects. For example, when a new system or workflow is implemented, measure pre- and post-implementation performance to quantify benefits (e.g., reduction in processing time or errors).
Conduct root-cause analyses of operational problems and provide data-driven recommendations for solutions (such as process changes, additional training, or resource adjustments).
Ad-Hoc Data Support
Serve as the go-to analytics resource for the Business Operations team.
Respond promptly to ad-hoc requests for data or analysis (e.g., providing analysis on delinquency ratio increase).
Deliver timely, well-explained insights to support business decisions.
Cross-Functional Analytics
Collaborate with the central Business Analytics team and other functions as needed.
Work under the mentorship of the Senior Data & Insights Analyst to incorporate advanced models or new data sources for complex analyses (for example, feeding Business Operational requirements into enterprise predictive models for fraud or credit loss analysis).
Ensure that all analyses and metrics for Business Operations are consistent with enterprise-wide standards.
Training & Data Enablement
Act as a data champion within Business Operations.
Help colleagues in Operations understand and utilize the available reports and data tools.
Train team members to interpret metrics and leverage self-service analytics (e.g., basic Tableau functions or SQL queries) for their daily management needs.
Promote a culture of continuous improvement and data-driven decision-making in Operational teams by making data accessible and understandable.
Cross‑Functional Coverage & Business Continuity
Provide backup analytical support and coverage for Sales & Marketing and other departments as needed, enabling consistent delivery of insights, operational reporting, and cross‑functional analytics support when required.


What You’ll Bring


Bachelor’s degree in Business, Operations Management, Data/Business Analytics, or a related field; strong foundation in quantitative analysis and process management preferred.
3-5 years of experience in data analysis, business intelligence, or operations analytics, with demonstrated support for operations or back‑office teams in process‑driven environments (e.g., financial services, automotive).
Experience in the financial services or captive finance industry (especially automotive finance) is preferred.
Proven experience analyzing process performance, productivity, service metrics, and operational KPIs, with familiarity in measuring and improving process‑oriented outcomes.
Strong proficiency in SQL and experience working with large datasets in data warehouse environments (e.g., Snowflake or similar).
Advanced Excel skills for analysis and ad‑hoc reporting.
Hands‑on experience developing and managing reports and dashboards using BI tools such as Tableau (preferred), ensuring a reliable single source of truth for operational data.
Solid understanding of operational metrics, SLAs, process mapping, and continuous improvement methodologies (PDCA, TPS, TBP); ability to identify trends, outliers, and root causes and translate findings into actionable recommendations.
Excellent communication skills with experience working cross‑functionally (Marketing, Sales, Finance, Risk, IT, Credit), presenting insights clearly to both technical and non‑technical stakeholders in fast‑paced environments.
Proactive, team‑oriented problem solver capable of managing multiple priorities, working independently when needed, and continuously learning new tools and analytical techniques.


What We’ll Bring


A work environment built on teamwork, flexibility, and respect
Competitive salary including bonus
Health and dental benefits effective immediately
Company pension plan with matched contribution
Associate vehicle discount program
Professional growth and development programs to help advance your career, as well as tuition reimbursement
Fitness reimbursement
Competitive time off benefits
Great company culture-Respect for people and continuous improvement is at our core
Hybrid work environment (based on role requirements)


The anticipated annual base salary for this position is $74,500 - 94,000. The offered salary will be based on the candidate’s experience, knowledge, skills and qualifications.

What You Should Know

Our success begins and ends with our people. We embrace diverse perspectives and value unique human experiences. We are proud to be an equal opportunity employer that celebrates the diversity of the communities where we live and do business. Applicants for our positions are considered without regard to race, ethnicity, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, age, disability, religion, or any other characteristics protected by law.

Title: ITSM Data & Reporting Analyst/Power BI
Description:
Are you ready to turn complex IT operations data into insights that improve service performance? Apply Now!

Working with one of our top financial clients, this role calls for an ITSM Data & Reporting Analyst/Power BI to transform ITSM and operational information into actionable reporting and analytics. You will design and evolve enterprise reporting solutions across Power BI, ServiceNow, and integrated data sources, enabling proactive, insight-driven operations. You’ll partner with technology, operations, and business stakeholders as a trusted advisor to shape reporting strategies that support critical banking services.

Pay rate range (CAD): $65.30/hr – $78.37/hr

Responsibilities


Design, develop, and maintain Power BI dashboards for ITSM performance, KPIs, and operational health
Integrate and analyse data across ServiceNow (Incident, Problem, Change, CMDB) and additional cloud, infrastructure, and application sources
Translate complex datasets into clear, executive-ready insights, highlighting trends, risks, and opportunities
Lead proactive trend analysis to identify systemic issues and continuous improvement opportunities
Gather and refine reporting requirements, prioritizing stakeholder needs and delivering scalable solutions
Define and maintain standard reporting frameworks, KPI definitions, and data models
Ensure data quality, governance, integrity, and documentation across all reporting solutions
Drive automation and continuous improvement initiatives to reduce manual effort and improve reporting efficiency
Facilitate cross-functional discussions to support data-informed decision-making


Desired Skill-Set


5 to 7+ years of experience in reporting, analytics, or business intelligence
5+ years of experience with Power BI development, including data modeling, DAX, and dashboard design
Strong understanding of ITIL frameworks and Site Reliability Engineering practices
Hands-on experience with ServiceNow ITSM data (Incident, Problem, Change, CMDB) and operational reporting
Ability to translate data into meaningful insights and executive narratives for business leadership
Analytical mindset with the ability to identify trends, patterns, and anomalies
Proven cross-functional experience working with technical and business stakeholders
Strong communication, facilitation, and documentation skills
Experience in IT environments, preferably within financial services
Bachelor’s degree required (Business, Finance, Engineering, Science, Mathematics, or related field preferred)


Nice to Have


Prior experience in financial services
Experience with business process mapping, requirements documentation, and solution design
Ability to influence across technical and business teams
High attention to detail with a focus on data accuracy and quality
Strong problem-solving skills and ability to manage competing priorities
Confidence working independently in a fast-paced, high-visibility environment
Creative problem-solving and solution proposal mindset

Title: Compliance Data and Business Intelligence Analyst
Description:
Are you passionate about protecting the environment? Do you go out of your way to reduce, reuse, and recycle? Do you envision a waste-free future?

If you answered yes to those questions, then we’d like to hear from you.

We are the Resource Productivity and Recovery Authority (RPRA), Ontario’s circular economy regulator.




Our vision is a waste-free and prosperous Ontario. And, our mission is to support waste reduction and resource recovery by enforcing compliance with Ontario’s producer responsibility laws, providing registry services to our regulated community, and reporting publicly on Ontario’s progress towards a circular economy.




We are looking for a talented and committed individual to join us as Compliance Data and Business Intelligence Analyst to support the government’s efforts to protect the environment and advance a new economy in which waste is reused, recycled, and reintegrated.




Job Summary




The Compliance Data & Business Intelligence Analyst supports the organization’s circular economy mandate by delivering high-quality analysis, analytics, reporting, and data stewardship across regulated resource recovery programs (including the Blue Box, tires, and all other RRCEA programs). You will need to have strong knowledge of compliance, policy, regulatory, and market frameworks, along with a deep understanding of waste management planning and operations across relevant sectors; specific knowledge of the Blue Box program is a strong asset. Using policy analysis and research methodologies, you will assess compliance trends, interpret regulatory requirements, and support evidence‑based decision‑making and program performance monitoring. Working closely with internal teams (e.g., Compliance, IT, Data Analytics, Finance) and external stakeholders (e.g., service providers, producers, the Ministry), you will ensure compliance data integrity, usability, and actionability in support of evidence-based decision-making, regulatory oversight, and compliance program performance monitoring.




Essential Duties and Responsibilities:




1) Data-Driven Compliance Decision Making

Develop processes and maintain regular compliance reporting, including the development of reporting procedures and policies, across regulated programs and operational areas, ensuring clarity, accuracy, and consistency.
Assist with cultivating data literacy in compliance staff, provide guidance and training regarding data tools and analytics; assess and address compliance data requests.
Produce deliverables such as operational performance summaries, risk assessment reports, and presentations that synthesize data trends, risks, and compliance recommendations.
Assist with the development and monitoring of KPIs and corporate performance measures to assess program effectiveness and identify continuous improvement opportunities.
Ability to support inspector workflows is required.




2) Data Stewardship & Quality Assurance

Act as a data steward for priority datasets, defining and maintaining:
Compliance data definitions and rules
Standardized reporting logic
Data quality controls and exception handling
Documentation and audit trails for repeatable reporting
Complete regular case data reviews, assessing case data against internal compliance procedures and policies and reporting on review outcomes to compliance leadership and in executive reports.
Support data governance activities including data request management, controlled access, and appropriate handling of sensitive or confidential information.
Support cross-functional projects such as registry improvements, public communications and reports, budgeting and fee setting, and systems/process enhancements.




3) Resource Recovery Program Analytics

Provide resource recovery data analytics support for all RRCEA programs, with a focus on Blue Box.
Support comparative analysis across jurisdictions (where relevant), and identify implications for program design, compliance approaches, or operational enhancements.
Engage with the Ministry, registrants and stakeholders to understand data needs and assess feasibility while considering RPRA’s Access and Privacy Code and resourcing.




4) Other Duties

Perform other related duties as assigned.




Job Qualifications




Education

Post-secondary education in business, data, finance, public policy, environmental studies, or other related disciplines or equivalent work experience.




Experience

At least 4 years’ progressive experience in business intelligence, data analytics, reporting, or program analytics (experience in regulated environments strongly preferred).
Demonstrable knowledge of EPR regulatory frameworks, municipal waste analysis, waste management or related sectors.
Prior compliance/regulatory experience is an asset.




Skills, Knowledge & Competencies

Sound knowledge of waste management planning and operations, both broadly and applicable to specific waste management sectors; specific knowledge in the Blue Box program an asset.
Sound knowledge of policy analysis and research methodology.
Strong ability to analyze and interpret quantitative and qualitative data to produce actionable recommendations.
Advanced proficiency working with: Excel, CRM data (Salesforce), Data visualizations.
Strong organization, prioritization, and multitasking skills in a complex, deadline-driven environment; thrives in a climate of change.
Strong written and verbal communication skills.
Knowledge and understanding of the RRCEA, WDTA, associated regulations and continued program and stewardship plans an asset.
Ability to understand and interpret financial and operational reports required under RPRA’s mandate.
High attention to detail and accuracy; strong judgment and discretion with confidential data.
Customer-service mindset and collaborative approach to stakeholder relationships.
Project coordination skills (planning, tracking, reporting, risk/issue logs).
Comfort working with compliance-focused data and risk analysis.
Ability and willingness to be trained and designated as an inspector under the RRCEA; compliance/regulatory experience is an asset.
Ability to communicate in French an asset.




Travel Requirements

Occasional travel within Ontario




Physical Demands

Office/Desk Job




Working with the Resource Productivity and Recovery Authority

You will be a part of a collaborative team doing ground-breaking and meaningful work with a critical environmental and economic mission.

This is a full-time permanent role, working Monday to Friday from 9:00 a.m. to 5:00 p.m., with flexibility as needed. Our highly attractive total compensation plan includes a competitive salary (commensurate with experience), health benefits, personal days, and three weeks of vacation to start.

We have a hybrid work arrangement and support flexible work schedules. Remote work is expected to be completed from a location within Ontario. Our office is conveniently located in North York on the Yonge subway line at Sheppard Avenue.

RPRA is committed to a professional, respectful work environment where employee safety and well-being are prioritized and harassment of any kind is not tolerated.

We strive to build a team that reflects the diversity of the community we work in and encourage applications from traditionally underrepresented groups such as women, visible minorities, Indigenous peoples, people identifying as LGBTQ2SI, veterans, and people with disabilities.

RPRA welcomes and encourages applications from people with disabilities. Accommodations are available on request for candidates taking part in all aspects of the selection process.

The Resource Productivity and Recovery Authority utilizes artificial intelligence (AI) to screen and assess applicants for this position based on pre-defined, job-related criteria. AI acts as a supporting tool, but all final decisions are made by human HR professionals and hiring managers.




Target salary range: Min: $$84,222 - $105,278




How to apply

Submit your cover letter and resume (Name_JobTitle_2026) with the Subject Line: Name -JobTitle - 2026 at: careers@rpra.ca

Title: Data Analyst 2.0 (Operations & Performance)
Description:
About The Opportunity

Our client is looking for a skilled and driven Operations Analyst to support data-driven decision-making and operational performance across a complex, high-volume environment.

This position is ideal for a candidate who enjoys working with large data sets, building dashboards, uncovering trends, and influencing business outcomes through reporting and analytics. You will play a key role in both day-to-day operations and strategic improvement initiatives , while collaborating closely with cross-functional teams.

What You’ll Be Responsible For

Operational Support & Data Management


Coordinate and execute daily operational transactions, including account updates, billing activities, and exception handling
Oversee and validate large volumes of data, ensuring accuracy and consistency across multiple systems
Proactively identify and correct data discrepancies and lead clean-up efforts
Act as a subject matter resource for internal teams requiring support on operational or account-related inquiries
Help maintain compliance with internal processes related to contracts and customer records



Analytics & Reporting


Create and maintain recurring and ad hoc reports to monitor operational performance
Interpret complex data sets (100K+ records) to identify trends, risks, and opportunities
Develop insights that support process optimization and business strategy
Present findings and recommendations to stakeholders, including leadership teams
Simplify technical outputs into clear, digestible summaries for non-technical audiences



Process Improvement & Projects


Contribute to continuous improvement initiatives aimed at enhancing workflows and system efficiency
Support system issue resolution by working with internal technology teams and vendors
Participate in cross-functional projects, either taking ownership or supporting delivery
Help define requirements for enhancements to systems, tools, or processes



What You Bring


Bachelor’s degree in Business, Finance, Engineering, Computer Science, or a related field
5+ years of experience in an operations, analytics, or data-focused role
Experience working in high-volume, process-driven environments (regulated industries are an asset)
Advanced to expert proficiency in MS Excel
Strong working knowledge of Power BI and Power Query
Proven experience handling and analyzing large datasets



Key Skills & Attributes


Strong analytical mindset with the ability to translate data into actionable insights
Confident communicator with the ability to present to management and executive audiences
Ability to explain technical concepts clearly to non-technical stakeholders
Highly organized with strong multitasking and prioritization skills
Comfortable working both independently and collaboratively
Flexible in taking on leadership responsibilities or supporting team initiatives



Why This Role?


Long-term career opportunity with growth potential
Exposure to cross-functional teams and business strategy
Opportunity to make a measurable impact through data and process improvement
Collaborative and team-oriented environment



Job Type & Location

This is a Permanent position based out of Toronto, ON.

Pay And Benefits

The pay range for this position is $75000.00 - $85000.00/yr.

Workplace Type

This is a hybrid position in Toronto,ON.

Title: Data Analytics Associate Co-op
Description:
We’re building a relationship-oriented bank for the modern world. We need talented, passionate professionals who are dedicated to doing what’s right for our clients.

At CIBC, we embrace your strengths and your ambitions, so you are empowered at work. Our team members have what they need to make a meaningful impact and are truly valued for who they are and what they contribute.

To learn more about CIBC, please visit CIBC.com

What You'll Be Doing

Join CIBC’s Business Banking Product Analysis and Insight team, where a diverse group of analytics professionals come together to support multiple areas across the bank. As a Data Analytics Associate, you’ll contribute to data transformation and analytics projects that impact products such as lending, mortgages, wealth management, Wood Gundy, and private banking. You’ll work closely with business stakeholders to understand their requirements and deliver actionable insights. This role is highly collaborative, involving cross-functional efforts with peers, directors, and independent business units. You’ll have the opportunity to learn from experienced leaders. If you enjoy working in a dynamic environment, solving complex business challenges, and networking across teams, this is the place for you.

At CIBC we enable the work environment most optimal for you to thrive in your role. You'll have the flexibility to manage your work activities within a hybrid work arrangement where you'll spend core days on-site, while other days will be remote.

How You'll Succeed

Data Analytics & Transformation – Analyze and transform data to support business objectives across lending, mortgages, wealth management, and commercial banking.

Stakeholder Engagement – Collaborate with business partners and stakeholders to understand requirements and deliver tailored solutions.

Reporting & Presentation – Export data and insights into Excel and PowerPoint, creating clear and impactful presentations for internal audiences.

Collaboration & Teamwork – Work closely with peers, directors, and independent business units, contributing to a culture of shared success.

Continuous Learning – Gain exposure to different coding styles and data sets, and learn directly from hands-on leaders who mentor and support your growth.

Who You Are

You’re a strong communicator. You can clearly articulate complex ideas and insights to both technical and non-technical audiences.

You have relevant technical skills. You bring proficiency in SQL, Excel, and MS Office, with experience in exporting and presenting data. Python or other coding experience, SaaS tools, and business intelligence reporting are assets.

You’re detail-oriented. You notice patterns, inconsistencies, and opportunities within large datasets, ensuring accuracy and quality in your work.

You thrive in collaborative environments. You enjoy working as part of a team and networking across different groups within the bank.

You’re self-managed and adaptable. You take initiative, manage your own tasks, and embrace new challenges with flexibility.

Values matter to you. You bring your real self to work, and you live our values - trust, teamwork, and accountability.

What CIBC Offers

At CIBC, your goals are a priority. We start with your strengths and ambitions as an employee and strive to create opportunities to tap into your potential.


We work to recognize you in meaningful, personalized ways including a competitive compensation, a banking benefit*, wellbeing support and additional offers such as employee and family assistance programs and MomentMakers, our social, points-based recognition program.
Our spaces and technological toolkit will make it simple to bring together great minds to create innovative solutions that make a difference for our clients.
Subject to program terms and conditions


What You Need To Know


CIBC is committed to creating an inclusive environment where all team members and clients feel like they belong. We seek applicants with a wide range of abilities and we provide an accessible candidate experience. If you need accommodation, please contact Mailbox.careers-carrieres@cibc.com

We’re building a relationship-oriented bank for the modern world. We need talented, passionate professionals who are dedicated to doing what’s right for our clients.

At CIBC, we embrace your strengths and your ambitions, so you are empowered at work. Our team members have what they need to make a meaningful impact and are truly valued for who they are and what they contribute.

To learn more about CIBC, please visit CIBC.com

What You'll Be Doing

Join CIBC’s Business Banking Product Analysis and Insight team, where a diverse group of analytics professionals come together to support multiple areas across the bank. As a Data Analytics Associate, you’ll contribute to data transformation and analytics projects that impact products such as lending, mortgages, wealth management, Wood Gundy, and private banking. You’ll work closely with business stakeholders to understand their requirements and deliver actionable insights. This role is highly collaborative, involving cross-functional efforts with peers, directors, and independent business units. You’ll have the opportunity to learn from experienced leaders. If you enjoy working in a dynamic environment, solving complex business challenges, and networking across teams, this is the place for you.

At CIBC we enable the work environment most optimal for you to thrive in your role. You'll have the flexibility to manage your work activities within a hybrid work arrangement where you'll spend core days on-site, while other days will be remote.

How You'll Succeed

Data Analytics & Transformation – Analyze and transform data to support business objectives across lending, mortgages, wealth management, and commercial banking.

Stakeholder Engagement – Collaborate with business partners and stakeholders to understand requirements and deliver tailored solutions.

Reporting & Presentation – Export data and insights into Excel and PowerPoint, creating clear and impactful presentations for internal audiences.

Collaboration & Teamwork – Work closely with peers, directors, and independent business units, contributing to a culture of shared success.

Continuous Learning – Gain exposure to different coding styles and data sets, and learn directly from hands-on leaders who mentor and support your growth.

Who You Are

You’re a strong communicator. You can clearly articulate complex ideas and insights to both technical and non-technical audiences.

You have relevant technical skills. You bring proficiency in SQL, Excel, and MS Office, with experience in exporting and presenting data. Python or other coding experience, SaaS tools, and business intelligence reporting are assets.

You’re detail-oriented. You notice patterns, inconsistencies, and opportunities within large datasets, ensuring accuracy and quality in your work.

You thrive in collaborative environments. You enjoy working as part of a team and networking across different groups within the bank.

You’re self-managed and adaptable. You take initiative, manage your own tasks, and embrace new challenges with flexibility.

Values matter to you. You bring your real self to work, and you live our values - trust, teamwork, and accountability.

What CIBC Offers

At CIBC, your goals are a priority. We start with your strengths and ambitions as an employee and strive to create opportunities to tap into your potential.

  • We work to recognize you in meaningful, personalized ways including a competitive compensation, a banking benefit*, wellbeing support and additional offers such as employee and family assistance programs and MomentMakers, our social, points-based recognition program.
  • Our spaces and technological toolkit will make it simple to bring together great minds to create innovative solutions that make a difference for our clients.
  • Subject to program terms and conditions

Title: Co-Op Student - Data Analytics & AI
Description:
About MealSuite

MealSuite builds end-to-end foodservice technology for healthcare and senior living organizations. Our mission is to help care teams deliver better dining experiences with less effort. We're a fast-growing team working on meaningful problems that directly impact patient and resident care.

About The Role

We're looking for a Data Analytics & AI Co-Op Student to join our Data Analytics & AI team. This position will be reporting to the Director, Data Analytics & AI, and is for the September 2026 - December 2026 co-op term.

What You'll Do


Conduct UAT testing for the HR7 data transfer project; document test results, defects, and check sign-off outcomes
Coordinate and launch a mini AI accelerator case; support scoping, stakeholder engagement, and progress tracking
Coordinate and gather workflow information from AI business cases; identify and document process bottlenecks and improvement opportunities
Assist in the delivery of AI training programs; support content preparation, session logistics, and participant coordination


You'll Also Work on the Following Projects:


AI Business Case Workflow Analysis - coordinate with stakeholders, gather workflow data, and deliver bottleneck analysis report (approximately 4 months to complete)


What You Bring


Current enrollment in a post-secondary program with a Canadian institution in Business, Finance, Information Technology, Human Resources, or related field
Basic understanding of AI concepts
Experience with digital productivity tools (Microsoft 365, Google Workspace, or similar)
Clear and concise communication skills with the ability to convey ideas effectively in both written and verbal form
Active listening skills, demonstrating attention to stakeholder needs, the ability to ask clarifying questions, and confirm understanding before acting
The ability to build relationships across different business functions and show a team-oriented attitude
Proactive communication and willingness to flag issues early, provide regular progress updates, and the confidence to escalate blockers without prompting
Ability to thrive in a collaborative, fast-moving environment
Ability to thrive in a collaborative, fast-moving environment


Compensation: $21.67 - $31.53 CAD per hour (as determined by work term)

This role requires working onsite in our Cambridge office (96 Grand Ave S Unit 101, Cambridge, ON N1S 2L9) two days per week.

Why You'll Love Working Here  


Hybrid flexibility - we value the collaboration, mentorship and learning that come from physically working next to one another, as well as the benefits that remote work can offer.  
Work-life balance - this is supported by the fact that more than 90% of current employees agree that their leader supports their wellbeing.  
Opportunities for career development and advancement - we support our employees in pursuing and achieving their professional goals.  
Purposeful work with a positive community impact - more than 90% of our North American employees agree that the company's purpose aligns with their personal values. Learn more about our values at  mealsuite.com/careers.


We want to ensure that every qualified individual has an equal opportunity to work with us. If you require accommodation to our application process, please contact accommodations@mealsuite.com. 

MealSuite uses AI-assisted tools during parts of the hiring process, including screening and workflow automation. All final hiring decisions are made by people.

This is a current vacancy, and we are actively hiring for this position.

Title: Analyst-Ops Intelligence and Performance (Contact Centre)
Description:
This position requires in-person collaboration up to five days a week. This arrangement is subject to change based on business requirements.

Why WestJet

Since our story took off in 1996, WestJet has strived to enrich the lives of everyone in our world; a career with us is no exception. We’re proud to be one of Canada’s most admired and respected corporate cultures and continuously aim for the highest standards of service and success.

Join us, this is where your career story takes off.

Analyst-Ops Intelligence and Performance (Contact Centre)


This is a hybrid position **


Overview Of The Role

WestJet’s Contact Centre is evolving toward a more intelligent, insight-driven operating model. As part of this transformation, the Operational Intelligence & Performance Analyst will play a key role in shifting the organization from reactive reporting to proactive, context-rich decision support.

This role serves as a centralized operational intelligence function—helping leaders understand what is happening, why it’s happening, and what actions to take next. By connecting insights across performance, quality, workforce, and operational conditions, you will enable faster, more consistent, and more effective decision-making.

Reporting to the Manager, Operational Support, you will collaborate closely with cross-functional teams to synthesize insights, identify risks and opportunities, and improve operational effectiveness across the Contact Centre.

Key Responsibilities

Operational Insights & Decision Support


Translate complex, multi-source data into clear, actionable insights for leaders
Provide context on performance trends, risks, and drivers to support decision-making
Deliver leader-facing insight summaries to guide focus, coaching, and prioritization
Proactively identify performance gaps, deterioration patterns, and recovery opportunities


Performance Enablement


Support leaders in identifying coaching priorities and operational interventions
Enable more effective leadership routines (e.g., 1:1s, performance reviews) through structured insights
Contribute to “next best action” frameworks aligned to KPIs and operational conditions


Reporting & Data Visualization


Design and maintain Power BI dashboards that integrate metrics with operational context
Evolve reporting from static dashboards to insight-driven decision tools
Strengthen KPI consistency, data definitions, and reporting standards across the organization


Standardization & Continuous Improvement


Help establish standardized performance interpretation and operational health indicators
Track interventions and outcomes to build organizational insight and consistency
Support the development of scalable approaches to identifying risks, trends, and opportunities


Cross-Functional Intelligence


Partner with Operations, WFM, Quality, and Program teams to connect insights across the service ecosystem
Surface trends, friction points, and opportunities to inform broader operational and strategic improvements


Experience & Qualifications


Bachelor’s degree in Information Technology, Computer Science, Engineering, or equivalent experience
3+ years of experience in an analytical role supporting operational decision-making
Strong experience building dashboards in Power BI


Technical Skills


Advanced SQL proficiency (required)
Knowledge of data warehousing, OLAP, and ETL frameworks
Experience working with Snowflake
Experience with tools/languages such as Alteryx, Python, R, or VBA (asset)


Core Capabilities


Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with the ability to connect data to business context
Ability to synthesize insights across multiple domains (performance, quality, workforce)
Excellent communication skills with experience presenting to diverse audiences
Proactive, curious, and comfortable working in evolving environments


Assets


Airline and/or Contact Centre experience
Understanding of Data Science or AI concepts


The Benefits Of Being a WestJetter

WestJet provides all WestJetters with a competitive total rewards package. On top of that, we offer:


A fun and friendly culture with colleagues who work together to win
Travel privileges for you and your family, effective from your start date
Savings and Benefit programs that are flexible to meet your specific needs


Think we’re a great fit? Apply now!

About WestJet Group Of Companies

WestJet took to the skies in 1996 with just over 200 employees and three aircraft operating service to five destinations. Since then, WestJet has pioneered low-cost travel in Canada, cutting airfares in half, and increasing the flying population in Canada by more than 50 per cent. Since the Sunwing Airlines integration in 2025, more than 15,000 WestJetters support nearly 200 aircraft and connect guests to our global network of more than 100 destinations across North America, Central America, the Caribbean, Europe and Asia. The WestJet Group is a major Canadian employer and includes WestJet Airlines, WestJet Encore and Sunwing Vacations Group. We’re Canada’s leading value airline and largest vacation provider, with a united purpose of providing affordable and accessible air and vacation travel to Canadians.

Our safety promise

At WestJet, the safety and security of our people and our guests is a core value and at the heart of what we do. Safety and security are shared responsibilities all team members are expected to use safe work practices to ensure their well-being and the safety of others. WestJet recognizes that the use of Alcohol and Drugs can adversely impact a safe work environment and the well-being of others including guests, suppliers and the public, as well as place WestJet's operations at risk. All roles that are identified as Safey-Sensitive are required to pass a Pre-Employment Alcohol and Drug Test as per WestJet's Alcohol and Drug Policy. 

Our commitment to diversity, equity and inclusion

We embrace what makes each of us unique, and what makes us uniquely WestJet. We welcome diverse skills, experiences and backgrounds so if you're passionate about this role, we encourage you to apply – even if you don't meet every qualification listed. WestJet is committed to inclusiveness, equity and accessibility throughout the selection process. If you require accommodation at any stage, please contact our Talent Acquisition team at talent@westjet.com.

We value the time and effort you put into your application. Our recruitment process is highly competitive and while every applicant will receive communication regarding their application status, only those selected to move forward will be contacted with next steps. If this role isn’t the right fit, we encourage you to explore future opportunities with us.

About

For more information about everything WestJet, please westjet.com/aboutus.

If you wish to withdraw your application or request that your personal information be deleted, please contact us at talent@westjet.com.

Title: Data Analyst - Fall 2026 Co-op/Intern
Description:
Job Description

YOUR NEXT CO-OP PLACEMENT

Work term: Summer/Fall 2026

Work term length: 4 - 8 months (Flexible)

From classroom to co-op to career, every step of your journey has an impact on your future. That’s why it makes sense to choose a co-op placement with a company that offers truly rewarding work and real-life experience. At Definity®, your contributions, creativity, and energy will be put to good use, and you’ll get to be involved with initiatives that support our strategic goals and use your skills in a supportive team environment.

If you’re looking for a company that takes care of its people — full time, part time, and co-ops alike — and has a track record of doing big things, get ready to love it here.

What can you expect in this role?

Reporting to the Director, Data management, you will be part of an innovative and dynamic team that translates business needs and opportunities into data-driven solutions for the Commercial Insurance business.

This role is designed to provide hands-on experience in data analytics, product development, and collaboration with business stakeholders, while supporting the creation of scalable data products on cloud platforms.

 What You’ll Be Doing


Support the design and development of data products for Commercial Insurance
Perform exploratory data analysis using SQL, Python, or similar tools
Contribute to data documentation, data quality, data process controls and governance activities.
Participate in requirement gathering sessions and help document business requirements
Learn and apply modern data tools, analytics practices, and Agile methodologies


What You Bring


Currently enrolled in a post-secondary program in Computer Science, Data Analytics, Mathematics, Actuarial Science, Engineering, or related field
Must have at least intermediate knowledge of SQL and/or Python (academic or work experience)
Knowledge of GCP platform, BigQuery, JIRA, GIT tools and prior experience in P&C insurance is an asset.
Strong analytical thinking and problem-solving skills
Interest in data analytics, data science, or data engineering
Strong communication skills and ability to collaborate with diverse teams


What’s in it for You?


Hands-on experience working on real-world data and business problems
Exposure to cloud-based data platforms and modern analytics tools
Opportunity to learn from experienced professionals in a collaborative team environment
Experience working in an Agile delivery model
Build practical skills in data analysis, visualization, and product development


Did you know?


We care about our co-ops: Definity’s co-op students are considered the best pipeline of talent for our full-time, permanent positions.
We care about students: Definity distributes $85,000 in scholarships to students across Canada each year.
We care about our community: Definity donates more than $1 million every year to causes that drive true change in the neighbourhoods in which we live and work.


What can Definity offer you?


Meaningful work forwarding our purpose of building a better world by helping our clients and communities adapt and thrive in their hardest times of need.
Culture of accountability, empowerment, and collaboration.
Opportunities to support personal and professional growth through real-life experience and unique learning opportunities. Our Leadership Essentials program builds knowledge and skills to Team Leaders to Directors
Opportunity to join our employee groups - focused on women's empowerment, LGBTQ+, anti-racism and cultural equity, and environmental activism
Determined to create positive change for our environment, our people, and our communities, now and in the future. Check our ESG work !


About Us

This position is being posted to fill an existing vacancy.

The hourly compensation for the role starts at $22.50 per hour and will increase based on factors including but not limited to, skills, education, experience, and the recommended rates as set by the respective educational institution.

We also take potential into consideration. If you don’t have this exact experience, but you know you have what it takes, be sure to give us more insight through your application and cover letter.

Our inclusive work environment welcomes diversity and supports accessibility. If you require accommodation at any time during the recruitment process, please let us know by contacting accessibility@economical.com.

Background checks

This role requires successful clearance of a background check (including criminal checks and leadership references).

About The Team

Definity is the parent company to some of Canada’s most long-standing and innovative insurance brands, including Economical Insurance, Sonnet Insurance, Family Insurance Solutions, and Petline Insurance. Our ambition is to be one of Canada’s leading and most innovative property and casualty insurers. We can’t do that without our people, so we embrace and encourage a culture that’s collaborative, ambitious, rewarding, and empowering.

We offer a flexible, hybrid work experience where employees work from the office and virtually depending on the type of work they are doing and who they are working with. Bring your true self and be a part of our journey. It’s better here.

Title: Data Engineer
Description:
Company: Parts Avatar

Location: Downtown Montreal, Quebec (Hybrid: 3 days in office, 2 days remote)

Employment Type: Full-time




About Parts Avatar

Parts Avatar approaches the entire automotive repair and aftermarket industry through a rigorous scientific and technological lens, positioning our e-commerce platform as just one core solution alongside a broader ecosystem of engineering innovations. We operate fundamentally as a technology enterprise, powering our mission through completely distinct, highly specialized Software Engineering and Data Science teams. These separate units dedicate themselves to mastering complex industrial, supply chain, and operational challenges, collaborating to pioneer smart, data-driven solutions that optimize workflows and completely redefine the automotive landscape. Join our growing team in a dynamic, deeply analytical, and innovative environment!




Job Summary

We are seeking a Python-focused Data Engineer to bridge the gap between data infrastructure and data science. In this role, you will lead the standardization and optimization of our data pipelines, specifically built for data science and machine learning projects. You will introduce modern technology stacks and MLOps practices to ensure our models are robust, scalable, and efficiently deployed into production environments.




Key Responsibilities

Work on the company's data pipeline, contributing heavily to its standardization, development, and optimization.

Build clean Python abstractions and automated pipelines to optimize data ingestion and preprocessing for analytics and machine learning.

Enforce rigorous data quality initiatives, testing, and validations to ensure data integrity across upstream and downstream systems.

Collaborate with data scientists to architect, package, configure, and deploy machine learning models using MLOps frameworks.

Evaluate and integrate new technologies to leverage and scale our current data science engineering stack.

Generate actionable insights for business and infrastructure improvements.




Qualifications

Education

Degree required in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or a related quantitative discipline.




Technical Skills

Expertise in Python development and comprehensive experience with data-handling packages (e.g., Pandas/Polars, NumPy).

Fluency in advanced SQL for complex data query logic.

Familiarity with cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, or Google Cloud) and Big Data frameworks.

Robust familiarity with MLOps workflows and automation.

Proficiency with Git version control and the Atlassian suite (Jira, Confluence).




Soft Skills

Curiosity and a strong desire to learn and grow.

Ability to take initiative, work independently, and collaborate effectively within a cross-functional squad.

Excellent multitasking capabilities.




Work Authorization

Must be eligible to work in Canada.




Language Requirements

Proficiency in English (spoken and written) is required, as English is the primary working language across teams in Ontario and Quebec. French is considered an asset.




Benefits

Competitive salary.

Performance bonus.

Comprehensive insurance plan.

Hybrid work schedule (3 days in office, 2 days work from home).

Opportunity to work with a dynamic and growing e-commerce leader.

Collaborative and supportive team environment.

Title: Category Insights Analyst (Open to New Graduates)
Description:
All About Wonderbrands

Wonderbrands, is a leading Canadian bakery with 140 years of experience in baking packaged breads. We are the makers of Wonder Bread, Country Harvest, D’Italiano, Gadoua, and Casa Mendosa. We have a network of 14 Bakery Manufacturing facilities across Canada.

Our Next Gen Talent Program Vision and Mission:

Empowering the Next Generation to create future leaders

The Wonderbrands Next Gen Talent Program supports high‑potential recent graduates as they develop into the next generation of leaders. At Wonderbrands, we believe in home-grown talent and cultivating an entrepreneurial, innovative culture where curiosity, bold thinking, and continuous improvement are embedded in everything we do.

Come join us on our journey to become the world’s great baker!

What Wonderbrands Offers:


We believe in Home Grown Talent, accelerated career growth with leadership training à Unleashing Your Potential
Many of our team members who joined us through similar “new graduate” programs were able to accelerate their career growth and reach high levels in our organization within a few years of joining us
Competitive compensation, health benefits, and a generous flexible medical/health spending account
Employer matching pension program
Tuition reimbursement
Discount program that covers almost everything under the sun - restaurants, gyms, shopping etc.



Summary

The Category Insights Analyst is responsible for transforming consumer, customer, market, and category data into actionable business insights that support growth, improve decision-making, and drive business performance.

This role plays an important part in helping the organization understand category trends, customer opportunities, pricing strategies, promotional effectiveness, and shelf optimization. Working closely with Sales, Marketing, Finance, and Revenue Growth Management teams, the successful candidate will analyze data, identify opportunities, and help translate insights into actions that create value for customers and consumers.

This is a Next Gen opportunity designed for individuals who are early in their careers and looking to build a strong foundation in commercial analytics, category management, and business strategy. We are looking for people with exceptional curiosity, critical thinking skills, and a passion for solving business problems. Prior industry experience is not required. Successful candidates will work alongside experienced leaders and subject matter experts who will provide mentorship, coaching, and hands-on development to help build a long-term career path.

Location: 1425 The Queensway, Etobicoke, Ontario

Primary Responsibilities Category Analysis & Storytelling


Analyze shipment, consumption, retail, customer, and market data to identify trends, opportunities, and business insights.
Transform complex data into clear, compelling stories and recommendations that support business decision-making.
Partner with Sales, Marketing, Finance, and other stakeholders to ensure insights are incorporated into business plans and actions.
Monitor category performance, market share, pricing, promotional activity, and distribution trends.
Identify emerging consumer behaviors, unmet needs, and growth opportunities.



Pricing & Promotional Analysis


Support the evaluation of pricing and promotional strategies to identify opportunities to improve business performance.
Analyze promotional effectiveness, including frequency, depth, and return on investment.
Assist with scenario analysis and business modeling to assess potential impacts of pricing and commercial decisions.
Support initiatives that optimize revenue, profitability, and market competitiveness.



Customer & Category Support


Support customer business reviews, annual planning processes, and sales presentations through data-driven insights and recommendations.
Partner with Sales and Marketing teams to identify assortment opportunities, distribution gaps, and growth initiatives.
Develop customer-specific analyses to support key retail partners.
Conduct ad hoc analyses to answer important business questions and support strategic initiatives.



Planogram & Shelf Optimization


Support the development and analysis of planograms to improve shelf productivity, assortment effectiveness, and product visibility.
Evaluate shelf performance using sales, velocity, distribution, and space allocation metrics.
Assist with assortment optimization, category reviews, seasonal transitions, and new product introductions.
Identify opportunities to improve category performance through data-driven shelf and assortment recommendations.



Data Analysis & Reporting


Develop and maintain recurring reports, dashboards, and performance scorecards.
Analyze sales, shipment, retail scan, consumer panel, and customer data to assess business performance.
Conduct root-cause analysis to understand performance drivers and identify actionable recommendations.
Continuously seek opportunities to improve reporting processes, analytical capabilities, and business insights.



What We're Looking For

We are seeking individuals who enjoy solving problems, working with data, and finding opportunities hidden within complex information. If you are intellectually curious, highly analytical, and excited about learning, we encourage you to apply—even if your experience does not perfectly match every item listed below.

Required Qualifications


Bachelor's degree in Business, Economics, Statistics, Mathematics, Engineering, Finance, Data Analytics, or a related field.
Strong analytical and critical thinking skills with the ability to identify patterns, evaluate information, and draw logical conclusions.
Demonstrated ability to work with data and translate findings into clear recommendations and actions.
Strong problem-solving capabilities and intellectual curiosity.
Excellent written, verbal, and presentation skills.
Strong attention to detail and commitment to delivering high-quality work.
Proficiency with Microsoft Excel and a willingness to learn new analytical tools and technologies.
Ability to manage multiple priorities and work effectively in a collaborative environment.



Nice to Have


Exposure to consumer packaged goods (CPG), retail, category management, sales, marketing, finance, or business analytics.
Experience working with large datasets through academic projects, internships, co-op placements, research, case competitions, or previous employment.
Familiarity with syndicated data sources such as NielsenIQ, Numerator, Circana, or retailer data.
Exposure to Power BI, Tableau, or similar visualization tools.
Exposure to planogram, assortment, or space management software.
Knowledge of pricing, promotions, revenue growth management, or category management concepts.



Most importantly, we are looking for individuals who demonstrate learning agility, curiosity, strong reasoning skills, and a desire to grow their careers.

FGF Group Competencies:

Adaptive Intelligence - Team Members respond effectively to challenges by evolving their skills, mindset, and approach.


Flexibility, Agility, Adaptability: Thrives in dynamic environments by embracing continuous change. Quickly adjusts to new priorities and actively seeks innovative solutions. Uses setbacks as opportunities for growth
and applies a relentless focus on improving outcomes. Approaches challenges with a proactive, solutions-driven mindset and openness to feedback.
Calculated Risk-Taking: Makes strategic decisions by evaluating options and creating contingency plans.



Collaborative Intelligence - Collaborative intelligence empowers teams to unlock collective strengths and achieve results through synergy and mutual trust.


Team First and Collaboration: Engages with Team Members and colleagues, builds relationships, and fosters an inclusive, innovative environment.



Entrepreneurial Intelligence - Equips Team Members to identify opportunities, overcome setbacks, and move ideas to execution effectively.


Continuous Improvement: Drives innovation and optimizes processes to enhance quality and productivity.
Customer and Product Focus: Addresses need proactively with accountability and a solution-oriented mindset.
Fierce Execution: Achieves results with a strategic and hands-on approach, learning from mistakes and building trust.



Leadership Competencies

Inspire & Energize Team Members


Continually inspires and develops Team Members and promote cross-functional collaboration.
Supports, shared knowledge and experience with your Team through authentic conversations and development planning
Communicates clear expectations
Uses insight and knowledge to influence others
Understands and acknowledge differences and modifies style to support unleashing potential
Demonstrates patience and empathy, while listening



Create Winning Relationships


Inspire enthusiasm & commitment by communicating a clear path to success
Empowers others by delegating and providing support and opportunities for growth
Identifies, raises and resolves conflict
Builds trust and models the “Coach Approach”



Entrepreneurial Leadership


Encourages informed risk-taking and supports learning from outcomes.
Creates an environment where your team enjoys working hard while having fun
Provides feedback to reinforce results



Disclaimer: The above describes the general responsibilities, required knowledge and skills. Please keep in mind that other duties may be added, or this description may be amended at any time.

In compliance with Ontario’s Bill 190, we confirm that this posting represents a current existing vacancy within our organization.

We affirm that our hiring process, including candidate screening, evaluation, and selection, does not involve the use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) or automated decision-making tools. All assessments and decisions are conducted by qualified human professionals to ensure fairness, transparency, and compliance with Bill 149.

Title: Data Developer
Description:
The salary range for this position is CAD $38.90/Hr. - CAD $55.92/Hr.

Come work as a Data Developer with Vancouver Coastal Health (VCH)!

Vancouver Coastal Health is looking for a Data Developer to join the Data and Analytics team. Apply today to join our team!

As a Data Developer with Vancouver Coastal Health you will


Provide advanced technical expertise in leading other data Developers to build and deploy data solutions and pipelines across on-premises, public, private, and hybrid cloud environments.
Support projects covering all programs/services within Data and Analytics and throughout Vancouver Coastal Health (VCH).
Be key member of the department’s team, and be responsible for designing, maintaining, and troubleshooting data models, data pipelines, and implementing business logic needed to meet a wide range of business requirements.
Work closely with project managers, leadership and other functions across the department and VCH.
Collaborate with the Principal Data Developers, VCH business and analytics teams, external vendors (e.g. Microsoft, AWS) and other internal or external teams on cross-functional projects.
Have a primary focus of ensuring Data and Analytics’ ability to deliver and promote on-premises and cloud-based data solutions within the approved enterprise environments, the Senior Data Developer will lead the effort in the areas of design, migration, optimization, and support experience in those environments.



This work is framed within VCH’s commitments to our pillars of Indigenous Cultural Safety, Anti-Racism, Equity, Diversity and Inclusion, and Planetary Health.

Education & Experience


Bachelor’s Degree in Computer Science, Information Systems Management or a data intensive related field plus at least seven (7) years’ experience as a Data Engineer two (2) of which will have been in a senior role or an equivalent combination of education, training and experience.
Data Engineering related certification from at least one cloud service provider required (e.g. Microsoft Azure Data Engineer Associate).



Knowledge & Abilities


Demonstrated ability to implement data infrastructure, database, and data solutions with on-prem and cloud tools.
Demonstrated hands-on experience optimizing data solutions and pipelines ensuring scalability, performance, and cost effectiveness are addressed.
Demonstrated proficiency developing in SQL languages such as T-SQL, PL/SQL, Databricks SQL.
Demonstrated proficiency in the use of Python, Scala, and Spark with an ability to write efficient and scalable code for data processing/transformation tasks.
Demonstrated understanding of coding best practices.
Experience in data modeling, ETL development, and data warehousing architecture with solid knowledge of various industry standards.
Understanding of software data development, deployment and release processes, and automated test frameworks.
Experience with cloud platforms such as Azure, AWS, or GCP and related data services (e.g Azure Data Factory, Azure Databricks, Azure Data Lake).
Strong analytical, prioritizing, interpersonal, problem-solving, presentation, budgeting, project management (from conception to completion), & planning skills.
Develops and maintains strong relationships with key internal and external partners to promote and enhance services provided with sensitivity to diversity and indigenous lens.
Practices the recognition, inclusion and support of all types and aspects of diversity at all stages of engagement, with an understanding of the issues faced by people from equity-deserving groups.
Strong interpersonal skills including strengths in working in a team environment with a client/user orientation.
Ability to work effectively with multi-disciplinary teams with different lines of reporting.
Excellent oral and written communication skills with the ability to effectively communicate complex ideas and processes in simple terminology to internal and external stakeholders, including all levels of management.
Demonstrated attention to detail and maintenance of high quality standards.
Demonstrated ability to work independently, meeting deadlines under pressure, with minimal supervision and frequent interruptions.
Works effectively under time pressure to meet deadlines, balance work priorities and resolve problems in a timely manner.
Physical ability to perform the duties of the position.



The hours of work including days off and work area may be subject to change consistent with operational requirements and the provision of the Collective Agreement and applicable statutes. Successful applicants may be required to complete a Criminal Records Review Check.

As per Ministry of Health policy, all health care workers working in publicly-funded health care facilities are required to report their past receipt of certain vaccines or history of certain infections. Collecting these records will allow for offering of any missing vaccines, and for appropriate actions to be taken in the event of any future exposure to a communicable disease or during outbreaks. For all new hires and appointments to Vancouver Coastal Health, you will be asked to provide this information as part of the onboarding process.

Why Join Vancouver Coastal Health (VCH)

VCH is a world class innovator in medical care, research and teaching, delivering service to more than one million BC residents. At VCH, we embrace thinking boldly, taking smart risks, and 'going first' when we believe it will lead to the best possible outcomes for patients and their families. We invite you to join us in creating healthy lives in healthy communities by showcasing our passion for care, connection to the communities we serve and our culture of teamwork that makes VCH a great place to work.


Comprehensive health benefits package, including MSP, extended health and dental and municipal pension plan
Grow your career with employer-paid training and leadership development opportunities
Wellness supports, including counselling, critical incident and innovative wellness services are available to employees and their immediate families
Award-winning recognition programs to honour staff, medical staff and volunteers
Access to exclusive discount offers and deals for VCH staff



At Vancouver Coastal Health, our vision of healthy lives in healthy communities inspires us and our values and pillars guide us. We support equitable practices, diverse teams and inclusive environments, which are vital to creating welcoming spaces where everyone can bring their whole selves to work and feel supported. We encourage applications from equity-deserving communities, including Indigenous Peoples and members of racialized groups, people with disabilities and people of all gender identities and expressions and sexual orientation.

Vancouver Coastal Health is proud to be recognized as one of Canada’s Top 100 Employers, BC’s Top Employers, Canada’s Best Diversity Employers and Canada's Top Employers for Young People in 2026.

Only short-listed applicants will be contacted for this posting.

***Employees of VCH must apply online via the Internal Career Portal on CareerHub, you are currently viewing the External Career Portal. Refer to the https//my.vch.ca/working-here/job-postings site for instructions on how to view internal job postings and how to apply as an employee. Current VCH employees who apply to this posting using this external site will be considered as an external candidate. Seniority will not apply.***

Thank you for your interest in Vancouver Coastal Health.
