Title: Analyst Business Intelligence
Description:
Value Proposition

As a leading partner in healthcare, Medline Canada's sole purpose is to make healthcare run better – this means being deeply committed to the health and well-being of our customers, employees, partners and our communities. We employ a diverse group of top talent focused on growing and making a difference in the lives of Canadians, coast-to-coast.

Compensation Is Only One Part Of The Employee Value Proposition That Medline Canada Offers Our Employees. As Part Of Our Mission, We Offer One Of The Most Competitive And Engaging Employee Experience Programs In Canada, Which Includes


Collaborative work environment with highly engaged employees
Market competitive compensation and benefits plan, including LTD & Insurance
RRSP with a company match
Annual Employee Appreciation Week
Paid vacation and personal days
Employee Assistance Program (EAP) Resources supporting mental, physical, family and financial well-being
Career growth and training programs
Open communication with Senior Leaders
Tailored incentives for eligible employee groups, such as home office reimbursement
Employee referral program
Education support programs
Fitness subsidy
Recognition programs
Social activities
And much more…


Job Description Summary

We are seeking an Analyst, Business Intelligence to support the design, development, and maintenance of interactive dashboards and reports for Inventory Management stakeholders. This role will work closely with Inventory Management, Accounting, FP&A, and business teams to translate financial data into meaningful insights that support decision-making.

The ideal candidate has strong experience in Microsoft Power BI, solid understanding of Inventory Management processes, and the ability to transform complex datasets into intuitive visualizations.

Responsibilities

Job Description


Design, develop, and maintain Power BI dashboards and reports for Inventory Management processes (e.g., inventory health, stock levels, turns, aging, service levels, fill rate, OTIF, and KPI tracking).
Collaborate with Inventory Management, Demand Planning, and Purchasing stakeholders to gather business requirements and translate them into scalable reporting and analytics solutions.
Build and optimize Power BI data models, measures, and relationships to support inventory visibility and decision-making.
Develop and maintain ETL processes using Power Query and related data transformation tools to integrate inventory, supply, and demand data.
Ensure data accuracy, consistency, and integrity across inventory reports and analytics outputs.
Manage and maintain Power BI workspaces, including access control, deployment pipelines, and governance practices.
Leverage Microsoft Fabric capabilities (e.g., dataflows, lakehouse, semantic models) to enhance data integration, scalability, and performance of inventory analytics.
Work with data from ERP systems (e.g., SAP), warehouse management systems, and other enterprise data sources.
Optimize report performance and troubleshoot issues related to data refresh, model performance, and visualization efficiency.
Document data models, report logic, KPIs, and business definitions related to inventory management.
Provide training and support to end-users on Power BI dashboards and self-service inventory analytics capabilities.
Support continuous improvement initiatives focused on inventory optimization, working capital reduction, and service level performance.
Leverage Python for advanced data analysis, automation, and data transformation (e.g., building data pipelines, performing complex calculations, and enhancing Power BI datasets through integrated workflows).


Qualifications


3–5 years of experience in BI/reporting roles, with at least 2–3 years of hands-on experience in Power BI.
Strong proficiency in:
Power BI Desktop & Service
Power Query (M language)
Data modeling concepts
Solid understanding of Inventory Management and accounting principles.
Strong SQL skills and experience working with relational databases.
Experience integrating data from ERP systems (e.g., SAP, Oracle, Dynamics, etc.) is an asset.
Hands-on experience with Power BI Service, including workspace management and deployment pipelines.
Familiarity with Microsoft Fabric (Dataflows Gen2, Lakehouse, or related components) is an asset.
Ability to translate business requirements into technical solutions.
Strong analytical, problem-solving, and communication skills.

The Anticipated Salary Range For This Position

$81,600.00 - $122,400.00 Annual

This job description is not intended to be all-inclusive. Employee may perform other related duties as required to meet the ongoing needs of the organization.

Medline Canada, Corporation is proud to be an equal opportunity employer.

About Medline Canada

Medline Canada stands at the forefront of healthcare, providing expertise and a breadth of solutions for every care setting. With 550+ employees, including 100 dedicated sales and clinical professionals and eight distribution centres located coast-to-coast, Medline Canada is proud to serve the entire continuum of care.

Our Commitment to Diversity and Inclusivity

Medline Canada is committed to diversity and inclusivity and we encourage applications from members of groups that have been historically disadvantaged and marginalized, including First Nations, Metis and Inuit peoples, Indigenous peoples of North America, racialized persons/persons of colour and those who identify as women, persons with disabilities and LGBTQ+ persons.

Our commitment to Accessibility

Medline Canada is committed to the principles of the accessibility in Canada. We endeavor to make our recruitment, assessment and selection processes as accessible as possible and provide accommodations as required for applicants with disabilities. If you require any accommodations at any point during the application and hiring process, please contact HRCanada@medline.com.

All qualified candidates are encouraged to apply; however, Canadians and permanent residents will be given priority.

  • Design, develop, and maintain Power BI dashboards and reports for Inventory Management processes (e.g., inventory health, stock levels, turns, aging, service levels, fill rate, OTIF, and KPI tracking).
  • Collaborate with Inventory Management, Demand Planning, and Purchasing stakeholders to gather business requirements and translate them into scalable reporting and analytics solutions.
  • Build and optimize Power BI data models, measures, and relationships to support inventory visibility and decision-making.
  • Develop and maintain ETL processes using Power Query and related data transformation tools to integrate inventory, supply, and demand data.
  • Ensure data accuracy, consistency, and integrity across inventory reports and analytics outputs.
  • Manage and maintain Power BI workspaces, including access control, deployment pipelines, and governance practices.
  • Leverage Microsoft Fabric capabilities (e.g., dataflows, lakehouse, semantic models) to enhance data integration, scalability, and performance of inventory analytics.
  • Work with data from ERP systems (e.g., SAP), warehouse management systems, and other enterprise data sources.
  • Optimize report performance and troubleshoot issues related to data refresh, model performance, and visualization efficiency.
  • Document data models, report logic, KPIs, and business definitions related to inventory management.
  • Provide training and support to end-users on Power BI dashboards and self-service inventory analytics capabilities.
  • Support continuous improvement initiatives focused on inventory optimization, working capital reduction, and service level performance.
  • Leverage Python for advanced data analysis, automation, and data transformation (e.g., building data pipelines, performing complex calculations, and enhancing Power BI datasets through integrated workflows).

Qualifications

  • 3–5 years of experience in BI/reporting roles, with at least 2–3 years of hands-on experience in Power BI.
  • Strong proficiency in:
    • Power BI Desktop & Service
    • Power Query (M language)
    • Data modeling concepts
  • Solid understanding of Inventory Management and accounting principles.
  • Strong SQL skills and experience working with relational databases.
  • Experience integrating data from ERP systems (e.g., SAP, Oracle, Dynamics, etc.) is an asset.
  • Hands-on experience with Power BI Service, including workspace management and deployment pipelines.
  • Familiarity with Microsoft Fabric (Dataflows Gen2, Lakehouse, or related components) is an asset.
  • Ability to translate business requirements into technical solutions.
  • Strong analytical, problem-solving, and communication skills.
The Anticipated Salary Range For This Position

$81,600.00 - $122,400.00 Annual

This job description is not intended to be all-inclusive. Employee may perform other related duties as required to meet the ongoing needs of the organization.

Medline Canada, Corporation is proud to be an equal opportunity employer.

About Medline Canada

Medline Canada stands at the forefront of healthcare, providing expertise and a breadth of solutions for every care setting. With 550+ employees, including 100 dedicated sales and clinical professionals and eight distribution centres located coast-to-coast, Medline Canada is proud to serve the entire continuum of care.

Our Commitment to Diversity and Inclusivity

Medline Canada is committed to diversity and inclusivity and we encourage applications from members of groups that have been historically disadvantaged and marginalized, including First Nations, Metis and Inuit peoples, Indigenous peoples of North America, racialized persons/persons of colour and those who identify as women, persons with disabilities and LGBTQ+ persons.

Our commitment to Accessibility

Medline Canada is committed to the principles of the accessibility in Canada. We endeavor to make our recruitment, assessment and selection processes as accessible as possible and provide accommodations as required for applicants with disabilities. If you require any accommodations at any point during the application and hiring process, please contact HRCanada@medline.com.

All qualified candidates are encouraged to apply; however, Canadians and permanent residents will be given priority.

Title: Data Analyst, HR
Description:
Who We Are




For more than 130 years, The Salvation Army has served people in need in communities across Canada and Bermuda. Building on our roots as a world-wide Christian church, each year we help more than 2 million people, providing necessities such as food, clothing and shelter. In addition, we support people experiencing unemployment, addiction and family challenges. We continually adapt and innovate to meet emerging needs and live out our mission, vision and values of hope, service, dignity and stewardship. As a faith- and values-based organization, we hire and serve people of all backgrounds and walks of life - there is a place for everyone to belong here.




Mission Statement




The Salvation Army exists to share the love of Jesus Christ, meet human needs and be a transforming influence in the communities of our world.




Job Description

Position Purpose:




The Data Analyst, HR exists to strengthen HR technology, analytics, and data integrity so that HR systems effectively support organizational goals. The role contributes by improving HR processes, ensuring accurate and reliable data through analysis, integration, and migration, and enabling robust reporting and analytics capabilities. By training and upskilling team members in Workday Reporting and partnering with stakeholders to define business needs, the role helps translate data into actionable insights. Ultimately, it empowers HR leaders to make informed, strategic decisions that enhance workforce planning and people outcomes across the organization.




This role is a 12 - month, temporary, fixed-term contract.




Key Accountabilities:

Elicit business, financial, and operational requirements across headquarters, divisions, ministry units, and frontline teams to ensure HR systems meet organizational needs.
Develop HR analytics capability by training team members on Workday reporting and creating high-quality, custom reports that support decision-making.
Assess HR system integrity through configuration reviews, interface evaluations, and data quality checks to improve reliability and consistency.
Collaborate cross‑functionally to define and recommend system enhancements, integrations, workflows, and release impacts that support digital transformation.
Establish reporting governance standards for Workday, including design, access, data integrity, and compliance, to ensure accurate and consistent reporting.
Consult on system releases and projects by providing insight on downstream impacts, risks, and requirements, and by escalating and resolving system issues as needed.
Prepare and execute testing and release management activities, including test plans, source-to-target data mapping, migration audits, and reconciliation reporting.
Oversee HR data assurance activities such as gap analysis, data extraction and conversion, validation controls, and integration mapping to ensure end‑to‑end data integrity.




Education And Experience Qualifications:

A university degree in computer science or an approved equivalent combination of education and experience.
7–10 years of experience leading and supporting data analysis and migration initiatives for cloud-based/SaaS HR system implementations.
3–5 years of hands-on experience in HR Reporting and Analytics, with a strong emphasis on statistical analysis and programming using R and/or Python.
Delivers advanced analytics and reporting solutions through strong expertise in Workday Reporting and Power BI.
Builds team capability by designing and delivering effective training, knowledge transfer, and practical guidance.
Navigates complex, multi-system and multi-entity environments while contributing to high-priority, high-visibility initiatives.
Collaborates effectively within Agile delivery teams on large-scale projects from analysis through implementation.
Utilizes advanced Microsoft Excel skills, with solid proficiency in Word and PowerPoint to support analysis and communication.
Demonstrates strong analytical, problem-solving, communication, adaptability, and continuous learning capabilities in fast-paced, changing environments.




Compensation

The target hiring range for this position is $85,321.60 to $106,641.60 with a maximum of $127,982.40. Placement in the salary range will be based on factors such as market conditions, internal equity, and candidate experience, skills, and qualifications relevant to the role.




The Salvation Army will provide reasonable accommodation upon request. Please email Recruitment.Accommodations@salvationarmy.ca if you have a need for any accommodation and we will be pleased to discuss this with you.

Title: Commercial Excellence Analyst
Description:
Location: Oakville, Canada
Department: Wound Care


At Mölnlycke, we help to improve patient outcomes around the world—and behind every great product is a team making better decisions through data.

We’re looking for a Commercial Excellence Analyst to join our Canadian team. This is a unique opportunity to step into a newly created role where you’ll play a key part in shaping how we use data to drive commercial success.

You won’t just be building reports—you’ll be influencing decisions, partnering across teams, and helping us translate complex data into meaningful action.

About The Role

As our Commercial Excellence Analyst, you’ll sit at the intersection of data, strategy, and collaboration. You’ll work closely with Sales, Finance, and Marketing to bring clarity to performance, identify opportunities, and support key commercial decisions.

This role is ideal for someone who enjoys both the technical side of data and the human side of storytelling and collaboration.

You’ll start by building and strengthening our reporting capabilities—and over time, you’ll have the opportunity to shape the role and make it your own.

What You’ll Do


Analyze commercial data to identify trends, risks, and growth opportunities
Build and enhance Power BI dashboards and reporting tools
Translate data into clear, actionable insights for non-technical stakeholders
Partner with Sales, Finance, and Marketing to support business decisions and performance tracking
Support pricing, contracts, and rebate analytics to ensure accuracy and visibility
Continuously improve reporting processes and data quality
Present findings and recommendations to stakeholders across the business


What You’ll Bring


Strong experience with Excel (pivot tables, lookups)
Experience building dashboards in Power BI or similar tools
Ability to work with and interpret complex datasets
Exposure to tools like Power Query, SQL, or similar is an asset


What Really Sets You Apart


You can translate data into insights that others can easily understand and act on
You’re comfortable stepping outside of your comfort zone and taking ownership of problems
You enjoy working cross-functionally and building relationships
You’re curious, adaptable, and motivated to keep learning in a changing data landscape
You bring a “can-do” mindset and enjoy contributing to a collaborative team environment


Why join us?

This is more than just an analyst role—it’s a chance to help build something new.


Be part of a close-knit, high-performing team where your voice is heard
Gain exposure across the business and work directly with commercial leaders
Enjoy the flexibility of a hybrid working model (2 days in-office)
Shape how data is used across the organization—and see the impact of your work
Grow your career in an environment that values curiosity, ownership, and collaboration


What To Expect

In your first months, you’ll focus on getting to know our data, systems, and stakeholders, while building foundational reporting. As you grow into the role, you’ll partner more closely with the business, influence decisions, and help evolve our commercial insights capabilities.

Ready to make a difference?

If you’re excited by the idea of turning data into impact—and want to be part of a team where you can truly make your mark—we’d love to hear from you.

Here’s what you’ll need:


Bachelor’s degree in Business, Data Science, Statistics, Mathematics, or a related field
1–5 years of experience in data analysis, business analysis, or a similar role
Proven ability to work with data to generate insights that support business decision-making
Experience building dashboards and reports using tools such as Power BI or similar
Advanced Excel skills (including pivot tables, lookups, and data manipulation)
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with attention to detail
Ability to communicate complex data clearly to both technical and non-technical audiences
Experience working cross-functionally with stakeholders such as Sales, Finance, or Marketing is preferred
Familiarity with tools such as Power Query, SQL, Fabric, or other data platforms is an asset


Our approach to Diversity and Inclusion

We’re building a workplace where everyone feels seen, heard, and valued. We believe that a diverse mix of people (from different cultures, ages, geographies, and genders) makes us stronger. It brings fresh perspectives, drives better decisions, and helps us deliver solutions that reflect the world we serve. Inclusion isn’t just a value; it’s how we work, grow, and lead together.

About Mölnlycke

Mölnlycke is a world-leading medical products and solutions company that equips healthcare professionals to achieve the best patient, clinical, and economic outcomes.

Our business is organized in the four business areas Wound Care, Operating Room Solutions, Gloves and Antiseptics, where customer centricity, sustainability and digitalization are at the heart of everything we do.

Mölnlycke employs around 8,400 people. The company headquarters are in Gothenburg, Sweden, and we operate in more than 100 countries worldwide. Since 2007, the company has been part of Investor AB, an engaged owner of high-quality, global companies, which was founded by the Wallenberg family in 1916. For more information, please visit www.molnlycke.com and www.molnlycke.com/careers

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Who we are

About Stripe

Stripe is a financial infrastructure platform for businesses. Millions of companies—from the world's largest enterprises to the most ambitious startups—use Stripe to accept payments, grow their revenue, and accelerate new business opportunities. Our mission is to increase the GDP of the internet, and we have a staggering amount of work ahead. That means you have an unprecedented opportunity to put the global economy within everyone's reach while doing the most important work of your career.

About The Team

Data Science at Stripe is a vibrant community where data analysts and data scientists learn and grow together. You'll work with some of the most fundamental data at Stripe, and use that data to help drive company-wide initiatives. We have a variety of Data Analytics roles and teams across Stripe and will seek to align you to the most relevant team based on your background.

What you'll do

In this role, you'll partner deeply with teams across Stripe to ensure that our users, our products, and our business have the models, data products, and insights needed to make decisions and grow responsibly. You'll work closely with partners to extract insights from the rich and complex data at Stripe. You'll also work with leaders to translate business needs into data problems. You'll build metrics, scalable data pipelines, dashboards, and reports to inform and run the business. You'll deliver actionable business recommendations through analyses and data storytelling.

Who you are

We're looking for someone who meets the minimum requirements to be considered for the role. If you meet these requirements, you are encouraged to apply. The preferred qualifications are a bonus, not a requirement.

Minimum Requirements


MS/MA + 2 years or BS/BA + 3 years of full-time experience exclusive of internships in Business Intelligence Engineering, Data Analyst, and Business Analyst roles
Proficiency in SQL
Proven ability to manage and deliver on multiple projects with great attention to detail
Ability to clearly communicate results and drive impact
Ability to design, implement, and maintain data pipelines and dashboards to generate actionable insights based on stakeholder requirements
Experience collaborating with cross-functional teams to deliver strategic insights, benchmarks, and analyses that provide recommendations
Ability to enable stakeholders and partners by building self-service tooling and providing training to empower stakeholder teams to be data literate and self-sufficient in autonomous reporting capabilities



Preferred Qualifications


Prior experience at a growth-stage internet or software company
Experience with distributed data frameworks like Spark to write and debug data pipelines
Good understanding of development processes and best practices like engineering standards, code reviews, and testing
Strong statistical knowledge
Working knowledge of Python
Experience creating leadership-level reporting, such as QBRs, MBRs
Proficiency with AI tools to accelerate model development, analysis, and coding



In-office expectations

Office-assigned Stripes in most of our locations are currently expected to spend at least 50% of the time in a given month in their local office or with users. This expectation may vary depending on role, team and location. For example, Stripes in Stripe Delivery Center roles in Mexico City, Mexico, Bengaluru, India, and Dublin, Ireland work 100% from the office. Also, some teams have greater in-office attendance requirements, to appropriately support our users and workflows, which the hiring manager will discuss. This approach helps strike a balance between bringing people together for in-person collaboration and learning from each other, while supporting flexibility when possible.

Pay and benefits

The annual salary range for this role in the primary location is CA$121,100 - CA$221,900. This range may change if you are hired in another location. For sales roles, the range provided is the role’s On Target Earnings (“OTE”) range, meaning that the range includes both the sales commissions/sales bonuses target and annual base salary for the role. This salary range may be inclusive of several career levels at Stripe and will be narrowed during the interview process based on a number of factors, including the candidate’s experience, qualifications, and specific location. Applicants interested in this role and who are not located in the primary location may request the annual salary range for their location during the interview process.

Specific benefits and details about what compensation is included in the salary range listed above will vary depending on the applicant’s location and can be discussed in more detail during the interview process. Benefits/additional compensation for this role may include: equity, company bonus or sales commissions/bonuses; retirement plans; health benefits; and wellness stipends.

Title: Database Marketing Analyst (Data Science) - Hybrid
Description:
Company Overview

The Globe and Mail is a national icon and one of Canada's most recognized media brands. We proudly serve as a trusted destination for Canadians seeking the highest caliber of journalism, and we've garnered international acclaim for our data visualization, design, and creative storytelling.

We are committed to fostering diversity and inclusivity by reflecting all Canadians in both the stories that we tell and the composition of our workforce. We are proud partners with organizations like Indigenous Works, Pride at Work, the Canadian Centre for Diversity and Inclusion, and we are a signatory of the BlackNorth Initiative. Recognizing the importance of work-life balance, we offer flexible work arrangements and support programs. We also invest in our employees' growth through training and mentorship opportunities, enabling you to expand your skills and embrace new challenges.

No matter your position at The Globe, you'll be an integral part of an organization dedicated to making a positive difference in Canada. Join us.

Position Overview

The Globe and Mail is seeking an experienced Database Analyst, Database Marketing (Revenue) to support our subscriber growth, engagement, and revenue strategies in a highly competitive digital landscape. Reporting to the Manager, Database Marketing, this role is responsible for building and maintaining high‑quality customer audiences, enabling data‑driven multi‑channel marketing initiatives, and delivering actionable insights that improve subscriber experiences.

The successful candidate will leverage advanced SQL, Customer Data Platforms (CDPs), and disparate customer data sources to power personalized campaigns across email, web, app, and off‑platform digital channels. This role plays a critical part in automating campaign workflows, ensuring data accuracy and privacy compliance, and acting as a steward of subscriber data quality. This is an existing vacancy, and this position is a hybrid role based in Toronto.

Key Responsibilities

Database Marketing & Campaign Enablement


Translate business and marketing requirements into executable database‑driven marketing campaigns, primarily for email and digital channels.
Build and maintain accurate and scalable audience segments using customer profile, transactional, and behavioral data.
Support a comprehensive subscriber contact strategy, including preference management, exclusions, and consent requirements.
Develop automated and recurring campaign solutions using Oracle SQL, PL/SQL, and JavaScript.


Segmentation, Journeys & Personalization


Design and implement customer journeys that support acquisition, retention, engagement, and revenue goals.
Leverage the Customer Data Platform to deliver personalized and targeted customer experiences.
Consolidate and harmonize data from multiple CEM, CRM, and digital data sources to support journey execution.


Testing, QA & Optimization


Design and execute test‑and‑learn frameworks, including A/B testing and target vs. control campaigns across channels.
Perform end‑to‑end quality assurance to ensure campaign logic, data outputs, and creative assets align with business requirements.
Identify efficiencies, risks, and optimization opportunities throughout the campaign lifecycle.


Analytics & Insights


Analyze campaign performance using SQL, Tableau, and Amplitude, identifying trends, insights, and areas for improvement.
Produce both ad‑hoc and recurring reporting to support revenue and engagement objectives.
Clearly communicate insights and recommendations to internal stakeholders to inform future strategy.


Data Stewardship & Collaboration


Partner with application development and analytics teams to support database design, coding standards, and QA processes.
Ensure all marketing data outputs are accurate, compliant, and privacy‑safe.
Act as an advocate for subscriber data protection, data hygiene, and compliance with privacy legislation.


Required

QUALIFICATIONS & EXPERIENCE:


Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Data Analytics, Database Marketing, or a related field.
3-5 years of experience performing database analytics or marketing operations supporting customer acquisition, retention, and engagement.
Advanced SQL skills (Oracle SQL / PL‑SQL), including automation of scheduled jobs and campaign workflows.
Strong experience working with CRM systems and marketing automation platforms, particularly Customer Data Platforms (CDPs).
Proven experience building email and SMS contact lists using SQL.
Solid understanding of relational database principles, data modeling, and data hygiene.
Strong analytical skills in data mining, statistical analysis, and reporting.
Excellent communication skills with the ability to explain technical concepts to non‑technical stakeholders.
Experience with big data and analytics platforms (e.g., Snowflake, Amplitude).
Working knowledge of JavaScript, Python, R, or HiveQL.
Hands‑on experience with Tableau or equivalent BI tools.
Working knowledge of HTML for email development.
Experience with AI‑enabled data cleaning, deduplication, and enrichment tools.


Core Competencies


Extremely detail‑oriented with strong quality‑assurance mindset
Ability to manage multiple initiatives under tight deadlines
Highly organized with strong prioritization skills
Collaborative team player who shares knowledge and best practices
Self‑motivated, adaptable, and comfortable working with minimal supervision


SALARY: The base salary for this role ranges from $72,400 to $90,500 per year. Placement within this range depends on a candidate’s level of experience, technical skills, and alignment with the role’s requirements. Candidates who have been identified as meeting the role’s requirements can expect to be near the midpoint of this range, while the upper end is reserved for those with specialized expertise or a strong track record of success in comparable roles. This role may also be eligible for The Globe’s Annual Incentive Plan, which is based on a combination of company and individual performance.

Why Choose The Globe

The Globe’s mission is to deliver essential content – news, information, analysis and insights – for aspiring individuals and strong communities. The Globe is committed to providing a respectful and inclusive workplace that upholds our values of integrity, collaboration, innovation and accountability.

As Canada’s most respected media brand, The Globe is dedicated to making a difference to Canada and you can make a difference by working with us.

We Offer


Competitive compensation to ensure we hire, retain and reward team members
Hybrid work environment that promotes work-life balance
Generous vacation and flexible work arrangements
Parental leave top-up
Competitive health and dental benefits
Defined Benefit pension plan
Annual wellness subsidy
On-site chiropractor and registered massage therapist
Employee and family assistance program
Free digital subscription to globeandmail.com and 40% off other Globe products
Education assistance for external training courses


Supporting Your Growth


We are committed to creating equitable opportunities for all employees, to enable everyone to reach their full potential. This commitment is embedded in our strategic plan and core values.
There are lateral and upward advancement opportunities for rewarding and developing careers.
We believe in mentorship and collaborative peer-to-peer learning and have both formal and informal programs in place to encourage knowledge-sharing.
We support continuing education and provide both internal and external opportunities for training and development.


AI DISCLOSURE: No artificial intelligence tools are used to screen, assess, or select applicants for this position. All hiring decisions are made by human reviewers. We ask candidates to engage authentically in interviews; AI may support preparation but should not be used during interviews or replace your own responses.

THE GLOBE AND MAIL IS DEDICATED TO DIVERSITY AND INCLUSION IN THE WORKPLACE

The Globe and Mail is committed to fostering an inclusive, accessible work environment, where all employees feel valued, respected and supported. We believe this strengthens our business and our journalism. We welcome and encourage applications from individuals from all groups, regardless of race, ethnicity, culture, gender, sexual orientation, religion, socio-economic status, age, and physical ability. The Globe and Mail offers accommodation for applicants with disabilities as part of its recruitment process. If you are contacted to arrange for an interview, please advise us if you require an accommodation.

Company Overview

The Globe and Mail is a national icon and one of Canada's most recognized media brands. We proudly serve as a trusted destination for Canadians seeking the highest caliber of journalism, and we've garnered international acclaim for our data visualization, design, and creative storytelling.

We are committed to fostering diversity and inclusivity by reflecting all Canadians in both the stories that we tell and the composition of our workforce. We are proud partners with organizations like Indigenous Works, Pride at Work, the Canadian Centre for Diversity and Inclusion, and we are a signatory of the BlackNorth Initiative. Recognizing the importance of work-life balance, we offer flexible work arrangements and support programs. We also invest in our employees' growth through training and mentorship opportunities, enabling you to expand your skills and embrace new challenges.

No matter your position at The Globe, you'll be an integral part of an organization dedicated to making a positive difference in Canada. Join us.

Position Overview

The Globe and Mail is seeking an experienced Database Analyst, Database Marketing (Revenue) to support our subscriber growth, engagement, and revenue strategies in a highly competitive digital landscape. Reporting to the Manager, Database Marketing, this role is responsible for building and maintaining high‑quality customer audiences, enabling data‑driven multi‑channel marketing initiatives, and delivering actionable insights that improve subscriber experiences.

The successful candidate will leverage advanced SQL, Customer Data Platforms (CDPs), and disparate customer data sources to power personalized campaigns across email, web, app, and off‑platform digital channels. This role plays a critical part in automating campaign workflows, ensuring data accuracy and privacy compliance, and acting as a steward of subscriber data quality. This is an existing vacancy, and this position is a hybrid role based in Toronto.

Key Responsibilities

Database Marketing & Campaign Enablement

  • Translate business and marketing requirements into executable database‑driven marketing campaigns, primarily for email and digital channels.
  • Build and maintain accurate and scalable audience segments using customer profile, transactional, and behavioral data.
  • Support a comprehensive subscriber contact strategy, including preference management, exclusions, and consent requirements.
  • Develop automated and recurring campaign solutions using Oracle SQL, PL/SQL, and JavaScript.

Segmentation, Journeys & Personalization

  • Design and implement customer journeys that support acquisition, retention, engagement, and revenue goals.
  • Leverage the Customer Data Platform to deliver personalized and targeted customer experiences.
  • Consolidate and harmonize data from multiple CEM, CRM, and digital data sources to support journey execution.

Testing, QA & Optimization

  • Design and execute test‑and‑learn frameworks, including A/B testing and target vs. control campaigns across channels.
  • Perform end‑to‑end quality assurance to ensure campaign logic, data outputs, and creative assets align with business requirements.
  • Identify efficiencies, risks, and optimization opportunities throughout the campaign lifecycle.

Analytics & Insights

  • Analyze campaign performance using SQL, Tableau, and Amplitude, identifying trends, insights, and areas for improvement.
  • Produce both ad‑hoc and recurring reporting to support revenue and engagement objectives.
  • Clearly communicate insights and recommendations to internal stakeholders to inform future strategy.

Data Stewardship & Collaboration

  • Partner with application development and analytics teams to support database design, coding standards, and QA processes.
  • Ensure all marketing data outputs are accurate, compliant, and privacy‑safe.

Title: BI & Data Analyst (12 Months Contract)
Description:
Choose a workplace that empowers your impact.

Join a global workplace where employees thrive. One that embraces diversity of thought, expertise and passion. A place where you can personalize your employee journey to be — and deliver — your best.

We are a leading global real estate investor, developer and manager. We combine our capital with our capabilities to create real estate that strengthens economies and communities. By prioritizing people, partnerships and places, we generate meaningful returns for OMERS members, enhance value for our capital partners and create a brighter world for our customers.

Join us to accelerate your growth & development, prioritize wellness, build connections, and support the communities where we live and work.

Don’t just work anywhere — come build tomorrow together with us.

Know someone at OMERS or Oxford Properties? Great! If you're referred, have them submit your name through Workday first. Then, watch for a unique link in your email to apply.

The Analyst, Business Intelligence acts as a business partner to our finance stakeholders, performing data analysis using a wide variety of applications (including Power BI and SQL), and driving automation effort. To succeed in this role, candidates must be able to drive project completion with strong communication and project management, combined with experience in data analytics, big data mining, and data flow and structure. This role will require effective customer service management, with a proven ability to build relationships between the business groups, financial team and IT department, while being able to multitask in a complex environment.

As a Member Of This Team, You Will


Collaborate with management teams to prioritize business, analytics and information needs and understand project requirements
Work with financial and operational data from primary or secondary data sources from different stakeholder teams and business functions
Seek efficiencies in existing processes and execute process improvement initiatives, including exploring and identifying potential new tools and technologies for enhancing reporting automation capabilities
Identify, analyze, interpret, and report on trends or patterns in complex data sets
Exploring large data sets and drawing meaningful conclusions and insights, including identifying critical metrics and KPIs
Filter and “clean” data by reviewing extracts, reports, printouts, and performance indicators to identify, troubleshoot and resolve problems, including data quality, integrity issues and follow data governance policies
Assist with the preparation and development of reporting, presentations and various materials for discussion at the executive level


To Succeed In This Role, You Have


Undergraduate degree in Accounting, Mathematics, Computer Science or a related field.
Have a minimum of 3+ years of demonstrated experience in a similar role (e.g. Data Analyst or BI Data Analyst).
Team oriented individual with strong communication skills, who is comfortable engaging with different groups within the organization (including non-technical audiences).
Thrives in dynamic environments by adapting to evolving requirements and refining approaches as new information emerges, even when processes are not fully defined.
Strong project management and organizational skills and ability to work under pressure and manage high priority projects with multiple or competing deadlines.
Willingness to learn new technologies, including RPA, with a strong inclination to improve processes and not settle for ‘status quo’ to generate efficiencies and improve capabilities.
Experience with Power BI, SQL, and advanced Excel functionality (including Power Query) to develop automation solutions. Experience with other BI Tools and VBA would be an asset.
Technical expertise in data mining and analysis, with an understanding of financial reporting concepts.
Demonstrate strong problem-solving skills and innovative approaches for resolution.


We believe that time together in the office is important for OMERS and Oxford, the strength of our employees, and the work we do for our pension members. In delivering on our pension promise, keeping us connected to our work and each other, our flexible hybrid work guideline requires teams to come in to the office 4 days per week.

This posting is for an existing vacancy.

The expected salary range for this position is $72,000.00 - $108,000.00 per year, prorated based on the term of the contract.

You may also be eligible to receive an annual Incentive Award pursuant to our Short-term Incentive plan and our Long-Term Incentive plan (if applicable), and to participate in our group benefits and retirement plans – details on these elements of compensation are included within OMERS & Oxford offer letters.

Oxford's purpose is to strengthen economies and communities through real estate.

Our people-first culture is at its best when our workforce reflects the communities where we live and work — and the customers we proudly serve.

From hire to retire, we are an equal opportunity employer committed to an inclusive, barrier-free recruitment and selection process that extends all the way through your employee experience. This sense of belonging and connection is cultivated up, down and across our global organization thanks to our vast network of Employee Resource Groups with executive leader sponsorship, our Purpose@Work committee and employee recognition programs.

Artificial intelligence (AI) tools are used to support certain stages of the OMERS recruitment process. While AI assists us in our process, human judgment and decision-making remain central to our candidate experience.

Title: Reporting Analyst
Description:
About Us

For over 50 years, our Property and Casualty insurance Company, CAA Insurance, has provided dependable and trustworthy service to protect Canadians and what matters to them. CAA Insurance Company offers a dynamic professional environment which strives to offer the ultimate customer experience. We share in each other’s successes, drive change and support each other while cultivating a great working environment to deliver on what is best for the customer.

Who we are

At CCG, we are committed to delivering an exceptional Associate experience. We offer:

Work-life harmony with access to an award-winning holistic wellness program,

Continuous learning through our robust corporate curriculum and education reimbursement program,

Incredible rewards, travel incentives, and product and service discounts,

Pay-for-performance and best-in-class recognition programs, and

Competitive benefits that include a defined contribution plan, personal spending account, and so much more.

Join our growing team where everyone belongs!

Position Details

What You Will Do


Analyze insurance performance historical data to understand the reasons for past performance and make recommendations that will influence the business growth and/or address portfolio / business concerns
Conduct regular, ongoing reviews on the sales performance measurements for each distribution channels, as well as portfolio analysis to assess quality of business being written
Recommend changes or adherence to prudent underwriting guidelines and practices
Respond to business needs by identifying appropriate information to analyze utilizing a variety of sources in data mining
Identify, understand, and communicate profitability drivers while collaborating with multi distribution channels, claims and accounting areas
Find ways to automate reports based on user requests or by observing frequency of ad-hoc reports being requested
Identify opportunities and responding to changes to ensure adherence to the corporate business success strategy
Communicate and consult proposed changes in underwriting rules, guidelines, practices and gaps based on findings
Provide Insurance field trend analysis, forecasts reports and findings that anticipate outcome
Ability to utilize EDW data / GW data, industry information and trend analysis to provide management recommendations
Provide detailed analysis of statistics that are outside of anticipated norms


Please note, salary range is not inclusive of total compensation. The expected salary range for this position will be impacted by factors such as the successful candidate’s skills, experience and working location, as well as the specific position’s business line, scope and level. Candidates with salary expectations outside of the range are encouraged to apply.

Who You Are


University Degree in Mathematics, Economics or Actuarial Science
3-5 years data reporting experience in the financial services industry. P&C Insurance industry experience is a definite asset.
Forecast reporting, data collection, data analysis, report generation and quality assurance experience is required
Professional designation preferred but not required: CIP/FCIP, CA. CMA. CPA, CGA or equivalent
Database Administration experience is a preferred asset
Working experience with MS Office products, SQL or database query tools including VBM and VBA is an asset
Power BI and Python experience are required
Excellent interpersonal, verbal and written communication skills
Excellent problem solver, detail oriented, ability to work in a team environment as well as work independently
Strong analytical and decision-making skills


Please note that internal applicants are asked to formally express their interest by applying online no later than 10 business days of the position being posted

Please note that we may use AI tools to help us through the recruitment process.

Our Commitment

We are an equal opportunity employer and are committed to providing employment accommodation in accordance with the Ontario Human Rights Code and the Accessibility for Ontarians with Disabilities Act, 2005 (AODA). CAA CCG will provide accommodations to job applicants with disabilities throughout the recruitment process. If you require an accommodation, please notify us and we will work with you to meet your needs.

Title: Analyst - Analytics Engineering - Toronto
Description:
Description

Location: Toronto Metropolitan Area

Base Salary: $95,000 – $105,000 (CAD)

Company Overview

Teragonia is the AI Operating System for Private Equity, helping sponsors and their portfolio companies turn fragmented operational data into decisions that move EBITDA. We operate in the highest-pressure segment of enterprise performance, where outcomes are time-bound by a 3 to 5 year hold period and execution velocity defines returns. Through Astradis™, our AI platform, we unify structured and unstructured data, generate prescriptive insights in near real time, and embed them directly into operating workflows with clear ownership and measurable impact . We partner with PE firms managing more than US$400B in assets and are rapidly expanding globally.

Our work is measured in EBITDA outcomes. Across PE portfolio companies, we drive 3% to 5% EBITDA uplift, accelerate roll-ups through standardized data and KPI frameworks, and maintain continuous exit readiness with high-fidelity performance instrumentation . We are replacing episodic consulting with a compounding AI platform that scales expertise across companies, industries, and ownership cycles. Teragonia is built for high-agency builders who want early responsibility, direct exposure to world's leading private equity firms and their portfolio CXOs, and the opportunity to help define the operating layer for value creation in the AI era.

Teragonia offers a comprehensive career development platform through cross-learning within our multidisciplinary structure. We nurture a diverse, inclusive, collaborative, respectful, and collegial environment. Our competitive compensation package aligns with the technology industry leaders, encompassing 401k contribution matching, medical insurance, and additional benefits.

Read more about our mission, vision, and values on our website:


About Teragonia: https://teragonia.com/company/
Our Values: https:// teragonia.com/careers/
Our Purpose: https://teragonia.com/value-orchestration/


Teragonia's Value Creation & Analytics (VC&A) Department Summary

Teragonia’s Value Creation & Analytics (VC&A) division bridges finance, consulting, and technology to support portfolio companies. We combine consulting rigor and AI to address complex challenges and enhance enterprise value through platform solutions and advisory. We prioritize professional growth through mentorship and cross-functional learning.

The Astradis platform provides real-time intelligence through a function-specific command center, combining structured and unstructured data via our Fibron AI layer. Our platform powers diagnostic dashboards, forecasting models, pricing tools, and operations modules, enabling clients to transition from reactive reporting to predictive strategy.

Requirements

Job Summary

We are looking for a talented Analytics Engineer to join our team as an Analyst. As an Analyst, you will work closely with our team of analytics engineers to design and develop advanced analytics solutions for our private equity-backed company clients. You will assist in understanding their business needs and developing tailored analytics solutions.

Responsibilities


Support a team of analytics engineers to design and develop advanced analytics solutions and be an active contributor to executing on engagement work
Assist Engagement Managers and Associates in working closely with clients to understand their business needs and develop tailored analytics solutions
Assist Engagement Managers and Associates in translating Client communicated business needs into an exploratory data analysis program and lead team efforts to execute
Assist analytics engineering teams in designing, developing, maintaining, and optimizing complex enterprise data models to help solve operational and financial business problems
Develop and maintain high-quality, visually appealing reports and dashboards to communicate complex analytics insights and recommendations to senior client executives, ensuring that findings are easy to understand and actionable by senior executives
Make a concerted effort to begin to develop and maintain strong relationships with clients and internal stakeholders
Participate in and assist in project meetings and contribute to project deliverables
Ability to work in a fast-paced environment, manage multiple projects simultaneously, and prioritize tasks to meet deadlines
Assist in developing and implementing process improvements to increase team efficiency and productivity
Collaborate with data engineers to design, develop, maintain, and optimize data pipelines critical to the integrity of the enterprise data models
Stay up-to-date with industry trends and best practices in data analytics and apply this knowledge to improve our solutions


Overview

As an Analyst, you will be a key contributor to our service delivery, innovation, and practice development. You will have the opportunity, and the management support, to develop specialized knowledge of data analytics, data science, and AI applications in a chosen industry or domain. You will possess excellent communication and presentation skills and be eager to develop as a key contributor in the Value Creation and Analytics (VC&A) Consulting division and develop executive presence as your career with us progresses to Associate, Manager, Principal, Director, and Managing Director levels. We will provide appropriate mentorship and company selected resources for your leadership skill development.

Technical Skills

As an Analyst, you will be quick to understand the client’s business needs and execute on delivering data-driven solutions. You will play a direct role in driving engagement workflows, deliverables, and timelines, ensuring the quality of enterprise-grade data pipelines and business intelligence applications. These efforts aim to enhance pricing, customer segmentation, sales effectiveness, customer lifetime value, attrition management, workforce scheduling, and cost optimization. You will be actively contributing to and delivering the team’s insights around the ideation, design and development of various analytics, data science and generative AI products, working hand-in-hand with our software engineers, data scientists, and AI engineers. 

The ideal candidate is a proactive problem-solver who takes full ownership of delivering high-quality client work. This individual thrives on delving into technical details to ensure the engagement team successfully meets deliverables by employing analytical techniques to develop crucial business insights for our clients. You will have excellent attention to detail and project management skills, essential for working effectively within our diverse, multidisciplinary team. As part of a collaborative group that includes business analysts, software engineers, data engineers, analytics engineers, data scientists, and AI engineers, you must demonstrate strong teamwork abilities. Your role will require not only individual excellence but also seamless integration and cooperation with team members to achieve shared goals and deliver impactful results.

Transforming low-hygiene data sets to develop organized databases with high fidelity is a necessary part of the job. The ideal candidate is an expert in data wrangling skills and possesses excellent knowledge of the software tools used for service delivery, including data manipulation and data visualization software. You will develop deep expertise in the Teragonia technology stack, for which we will provide appropriate training resources to the extent such tools are new to you. 

Engagement and Client Management

You could be delivering multiple client engagements simultaneously, or a large and complex engagement, and the scope of the engagement could evolve based on changing client needs. The financial sponsor universe demands actionable insights in a relatively short timeframe. The ideal candidate will be excited about driving critical client deliverables and solving a variety of business problems in parallel, in a fast-paced and dynamic environment.

The engagement planning and management are critical to maintaining high quality delivery and efficiency. You will actively support the Engagement Manager and Associate by delivering on the engagement plan and strategy and contribute your own ideas, asking questions, challenging the ideas and timelines presented to ensure that high quality client deliverables can be delivered in the timeframe our clients request them. You will also support the Engagement Manager and Associate with assessing the project management plan including resource allocation, tasks assignment, priorities, timelines, budget vs actual, fee updates, and more. 

You will have strong communication and presentation skills and be able to clearly articulate to the team, engagement leader(s) and the client, the business applications and impact of the data analytics assets delivered.

Other Key Responsibilities


Innovation – Support cross functional innovation initiatives
Specialization – Develop a sector or domain specialty
Recruitment – Actively contribute to Teragonia’s recruitment efforts to attract top talent
Marketing and business development – Assist executive leadership and officers with strategic marketing initiatives
Thought leadership - Assist with conducting research for thought leadership initiatives


Requirements

Necessary Qualifications


Bachelor’s degree in Business Analytics, Computer Science, Information Systems, Industrial Engineering, or a related field with stellar academic performance from leading universities
2 years of experience in analytics engineering, data engineering, software engineering or a related field at global consulting firms, top financial services firms, or high-growth startups
Strong interest in learning how analytics insights drive business value
Advanced programming skills in SQL and Python
Experience in performing exploratory data analysis in a professional setting
Experience with data visualization tools such as Tableau or PowerBI
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
Excellent communication and interpersonal skills


Preferred Qualifications


Experience with cloud-based analytics platforms and big data technologies such as Snowflake, GCP, DBT, FiveTran, Dataiku, etc.
High business acumen and advanced understanding of connectivity between operational data and financial data (ERP CRM PoS HRIS P&L / trial balance / journal entries, etc.)
Experience with data modeling methodologies and concepts (e.g., Kimball Modeling, Data Vault, Data Mesh, etc.)
Strong experience with data modeling
Certification in data science or analytics (e.g., Certified Analytics Professional)
Master's in Business Analytics, Computer Science, or similar advanced degree


Benefits

Canadian Benefits


Competitive Total Compensation - Market-benchmarked salary and bonus structure to attract and retain top talent.
Additional Compensation - Performance-based incentives and signing bonus, as applicable.
Visa Sponsorship - May be available for eligible candidates to join our diverse team subject to applicable laws and regulations in Canada.
RRSP Plan with Employer Contributions - We help secure your future with matching RRSP contributions up to 6.0% of your annual base salary.
WSIB Coverage - Workplace Safety and Insurance Board coverage as required by Ontario law.
Professional Development Opportunities - We will sponsor company-selected training sessions, conferences, and certifications to foster your growth.
Meal Stipend - Reimbursement for lunch at the office, and dinner too if you are working past 7 pm, up to a limit.
Fitness-Wellness Stipend - For fitness & wellness programs, such as gym memberships, up to a limit.
Paid Time Off (PTO) - Eligible for 15 to 20 days PTO based on applicable level. Vacation pay is calculated as required by the Ontario Employment Standards Act. Vacation time may be carried over up to a maximum allowable under ESA requirements.
Statutory Holidays - ESA statutory holidays as well as additional company holidays: New Year’s Day, Family Day, Good Friday, Victoria Day, Canada Day, Labour Day, Thanksgiving, Christmas Day, Boxing Day, Easter Monday, Civic Holiday, Remembrance Day
Comprehensive Healthcare, Vision, and Dental Insurance - We prioritize your health and wellness with insurance coverage through leading Canadian providers equivalent to or exceeding provincial requirements.
Fertility and family-building benefits through leading Canadian healthcare providers.
Flexible Benefits Plan - We provide the opportunity to manage your healthcare expenses efficiently through Canadian tax-advantaged benefit plans where available.
Mental Health Support - Access to mental health support through Canadian mental health providers covered under our healthcare plan.
Health Navigation Services - Expert assistance to help you navigate the complexities of the Canadian healthcare system.
Disability Insurance - We provide short term disability insurance through a leading Canadian provider covering up to 26 weeks at 60% of weekly earnings up to $1,500, exceeding Employment Insurance requirements.
Sick Days - 5 paid sick days annually that reset each year, as required under the Ontario Employment Standards Act. Additional unpaid sick leave available as per ESA requirements.
Leaves of Absence - Teragonia provides unpaid statutory leaves as required by the Ontario Employment Standards Act, 2000 (ESA), including pregnancy, parental, caregiver, and bereavement leave. Eligibility and duration follow provincial regulations. Additional unpaid leaves or flexible arrangements may be considered on a case-by-case basis.
Paid Parental Leave - Paid Parental Leave of 12 weeks after 6 months of service with the company.


Flexible Work Policy

We promote flexible work to support wellbeing and performance. Remote work is available though in-person collaboration is encouraged for planning and team connection. Toronto based employees have no mandated office days but are expected in person for key meetings. Employees outside Toronto may work remotely with occasional travel for events. Early career professionals with less than three years of experience should live within commuting distance to receive in person mentorship. Relocation support is available on a case-by-case basis.

Working Hours and ESA Exemption

This position is classified as a specialized professional role under O. Reg. 285/01, s. 2 of Ontario's Employment Standards Act, 2000 (ESA) and is therefore exempt from overtime pay requirements. While standard hours of work are expected to be approximately 40 hours per week, additional hours may be required to meet client deliverable sand business needs. Such expectations are reflected in the annual salary.

Canadian Experience

We welcome applications from all qualified candidates. Canadian work experience is not a requirement for this position.

Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion Statement

We are an equal opportunity employer and are committed to providing a respectful, inclusive, harassment free workplace. We hire based on merit and qualifications in line with the Ontario Human Rights Code and Employment Standards Act. We welcome applicants from all backgrounds.

Accommodation For Disabilities

We are committed to dignity, independence, inclusion, and equal opportunity under the Ontario Human Rights Code and AODA. We provide reasonable accommodations during recruitment and employment. Employees needing support should contact their manager or Human Resources representative.

Retention Notice

In compliance with Ontario's employment standards, Teragonia retains copies of all publicly advertised job postings and associated application forms for a period of three years.

Recruiting Process

Our recruitment process is designed to ensure a thoughtful and comprehensive evaluation of candidates, as well as alignment with the team’s objectives and company values. Candidates are encouraged to ask questions and engage openly throughout. The process typically involves the following stages:


Initial Interviews with Managers – Two interviews focused on fit and goals
Technical Interviews with Principals – One or two sessions assessing skills and problem-solving
Final Interview with Founders – Discuss strategic alignment and long-term potential


AI Use Disclosure

We use artificial Intelligence (AI) tools to support the screening and assessment.

Accessibility in Recruitment

We are committed to accessibility. If you require accommodation during any stage of the recruitment process, please contact us in advance. We will support you in accordance with the Ontario Human Rights Code and the Accessibility for Ontarians with Disabilities Act (AODA).

Title: Data Architect & Business Intelligence
Description:
When applicable, Bombardier promotes flexible and hybrid work policies.

Why join us?

At Bombardier, we design, build and maintain the world’s peak-performing aircraft for the world’s most discerning people and businesses, governments and militaries. We have been successful in setting the highest standards by putting our people at the heart of it all, and defining excellence, together. 

Working at Bombardier means operating at the highest level. Every day, you are part of a team that delivers superior experiences and products, pushing the boundaries of what's possible in our industry and beyond. By prioritizing employee growth and development, we empower everyone to reach their full potential on their own terms, because the best work happens when you are free to be yourself and share your unique expertise.

Benefits

Our Benefits

With our employees’ well-being top of mind, we offer a comprehensive and competitive Benefits Program, which includes the following:


Insurance plans (Medical, life insurance, and more)
Employee Assistance Program
Competitive base salary



What are your contributions to the team?


Participate in our data transformation with the design, development, and implementation of business intelligence solutions across materials management focused on driving actions in support of our core business objectives.
Develop SQL scripts to create reports from our data warehouse (SQL server) or Microsoft Azure Data Lake.
Support and develop Python scripts for data transformation (ETL) to reproduce existing analysis tool or to build new ones in collaboration with other business analysts.
Develop, improve and maintain our Data Governance tools in Access & Power BI.
Facilitate collaborative sessions with cross functional business stakeholders to drive discovery, data analysis, review to gain a more in-depth understanding of problem statements, define business requirements and document the project scope.
Lead and/or participate in continuous improvement initiatives to augment the materials management team’s operational processes efficiencies.  
Analyze, develop problem statements, and utilize data to drive solutions and insight into our inventory control practices and supply chain processes within a worldwide parts network to improve parts availability and / or inventory turns.
Validate all development work for quality and ensure requirements are met with the relevant stakeholders.
Conduct walk-through and sign off sessions. 
Build and maintain a repository of reusable documentation to support future initiatives and training.
Provide ad-hoc analysis requirements as needed



How to thrive in this role?


You hold a Bachelor degree in Computer or Software Engineering, or other related fields combined with 2 to 5 years of experience in developing business intelligence solutions in a supply chain environment.  
Masters or other relevant certification, after-market supply chain management and aerospace industry context knowledge are considered as strong assets.
You exhibit a continuous improvement mindset with relevant experience in improving business processes. 
You have a passion and strong capacity for big data analytics using relevant technologies to automate, perform data transformation, build business intelligence, and improve analytical insights. 
You hold knowledge of core supply chain concepts, processes, and systems. Able to understand how different departments and processes intersect to impact supply chain processes. 
You have appropriate interpersonal skills and communication methods to effectively develop presentations, present business cases and communicate to all levels of the organization. 
You are comfortable working with big data, cloud-based computing, interacting with IT solutions and technical personnel.  
You are proficient with MS Suite (Required), Advance excel (Required), Power BI (Required), Azure Synapse (Required), Azure Databricks (Required), Python (Required),  SQL (Required), Access (Preferred) with the ability and excitement to learn new tools. 
Occasional travel may be required in this role.  
Bilingualism in French and English is mandatory for Montreal, and an asset for Toronto.



Now that you can see yourself in this role, apply and join the Bombardier Team!

Please note: You don’t need all the skills, knowledge, and experience listed to apply for this position! We’re not looking for the perfect candidate, we’re looking for great talent and passionate individuals.

Bombardier is an equal opportunity employer and encourages persons of any race, religion, ethnicity, gender identity, sexual orientation, age, immigration status, disability, or other applicable legally protected characteristics to apply.

Title: Business Analyst (Burlington)
Description:
At Wolseley Canada, our leading-edge is the knowledge and insight found in every corner of our organization. Here, you’ll build strong relationships and support critical industries that touch the lives of Canadians. Every day, we show up with intention and pride; in ourselves, our associates, and our customers. You’ll have a solid foundation to try new things, uncover new possibilities, challenge yourself, and grow your career. Does this sound like somewhere you’d like to be?

Wolseley Canada is the leading wholesale distributor of plumbing, HVAC/R, waterworks and industrial products in the country and working here means, a network of possibilities, a place you are valued and a higher standard.

Join the Pros!

What Will You Do


Support data development efforts within Supply Chain; collaborates on special projects to support processes and operations through effective use of technology and standard operating procedures.
Utilize database to pull, track, and distribute data for Weekly Maintenance Tools, ensuring this process is consistently completed on schedule.
Complete stock maintenance uploads within divisions to effectively support replenishment and demand planning requirements.
Support data quality and logic of internal optimization tools as these tools roll-out.
Responds to internal inquiries for information, creates reports to support quality data output and to provide management information, generates Supply Chain reports as well as ad-hoc pricing analysis when needed.
Complete the daily out of stock review, identifying trends and inventory depth opportunities that could result in out of stocks.
Analyze inventory and sales data as required to support proposed promo position and evaluate impact on current and future inventory levels to determine the best way to support customers.
Execute regional and SKU level plans to drive availability of top selling items
Evaluate minimum presentation/ Bin fill quantities, stock balancing, and inventory variances at store and make required changes and/or recommendations as required.


Target Competencies


Drive for Results: Sets plans and prioritizes, working with others to ensure that they understand their assignments and performance expectations. Measures progress towards goals and quickly redirects efforts as needed. Analyzes job duties to determine wasteful practices that could be improved.
Strategic Approach: Takes a long-term view of organizational success; Works to clarify long term organizational goals; Able to stand back from immediate problems to focus on overarching themes; Solution oriented.


What Will You Bring


1-3 years prior experience in a Supply Chain role is preferred.
Experience with business intelligence/decision support software

Title: Marketing Analyst
Description:
Job Description:

Rakuten International is a division of Rakuten Group, Inc., a Japanese global technology leader in services that empower individuals, communities, businesses and society. Headquartered in San Mateo, California with more than 4,000 employees worldwide, the Rakuten International business portfolio includes market leaders in e-commerce, digital marketing, advertising, communications and entertainment. We create products and services that provide exceptional value by aligning members and the businesses that want to engage them in a shared community.

Rakuten is a leading shopping platform that offers Cash Back on purchases from your favorite brands. By partnering with thousands of brands in apparel, beauty and wellness, dining, grocery, travel, on-demand services, subscription boxes, and more, Rakuten helps members save and get more on everyday purchases. Since its founding in 1999, Rakuten has become the largest and most rewarding shopping program, and its members have earned $4.6 billion in Cash Back just for shopping through Rakuten. For more information, visit Rakuten.com.

Job Summary:

We are looking for a results-driven Data Analyst who can transform complex data into clear, actionable insights that support strategic and operational business decisions. The ideal candidate brings strong analytical thinking, hands-on experience with SQL and Python, and the ability to communicate findings clearly to both technical and non-technical stakeholders. You thrive in ambiguous environments, can move fluidly between business questions and technical execution, and are comfortable using modern AI-assisted tools to improve analytical speed, code quality, and workflow automation.

Key Responsibilities:


Own and monitor key business and marketing KPIs, tracking performance and conducting deep-dive analyses to identify trends, risks, and growth opportunities
Translate business problems into analytical frameworks and deliver clear, actionable recommendations to technical and non-technical stakeholders
Write efficient, scalable SQL queries to extract, transform, and validate data from multiple sources, ensuring metric accuracy and consistency
Perform exploratory data analysis and statistical modeling using Python to inform strategic and operational decisions
Apply statistical techniques to support experimentation, A/B testing, and causal analysis
Build and maintain dashboards and automated reporting using BI tools (Tableau, Power BI, Looker) and develop scalable, reusable analytical assets
Build, validate, and interpret machine learning models, including segmentation, propensity modeling, forecasting, and predictive scoring
Evaluate and apply AI tools responsibly, ensuring analytical outputs are accurate, explainable, reproducible, and aligned with business context.


Minimum Requirements:


2–5 years of experience in data analytics, data science, or a related role
Advanced SQL proficiency (complex joins, window functions, performance optimization)
Strong Python skills for data analysis and data science (pandas, numpy, scikit-learn)
Solid understanding of statistics, probability, and analytical best practices
Experience with experimentation, hypothesis testing, or causal analysis
Hands-on experience with BI and visualization tools (Tableau, Power BI, ThoughtSpot, etc.)
Proven ability to translate business questions into analytical frameworks and communicate insights clearly
Practical experience using Claude Code or similar AI-assisted coding and analytics tools, with the ability to critically review and validate AI generated output.


Preferred Qualifications:


Hands-on experience building and validating machine learning models (regression, classification, clustering, time-series forecasting)
Experience supporting ML workflows- feature engineering, model selection, validation, and performance tracking
Familiarity with deploying or monitoring ML models in production environments
Experience with cloud data warehouses (Snowflake, BigQuery, Redshift)
Background in e-commerce, marketing, product, or growth analytics
Experience building more scalable, automated, and AI-enabled analytics workflows.


Five Principles for Success

Our worldwide practices describe specific behaviors that make Rakuten unique and united across the world. We expect Rakuten employees to model these 5 Shugi Principles of Success.

Always improve, Always Advance - Only be satisfied with complete success - Kaizen

Passionately Professional - Take an uncompromising approach to your work and be determined to be the best

Hypothesize - Practice - Validate – Shikumika - Use the Rakuten Cycle to succeed in unknown territory

Maximize Customer Satisfaction - The greatest satisfaction for our teams is seeing their customers smile

Speed!! Speed!! Speed!! - Always be conscious of time - take charge, set clear goals, and engage your team

Rakuten is an equal opportunity employer. Accessibility accommodations for candidates with disabilities participating in the selection process are available on request. Any information received related to accommodation needs of applicants will be addressed confidentially.

Rakuten would like to thank all applicants for their interest in this role however only qualified candidates will be shortlisted.

Beware of fraudulent job offers claiming to be from Rakuten. Rakuten does not send unsolicited job offers or request money during the recruitment process. Learn more: https://rakutenemploymentalert.com/

At the time of posting, Rakuten expects the Compensation (base salary + discretionary bonus) for this role to be within the range shown below. Individual compensation will vary based on job-related factors, including the skills, qualifications, and experience of the successful candidate as well as business need and geographic location. The successful applicant for this role will be eligible for stock options, health, vision, dental insurance, RRSP matching, Personal Time Off (PTO), Volunteer Time Off (VTO), and other employee benefits as the company implements.

CAD $55,456.00 - 85,923.20 annually
