Title: Reporting Analyst
Description:
About Us

For over 50 years, our Property and Casualty insurance Company, CAA Insurance, has provided dependable and trustworthy service to protect Canadians and what matters to them. CAA Insurance Company offers a dynamic professional environment which strives to offer the ultimate customer experience. We share in each other’s successes, drive change and support each other while cultivating a great working environment to deliver on what is best for the customer.

Who we are

At CCG, we are committed to delivering an exceptional Associate experience. We offer:

Work-life harmony with access to an award-winning holistic wellness program,

Continuous learning through our robust corporate curriculum and education reimbursement program,

Incredible rewards, travel incentives, and product and service discounts,

Pay-for-performance and best-in-class recognition programs, and

Competitive benefits that include a defined contribution plan, personal spending account, and so much more.

Join our growing team where everyone belongs!

Position Details

What You Will Do


Analyze insurance performance historical data to understand the reasons for past performance and make recommendations that will influence the business growth and/or address portfolio / business concerns
Conduct regular, ongoing reviews on the sales performance measurements for each distribution channels, as well as portfolio analysis to assess quality of business being written
Recommend changes or adherence to prudent underwriting guidelines and practices
Respond to business needs by identifying appropriate information to analyze utilizing a variety of sources in data mining
Identify, understand, and communicate profitability drivers while collaborating with multi distribution channels, claims and accounting areas
Find ways to automate reports based on user requests or by observing frequency of ad-hoc reports being requested
Identify opportunities and responding to changes to ensure adherence to the corporate business success strategy
Communicate and consult proposed changes in underwriting rules, guidelines, practices and gaps based on findings
Provide Insurance field trend analysis, forecasts reports and findings that anticipate outcome
Ability to utilize EDW data / GW data, industry information and trend analysis to provide management recommendations
Provide detailed analysis of statistics that are outside of anticipated norms


Please note, salary range is not inclusive of total compensation. The expected salary range for this position will be impacted by factors such as the successful candidate’s skills, experience and working location, as well as the specific position’s business line, scope and level. Candidates with salary expectations outside of the range are encouraged to apply.

Who You Are


University Degree in Mathematics, Economics or Actuarial Science
3-5 years data reporting experience in the financial services industry. P&C Insurance industry experience is a definite asset.
Forecast reporting, data collection, data analysis, report generation and quality assurance experience is required
Professional designation preferred but not required: CIP/FCIP, CA. CMA. CPA, CGA or equivalent
Database Administration experience is a preferred asset
Working experience with MS Office products, SQL or database query tools including VBM and VBA is an asset
Power BI and Python experience are required
Excellent interpersonal, verbal and written communication skills
Excellent problem solver, detail oriented, ability to work in a team environment as well as work independently
Strong analytical and decision-making skills


Please note that internal applicants are asked to formally express their interest by applying online no later than 10 business days of the position being posted

Please note that we may use AI tools to help us through the recruitment process.

Our Commitment

We are an equal opportunity employer and are committed to providing employment accommodation in accordance with the Ontario Human Rights Code and the Accessibility for Ontarians with Disabilities Act, 2005 (AODA). CAA CCG will provide accommodations to job applicants with disabilities throughout the recruitment process. If you require an accommodation, please notify us and we will work with you to meet your needs.

Title: Data Quality Analyst
Description:
Job Description

What is the opportunity?

In the increasingly complex world of financial services, accurate and complete data is critical for risk management and regulatory compliance. Ensuring data integrity begins with an understanding of financial services data, data quality controls and identifying and resolving data issues.

As a Data Quality Analyst focusing on Data Quality with the Shared Data Management Office (SDMO), you will analyze the critical data, build and deploy data quality checks for business partners across the organization including Finance, Group Risk Management, Personal & Commercial Banking, Capital Markets, and Wealth Management.

What will you do?


Perform deep data analysis to identify trends and patterns in the data
Design controls to detect data quality issues
Work using Agile methodology to automate the controls using various tools/technologies
Profile business, financial and reporting data through use of cutting edge data/analytic tools.
Collaborate with various stakeholders to understand business needs, and create user stories and business requirements


What do you need to succeed?

Must-have


2+ years of professional work experience, with 2 years of analysis experience working in a data-rich industry (such as Banking, Insurance, Capital Markets, Retail etc.)
Education in Science, Engineering, Business, Finance or any other analytical discipline or equivalent work experience
Intermediate to advanced knowledge of data analysis methods and the ability to analyze and resolve complex data related issues.
SQL skills (or other data analysis tools/packages) to extract data and perform various types of functions/operations to gain insight
Excellent communication - strong verbal, written and presentation skills
Attention to detail and ability to work independently and within a team


Nice-to-have


Understanding of Data Quality concepts and processes
Understanding of basic Banking/Finance terms and processes
Understanding of data flows and processing
Experience using IBM’s DataStage or Information Analyzer software


If your specific skills don't necessarily align with the description and you feel your background, experience and passion are close to what we're looking for we would love for you to apply.

What’s in it for you?

We thrive on the challenge to be our best, progressive thinking to keep growing, and working together to deliver trusted advice to help our clients thrive and communities prosper. We care about each other, reaching our potential, making a difference to our communities, and achieving success that is mutual.


A comprehensive Total Rewards Program including bonuses and flexible benefits and competitive compensation
Leaders who support your development through coaching and managing opportunities
Work in a dynamic, collaborative, progressive, and high-performing team
Opportunities to do challenging work


Job Skills

Critical Thinking, Data Administration, Database Structures, Data Modeling, Data Movement, Detail-Oriented, Group Problem Solving, Information Capture, Standard Operating Procedure (SOP), Teamwork

Title: Data Analyst, Product (Banking and Payments)
Description:
About Float

Float is on a mission to simplify finance for Canadian businesses, empowering them to eliminate complexity and unlock new opportunities. Through our innovative platform, Float enables businesses to streamline financial operations and optimize cash flow, so they can focus on what matters most: growth.

As one of Canada’s fastest growing companies and top-rated startups in 2025, 2024 and 2023, Float is customer-obsessed, passionate and entrepreneurial, with a team that includes leaders from Uber, Stripe, Shopify, Top Hat, Ada, Doordash, Snowflake, and Wealthsimple.

At Float, everyone is an owner, bringing their unique perspective to our team and product. Your voice is important, and we take having a culture based on feedback seriously. We openly share our thoughts and differing opinions so we can continue to improve. We do our best to keep our decision-making decentralized so that all team members feel ownership in our success.

Our Product

Float is Canada's complete business finance platform, combining modern financial services and software to help businesses spend, save, and grow. Trusted by 6000+ Canadian companies, Float provides high-limit corporate cards, automated expense management, next-day bill payments, high-yield accounts and fast, friendly support—all built in Canada, for Canada. Float is backed by world-class venture and fintech investors, including Growth Equity at Goldman Sachs Alternatives and OMERS Ventures, partners behind our $70 million Series B raise.

Our team is a collection of ambitious, collaborative and mission-driven people from all walks of life but with one goal: helping Canadian companies not just survive but thrive. And we’re looking for bold innovators to help shape the future of business finance in Canada.

AI Use in Our Hiring Process

We use technology, including artificial intelligence (AI), to support parts of our hiring process. This may include AI-assisted scheduling and candidate communications, and AI-generated interview notes, guides or summaries to help our team focus on the conversation. All hiring decisions are made by our hiring team.

About The Role

Float is building the financial operating system for Canadian businesses — corporate cards, business bank accounts, bill payments, all in one place. Reporting to the Head of Data, this role is embedded inside the product teams behind two of those core pillars: banking infrastructure and payments.

Both are high-complexity, high-stakes areas that generate a dense mix of financial, behavioral, and operational data. This role owns that data layer. You're building cohorts before anyone asks, designing measurement frameworks before features go to engineering, and debugging numbers to the source — pipeline issue, definition mismatch, or real product signal.

You're also someone who uses AI to move faster without losing rigour. Getting useful outputs from a model means knowing the business and data well enough to give it the right context — that's part of the job too.

What You'll Be Doing


Define what needs to be measured before features ship and support the instrumentation to measure it. You translate roadmap decisions into measurement requirements.
Design and get broad alignment on measurement frameworks before features go to engineering — hypothesis, cohorts, success criteria.

Debug ambiguous numbers to the source — pipeline issue, definition mismatch, or real product signal. You close the loop.

Use AI as a multiplier — accelerate analysis, generate first-pass SQL, and pressure-test hypotheses faster than you could alone. You know how to give a model the business and data context that makes its outputs impactful.
Take your share of ad-hoc requests on rotation — owning your product area gives the broader data team meaningful breathing room.


What Success Looks Like


Get Embedded: Both team leads treat you as the owner of their data layer. KPI definitions are agreed upon, documented, and tied to reliable dbt models. Gaps are flagged with a fix in motion.
Build the Measurement Layer: Features ship with measurement frameworks designed upfront. Dashboards for the core banking and payments metrics are clean, trusted, and traceable.
Drive Product Evidence: Product decisions are shaped by your analysis before they are made. When a team lead is asked by leadership whether a feature is landing, they already know — because you’ve built the infrastructure that makes the question answerable.


What We're Looking For


Product analytics depth — you've been embedded in a product team, own activation funnels and cohort retention natively, and know how to instrument a feature before it ships.
Experimentation and measurement design — you design A/B tests and quasi-experiments from the ground up: hypothesis, randomization unit, sample size and power calculations, guardrail metrics, and holdout design. You build pre/post and diff-in-diff analyses where randomization isn't feasible, and you know how to communicate the difference in confidence between the two
Data layer ownership — you're the DRI for data quality in your product area: auditing dbt models, writing briefs for analytics engineering, and debugging ambiguous numbers to the source.
Expert-level SQL — you build complex multi-step queries across large behavioral datasets, optimize for performance, and explain the logic to non-technical stakeholders.
Statistical reasoning — you understand distributions, confidence intervals, significance testing, and sample size constraints. You design for confounders, know when you have enough signal, and communicate uncertainty in plain language.
Data modeling literacy — you translate business logic into data requirements, specify the grain a table needs, and have informed opinions on how metrics get structured in dbt.
Comfort with imperfect data — you make a reasoned call, state your confidence level, and give stakeholders something actionable rather than waiting for clean infrastructure.
Stakeholder fluency — you work effectively across Product, Design, Engineering, and Product Marketing, and hold your ground respectfully when data contradicts what the team wants to believe.
AI as an accelerator — you've used LLMs to write SQL, draft frameworks, or structure analysis, and you know where they're reliable and where they need guardrails.
2–4+ years in product analytics, ideally embedded in a product team at a high-growth fintech or SaaS company.
BI tool experience — Metabase, Looker, Tableau, Sigma, or similar.
dbt-comfortable (reading models, understanding lineage) with Snowflake or a comparable cloud warehouse.
Fintech familiarity — attach rate, payment rails, KYC, AP workflows, pre-authorized payments, balance/deposit metrics, churn dynamics — is a meaningful differentiator.


Our Hiring Philosophy

We hire for character, curiosity, and learning velocity before credentials. Titles matter less than impact, and every hire is expected to raise the bar for Float as a whole.

Why You Should Join


Work at one of Canada's fastest-growing fintech companies
Make a real impact in a high-autonomy, high-growth role
Collaborate with an ambitious and supportive team
Competitive compensation, equity options, and benefits
Hybrid work model – we are based in Toronto with in-office days for connection and collaboration
Enjoy catered team lunches every Tuesday, Wednesday and Thursday
Bring your pup to our dog-friendly office
Thrive in a high-trust, high-performance culture where your work truly matters


In Short

At Float, you’ll thrive if you’re bold, curious, and eager to make a real impact. We're building something special—and having a lot of fun along the way. If you’re excited to build, grow, and win together, we’d love to meet you.

We’re committed to building a workplace that’s welcoming and accessible for everyone. If you need any accommodations during the hiring process or once you join Float, just let us know! You can reach out to Vic (victoria@floatfinancial.com), and we’ll work with you to make sure you have what you need to succeed.

Title: Business Intelligence Analyst
Description:
Job Summary

The Business Intelligence (BI) Analyst for the Specialty Reporting Services team will turn data into information, information into insight and insight into business decisions. The team member will have the ability to develop complex and appealing visualizations using a variety of BI Tools based on tightly established client turnaround times.

Duties And Responsibilities


Implementation of new reporting processes and procedures as required such as moving to automated reporting methods
Analyze reports for trends and variances across the programs and communicate findings
Design and prepare monthly, bimonthly or quarterly business reports including metrics/volumes and significant case summaries to clients using a variety of tools, including; Excel, Power BI, SSRS, PowerPoint
Convert data to usable information to drive growth, individual contract KPI’s and increased productivity
Act as the subject matter expert for specific Bayshore Specialty Rx Ltd data sets and act as a liaison with internal management and clients as required
Test and debug reporting and analysis using standard tools including but not limited to Excel,


Access, CRM and other database management tools/systems


Interpret data and develop actionable recommendations for business leaders
Identify, analyze, and interpret trends or patterns in complex data sets
Locate and define new process improvement opportunities
Ability to respond to customer inquiries and reporting needs efficiently as well as adapt to rapidly evolving client requirements
Knowledge of SQL queries to acquire data from primary and secondary data sources is desirable
Formulating and communicating meaningful and actionable process plans
Participate in ongoing internal and/or external continuing education activities
Adhere to Bayshore Policies and Procedure
Participate in quality activities and continuous improvement initiatives in keeping with the company’s Quality Management System
Participates in proactive Health & Safety activities while performing all duties. Is responsible to notify immediate Supervisor of any health and safety risks or concerns. Responsible for completing Accident Reports for all direct reports who injure themselves on the job, within 24 hours of the incident.
Maintains confidentiality of client and corporate information and discusses same only with appropriate Bayshore personnel


Qualifications

EDUCATION


Bachelor’s degree in information technology, statistics or data analysis


Experience


Expert level Power BI, Excel, and Dashboarding Skills
Minimum 2 years of experience working with complex data sets
Minimum 2 years of experience in analyzing and presenting results to stakeholders and clients
Minimum 2 years of experience with SQL and VBA is an asset
Data mining and in-depth knowledge of Data Science techniques is an assent
Experience working with pharmaceutical data is an asset


Other Skills And Abilities


Strong written and verbal communication skills


Primary Location

ON-Mississauga

Title: Analyst - Analytics Engineering - Toronto
Description:
Description

Location: Toronto Metropolitan Area

Base Salary: $95,000 – $105,000 (CAD)

Company Overview

Teragonia is the AI Operating System for Private Equity, helping sponsors and their portfolio companies turn fragmented operational data into decisions that move EBITDA. We operate in the highest-pressure segment of enterprise performance, where outcomes are time-bound by a 3 to 5 year hold period and execution velocity defines returns. Through Astradis™, our AI platform, we unify structured and unstructured data, generate prescriptive insights in near real time, and embed them directly into operating workflows with clear ownership and measurable impact . We partner with PE firms managing more than US$400B in assets and are rapidly expanding globally.

Our work is measured in EBITDA outcomes. Across PE portfolio companies, we drive 3% to 5% EBITDA uplift, accelerate roll-ups through standardized data and KPI frameworks, and maintain continuous exit readiness with high-fidelity performance instrumentation . We are replacing episodic consulting with a compounding AI platform that scales expertise across companies, industries, and ownership cycles. Teragonia is built for high-agency builders who want early responsibility, direct exposure to world's leading private equity firms and their portfolio CXOs, and the opportunity to help define the operating layer for value creation in the AI era.

Teragonia offers a comprehensive career development platform through cross-learning within our multidisciplinary structure. We nurture a diverse, inclusive, collaborative, respectful, and collegial environment. Our competitive compensation package aligns with the technology industry leaders, encompassing 401k contribution matching, medical insurance, and additional benefits.

Read more about our mission, vision, and values on our website:


About Teragonia: https://teragonia.com/company/
Our Values: https:// teragonia.com/careers/
Our Purpose: https://teragonia.com/value-orchestration/


Teragonia's Value Creation & Analytics (VC&A) Department Summary

Teragonia’s Value Creation & Analytics (VC&A) division bridges finance, consulting, and technology to support portfolio companies. We combine consulting rigor and AI to address complex challenges and enhance enterprise value through platform solutions and advisory. We prioritize professional growth through mentorship and cross-functional learning.

The Astradis platform provides real-time intelligence through a function-specific command center, combining structured and unstructured data via our Fibron AI layer. Our platform powers diagnostic dashboards, forecasting models, pricing tools, and operations modules, enabling clients to transition from reactive reporting to predictive strategy.

Requirements

Job Summary

We are looking for a talented Analytics Engineer to join our team as an Analyst. As an Analyst, you will work closely with our team of analytics engineers to design and develop advanced analytics solutions for our private equity-backed company clients. You will assist in understanding their business needs and developing tailored analytics solutions.

Responsibilities


Support a team of analytics engineers to design and develop advanced analytics solutions and be an active contributor to executing on engagement work
Assist Engagement Managers and Associates in working closely with clients to understand their business needs and develop tailored analytics solutions
Assist Engagement Managers and Associates in translating Client communicated business needs into an exploratory data analysis program and lead team efforts to execute
Assist analytics engineering teams in designing, developing, maintaining, and optimizing complex enterprise data models to help solve operational and financial business problems
Develop and maintain high-quality, visually appealing reports and dashboards to communicate complex analytics insights and recommendations to senior client executives, ensuring that findings are easy to understand and actionable by senior executives
Make a concerted effort to begin to develop and maintain strong relationships with clients and internal stakeholders
Participate in and assist in project meetings and contribute to project deliverables
Ability to work in a fast-paced environment, manage multiple projects simultaneously, and prioritize tasks to meet deadlines
Assist in developing and implementing process improvements to increase team efficiency and productivity
Collaborate with data engineers to design, develop, maintain, and optimize data pipelines critical to the integrity of the enterprise data models
Stay up-to-date with industry trends and best practices in data analytics and apply this knowledge to improve our solutions


Overview

As an Analyst, you will be a key contributor to our service delivery, innovation, and practice development. You will have the opportunity, and the management support, to develop specialized knowledge of data analytics, data science, and AI applications in a chosen industry or domain. You will possess excellent communication and presentation skills and be eager to develop as a key contributor in the Value Creation and Analytics (VC&A) Consulting division and develop executive presence as your career with us progresses to Associate, Manager, Principal, Director, and Managing Director levels. We will provide appropriate mentorship and company selected resources for your leadership skill development.

Technical Skills

As an Analyst, you will be quick to understand the client’s business needs and execute on delivering data-driven solutions. You will play a direct role in driving engagement workflows, deliverables, and timelines, ensuring the quality of enterprise-grade data pipelines and business intelligence applications. These efforts aim to enhance pricing, customer segmentation, sales effectiveness, customer lifetime value, attrition management, workforce scheduling, and cost optimization. You will be actively contributing to and delivering the team’s insights around the ideation, design and development of various analytics, data science and generative AI products, working hand-in-hand with our software engineers, data scientists, and AI engineers. 

The ideal candidate is a proactive problem-solver who takes full ownership of delivering high-quality client work. This individual thrives on delving into technical details to ensure the engagement team successfully meets deliverables by employing analytical techniques to develop crucial business insights for our clients. You will have excellent attention to detail and project management skills, essential for working effectively within our diverse, multidisciplinary team. As part of a collaborative group that includes business analysts, software engineers, data engineers, analytics engineers, data scientists, and AI engineers, you must demonstrate strong teamwork abilities. Your role will require not only individual excellence but also seamless integration and cooperation with team members to achieve shared goals and deliver impactful results.

Transforming low-hygiene data sets to develop organized databases with high fidelity is a necessary part of the job. The ideal candidate is an expert in data wrangling skills and possesses excellent knowledge of the software tools used for service delivery, including data manipulation and data visualization software. You will develop deep expertise in the Teragonia technology stack, for which we will provide appropriate training resources to the extent such tools are new to you. 

Engagement and Client Management

You could be delivering multiple client engagements simultaneously, or a large and complex engagement, and the scope of the engagement could evolve based on changing client needs. The financial sponsor universe demands actionable insights in a relatively short timeframe. The ideal candidate will be excited about driving critical client deliverables and solving a variety of business problems in parallel, in a fast-paced and dynamic environment.

The engagement planning and management are critical to maintaining high quality delivery and efficiency. You will actively support the Engagement Manager and Associate by delivering on the engagement plan and strategy and contribute your own ideas, asking questions, challenging the ideas and timelines presented to ensure that high quality client deliverables can be delivered in the timeframe our clients request them. You will also support the Engagement Manager and Associate with assessing the project management plan including resource allocation, tasks assignment, priorities, timelines, budget vs actual, fee updates, and more. 

You will have strong communication and presentation skills and be able to clearly articulate to the team, engagement leader(s) and the client, the business applications and impact of the data analytics assets delivered.

Other Key Responsibilities


Innovation – Support cross functional innovation initiatives
Specialization – Develop a sector or domain specialty
Recruitment – Actively contribute to Teragonia’s recruitment efforts to attract top talent
Marketing and business development – Assist executive leadership and officers with strategic marketing initiatives
Thought leadership - Assist with conducting research for thought leadership initiatives


Requirements

Necessary Qualifications


Bachelor’s degree in Business Analytics, Computer Science, Information Systems, Industrial Engineering, or a related field with stellar academic performance from leading universities
2 years of experience in analytics engineering, data engineering, software engineering or a related field at global consulting firms, top financial services firms, or high-growth startups
Strong interest in learning how analytics insights drive business value
Advanced programming skills in SQL and Python
Experience in performing exploratory data analysis in a professional setting
Experience with data visualization tools such as Tableau or PowerBI
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
Excellent communication and interpersonal skills


Preferred Qualifications


Experience with cloud-based analytics platforms and big data technologies such as Snowflake, GCP, DBT, FiveTran, Dataiku, etc.
High business acumen and advanced understanding of connectivity between operational data and financial data (ERP CRM PoS HRIS P&L / trial balance / journal entries, etc.)
Experience with data modeling methodologies and concepts (e.g., Kimball Modeling, Data Vault, Data Mesh, etc.)
Strong experience with data modeling
Certification in data science or analytics (e.g., Certified Analytics Professional)
Master's in Business Analytics, Computer Science, or similar advanced degree


Benefits

Canadian Benefits


Competitive Total Compensation - Market-benchmarked salary and bonus structure to attract and retain top talent.
Additional Compensation - Performance-based incentives and signing bonus, as applicable.
Visa Sponsorship - May be available for eligible candidates to join our diverse team subject to applicable laws and regulations in Canada.
RRSP Plan with Employer Contributions - We help secure your future with matching RRSP contributions up to 6.0% of your annual base salary.
WSIB Coverage - Workplace Safety and Insurance Board coverage as required by Ontario law.
Professional Development Opportunities - We will sponsor company-selected training sessions, conferences, and certifications to foster your growth.
Meal Stipend - Reimbursement for lunch at the office, and dinner too if you are working past 7 pm, up to a limit.
Fitness-Wellness Stipend - For fitness & wellness programs, such as gym memberships, up to a limit.
Paid Time Off (PTO) - Eligible for 15 to 20 days PTO based on applicable level. Vacation pay is calculated as required by the Ontario Employment Standards Act. Vacation time may be carried over up to a maximum allowable under ESA requirements.
Statutory Holidays - ESA statutory holidays as well as additional company holidays: New Year’s Day, Family Day, Good Friday, Victoria Day, Canada Day, Labour Day, Thanksgiving, Christmas Day, Boxing Day, Easter Monday, Civic Holiday, Remembrance Day
Comprehensive Healthcare, Vision, and Dental Insurance - We prioritize your health and wellness with insurance coverage through leading Canadian providers equivalent to or exceeding provincial requirements.
Fertility and family-building benefits through leading Canadian healthcare providers.
Flexible Benefits Plan - We provide the opportunity to manage your healthcare expenses efficiently through Canadian tax-advantaged benefit plans where available.
Mental Health Support - Access to mental health support through Canadian mental health providers covered under our healthcare plan.
Health Navigation Services - Expert assistance to help you navigate the complexities of the Canadian healthcare system.
Disability Insurance - We provide short term disability insurance through a leading Canadian provider covering up to 26 weeks at 60% of weekly earnings up to $1,500, exceeding Employment Insurance requirements.
Sick Days - 5 paid sick days annually that reset each year, as required under the Ontario Employment Standards Act. Additional unpaid sick leave available as per ESA requirements.
Leaves of Absence - Teragonia provides unpaid statutory leaves as required by the Ontario Employment Standards Act, 2000 (ESA), including pregnancy, parental, caregiver, and bereavement leave. Eligibility and duration follow provincial regulations. Additional unpaid leaves or flexible arrangements may be considered on a case-by-case basis.
Paid Parental Leave - Paid Parental Leave of 12 weeks after 6 months of service with the company.


Flexible Work Policy

We promote flexible work to support wellbeing and performance. Remote work is available though in-person collaboration is encouraged for planning and team connection. Toronto based employees have no mandated office days but are expected in person for key meetings. Employees outside Toronto may work remotely with occasional travel for events. Early career professionals with less than three years of experience should live within commuting distance to receive in person mentorship. Relocation support is available on a case-by-case basis.

Working Hours and ESA Exemption

This position is classified as a specialized professional role under O. Reg. 285/01, s. 2 of Ontario's Employment Standards Act, 2000 (ESA) and is therefore exempt from overtime pay requirements. While standard hours of work are expected to be approximately 40 hours per week, additional hours may be required to meet client deliverable sand business needs. Such expectations are reflected in the annual salary.

Canadian Experience

We welcome applications from all qualified candidates. Canadian work experience is not a requirement for this position.

Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion Statement

We are an equal opportunity employer and are committed to providing a respectful, inclusive, harassment free workplace. We hire based on merit and qualifications in line with the Ontario Human Rights Code and Employment Standards Act. We welcome applicants from all backgrounds.

Accommodation For Disabilities

We are committed to dignity, independence, inclusion, and equal opportunity under the Ontario Human Rights Code and AODA. We provide reasonable accommodations during recruitment and employment. Employees needing support should contact their manager or Human Resources representative.

Retention Notice

In compliance with Ontario's employment standards, Teragonia retains copies of all publicly advertised job postings and associated application forms for a period of three years.

Recruiting Process

Our recruitment process is designed to ensure a thoughtful and comprehensive evaluation of candidates, as well as alignment with the team’s objectives and company values. Candidates are encouraged to ask questions and engage openly throughout. The process typically involves the following stages:


Initial Interviews with Managers – Two interviews focused on fit and goals
Technical Interviews with Principals – One or two sessions assessing skills and problem-solving
Final Interview with Founders – Discuss strategic alignment and long-term potential


AI Use Disclosure

We use artificial Intelligence (AI) tools to support the screening and assessment.

Accessibility in Recruitment

We are committed to accessibility. If you require accommodation during any stage of the recruitment process, please contact us in advance. We will support you in accordance with the Ontario Human Rights Code and the Accessibility for Ontarians with Disabilities Act (AODA).

  • Our Values: https:// teragonia.com/careers/
  • Our Purpose: https://teragonia.com/value-orchestration/

Teragonia's Value Creation & Analytics (VC&A) Department Summary

Teragonia’s Value Creation & Analytics (VC&A) division bridges finance, consulting, and technology to support portfolio companies. We combine consulting rigor and AI to address complex challenges and enhance enterprise value through platform solutions and advisory. We prioritize professional growth through mentorship and cross-functional learning.

The Astradis platform provides real-time intelligence through a function-specific command center, combining structured and unstructured data via our Fibron AI layer. Our platform powers diagnostic dashboards, forecasting models, pricing tools, and operations modules, enabling clients to transition from reactive reporting to predictive strategy.

Requirements

Job Summary

We are looking for a talented Analytics Engineer to join our team as an Analyst. As an Analyst, you will work closely with our team of analytics engineers to design and develop advanced analytics solutions for our private equity-backed company clients. You will assist in understanding their business needs and developing tailored analytics solutions.

Responsibilities

  • Support a team of analytics engineers to design and develop advanced analytics solutions and be an active contributor to executing on engagement work
  • Assist Engagement Managers and Associates in working closely with clients to understand their business needs and develop tailored analytics solutions
  • Assist Engagement Managers and Associates in translating Client communicated business needs into an exploratory data analysis program and lead team efforts to execute
  • Assist analytics engineering teams in designing, developing, maintaining, and optimizing complex enterprise data models to help solve operational and financial business problems
  • Develop and maintain high-quality, visually appealing reports and dashboards to communicate complex analytics insights and recommendations to senior client executives, ensuring that findings are easy to understand and actionable by senior executives
  • Make a concerted effort to begin to develop and maintain strong relationships with clients and internal stakeholders
  • Participate in and assist in project meetings and contribute to project deliverables
  • Ability to work in a fast-paced environment, manage multiple projects simultaneously, and prioritize tasks to meet deadlines
  • Assist in developing and implementing process improvements to increase team efficiency and productivity
  • Collaborate with data engineers to design, develop, maintain, and optimize data pipelines critical to the integrity of the enterprise data models
  • Stay up-to-date with industry trends and best practices in data analytics and apply this knowledge to improve our solutions

Overview

As an Analyst, you will be a key contributor to our service delivery, innovation, and practice development. You will have the opportunity, and the management support, to develop specialized knowledge of data analytics, data science, and AI applications in a chosen industry or domain. You will possess excellent communication and presentation skills and be eager to develop as a key contributor in the Value Creation and Analytics (VC&A) Consulting division and develop executive presence as your career with us progresses to Associate, Manager, Principal, Director, and Managing Director levels. We will provide appropriate mentorship and company selected resources for your leadership skill development.

Technical Skills

As an Analyst, you will be quick to understand the client’s business needs and execute on delivering data-driven solutions. You will play a direct role in driving engagement workflows, deliverables, and timelines, ensuring the quality of enterprise-grade data pipelines and business intelligence applications. These efforts aim to enhance pricing, customer segmentation, sales effectiveness, customer lifetime value, attrition management, workforce scheduling, and cost optimization. You will be actively contributing to and delivering the team’s insights around the ideation, design and development of various analytics, data science and generative AI products, working hand-in-hand with our software engineers, data scientists, and AI engineers. 

The ideal candidate is a proactive problem-solver who takes full ownership of delivering high-quality client work. This individual thrives on delving into technical details to ensure the engagement team successfully meets deliverables by employing analytical techniques to develop crucial business insights for our clients. You will have excellent attention to detail and project management skills, essential for working effectively within our diverse, multidisciplinary team. As part of a collaborative group that includes business analysts, software engineers, data engineers, analytics engineers, data scientists, and AI engineers, you must demonstrate strong teamwork abilities. Your role will require not only individual excellence but also seamless integration and cooperation with team members to achieve shared goals and deliver impactful results.

Transforming low-hygiene data sets to develop organized databases with high fidelity is a necessary part of the job. The ideal candidate is an expert in data wrangling skills and possesses excellent knowledge of the software tools used for service delivery, including data manipulation and data visualization software. You will develop deep expertise in the Teragonia technology stack, for which we will provide appropriate training resources to the extent such tools are new to you. 

Engagement and Client Management

You could be delivering multiple client engagements simultaneously, or a large and complex engagement, and the scope of the engagement could evolve based on changing client needs. The financial sponsor universe demands actionable insights in a relatively short timeframe. The ideal candidate will be excited about driving critical client deliverables and solving a variety of business problems in parallel, in a fast-paced and dynamic environment.

The engagement planning and management are critical to maintaining high quality delivery and efficiency. You will actively support the Engagement Manager and Associate by delivering on the engagement plan and strategy and contribute your own ideas, asking questions, challenging the ideas and timelines presented to ensure that high quality client deliverables can be delivered in the timeframe our clients request them. You will also support the Engagement Manager and Associate with assessing the project management plan including resource allocation, tasks assignment, priorities, timelines, budget vs actual, fee updates, and more. 

You will have strong communication and presentation skills and be able to clearly articulate to the team, engagement leader(s) and the client, the business applications and impact of the data analytics assets delivered.

Other Key Responsibilities

  • Innovation – Support cross functional innovation initiatives
  • Specialization – Develop a sector or domain specialty
  • Recruitment – Actively contribute to Teragonia’s recruitment efforts to attract top talent
  • Marketing and business development – Assist executive leadership and officers with strategic marketing initiatives
  • Thought leadership - Assist with conducting research for thought leadership initiatives

Requirements

Necessary Qualifications

  • Bachelor’s degree in Business Analytics, Computer Science, Information Systems, Industrial Engineering, or a related field with stellar academic performance from leading universities
  • 2 years of experience in analytics engineering, data engineering, software engineering or a related field at global consulting firms, top financial services firms, or high-growth startups
  • Strong interest in learning how analytics insights drive business value
  • Advanced programming skills in SQL and Python
  • Experience in performing exploratory data analysis in a professional setting
  • Experience with data visualization tools such as Tableau or PowerBI
  • Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
  • Excellent communication and interpersonal skills

Preferred Qualifications

  • Experience with cloud-based analytics platforms and big data technologies such as Snowflake, GCP, DBT, FiveTran, Dataiku, etc.
    • High business acumen and advanced understanding of connectivity between operational data and financial data (ERP CRM PoS HRIS P&L / trial balance / journal entries, etc.)
  • Experience with data modeling methodologies and concepts (e.g., Kimball Modeling, Data Vault, Data Mesh, etc.)
  • Strong experience with data modeling
  • Certification in data science or analytics (e.g., Certified Analytics Professional)
  • Master's in Business Analytics, Computer Science, or similar advanced degree

    • Benefits

    Canadian Benefits

    • Competitive Total Compensation - Market-benchmarked salary and bonus structure to attract and retain top talent.
    • Additional Compensation - Performance-based incentives and signing bonus, as applicable.
    • Visa Sponsorship - May be available for eligible candidates to join our diverse team subject to applicable laws and regulations in Canada.
    • RRSP Plan with Employer Contributions - We help secure your future with matching RRSP contributions up to 6.0% of your annual base salary.
    • WSIB Coverage - Workplace Safety and Insurance Board coverage as required by Ontario law.
    • Professional Development Opportunities - We will sponsor company-selected training sessions, conferences, and certifications to foster your growth.
    • Meal Stipend - Reimbursement for lunch at the office, and dinner too if you are working past 7 pm, up to a limit.
    • Fitness-Wellness Stipend - For fitness & wellness programs, such as gym memberships, up to a limit.
    • Paid Time Off (PTO) - Eligible for 15 to 20 days PTO based on applicable level. Vacation pay is calculated as required by the Ontario Employment Standards Act. Vacation time may be carried over up to a maximum allowable under ESA requirements.
    • Statutory Holidays - ESA statutory holidays as well as additional company holidays: New Year’s Day, Family Day, Good Friday, Victoria Day, Canada Day, Labour Day, Thanksgiving, Christmas Day, Boxing Day, Easter Monday, Civic Holiday, Remembrance Day
    • Comprehensive Healthcare, Vision, and Dental Insurance - We prioritize your health and wellness with insurance coverage through leading Canadian providers equivalent to or exceeding provincial requirements.
    • Fertility and family-building benefits through leading Canadian healthcare providers.
    • Flexible Benefits Plan - We provide the opportunity to manage your healthcare expenses efficiently through Canadian tax-advantaged benefit plans where available.
    • Mental Health Support - Access to mental health support through Canadian mental health providers covered under our healthcare plan.
    • Health Navigation Services - Expert assistance to help you navigate the complexities of the Canadian healthcare system.
    • Disability Insurance - We provide short term disability insurance through a leading Canadian provider covering up to 26 weeks at 60% of weekly earnings up to $1,500, exceeding Employment Insurance requirements.
    • Sick Days - 5 paid sick days annually that reset each year, as required under the Ontario Employment Standards Act. Additional unpaid sick leave available as per ESA requirements.
    • Leaves of Absence - Teragonia provides unpaid statutory leaves as required by the Ontario Employment Standards Act, 2000 (ESA), including pregnancy, parental, caregiver, and bereavement leave. Eligibility and duration follow provincial regulations. Additional unpaid leaves or flexible arrangements may be considered on a case-by-case basis.
    • Paid Parental Leave - Paid Parental Leave of 12 weeks after 6 months of service with the company.

    Flexible Work Policy

    We promote flexible work to support wellbeing and performance. Remote work is available though in-person collaboration is encouraged for planning and team connection. Toronto based employees have no mandated office days but are expected in person for key meetings. Employees outside Toronto may work remotely with occasional travel for events. Early career professionals with less than three years of experience should live within commuting distance to receive in person mentorship. Relocation support is available on a case-by-case basis.

    Working Hours and ESA Exemption

    This position is classified as a specialized professional role under O. Reg. 285/01, s. 2 of Ontario's Employment Standards Act, 2000 (ESA) and is therefore exempt from overtime pay requirements. While standard hours of work are expected to be approximately 40 hours per week, additional hours may be required to meet client deliverable sand business needs. Such expectations are reflected in the annual salary.

    Canadian Experience

    We welcome applications from all qualified candidates. Canadian work experience is not a requirement for this position.

    Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion Statement

    We are an equal opportunity employer and are committed to providing a respectful, inclusive, harassment free workplace. We hire based on merit and qualifications in line with the Ontario Human Rights Code and Employment Standards Act. We welcome applicants from all backgrounds.

    Accommodation For Disabilities

    We are committed to dignity, independence, inclusion, and equal opportunity under the Ontario Human Rights Code and AODA. We provide reasonable accommodations during recruitment and employment. Employees needing support should contact their manager or Human Resources representative.

    Retention Notice

    In compliance with Ontario's employment standards, Teragonia retains copies of all publicly advertised job postings and associated application forms for a period of three years.

    Recruiting Process

    Our recruitment process is designed to ensure a thoughtful and comprehensive evaluation of candidates, as well as alignment with the team’s objectives and company values. Candidates are encouraged to ask questions and engage openly throughout. The process typically involves the following stages:

    • Initial Interviews with Managers – Two interviews focused on fit and goals
    • Technical Interviews with Principals – One or two sessions assessing skills and problem-solving
    • Final Interview with Founders – Discuss strategic alignment and long-term potential

    AI Use Disclosure

    We use artificial Intelligence (AI) tools to support the screening and assessment.

    Accessibility in Recruitment

    We are committed to accessibility. If you require accommodation during any stage of the recruitment process, please contact us in advance. We will support you in accordance with the Ontario Human Rights Code and the Accessibility for Ontarians with Disabilities Act (AODA).

Title: BI & Data Analyst (12 Months Contract)
Description:
Choose a workplace that empowers your impact.

Join a global workplace where employees thrive. One that embraces diversity of thought, expertise and passion. A place where you can personalize your employee journey to be — and deliver — your best.

We are a leading global real estate investor, developer and manager. We combine our capital with our capabilities to create real estate that strengthens economies and communities. By prioritizing people, partnerships and places, we generate meaningful returns for OMERS members, enhance value for our capital partners and create a brighter world for our customers.

Join us to accelerate your growth & development, prioritize wellness, build connections, and support the communities where we live and work.

Don’t just work anywhere — come build tomorrow together with us.

Know someone at OMERS or Oxford Properties? Great! If you're referred, have them submit your name through Workday first. Then, watch for a unique link in your email to apply.

The Analyst, Business Intelligence acts as a business partner to our finance stakeholders, performing data analysis using a wide variety of applications (including Power BI and SQL), and driving automation effort. To succeed in this role, candidates must be able to drive project completion with strong communication and project management, combined with experience in data analytics, big data mining, and data flow and structure. This role will require effective customer service management, with a proven ability to build relationships between the business groups, financial team and IT department, while being able to multitask in a complex environment.

As a Member Of This Team, You Will


Collaborate with management teams to prioritize business, analytics and information needs and understand project requirements
Work with financial and operational data from primary or secondary data sources from different stakeholder teams and business functions
Seek efficiencies in existing processes and execute process improvement initiatives, including exploring and identifying potential new tools and technologies for enhancing reporting automation capabilities
Identify, analyze, interpret, and report on trends or patterns in complex data sets
Exploring large data sets and drawing meaningful conclusions and insights, including identifying critical metrics and KPIs
Filter and “clean” data by reviewing extracts, reports, printouts, and performance indicators to identify, troubleshoot and resolve problems, including data quality, integrity issues and follow data governance policies
Assist with the preparation and development of reporting, presentations and various materials for discussion at the executive level


To Succeed In This Role, You Have


Undergraduate degree in Accounting, Mathematics, Computer Science or a related field.
Have a minimum of 3+ years of demonstrated experience in a similar role (e.g. Data Analyst or BI Data Analyst).
Team oriented individual with strong communication skills, who is comfortable engaging with different groups within the organization (including non-technical audiences).
Thrives in dynamic environments by adapting to evolving requirements and refining approaches as new information emerges, even when processes are not fully defined.
Strong project management and organizational skills and ability to work under pressure and manage high priority projects with multiple or competing deadlines.
Willingness to learn new technologies, including RPA, with a strong inclination to improve processes and not settle for ‘status quo’ to generate efficiencies and improve capabilities.
Experience with Power BI, SQL, and advanced Excel functionality (including Power Query) to develop automation solutions. Experience with other BI Tools and VBA would be an asset.
Technical expertise in data mining and analysis, with an understanding of financial reporting concepts.
Demonstrate strong problem-solving skills and innovative approaches for resolution.


We believe that time together in the office is important for OMERS and Oxford, the strength of our employees, and the work we do for our pension members. In delivering on our pension promise, keeping us connected to our work and each other, our flexible hybrid work guideline requires teams to come in to the office 4 days per week.

This posting is for an existing vacancy.

The expected salary range for this position is $72,000.00 - $108,000.00 per year, prorated based on the term of the contract.

You may also be eligible to receive an annual Incentive Award pursuant to our Short-term Incentive plan and our Long-Term Incentive plan (if applicable), and to participate in our group benefits and retirement plans – details on these elements of compensation are included within OMERS & Oxford offer letters.

Oxford's purpose is to strengthen economies and communities through real estate.

Our people-first culture is at its best when our workforce reflects the communities where we live and work — and the customers we proudly serve.

From hire to retire, we are an equal opportunity employer committed to an inclusive, barrier-free recruitment and selection process that extends all the way through your employee experience. This sense of belonging and connection is cultivated up, down and across our global organization thanks to our vast network of Employee Resource Groups with executive leader sponsorship, our Purpose@Work committee and employee recognition programs.

Artificial intelligence (AI) tools are used to support certain stages of the OMERS recruitment process. While AI assists us in our process, human judgment and decision-making remain central to our candidate experience.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
About GoBolt

Our Mission:

At GoBolt, we provide a technology-driven, customer-centric and sustainable approach to ecommerce and last-mile logistics for businesses of all sizes – from local brands to large national retailers. By operating as an extension of our customer's team, we deliver best-in-class services every step of the way. We're building North America’s largest electric vehicle fleet to help bring sustainability to an industry notorious for its heavy impact on the environment. We are in high growth mode and building for the future, which is where you come in!

We are on the lookout for a skilled, operationally minded, Data Analyst. This role will play a critical role in building and owning dashboards, metrics, and data models to support our operations and account management teams.

You will be responsible for deeply understanding our core metrics and ensuring we have actionable and insightful dashboards and data models to support our business. This includes dashboard development, data modelling, data cleanup, and ad-hoc analysis and reporting. This role will work very closely with our operations team and require some time spent in our warehouses.

Base Salary Range: 70k-80k

We are actively hiring to fill this specific position immediately.

Hybrid role based in Downtown Toronto

What You'll Do

Develop and Maintain Our Data Platform:


Collaborate with cross-functional teams to design, develop, and maintain our analytics platform.
Identify and source new data to enrich our existing data platform.
Partner with our product and engineering teams to build data pipelines and integrations for seamless data ingestion.
Implement data governance practices to ensure data quality, consistency, and security.


Measure And Report On Key Performance Indicators (KPIs)


Maintain our source of truth documentation for KPI definitions and data sources across the business.
Ensure core dashboards are well-maintained and groomed to reduce conflicting information, streamline data usage, and drive actionable insights.
Conduct ad-hoc analysis to support operational efficiency and revenue retention.


Troubleshoot And Clean Up Data


Monitor the data platform for bugs, anomalies, or data quality issues.
Investigate and troubleshoot issues related to data accuracy, completeness, or consistency.
Conduct data cleaning and normalization to ensure data integrity and reliability.


What We're Looking For


1-2 years of experience as a data analyst or in a similar role.
Strong proficiency in SQL for querying, transforming, and manipulating data
Experience with a Business Intelligence tool: You are comfortable building dashboards and reports in BI tools(Looker, Tableau, Mode, Metabase etc.). We use Looker and Metabase at GoBolt
A background in Computer Science, Analytics, or a related field.
Excellent communication skills to collaborate effectively with cross-functional teams and explain complex concepts to non-technical stakeholders.
A willingness to go deep into our operations to understand processes and metrics and help shape their development over time.


Bonus Points For


Experience with operations and logistics, having handled data related to inventory, warehousing, last-mile delivery, and transportation.
Familiarity with data modeling concepts and best practices, especially within a supply chain or logistics context.
Experience with dbt (data build tool) for modular and version-controlled data transformations
Proficiency in Python and scripting, including integrating data from external sources.
Familiarity with version control, with a working knowledge of Git for collaborative development.
Experience with cloud computing platforms like GCP or AWS


Why Join Us?

We are innovative, goal-oriented, and are always on the search for energetic and passionate people who are eager to learn and would like to have a rare opportunity to grow within one of Canada's fastest-growing companies.

We offer a workplace where respect, diversity, and inclusion are the cornerstones of our culture. Our team members are encouraged to bring their authentic selves to work, contributing to our collective mission in their unique ways. Aside from an amazing culture, we also offer competitive salaries and a comprehensive benefits package including health, dental, and vision plans for you and your family.

If you are eager to make a difference and contribute to a fast paced exciting environment, we would love to hear from you.

GoBolt is an equal-opportunity employer that recognizes the value and importance of a diverse workforce. GoBolt is committed to providing assistance during all phases of the employment cycle, including recruiting through to the end of the employment relationship. If you require accommodation because of injury, illness, or disability during any portion of GoBolt’s recruiting process, please let us know by contacting your recruiter's point of contact, and we will do everything we can to accommodate you.

At GoBolt, we value innovation and efficiency. In line with Ontario’s Working for Workers Act, we utilize AI-powered tools to assist in the screening and initial assessment of candidates for this position. This helps us focus on your skills and reduces bias. While AI helps us get started, your journey with us is guided by real people. Every final decision is made by a member of our team who values the unique perspective you bring.

Title: IT - Business Intellience Analyst
Description:
At Achev, we believe that everyone deserves an equal opportunity to build a better life and a successful future. For more than 30 years, we have dedicated ourselves to helping diverse Canadians and newcomers achieve their full potential. Today, we are one of the largest providers of employment, newcomer, language, and youth services in the Greater Toronto Area. Our dedicated team of nearly 400 staff delivers a wide range of innovative, high-quality, and personalized programs and services that empower our clients to prosper and our communities to thrive.We thrive in a OneAchev culture where collaboration and an integrated service delivery will positively impact Achev’s resiliency, agility, success, and growth.

Join us in helping diverse Canadians and newcomers achieve their full potential!

Achev’s latest Impact Report: www.Achev.ca

Summary

The Business Intelligence Analyst is responsible for designing and delivering impactful, user-focused dashboards and insights that drive data-informed decision making. This role combines strong technical expertise with data storytelling, translating complex data into clear, actionable insights for business stakeholders.

The ideal candidate has a passion for creating visually compelling dashboards, a deep understanding of data, and the ability to confidently communicate findings to both technical and non-technical audiencies.

Duties And Responsibilities


Design, develop, and maintain intuitive, visually compelling Power BI dashboards and reports that drive business decision-making
Translate business requirements into meaningful metrics, KPIs, and data models, ensuring alignment with organizational goals
Apply data visualization best practices to create dashboards that are clear, user-friendly, and optimized for usability and engagement
Partner with business stakeholders to understand reporting needs, gather requirements, and deliver actionable insights
Present data insights and recommendations to technical and non-technical audiences, including leadership
Prototype and iterate dashboard solutions based on user feedback and evolving business needs
Ensure data accuracy, consistency, and integrity across reports and data sources
Work closely with DBAs and data engineers to define and optimize data models, data pipelines, and ERDs
Write and optimize SQL queries to extract, transform, and analyze data from multiple sources
Monitor and maintain Power BI environments, including report deployment and performance optimization
Manage task priorities, timelines, and deliverables while communicating progress and risks effectively

Strong analytical thinking and problem-solving skills, with attention to detail and data accuracy
Excellent interpersonal, communication, and presentation skills

Preferred / Nice to Have:

Familiarity with UX/UI principles in dashboard design

Title: Analyst, Data Management
Description:
Location

Brookfield Place - 181 Bay Street

Brookfield Culture

Brookfield has a unique and dynamic culture. We seek team members who have a long-term focus and whose values align with our Attributes of a Brookfield Leader: Entrepreneurial, Collaborative and Disciplined. Brookfield is committed to the development of our people through challenging work assignments and exposure to diverse businesses.

Job Description

The Analyst, Data Management is responsible for ensuring the completeness, reliability, and controls of investment-related data and records. This information is relied upon by the Investments, Accounting, Risk, and Legal teams to support reporting, compliance, and decision-making. The role involves maintaining data quality, implementing control processes, coordinating updates across systems, and ensuring that information is consistent, timely, and aligned with internal policies and regulatory requirements. The analyst collaborates with cross-functional teams to resolve discrepancies, strengthen reporting capabilities, and enhance data management workflows.

Responsibilities


Oversee the execution and maintenance of business and control processes that ensure security master records are properly established, maintained, and delivered to relevant stakeholders in a complete, reliable, and timely manner.
Supports the identification and resolution of business process gaps that hinder the Operation’s team from delivering reliable daily security master reference data.
Collaborates with other teams to assess and prioritize data needs and deficiencies and communicates with senior management to ensure requirements are addressed and gaps are resolved in a timely and effective manner.
Contributes to cross-functional and organization-wide initiatives, such as Aladdin MVP, focused on enhancing the overall quality of security data.
Partners with internal teams and external auditors to enhance the design and effectiveness of existing control frameworks, and to recommend and implement additional controls where necessary.
Executes additional tasks and responsibilities as required.


Qualifications And Requirements


Bachelor’s degree (B.A.) with at least two years of relevant experience.
Experience with order management systems (e.g., Aladdin), data vendors (e.g., Bloomberg, Refinitiv), reconciliation tools (e.g., DUCO), and accounting systems (e.g., Clearwater).
Knowledge of private assets, fixed income, and structured products is preferred.
Experienced in handling and organizing large volumes of data.
Proficient in Microsoft Excel, including Macros, to analyze and manipulate data efficiently.
Understands regulatory-driven asset classification and its impact on rating and reporting processes
Strong communication and interpersonal skills, including verbal, written, and presentation abilities, with a proven ability to work effectively with colleagues, clients, and external partners.
Detail-oriented and analytical, skilled at reviewing data, solving practical problems, and drawing accurate conclusions.
Highly organized and adaptable, able to manage multiple priorities, meet deadlines, and perform under pressure in a fast-paced environment.
Technical proficiency, including Microsoft Office, SQL, and the preparation of reports, business correspondence, and procedural documentation.


Salary Range: $65,000 - $80,000 CAD

Position Opening Reason:

New Position

Brookfield is committed to creating an accessible and inclusive organization. We are committed to providing barrier-free and accessible employment practices in compliance with the Accessibility for Ontarians with Disabilities Act. Should you require a Human Rights Code-protected accommodation through any stage of the recruitment process, please make them known when contacted and we will work with you to meet your needs.

Title: Product Data Analyst
Description:
Wand makes gaming magical. Through game customization and guidance, we build tools that helps players have more fun in their favorite games.

Our platform works across thousands of PC games, ensuring that great games are accessible to everyone, regardless of time constraints, skill level, or accessibility needs. We want to build the future of game assistance, and we're hoping you'll join us.

About The Role

We're hiring a Product Analyst to own the metrics that matter most and chase down the questions nobody else has time for. You'll partner with Product, Growth, Marketing, and Finance — not as a service desk, but as a peer who pushes back when the question being asked isn't the right one.

This is a senior IC role. You'll set the analytical bar, define the metrics frameworks the rest of the team relies on, and run the experiments that decide what ships. Our data engineering team owns the pipelines; you'll focus on the analysis, the recommendation, and the decision that follows.

What You'll Own

The metrics that run the business. You'll own the dashboards and definitions Product, Growth, Marketing, and Finance rely on every day. When a number moves, you'll know before anyone else — and you'll know why. You'll build in Hex, document in our spec, and enforce consistent definitions across teams.

Deep investigation. When retention dips or a funnel leaks, you won't stop at the symptom. You'll follow the thread — through cohorts, segments, the quirks of specific games — until you can name the cause and recommend a fix. You'll notice when a chart doesn't look right and you won't leave it alone.

Experimentation. You'll design and analyze A/B tests on product changes, pricing, onboarding, and growth initiatives. You'll set sample sizes, call stat sig, and write the readouts. When a result is noisy or a test is underpowered, you'll say so — even when the PM is ready to ship.

Strategic partnership. You'll work directly with our leaders on the questions that don't have obvious answers. What are players actually doing in Game Guide? Which customizations predict long-term retention? Why does Wand Pro convert better in some game categories than others? You'll frame the question, run the analysis, and deliver the recommendation.

What You'll Be Measured On

The decisions you unblocked. The metrics you improved. The bad ideas you killed before they shipped. The questions you answered before anyone thought to ask them.

Minimum Qualifications


5 years in a Data Analyst, Product Analyst, or Business Intelligence role (excluding internships)
SQL fluency — complex joins, incremental computation, window functions, query optimization; comfortable with large, messy datasets
Python for analysis — pandas and statistical libraries; you don't freeze when the answer requires code
Experimentation chops — hypothesis testing, sample sizing, and the judgment to tell a p-value from an insight
A track record of moving metrics you cared about (we'll ask you to walk us through one)
Direct communication — you can tell a leader an uncomfortable answer without burying it in caveats


Preferred qualifications


Degree in a quantitative field
Hex, dbt, and Bigquery experience
Prior growth-stage consumer software or gaming experience
Causal inference methods (diff-in-diff, regression discontinuity, propensity matching)
Prior work on LTV, retention, or subscription models
Experience building metrics frameworks or KPI hierarchies from scratch


How We Work

Fully remote, with a few hours of daily overlap across US time zones. We ship fast, write things down, and don't run meetings that could have been a Loom. We play games. You probably should, too.

Join us in building the future of PC gaming.

Wand is an equal opportunity employer. We build tools for every kind of player, and we hire the same way.

Compensation Range: $145K - $175K

Description:
Job Summary

As a Report Developer is responsible for writing complex scripts to extract data for the purposes of building client reports together with Microsoft CRM development skills to customize the current database to support future reporting requirements.

Duties And Responsibilities


Design forms, workflow processes, web services and other components needed to meet the business requirements of the departmental customers utilizing Microsoft CRM
Assist and support other programmers in development, techniques and coding assignments
Develop efficient data solutions through MS application stack including MS SQL Management Studio, SSRS, SSIS, SSAS, Power BI, Data repository, Data Lake reporting solutions
Acquire data from primary or secondary data sources and maintain databases/data systems

Generating and distributing reports internally and externally
