Pay Transparency - North America

Compensation And Benefits

The ranges listed below are what EA in good faith expects to pay applicants for this role in these locations at the time of this posting. If you reside in a different location, a recruiter will advise on the applicable range and benefits. Pay offered will be determined based on a number of relevant business and candidate factors (e.g. education, qualifications, certifications, experience, skills, geographic location, or business needs).

PAY RANGES

  • British Columbia (depending on location e.g. Vancouver vs. Victoria) *$122,300 - $170,700 CAD

Pay is just one part of the overall compensation at EA.

For Canada, we offer a package of benefits including vacation (3 weeks per year to start), 10 days per year of sick time, paid top-up to EI/QPIP benefits up to 100% of base salary when you welcome a new child (12 weeks for maternity, and 4 weeks for parental/adoption leave), extended health/dental/vision coverage, life insurance, disability insurance, retirement plan to regular full-time employees. Certain roles may also be eligible for bonus and equity.

About Electronic Arts

We’re proud to have an extensive portfolio of games and experiences, locations around the world, and opportunities across EA. We value adaptability, resilience, creativity, and curiosity. From leadership that brings out your potential, to creating space for learning and experimenting, we empower you to do great work and pursue opportunities for growth.

We adopt a holistic approach to our benefits programs, emphasizing physical, emotional, financial, career, and community wellness to support a balanced life. Our packages are tailored to meet local needs and may include healthcare coverage, mental well-being support, retirement savings, paid time off, family leaves, complimentary games, and more. We nurture environments where our teams can always bring their best to what they do.

Electronic Arts is an equal opportunity employer. All employment decisions are made without regard to race, color, national origin, ancestry, sex, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, age, genetic information, religion, disability, medical condition, pregnancy, marital status, family status, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law. We will also consider employment qualified applicants with criminal records in accordance with applicable law. EA also makes workplace accommodations for qualified individuals with disabilities as required by applicable law.

Title: Data Security & DLP Analyst
Description:
Data Security & DLP Analyst (AI Training)

About The Role

At Alignerr, we partner with the world's leading AI research teams and labs to build and train cutting-edge AI models. We're looking for data security professionals who understand how sensitive information is exposed, mishandled, and protected in the real world.

This is a unique opportunity to apply your hands-on security expertise to a high-impact problem: teaching AI systems to reason accurately about data risk, DLP controls, and information security scenarios. Your real-world knowledge directly shapes how frontier AI understands and responds to data security challenges.


Organization: Alignerr
Type: Hourly Contract
Location: Remote
Commitment: 10–40 hours/week


What You'll Do


Analyze realistic data security and DLP scenarios spanning cloud, SaaS, and enterprise environments
Classify data sensitivity levels, exposure pathways, and policy violations
Evaluate prevention, detection, and incident response strategies for accuracy and completeness
Generate, label, and validate data security cases used to train and benchmark AI systems
Apply your practical understanding of how data risk plays out across modern organizations


Who You Are


2+ years of experience in data security, compliance, security operations, or a related field
Hands-on familiarity with DLP tools, data classification frameworks, and privacy or regulatory concepts (e.g., GDPR, HIPAA, CCPA)
Practical understanding of how sensitive data moves, leaks, and gets exposed in real environments
Strong written communication skills and ability to document findings clearly
Self-motivated and comfortable working independently on task-based assignments


Nice to Have


Experience with cloud security (AWS, Azure, GCP) or SaaS security posture management
Background in security policy development, risk assessment, or data governance
Familiarity with frameworks like NIST, ISO 27001, or CIS Controls
Prior exposure to AI, machine learning, or data annotation workflows


Why Join Us


Work directly on frontier AI systems alongside world-class research teams
Fully remote and flexible — work on your own schedule
Freelance perks: autonomy, variety, and global collaboration
Contribute to meaningful work that makes AI safer and more security-aware
Potential for ongoing work and contract extension

Title: Intermediate Mining Data Analyst
Description:
Be part of something incredible. Metals are necessary for our world, but mining historically faced issues with waste, sustainability, and inefficiencies. MineSense is changing that. Our solutions reduce mining waste and environmental impacts while increasing profits and efficiencies. Backed by the best in venture capital and government funds, and recognized by tech industry and sustainability programs, MineSense is doing what many said could not be done: improving mining for people and planet.







We are looking to add an Intermediate Mining Data Analyst to our GeoScience team. The role will report to the Manager, GeoScience and be based out of the Vancouver office. The ideal candidate will have a strong interest in detailed data analysis and statistics within the mining industry. You will work with clients globally, to incorporate MineSense solutions into their ore control processes and show them the value of using our systems. If you enjoy working with data, collaborating with multidisciplinary groups, and have a mining, geology, or geophysics background, we want to hear from you!













Job Responsibilities:




Helping new and existing clients leverage MineSense technology in their grade control engineering processes, which includes developing client facing reports and presentations.
Collaborating with internal teams to develop prediction models by correlating mine grade measurements or estimates with our sensor results to identify patterns, trends or other data features that relate to grade, texture, mineralogy, or lithology.
Working with the Field Service and Product Development team to further enhance our products and services through system data analysis and data quality assurance/control.
Determining and tracking data model accuracy and precision through comparison with sampled assay grades or grade estimates.
Reporting and presenting data results/trends to internal teams, as well as external clients.
Working closely with the Data team to create queries, scripts, and reports while ensuring data is well organized and maintained.
Building and maintaining good working relationships with our clients.
Promotes a positive company culture through sharing of information and supporting the learning and development of all colleagues.
Additional duties as occasionally required by your manager.




Qualifications:

Degree in Earth Sciences (Geology, Geophysics), Geostatistics, Data Analytics, Mining Engineering or related field.
Minimum of 2-4 years of relevant working experience, where experience in mining is preferred.
Experience performing statistical analysis or data science in a work setting is preferred.
Mining experience with in-grade control or working with ore control models is preferred.
Experience with data analysis tools such as python or other statistical packages.
Experience with SQL is preferred.
Experience and passion for solving analytical problems involving diverse data sets using quantitative approaches to generate insights.
Attention to detail and an interest in problem solving.
Oral and written proficiency in English is required.
Additional oral and written proficiency in Spanish is strongly preferred.
The ability to travel and support global offices is required (10-30%).
Legally able to work in Canada.




The salary offered to the successful candidate will be based on their experience, knowledge and skills they bring to MineSense, market data based on their location as well as internal equity. Keeping this mind, the salary offered can be outside of the range posted.




Our total rewards package offers market-researched benefits, promotes internal equity, and supports team members at all levels of our organization, and includes the following:




Our total rewards package includes:

· A competitive salary and bonus.

· Matching RRSP or 401K program (for Canadian and US employees)

· A comprehensive benefit program including an employee assistance program.

· Learning and career development programs and resources (live sessions and on-demand)

· Transit Subsidy (for roles based in Vancouver, BC)

· Hybrid work (for roles that do not require to be onsite)

· 3 weeks' vacation or more depending on the position

· Global and local company organized social events and celebrations to connect with your team




Here's what you can expect as a part of the MineSense Team:




When you join MineSense, a trailblazing Canadian mining technology company, you become a part of our purpose to maximize global metals and minimize global impacts. We’re not just a company; we’re a Community of Innovators who forge a path toward a sustainable future. Here’s why we want you to be excited:




Purpose-Driven: At MineSense, we don’t just clock in—we live our purpose. We’re on a mission to revolutionize the mining industry, and every day, we’re one step closer to a cleaner, greener world.

Values That Resonate: Our core values—Innovation, Belonging, Resilience, and Adding Value—aren’t just buzzwords. They’re the bedrock of our culture. When you step into our team, you’re not just an employee; you’re a steward of these values.

Impactful Work: Imagine contributing to a cleaner, more sustainable planet. At MineSense, you’re shaping the future of cleantech and developing cutting-edge technology that literally hasn't been developed before. Your work matters, and it echoes beyond our walls.

People-Centric: We’re not just about profits; we’re about people. Our organization cares deeply about its employees. When you join us, you become part of a community that supports growth, well-being, and success.




Ready to make your mark? Join MineSense and be part of something bigger than yourself.




MineSense is an equal opportunity employer and is committed to providing accommodation to individuals with disabilities. Should you require accommodation during the recruitment or hiring process, please feel free to indicate your needs while applying. Information received related to accommodation needs of applicants will be addressed confidentially.

All applications are welcome, even if you don't meet all the requirements, so please do not hesitate to apply.







We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

Title: Market Analyst
Description:
Job Description

Onni is seeking a highly skilled and analytical Equity Market Analyst to join our dynamic team. The successful candidate will play a pivotal role in evaluating publicly/privately traded companies, with a strong focus on the mining sector. This position requires at least 5 years of proven experience in equity research, investment analysis, or a related field.

Perks & Benefits


Competitive pay & a Comprehensive Benefits Package.
Benefits include company-paid extended health care, dental care, vision care, life insurance, critical illness, and critical life insurance.
Annual Education Allowance
Generous Referral Program
Fun & collaborative company culture with lots of team building events
Local discounts to restaurants, clothing stores, personal training, and more
Free access to a fully equipped gym
Friends and Family rates for hospitality brands.


What You Will Do


Conduct detailed financial analysis and valuations of equities, particularly within the resource and mining industries.
Build and maintain financial models, including DCF, comparables, and scenario analysis.
Monitor and interpret relevant macroeconomic, industry, and company-specific developments that impact investment opportunities.
Generate investment ideas supported by thorough research, and communicate these to the broader investment team through written reports and presentations.
Track key performance indicators (KPIs), commodity price trends, and regulatory changes affecting mining and related companies.
Collaborate with portfolio managers and other analysts to develop sector-specific strategies and identify potential investment risks.


What You Will Bring


Minimum 5 years of experience as an equity research analyst, buy-side/sell-side analyst, or a similar role in investment banking or asset management.
Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Economics, Accounting, or a related discipline (Master’s or CFA preferred).
Deep sector knowledge of commodities, mining operations, and resource-based economies is highly desirable.
Demonstrated track record of actionable investment insights and analytical rigor.
Strong preference for candidates with experience analyzing or working within the mining or natural resources sector.
Solid understanding of global financial markets, with a strong grasp of equity valuation techniques.
Proficiency in financial modeling and analytical tools (Excel, Bloomberg, Capital IQ, etc.).
Exceptional written and verbal communication skills.
Ability to work independently while contributing to a collaborative team environment.
CFA designation or progress toward it is an asset.


About The Company

For over half a century, Onni has been building communities for people to live, work, and play. Our success reflects our commitment to our employees and partners, and our dedication to quality construction, innovation, sustainability, and customer satisfaction. Our expertise expands across North America, with offices in Vancouver, Toronto, Los Angeles, Seattle, Phoenix, and Chicago.

We thank all applicants for their interest; however, only those selected for an interview will be contacted. No phone calls, please.

Salary Range

$120,000 - $250,000

About The Company

Onni

For over half a century, Onni has been building communities for people to live, work, and play. Our success reflects our commitment to our employees and partners, and our dedication to quality construction, innovation, sustainability, and customer satisfaction. Our expertise expands across North America, with offices in Vancouver, Toronto, Los Angeles, Seattle, Phoenix, and Chicago.

AI Use

This role may involve the use of artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support research, analysis, content development, design, reporting, or operational efficiency. Employees are expected to use AI responsibly and in compliance with company policies, data privacy requirements, confidentiality obligations, and applicable laws.

All AI-generated or AI-assisted outputs must be reviewed for accuracy, quality, and appropriateness before use. Employees are also expected to disclose when AI tools have been used in the creation of work and must not present AI-generated content as solely their own original work.

How To Apply

Please apply through the link on the job posting and attach your resume and any other required documents.

We thank all applicants for your interest in the Onni Group. Note that only those applicants under consideration will be contacted.

Title: IT Support Specialist - VAN-1054
Description:
PBX Engineering is seeking a passionate IT Support Specialist to work out of our Vancouver office.

This role will provide Tier 1 and Tier 2 IT support for all offices with escalation of complex issues to the Operations Lead and Senior IT Administrator as required.

About PBX Engineering

We are an electrical, systems, and software engineering firm, with offices in BC and Alberta, and expansion occurring in Ontario and Atlantic Canada. For more than 28 years we have diligently curated steady growth to build a successful company of over 150 employees.

Our extensive, diverse, and award-winning project portfolio includes infrastructure that positively impacts the public and the communities where we all live and work.

About The Role

Working closely with the Operations Lead and Senior IT Administrator, the IT Support Specialist will be an active member of the Operations team, with responsibility for tasks in the following areas:


Provide day‑to‑day in‑person and/or remote end‑user IT support for the Vancouver, Victoria, Calgary, and Squamish offices and remote staff
Provide frontline IT support to staff by troubleshooting technical issues and responding to helpdesk ticket service requests in accordance with internal service response expectations
Install firmware updates and performance/security patches on Windows servers and workstations
Configure new and replacement Windows laptops, workstations, and test servers
Set up and maintain Microsoft Office 365/Azure environment, Windows Active Directory (AD), Entra ID, and cloud file share accounts
Support user lifecycle management (new hires, internal moves, and exits), including account provisioning, access changes, and decommissioning
Monitor data backups using Backup Exec and Altaro, including supporting restore testing and issue remediation
Create, maintain, and back up VMware virtual machines
Set up and troubleshoot LAN, WAN, Wi‑Fi, and VPN connectivity
Install and update specialized and company‑licensed applications and software on servers and staff workstations/laptops
Assist with server, workstation, and network disaster‑recovery tasks
Update and maintain IT documentation, including manuals, standard operating procedures, knowledge‑base articles, and inventories
Assist with the administration and day‑to‑day management of the organization’s firewall and network security systems
Create, modify, and document firewall rules to support business applications and user access requests
Work with senior IT staff to implement security best practices, updates, and firmware upgrades
Support VPN configuration and basic troubleshooting for hybrid and remote users
Support endpoint management and configuration
